gmw_00002335.name: SPT0311-58W
gmw_00002335.overview: SPT0311-58W is a heavily obscured starburst galaxy located at a redshift of 6.9 [arXiv:1904.11185, arXiv:2301.02313]. It has a total stellar mass of approximately 1.5 billion solar masses [arXiv:2301.02313] and a [C II] linewidth measured at 1000 km/s [arXiv:1904.11185].

sources.summary: The paper describes SPT0311-58W as the West component of a z=6.9 system with gas, dust, size, and linewidth constraints, finding SNe could explain its dust with limited destruction.

quotes.quote: SPT-S J031132-5823.4 (SPT0311–58) was discovered using the South Pole Telescope Mocanu2013 , and confirmed at a redshift of 6.9 Strandet2017 . With high-resolution ALMA observations, detected continuum, [ CII ] 158 m and [ OIII ] 88 m lines of two components of SPT0311–58, named East and West, and determined their gas and dust masses. Only for SPT0311–58 East was the stellar mass determined because no stellar emission was detected for the West component.

Based on the detection of the [ CII ] emission line HIMIKOclump,SPT0311 , the sizes of these galaxies were measured as 3.9 1.1 1.7 1.1 kpc for HIMIKO, 2.2 kpc for SPT0311-58E, and 7.5 2.0 kpc for SPT0311-58W. The [ CII ] linewidths were measured as 180 50 kms ^ 1 for HIMIKO, 500 kms ^ 1 for SPT0311-58E, and 1000 kms ^ 1 for SPT0311-58W.

In the case of galaxies SPT0311-58W, J1342+0928, and A2744 YD4 it is possible that SNe are responsible for the observed dust. Some dust destruction, but not a significant amount, by SNe would be allowed in these cases as the derived dust yields are above 0.1 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper describes the W galaxy as a hyper-luminous, heavily obscured starburst with a 230 solar-mass-per-year Pa SFR lower limit and a complex Pa velocity offset from [C II].
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The Pa spectra were extracted in an aperture radius equal to 0.5 ( 2.7 kpc) and centred at positions (RA [ deg ] , Dec [ deg ] ) equal to (47.8891167, 58.3926333) for the E galaxy and (47.888027, 58.392719) for the W galaxy (see white apertures in Figure fig:MRS_app ).

quotes.quote: Even though the ALMA observations imply very high star formation rates (SFRs) in the eastern (E) and western (W) galaxies of the system, the H line is, strikingly, not detected in our MRS observations. This fact, together with the detection of the ionised gas phase in Pa , implies very high internal nebular extinction with lower limits ( A_ V ) of 4.2 (E) and 3.9 mag (W) as well as even larger values (5.6 (E) and 10.0 (W)) by spectral energy distribution (SED) fitting analysis. The extinction-corrected Pa lower limits of the SFRs are 383 and 230 M _ yr ^ 1 for the E and W galaxies, respectively. This represents 50 of the SFRs derived from the [ C II ] 158 m line and infrared light for the E galaxy and as low as 6 for the W galaxy. The MIRIM observations reveal a clumpy stellar structure, with each clump having 3 to 5 10 ^ 9 M _ mass in stars, leading to a total stellar mass of 2.0 and 1.5 10 ^ 10 M _ for the E and W galaxies, respectively.

The situation in the W galaxy is more complex, as it appears to be a velocity offset by about +700 km s ^ 1 in the Pa relative to the [ C II ] emitting gas. The nature of this offset and its reality are not fully established and require further investigation.

gmw_00002336.name: SXDF-N1006-2
gmw_00002336.overview: SXDF-N1006-2 is a Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z = 7.2 [arXiv:1606.04989, arXiv:1904.11185]. Observations reveal detected [O III] 88 ̅m emission, but the galaxy exhibits very weak dust continuum and [C II] emissions [arXiv:1606.04989].

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA [O III] detection from the z=7.2 LAE SXDF-NB1006-2, finding weak dust and [C II] emission and unusually low dust-to-metal content.

quotes.quote: To examine the [ O iii ] 88 m line in high- z LAEs, we performed ALMA observations of an LAE at z=7.2 , SXDF-NB1006-2, discovered with the Subaru Telescope . We have also obtained ALMA data of the [ C ii ] 158 m line of this galaxy. The observations and the data reduction are described in . The [ O iii ] line is detected with a significance of 5.3 (Figure 1A) and the obtained line flux is 6.2 10^ 21 W m ^ 2 ; the corresponding luminosity is 3.8 10^ 35 W (Table 1). The [ C ii ] line is not detected at the position of the [ O iii ] emission line and we take the 3 upper limit for the [ C ii ] line flux as <5.3 10^ 22 W m ^ 2 .

The [ O iii ] /far-UV luminosity ratio of SXDF-NB1006-2 is similar to those of nearby dwarf galaxies with an oxygen abundance of 10 to 60 that of the Sun (Fig. 3A), which suggests that the oxygen abundance estimated from the SED modeling is reasonable and chemical enrichment in this young galaxy has already proceeded. On the other hand, the dust IR continuum and the [ C ii ] line of SXDF-NB1006-2 are very weak relative to those of the nearby dwarf galaxies (Fig. 3, B and C). The galaxies at z 3 to 4, from which the [ O iii ] line was detected previously, are IR luminous dusty ones . Their [ O iii ] /IR and [ O iii ] / [ C ii ] luminosity ratios are similar to those of nearby spiral galaxies and are at least one order of magnitude smaller than those of SXDF-NB1006-2. The high [ O iii ] /IR ratio of SXDF-NB1006-2 despite a degree of chemical enrichment (or so-called metallicity) similar to that of the nearby dwarf galaxies indicates a very small mass fraction of dust in elements heavier than helium (or dust-to-metal mass ratio) in SXDF-NB1006-2.

sources.summary: The paper forecasts SXDF-NB1006-2 as the best ALMA 52 micron follow-up target, expecting detectable emission and improved gas-density and metallicity constraints for this high-redshift galaxy.

quotes.quote: Among the current set of ten [ ] 88 m emitters at 6 < z 9 , we forecast 52 m detections (at 6- ) in SXDF-NB1006-2, B14-6566, J0217-0208, and J1211-0118 within on-source observing times of 2-10 hours, provided their gas densities are larger than about n_ H 10^2-10^3 cm ^ 3 . Other targets generally require much longer integration times for a 6- detection. Either successful detections of the 52 m line, or reliable upper limits, will lead to significantly tighter constraints on ISM parameters.

In the high and intermediate density cases, SXDF-NB1006-2 requires the least time for a significant detection ( 2-6 hours). We therefore suggest SXDF-NB1006-2 as a promising first target for an ALMA 52 m follow up measurement.

For instance, in the maximal line ratio case it will be possible to decisively show that SXDF-NB1006-2 has a high metallicity Z 0.092Z_ at 95 confidence, while based on L_ 10 / SFR alone this galaxy's metallicity may be as low as Z=0.011Z_ , again at 95 significance.

sources.summary: The paper describes SXDF-N1006-2 as a z=7.2 Ly-alpha emitter with ALMA [OIII] dynamical-mass and continuum-limit measurements, leaving its dust-production mechanism unconstrained by current upper limits.

quotes.quote: SXDF-N1006-2 was discovered during a survey for Ly Emitters (LAEs) by the Subaru Telescope Shibuya and is located at z = 7.2. used ALMA to detect the [ OIII ] 88 m emission line and, assuming that the galaxy is a circular disk, determined its dynamical mass (Table Tab1 ). The upper limit on the continuum flux at band 6 (1.33 mm) of <0.042 mJy was also obtained.

HIMIKO, CR7, and SXDF only have upper limits for the mass of dust, so we cannot rule out either SNe or AGB stars as dust producers. We can only conclude that one SN in these galaxies could not produce more than 0.01 M_ of dust.

gmw_00002337.name: J1342+0928
gmw_00002337.overview: ULAS J1342+0928 is an ultraluminous quasar at a redshift of z = 7.54 [arXiv:1712.03306, arXiv:1802.06066]. It hosts a supermassive black hole accreting near the Eddington limit [arXiv:1712.03306, arXiv:2404.02960] within an evolved host galaxy rich in gas and dust [arXiv:2404.02960].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1342+0928 as an ultraluminous z=7.54 quasar whose massive black hole strains the CDM timeline but fits R_h=ct growth with Eddington-limited accretion.

quotes.quote: The recent discovery of ULAS J134208.10+092838.61 (henceforth J1342+0928) (Banados et al. 2017), an ultraluminous quasar at redshift z=7.54 , emphasizes more than ever the time compression problem in the early CDM universe. Weighing in at a mass of M=7.8^ +3.3 _ 1.9 10^8 M_ , this supermassive black hole should have taken over 820 Myr to grow via standard Eddington-limited accretion. Yet we see it barely several hundred Myr after Pop II and III supernovae could have created the 5-25 M_ seeds to initiate the black-hole growth. Worse, this timeline would suggest that J1342+0928 started growing 130 Myr before the big bang, which is completely unrealistic (Melia 2013a; Melia McClintock 2015). And what is particularly challenging to the concordance model is that J1342+0928 is seen to be accreting at 1.5^ +0.5 _ 0.4 times the Eddington rate, arguing against any attempt to mitigate the compression problem by invoking exotic, greatly super-Eddington growth (Volonteri Rees 2005; Pacucci et al. 2015; Inayoshi et al. 2016).

In this paper, we aim to demonstrate that the discovery of this quasar instead strongly confirms the cosmological timeline predicted by the R_ h =ct universe. We assume conventional Eddington-limited accretion and the time versus redshift relation in this model to calculate when a seed needed to start growing as a function of its mass in order to reach the observed mass of J1342+0928 at z=7.54 . Contrary to the tension created in the standard model by the appearance of this massive quasar so early in its history, we find that in the R_ h =ct cosmology, a 10 M_ seed at z 15 (the start of the Epoch of Reionization at t 878 Myr) would have easily grown into an 8 10^8 M_ black hole at z=7.54 ( t 1.65 Gyr) via conventional Eddington-limited accretion.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes ULAS J1342+0928 as a z=7.54 quasar with a prominent damping wing, very small proximity zone, and evidence for a substantially neutral surrounding IGM.

quotes.quote: Analysis of the recently discovered quasar ULAS J1342+0928 Banados18 at z=7.54 suggests that it exhibits a much more prominent damping wing absorption signal than ULAS J1120+0641, consistent with a predominantly neutral IGM. However, its C 4 line exhibits a blueshift more than twice that of ULAS J1120+0641, and so only a very small number of similar quasars exist in lower redshift samples. Banados18 estimated the intrinsic spectrum of ULAS J1342+0928 by constructing a composite spectrum from 46 SDSS/BOSS quasars with similar C 4 emission line properties and estimated the uncertainty via measuring the residuals between the composite and its constituent quasar spectra.

In Figure fig:pisco_pca , we show the red-side fit to the Magellan/FIRE + Gemini/GNIRS spectrum of ULAS J1342+0928 and the predicted blue-side (bias-corrected) continuum from Paper I. We find a best-fit template redshift of z=7.4438 , a blueshift of v=3422 km/s from the systemic frame ( z=7.5413 , Venemans17b ). The red-side spectrum is very different from a typical quasar, however, similar examples do exist in our PCA training set (Paper I), and the PCA model is capable of broadly reproducing the spectrum.

The damping wing signal is clearly stronger and the proximity zone is even smaller than ULAS J1120+0641, despite the slightly higher luminosity of ULAS J1342+0928. Both of these properties point towards a substantially neutral IGM surrounding ULAS J1342+0928.

sources.summary: The paper studies ULAS J1342+0928 as a z=7.54 luminous quasar with a massive accreting black hole, evolved dusty host, and nearby candidate galaxies but no strong LBG overdensity.

quotes.quote: We investigate the 1.1 pMpc ^2 environment of the z = 7.54 luminous quasar ULAS J1342+0928. We search for Lyman-break galaxy candidates (LBG) using deep imaging from 1 the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) \ in the ACS/F814W, WFC3/F105W/F125W bands, and Spitzer/IRAC at 3.6 m and 4.5 m. We report a z_ phot = 7.69^ +0.33 _ 0.23 LBG with mag_ F125W = 26.41 at 223 projected-pkpc from the quasar. Together with one dust-obscured galaxy previously found with ALMA at 27 projected-pkpc and z_ [ C II ] =7.5341 0.0009 venemans_kiloparsec-scale_2020 , suggest a galaxy overdensity around ULAS J1342+0928.

This quasar hosts one of the earliest and most massive SMBHs with a mass 0.9 10^9 , that is actively accreting at near Eddington rates with L_ bol /L_ Edd 1.1 onoue_no_2020 . The host galaxy is already evolved with high amount of gas and dust resulting in SFR of 150 \ yr ^ 1 , with a metallicity comparable to the solar neighborhood novak_alma_2019 . Additionally, a study of the optical/NIR spectrum of \ identified a strong absorber at z=6.8 on its line-of-sight simcoe_interstellar_2020 . This massive and active quasar in the early universe is an ideal candidate to now look for a galaxy overdensity and trace its large-scale structure.

sources.summary: The paper applies QSANNdRA to ULAS J1342+0928, treats it as an outlying z=7.5413 quasar, and constrains the IGM neutral fraction from its reconstructed spectrum.

quotes.quote: In comparison with the extended PCA model, QSANNdRA further achieves an improvement of 22.1 and 16.8 when evaluated on low-redshift quasars most similar to the two high-redshift quasars under consideration, ULAS J1120+0641 at z=7.0851 and ULAS J1342+0928 at z=7.5413 , respectively. Using our more accurate reconstructions of these two z>7 quasars, we estimate the neutral fraction of the IGM using a homogeneous reionization model and find x _ H 1 = 0.25^ +0.05 _ 0.05 at z=7.0851 and x _ H 1 = 0.60^ +0.11 _ 0.11 at z=7.5413 .

In particular, we aim to apply our model to the combined VLT/FORS + Gemini/GNIRS spectrum of ULAS J1120+0641 2011Natur.474..616M and the Magellan/FIRE + Gemini/GNIRS spectrum of ULAS J1342+0928 2018Natur.553..473B . Applying the trained autoencoder to ULAS J1120+0641 and ULAS J1342+0928 and calculating their corresponding mean squared errors revealed that they fall onto the 48th percentile and the 99th percentile in our low-redshift distribution (see figure fig:hist ). While ULAS J1120+0641 seems to be well represented in our low-redshift dataset, ULAS J1342+0928 falls onto the tail-end of our distribution. On the 100 nearest neighbours of ULAS J1342+0928 (at z=7.5413 , shown in purple), we achieve a 16.8 improvement in (left) and a 17.5 improvement in _ (right). Given that we are now confident that our model generalizes to high-redshift objects and performs better than other models in the literature, in this section, we apply QSANNdRA to two high-redshift quasars, namely ULAS J1120+0641 at z=7.0851 2011Natur.474..616M,2017ApJ...837..146V , and ULAS J1342+0928 at z=7.5413 2018Natur.553..473B to reconstruct their blue-side spectra and constrain the neutral gas fraction at their corresponding redshifts.

sources.summary: The paper treats J1342+0928 as a z=7.54 quasar host with [CII], continuum, dust, and molecular-gas constraints, finding SNe could explain its observed dust with limited destruction.

quotes.quote: J1342+0928 is a quasar located at z = 7.54, first detected by Banados2018 . The detection of the [ CII ] 158 m line emission allowed J1342 to determine the dynamical mass of this galaxy based on the virial theorem, and the assumption that the [ CII ] emission comes from a rotating disk. From the detection of the 223.5 GHz continuum, the mass of dust in this galaxy was determined (Table Tab1 ). The limit on the molecular gas mass was derived from the observations of the CO(3-2) line (Table Tab1 ).

In the case of galaxies SPT0311-58W, J1342+0928, and A2744 YD4 it is possible that SNe are responsible for the observed dust. Some dust destruction, but not a significant amount, by SNe would be allowed in these cases as the derived dust yields are above 0.1 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper treats ULAS J1342 + 0928 as a luminous z>7.5 quasar whose damping wing spectrum may be contaminated by proximate low-ionization absorbers rather than only neutral IGM absorption.

quotes.quote: In particular, they uncovered a complex of low-ionization metal absorption systems at z>7.3 towards the quasar ULAS J1342 + 0928 (henceforth J1342), a luminous z>7.5 quasar whose damping wing signal has been well-studied Banados18,Davies18b,Greig19,Greig22,Durovcikova20,Reiman20 . The highest redshift absorption system found by Christensen23 is at z=7.476 , only 2300 km/s from the systemic redshift of the quasar, and could contaminate the observed damping wing if its associated H 1 column density is large enough.

In this work, we have investigated the implications of the discovery of weak low-ionization absorption systems within several thousand km/s from the quasar ULAS J1342 + 0928 for its use as a probe of the neutral intergalactic medium during the epoch of reionization. From the equivalent widths of the Mg 2 and O 1 absorption lines, we infer plausible column densities for O 1 , and convert these into H 1 column densities assuming a range of oxygen abundances. For [ O / H ] 3 , the strongest absorbers reach high enough N_ HI , into the damped Ly regime with N_ HI >20.3 , to have an appreciable contribution to the absorption signal seen in the quasar spectrum. At a metallicity of [ O / H ] =-3.5 , the proximate absorbers are strong enough to dominate the observed absorption signal, while much lower metallicites [ O / H ] 4 would lead to stronger absorption than allowed by the observed spectrum.

sources.summary: ULAS J1342+0928 is treated as an early high-redshift quasar that could form like J1120 from a direct-collapse seed, with simulated black-hole mass and accretion matching observations.

quotes.quote: But the recent discoveries of ULAS J1120+0641, a 2 10^9 \ BH at z = 7.1, and ULAS J1342+0928, a 8.0 10^ 8 \ BH at z = 7.5, now push the era of quasar formation up to just 690 Myr after the Big Bang. Here we report new cosmological simulations of SMBHs with X-rays fully coupled to primordial chemistry and hydrodynamics that show that J1120 and J1342 can form from direct collapse black holes (DCBHs) if their growth is fed by cold, dense accretion streams, like those thought to fuel rapid star formation in some galaxies at later epochs.

While our numerical simulations show that J1120 can be explained as a DCBH that formed at the intersection of cold accretion streams at z 20, they also reveal that J1342 could have formed in the same way. The BH reaches 1.18 10^ 8 \ by z = 7.5 , slightly above the error bar in observed J1342 mass but well within the uncertainty of a factor of three associated with the use of the Mg II line as a proxy for its luminosity. As shown in the right panel of Fig. fig:bhmass , we also obtain accretion rates of 27 \ yr ^ 1 at this redshift, in good agreement with observations.

sources.summary: The paper models ULAS J1342+0928 at z=7.54, emphasizing extreme emission-line properties, a large C 4 blueshift, damping-wing-like absorption redward of Ly, and continuum-prediction calibration.

quotes.quote: We then computed predictions for the blue-side continua of the two quasars currently known at z>7 : ULAS J1120+0641 ( z=7.09 ) and ULAS J1342+0928 ( z=7.54 ). Both of these quasars are known to exhibit extreme emission line properties, so we individually calibrated the precision of the continuum predictions from similar quasars in the training set. We find that both z>7 quasars, and in particular ULAS J1342+0928, show signs of damping wing-like absorption at wavelengths redward of Ly .

The two quasars known at z>7 , and in particular the newly discovered highest-redshift quasar ULAS J1342+0928 Banados17 , show very large C 4 blueshifts relative to the general quasar population. The blueshift of the C 4 emission line is correlated with properties of the Ly emission line (e.g. Richards11 ), and thus must be properly accounted for when modeling the Ly region of high-redshift quasars BB15 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the z=7.54 QSO ULASJ1342+0928/J1342 for Ly damping-wing absorption, reconstructs its intrinsic profile, and finds no strong evidence for a significantly neutral IGM.

quotes.quote: The recent detection of ULASJ1342+0928, a bright QSO at z=7.54 , provides a powerful probe of the ionisation state of the intervening intergalactic medium, potentially allowing us to set strong constraints on the epoch of reionisation (EoR). Here we quantify the presence of Ly damping wing absorption from the EoR in the spectrum of ULASJ1342+0928. Our Bayesian framework simultaneously accounts for uncertainties on: (i) the intrinsic QSO emission (obtained from reconstructing the \ profile from a covariance matrix of emission lines) and (ii) the distribution of regions during reionisation (obtained from three different 1.6 ^3 Gpc ^3 simulations spanning the range of plausible EoR morphologies).
We do not find strong evidence for J1342 to be in a significantly neutral IGM. Depending on the EoR model, the spectrum is consistent with being in a fully-ionised Universe at 1--2 .

sources.summary: The paper describes J1342+0928 as a very distant HYPERION QSO with ALMA/NOEMA constraints on dust, star formation, and multiple ISM fine-structure lines.

quotes.quote: ULAS J134208.10+092838.35 (hereafter J1342+0928) at z=7.54 , the most distant QSO known to date, was discovered by banados2018 who reported an absolute AB magnitude M_ 1450 =-26.8 , bolometric luminosity of L_ bol =10^ 13 \ L_ , and an SMBH mass of 8 10^8 \ M_ . It was followed-up with NOEMA resulting in the detection of bright [ CII ] emission and upper limits on several CO lines venemans2017a . novak2019 presented ALMA observations of the dust continuum and the ISM of the host galaxy J1342+0928. They well constrained the Rayleigh-Jeans tail of the dust SED, deriving a M_ dust =3.5 10^7 \ M_ and a SFR 150 \ M_ yr^ 1 , fixing the temperature at 47 K. They also detected many atomic fine structure line, such as [ CII ] , [ NII ] , [ OIII ] , and limits on [ CI ] , [ OI ] and multiple CO transitions (with a tentative stack detection).

sources.summary: The paper describes ULAS J1342+0928 as a z>7 quasar at z=7.54 found from UKIDSS, WISE, and DECaLS data, superseding ULAS J1120+0641 as the redshift record holder.

quotes.quote: Being observed only one billion years after the Big Bang, z 7 quasars are a unique opportunity for exploring the early Universe. However, only two z 7 quasars have been discovered in near-infrared surveys: the quasars ULAS J1120+0641 and ULAS J1342+0928 at z = 7.09 and z = 7.54 , respectively.

The use of such data allowed Mortlock2011 to discover the previous redshift record holder, ULAS J1120+0641 at z=7.09 , before it was superceded by ULAS J1342+0928 at z=7.54, for which Banados2018 mined data from the UKIDSS Large Area Survey Lawrence2007 , the Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer ALLWISE, Wright2010 , and the DECam Legacy Survey (DECaLS http://legacysurvey.org/decamls/ ). However, despite all these efforts, these two quasars are the only ones found at redshift z > 7 to date.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes ULAS J1342+0928's rest-frame UV absorption to measure ionizing photons, luminosity, and radiative efficiency, finding evidence for damping absorption and low efficiency.

quotes.quote: The two highest redshift quasars known, ULAS J1120+0641 Mortlock11 (henceforth J1120+0641) at z=7.09 , and ULAS J1342+0928 Banados18 (henceforth J1342+0928) at z=7.54 , both exhibit truncated proximity zones Bolton11,Davies18b (compared to similarly-luminous quasars at z 6 - 6.5 , Eilers17 ) and show strong evidence for damping wing absorption Mortlock11,Bolton11,Greig17b,Banados18,Davies18b .

For J1120+0641 and J1342+0928, we compute ionizing photon emission rates of N _ ion ^ J1120 =1.2 10^ 57 s ^ 1 and N _ ion ^ J1342 =1.4 10^ 57 s ^ 1 , and bolometric luminosities of L_ bol ^ J1120 =2.7 10^ 13 L_ and L_ bol ^ J1342 =3.1 10^ 13 L_ , respectively.
The posterior median radiative efficiencies of J1120+0641 and J1342+0928 are 0.08 and 0.1 , respectively, and the canonical 10 is ruled out at greater than 98 per cent probability by each quasar.

sources.summary: The appendix says this quasar lacks one detected line near a noisy order edge, but another line was modeled and extremely broad lines used single Gaussian components.

quotes.quote: ULAS J1342+0928 The line is not detected in this spectrum as it falls close to the red edge of the last echelle order, which is dominated by noise. However, we are able to include the line in our model. Due to the extremely broad nature of the and lines both were modeled using only a single Gaussian component each.

sources.summary: The paper includes J1342+0928 as a z=7.54 quasar observed by Chandra, detected by wavdetect, and identified as the most distant quasar in its sample.

quotes.quote: Specifically, J112001.48+064124.3 hereafter J1120+0641, Mortlock11 at z=7.09 was observed in Cycle 12 Page14 , J134208.10 +092838.6 hereafter J1342+0928, Banados18b at z=7.54 was observed in Cycle 18 Banados18a , and four other quasars were observed in Cycle 19 Vito19 .
Although J1342+0928, the most distant quasar in our sample, is far from the relation found by , such outliers in SDSS quasars with smaller blueshift velocities are also seen in this plot.

sources.summary: The paper treats J1342+0928 as one of four most distant quasars whose SMBH can be explained by a Supermassive Dark Star heavy-seed pathway rather than only direct collapse.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we demonstrate that Supermassive Dark Stars (SMDSs) offer an equally valid solution to the mystery of the first quasars, by examining the four most distant known quasars: UHZ1, J0313-1806, J1342+0928, and J1007+2115, with particular emphasis on UHZ1.

In this paper we point out an alternative interpretation: the mystery posed by the SMBHs powering the most distant four quasars (UHZ1, J0313-1806, J1342+0928, and J1007+2115) can be equally well solved by SMBHs seeded by Supermassive Dark Stars.
Thus, SMDSs can provide natural seeds for the SMBHs powering the most distant quasars: UHZ1, J0313–1806, J1342+0928, J1007+2115, for which a low mass seed is highly unlikely  Bogdan:2023UHZ1 .

gmw_00002338.name: MACS0416 Y1
gmw_00002338.overview: MACS0416 Y1 is a bright Lyman-break galaxy [arXiv:1904.11185] at a spectroscopic redshift of approximately z = 8.31 [arXiv:1806.04132, arXiv:2310.03063]. It features a young, star-forming stellar component [arXiv:1806.04132] with three ionized clumps [arXiv:2303.11539], turbulent gas [arXiv:2303.11539], and a possible feedback-driven superbubble [arXiv:2303.11539].

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA detection of [O III] and dust continuum in this z=8.3118 lensed LBG, deriving dust, infrared luminosity, stellar mass, metallicity, and star-formation properties.

quotes.quote: This detection confirms the LBG with a spectroscopic redshift of z = 8.3118 0.0003 , making this object one of the furthest galaxies ever identified spectroscopically. The observed 850 flux density of 137 26 Jy corresponds to a de-lensed total infrared (IR) luminosity of = (1.7 0.3) 10^ 11 L_ if assuming a dust temperature of T_ dust = 50 K and an emissivity index of = 1.5 , yielding a large dust mass of 4 10^6 M_ . The ultraviolet-to-far IR spectral energy distribution modeling where the emissivity model is incorporated suggests the presence of a young ( _ age 4 Myr), star-forming (SFR 60 M_ yr ^ 1 ), moderately metal-polluted ( Z 0.2 Z_ ) stellar component with a mass of M_ star = 3 10^8 M_ . An analytic dust mass evolution model with a single episode of star-formation does not reproduce the metallicity and dust mass in _ age 4 Myr, suggesting a pre-existing evolved stellar component with M_ star 3 10^9 M_ and _ age 0.3 Gyr as the origin of the dust mass. galaxies: formation --- galaxies: ISM --- galaxies: high-redshift --- dust, extinction How and when metal enrichment happened in the epoch of reionization (EoR) is one of the most fundamental questions in modern astronomy.

The peak ICRS position is ( _ ICRS , _ ICRS )= (04^h16^m09 423 0 002, 24 05' 35 50 0 01) . This elongation is not likely due to the cluster magnification but the intrinsic shape of the LBG, because its elongation does not align with the lensing shear direction. th Blind Detection of [ O III ] 88 At the position of the dust emission, we detect an emission line feature at 364.377 0.012 GHz, strongly suggesting the 88 emission line at z = 8.3118 0.0003 (Figure fig:spectrum ).

sources.summary: The paper resolves MACS0416 Y1 with ALMA at z=8.312, finding three ionized clumps, separated dust and UV structures, turbulent gas, and a possible feedback-driven superbubble.

quotes.quote: We present the results of 300 pc resolution ALMA imaging of the 88 line and dust continuum emission from a z = 8.312 Lyman break galaxy MACS0416 Y1. The velocity-integrated emission has three peaks which are likely associated with three young stellar clumps of MACS0416 Y1, while the channel map shows a complicated velocity structure with little indication of a global velocity gradient unlike what was found in 158 at a larger scale, suggesting random bulk motion of ionized gas clouds inside the galaxy. In contrast, dust emission appears as two individual clumps apparently separating or bridging the /stellar clumps. The cross correlation coefficient between dust and ultraviolet-related emission (i.e., and ultraviolet continuum) is unity on a galactic scale, while it drops at < 1 kpc, suggesting well mixed geometry of multi-phase interstellar media on sub-kpc scales.

If the cutoff scale characterizes different stages of star formation, the cutoff scale can be explained by gravitational instability of turbulent gas. We also report on a kpc-scale off-center cavity embedded in the dust continuum image. This could be a superbubble producing galactic-scale outflows, since the energy injection from the 4 Myr starburst suggested by a spectral energy distribution analysis is large enough to push the surrounding media creating a kpc-scale cavity.

sources.summary: The paper describes MACS0416 Y1 as a bright z~8 Lyman-break galaxy with [OIII], dust continuum, and stellar-mass measurements, but says stellar-mass uncertainty prevents identifying its dust-production mechanism.

quotes.quote: MACS0416 Y1 is one of the brightest Lyman Break Galaxies (LBGs) at z 8 Infante2015,Laporte2015 . Using the detection of the [ OIII ] 88 m line and the dust continuum, MACS confirmed its redshift as 8.3118, and measured its dust mass (assuming a dust temperature of 40 and 50 K). From the optical emission the stellar mass was determined (Table Tab1 ). In our analysis, the lower error bar for the stellar mass was modified disregarding model solutions with ages lower than one million years (Y. Tamura, private communication).

For the last galaxy, MACS0416 Y1, we also cannot rule out the dust production mechanism because the large uncertainty on the measurement of its stellar mass results in a derived dust yield consistent with zero.

sources.summary: The paper finds MACS0416 Y1 photometry and IRAC excess can be fit, but the models struggle with ALMA flux and continuum; its two-component age estimate is a lower limit.

quotes.quote: For Y1 and B14, on the other hand, the HST photometry and Spitzer /IRAC excess are well reproduced, but the models are unable to simultaneously match both the [ ] 88 m flux and dust continuum (in the case of B14, the [ ] 88 m flux is matched but the dust continuum is underpredicted, and in the case of Y1 the [ ] 88 m flux is underpredicted and dust continuum overpredicted). In comparing these fits to those assuming a single-component only, although the HST photometry can be reasonably well-reproduced for JD1 and YD4, the single-component fits fail to simultaneously reproduce both the ALMA constraints and the Spitzer /IRAC excess. In the cases of Y1 and B14, the ALMA constraints are better matched by the one-component fit, however the Spitzer /IRAC excess is only partially matched.

For JD1, YD4 and Y1, the contribution to the IRAC fluxes from the recent burst of star formation is only 10-30 and the older component (characterised by Balmer ratios of 2) dominates the flux at 70 . This is due primarily to the relative weakness of the [ ] +H lines, for which we measure an equivalent width, EW( [ ] +H ) 25-106 \ (compared to EW( [ ] +H ) 450-7700 \ for a single-component). The exception to this trend is B14, whose IRAC fluxes remain dominated by the younger component by 60-75 (EW( [ ] +H ) 200 \ for the two-component model and EW( [ ] +H ) 3460 \ for the one-component model). The total stellar mass (corrected for lensing for those lensed sources) for all of these fits range from log M _ * =8.88-10.19 M _ , with virtually all of the total stellar mass also coming from the earlier period of star formation and the most recent burst contributing primarily through the presence of weak-to-moderate nebular emission. Furthermore, we also note a difference in the ages of the galaxies, as determined by the onset of the earlier burst in the two-component model or the age of the single-component model. We find an increase in age for each of the four galaxies when multiple components are used, with ages of 80, 260, 140 and 20 Myrs characterising the two-component fits of JD1, YD4, Y1, and B14, and such ages decreasing down to 1, 2, 1 and 3 Myrs assuming on a single burst of recent star formation. In the case of Y1, the age estimate from the two-component fit is in fact a lower limit, since we only have an upper limit for the Spitzer /IRAC 3.6 m photometry.

sources.summary: The paper reports that MACSJ0416-Y1 has co-spatial FIR line detections consistent with prior work, with carbon-line emission more extended than oxygen-line emission.

quotes.quote: For the remaining systems (MACSJ0416-Y1, B14-65666, J0235, J1211, and J0217) for which co-spatial \ and \ emissions have been already reported in the literature Tamura:2019, Hashimoto:2019,Harikane:2019, Bakx:2020 , we have found results consistent with previous works. In Table tab:summary we list the \ and \ luminosities. Since for these galaxies the two FIR lines have been detected with high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR >8 ), we take advantage of these observations to investigate the extent of the FIR lines, as shown in Fig. fig:size (and Table tab:appendix ). We notice that \ is systematically larger than the \ and its extent is about two times larger than that of oxygen line. This is consistent with recent simulations by where the \ is concentrated in a compact region of 0.85 kpc, while the \ arise from a more extended area with a radius of 1.54 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper uses MACS0416-Y1 as the demonstration case for its single-band ALMA dust method, estimating a high dust temperature while treating the z=8.3118 source as dust-temperature unconstrained.

quotes.quote: to demonstrate the method proposed herein, we determined the dust temperature ( T_ d =95^ +13 _ 17 K ) of a z 8.3 star-forming galaxy, MACS0416-Y1.

MACS0416-Y1 ( z=8.3118 ) exhibits a significant dust continuum detection in band-8 ( _ rest 90 m ; Tamura2019 ); however, observation of the sensitive follow-up band-5 ( _ rest 160 m ) resulted in non-detection of the dust continuum. The strong lower limits of the dust temperature show extremely high dust temperatures of T_ d 80 K Bakx2020 . We therefore treat MACS0416-Y1 as a dust-temperature unconstrained sample. We used flux density measurements from Bakx2021 and size measurements from Tamura2019 .

sources.summary: The paper includes MACS 0416-Y1 in the CANUCS double-break sample, measuring its JWST photometry, lensing magnification, and a NIRSpec redshift consistent with the previous ALMA redshift.

quotes.quote: Finally, we note that our sample includes the MACS 0416-Y1 source from (i.e., id=3107165). This source is extended and has a reported ALMA redshift of z=8.3118 Tamura2019 . We measure its magnitude in F444W =24.74 0.06 and its magnification to be =1.63 0.01 , thus meeting the selection criteria even accounting for lensing.

One of the five galaxies (id=3107165) was previously observed with ALMA and the spectroscopic redshift was reported to be z_ spec =8.3118 MACS 0416-Y1 Tamura2019 . Our NIRSpec redshift of this galaxy is z_ spec =8.3184 0.0005 , which is in close agreement with the previous ALMA measurement.

sources.summary: The paper identifies HFF2C-9-1 as a bright z ~ 9 candidate missed by CLASH because key infrared coverage was incomplete, while full HFF photometry places it in the z ~ 9 selection region.

quotes.quote: However, the two significantly brighter objects, HFF2C-9-1 and HFF2C-9-2 (which both have H_ 160 26.5 ) should certainly have been detected in the CLASH imaging, and so we have explored carefully why these objects might not have been reported as z 9 galaxies by the CLASH studies. It transpires that the first of these objects, HFF2C-9-1, fell just outside the J_ 125 and J_ 140 imaging (and very close to the edge of the H_ 160 imaging) obtained in the CLASH programme (although it is covered by the CLASH J_ 110 imaging), and so could not be included in the colour-colour criterion applied by Bouwens et al. (2014). Given that we now have coverage of this object in Y_ 105 , J_ 125 , J_ 140 , H_ 160 we have combined the new HFF photometry with the CLASH J_ 110 photometry to deduce where this object would appear in the colour-selection plot utilised by Bouwens et al. (2014).
Fig. 4 demonstrates that HFF2C-9-1 would indeed have been selected as a z 9 galaxy by Bouwens et al. (2014) had the complete near-infrared photometry been available.

sources.summary: The paper identifies M0416 99 as a z=8.31 ALMA-confirmed galaxy, notes the lack of X-shooter rest-frame UV lines, and reports comparable KMOS flux limits.

quotes.quote: M0416 99 (a.k.a M0416 Y1) was also confirmed via [ ] 88 m emission in ALMA observations at z=8.31 by Tamura2018 . They also observed the object with X-shooter and found no rest-frame UV emission lines, with a 5 \ flux limit of <8.0 10^-18 \ (if the line if offset by up to 250 ). Our KMOS flux median limits are of a comparable depth ( <3.4 10^-18 ).

sources.summary: For Y1 at redshift 8.3, the paper finds loose metallicity lower bounds, comparatively high allowed gas-density limits, and notes tension with another metallicity-limit method.

quotes.quote: For example, the 1- ( 2- ) lower bound on the metallicity for J0217, at redshift 6.2, is 0.59 Z_ ( 0.42 Z_ ). On the other hand, the lower bound on metallicity is rather loose for some of the galaxies in the sample, such as Y1 at redshift 8.3, for which Z 1.2 10^ 2 Z_ and Z 1.6 10^ 3 Z_ at 1- and 2- confidence, respectively. Likewise, the gas density bounds are variable with n_ H 25 (63) cm ^ 3 at 1- ( 2- ) confidence for J0217, but n_ H 1.0 10^3 (6.3 10^3) cm ^ 3 at 1- ( 2- ) for Y1.

The one exception is Y1, for which our lower bound is 0.7 dex smaller than their limit; this may be related to the treatment of the very asymmetric error bar in this galaxy or it may owe to differences in our methodologies.

gmw_00002339.name: A2744 YD4
gmw_00002339.overview: A2744 YD4 is a gravitationally lensed Lyman-break galaxy located at a redshift of z = 8.38 [arXiv:1904.11185, arXiv:1809.11032]. It is characterized by a high dust temperature [arXiv:1802.07772], intense oxygen emission [arXiv:1906.01937], and early dust production likely driven by supernovae [arXiv:1904.11185].

sources.summary: The paper describes A2744 YD4 as a lensed z=8.38 Lyman-break galaxy with ALMA redshift and dust measurements, finding SNe could produce its dust if destruction is limited.

quotes.quote: A2744 YD4 at z = 8.38 is an LBG lensed by the Pandora Cluster. This object was observed in the Hubble Frontier Fields (HFF) by . The ALMA detection of the [ OIII ] 88 m line allowed the confirmation of its redshift A2744 . Based on the dust continuum detection and the optical emission, estimated the mass of dust and stars in this galaxy (Table Tab1 ).

In the case of galaxies SPT0311-58W, J1342+0928, and A2744 YD4 it is possible that SNe are responsible for the observed dust. Some dust destruction, but not a significant amount, by SNe would be allowed in these cases as the derived dust yields are above 0.1 M_ . This is in line with the result of Gall2018 that dust in distant galaxies (including A2744 YD4) was formed by SNe, which requires very little dust destruction. Again, the required dust yields per AGB star for these three galaxies are significantly above the theoretical limit, so AGB stars have not contributed substantially to the dust production in these galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper treats A2744 2248 as a z=8.38 confirmed galaxy with tentative Ly-alpha below the KMOS limit, finding it has negligible effect on neutral-fraction inference.

quotes.quote: A2744 2248 (a.k.a. A2744 YD4) was confirmed at z=8.38 via [ ] 88 m emission in ALMA, and a tentative \ emission line was also reported with line flux (1.82 0.64) 10^ 18 \ and EW=10.7 2.7 Laporte2017 , which is well below our limit for that object. As discussed in Section  sec:reionization_infer we find that treating the object as a detection in our inference has a negligible impact on our inferred limits on the neutral fraction.

We also tested the inference with and without including the z=8.38 marginal detection of \ in object A2744 2248 by Laporte2017 with spectroscopic confirmation from \ emission in ALMA observations. We use the EW reported by Laporte2017 W=10.7 2.7 , which is well below our 5 limit for that object ( <53 ). Despite the potential detection, the posterior distribution for this single object strongly favours a mostly neutral IGM due to its very low EW and low significance. We did our inference using both our KMOS spectra and the Laporte2017 measurement for this object and found it to have a negligible impact on our final posterior (changing the inferred limit by only 0.01 ), demonstrating that deep limits on non-detections have a lot of power in our inferences. Our quoted posterior limits include the object as a non-detection.

sources.summary: A2744 09 is a massive A2744 RPS galaxy with a long south-west gas tail, undisturbed stellar kinematics, low tail extinction, and possible ionization-cone signatures.

quotes.quote: A2744 09 is one of the most massive galaxies subject to ram-pressure stripping in A2744 ( (M_ /M_ )=10.57 ). From the main body of the galaxy a long gas tail extends towards south-west, partially maintaining the velocity gradient of the gas in the disk, at least in the first part of the tail. Again, the stellar kinematics is undisturbed and substantially coincident with the gas rotation in the disk ( 150 ). The tail length is 10 arcsec i.e.

42 kpc at the cluster redshift. The gas extinction is very low in the tail, but its value rises within the stellar disk in the east part where the line ratios indicate the presence of a LINER-like, possibly shocked region (two lowest panels in the right columns of Fig. fig:a2744_04_09 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats RXCJ 0014.3-3022 SE B as a main BCG component in a close paired system whose surrounding diffuse light is bluer than the BCG cores.

quotes.quote: The second visual pair of BCGs, to the south-east, (i.e., RXCJ 0014.3 3022 SE A and B) is actually made of two very close pairs of galaxies at consistent photometric redshifts. A main component (i.e., one of the two BCGs) can be clearly identified in each of these very close pairs (see also Fig. FigBR1b ). The two BCGs are separated by 120 kpc on the plane of the sky and by 4119 416 km s^ 1 (rest frame) along the line of sight (from Braglia et al. 2007). Hence, likely RXCJ 0014.3 3022 SE is not an interacting pair.

Conversely, we find that the B-R colour of the diffuse stellar emission detected around the two pairs of BCGs in the merging cluster RXCJ 0014.3 3022 is significantly bluer than the central regions of the four BCGs. If the contribution of intracluster stars to the diffuse stellar emission around BCGs is not negligible, this means that the intracluster light has multiple origins, possibly linked to the dynamical state of a cluster.

sources.summary: The lens model treats BCG-N as one of two brightest cluster galaxies with individually optimized mass and ellipticity, centered halo components, nearby radial arcs, and reference luminosity.

quotes.quote: The second term corresponds to the mass contribution of the two brightest cluster galaxies (BCGs), BCG-N and BCG-S in Figure fig:RGB , which are individually optimized in the lens model.

The cluster-scale component of our new lens models ( _i^ halo ) is parametrized by two not truncated elliptical dPIEs, which are centered on the BCGs, denominated BCG-N and BCG-S in Figure fig:RGB . The halos are left free to move within a small range around the BCG positions (see table:inout_lensing ). Due to the presence of radial arcs in the vicinity of both BCGs (namely systems 2 and 4, see Figure fig:RGB and Table tab:multiple_images ), and to improve their reconstruction, the parameters describing the mass contribution, and the ellipticity, of both BCGs are individually optimized (i.e., they are modeled outside of the scaling relations). This consists of 4 additional free parameters for each profile.
The reference luminosity L_ ref =17.34 corresponds to the BCG-N (see Figure fig:RGB ) magnitude in the HST F160W band.

sources.summary: The paper models A2744 YD4 as a lensed z=8.38 dusty galaxy, finding a higher star formation rate, low dust-to-metal ratio, hot dust, and smaller dust mass than prior SED estimates.

quotes.quote: In particular, recently, 17cit hereafter Laporte2017 reported the detection of a very young galaxy (A2744 YD4), lensed by Abell 2744, that is located at the fringe of Cosmic Dawn, with a redshift of z=8.38 . Using multi-wavelength data from HST, XSHOOTER, and ALMA, they measure its SED from the rest frame UV to the far Infrared (FIR). They estimate the stellar/dust mass, star formation rate (SFR), and other properties using both simple analytic estimates and SED fitting, and find A2744 YD4 to have a stellar mass of around 10 ^9 M _ , a SFR of about 20 M _ /yr, and a dust mass of 6 10^6 M _ .

The key findings are: a) The star formation rate of A2744 YD4 is SFR = 78 , i.e. a factor 4 higher compared to the Spectral Energy Distribution fitting employed by . b) Dust attenuation is consistent with a Milky Way extinction curve and we find a mean optical depth _V=1.4 . ) The dust-to-metal ratio is low, f_ d 0.08 , implying that early dust formation is rather inefficient (or that dust destruction is very efficient). d) The luminosity-weighted dust temperature is high, T_d=91 23 K , as a result of the intense interstellar radiation field ( 100 MW). e) Due to the high T_d the ALMA Band 7 detection can be explained by a limited dust mass, M_d=1.6 10^6 M_ . f) Higher dust temperatures might solve the puzzling low infrared excess recently deduced for high- z galaxies from the IRX- relation.

Therefore, our simulations suggest that the physical properties of A2744 YD4 may be significantly different from the parameter estimation in the literature, that is, it might be characterized by a large SFR around 80 and a smaller dust mass of 10^6 , with a significant fraction of the dust heated to high temperatures, boosting the IR.

sources.summary: The paper targets A2744 YD4 with ALMA, confirming strong O III but non-detecting C II, and compares its weak C II emission with simulations and early-galaxy ISM diagnostics.

quotes.quote: Here we present new ALMA data for the two most distant, gravitationally-lensed and spectroscopically-confirmed galaxies, A2744 YD4 at z= 8.38 and MACS1149 JD1 at z= 9.11, both of which reveal intense [ O iii ] 88 m emission. In both cases we provide stringent upper limits on the presence of [ C ii ] 158 m with respect to [ O iii ] 88 m.

The lower spectral window used to observe A2744 YD4 is centred on the frequency where [ C ii ] 158 m is expected at z= 8.38, and its width covers the redshift range 8.26 < z < 8.43. The total exposure time on source was 3.8hrs.

Examining a recent semi-analytical model of galaxy evolution Lagache2018 in over 10 ^3 simulated objects at z 8 (Figure fig5 ) and focusing now only on the two highest-redshift sources, A2744 YD4 and MACS1149 JD1, we find 75 simulated objects that have extreme properties similar to A2744 YD4 (i.e. SFR from 1 to 35 M_ yr ^ 1 ; L _ [ CII ] < 2.0 10 ^7 L _ ; (M _ [ M _ ] ) from 8.8 to 9.7) with as mean properties <M _ >=1.3 10 ^9 M _ , <L _ [ CII ] >=9.4 10 ^6 and gas-phase metallicity <Z _g >=0.20.

sources.summary: The paper models A2744 YD4 with ALMA constraints, finding two-component fits reproduce its dust, IRAC excess, and [OIII] 88 micron flux better than a single young component.

quotes.quote: Currently, four of the 13 z> 7 spectroscopically-confirmed galaxies with Spitzer /IRAC excesses listed in Table tab:z7 have the appropriate ALMA data: JD1, A2744 YD4, MACS0416 Y1 and B14-65666 (henceforth YD4, Y1 and B14 for convenience), see Table tab:z7oiii . To determine accurate SEDs, for each of the aforementioned galaxies we use the relevant references in Table tab:z7 (and references therein) to compile HST ACS+WFC3/IR, ( B_ 435 , V_ 606 , I_ 814 , Y_ 105 , J_ 125 , JH_ 140 and H_ 160 bands), VLT/HAWK-I K _ s , as well as Spitzer /IRAC [ 3.6 ] and [ 4.5 ] photometry.

To illustrate how the ALMA observations may differentiate between intense nebular emission and starlight in explaining the IRAC colours, we consider the case of YD4 since, for this source, all photometric points redward of the Lyman break and the ALMA spectroscopic constraints are robustly measured.

For two of the four galaxies (JD1 and YD4), the best-fit model correctly predicts the presence or upper limit of dust mass based on the ALMA continuum detections, whilst simultaneously reproducing the HST photometry, Spitzer /IRAC excess, and [ ] 88 m flux, within the error bars. For Y1 and B14, on the other hand, the HST photometry and Spitzer /IRAC excess are well reproduced, but the models are unable to simultaneously match both the [ ] 88 m flux and dust continuum (in the case of B14, the [ ] 88 m flux is matched but the dust continuum is underpredicted, and in the case of Y1 the [ ] 88 m flux is underpredicted and dust continuum overpredicted). In comparing these fits to those assuming a single-component only, although the HST photometry can be reasonably well-reproduced for JD1 and YD4, the single-component fits fail to simultaneously reproduce both the ALMA constraints and the Spitzer /IRAC excess.

sources.summary: Galaxy 4423 in A2744 provides the photometric template for mock kinematic simulations: its well fitted MGE and large extent define the surface-brightness, SNR, Voronoi-binned, and total-potential models.

quotes.quote: The underlying photometry of galaxy 4423 in cluster A2744 was chosen to construct the model surface brightness map, as it has a well fitted MGE and large spatial extent.

The MGE measured on HST pixels was resampled onto a coordinate grid with a MUSE pixel scale of 0.2", with the surface brightness constructed as an `apparent' surface brightness in units of magnitudes per square arcsecond. A polynomial fit to empirical surface brightness and SNR measurements was used to give each pixel of the mock galaxy a SNR. The galaxy was then thresholded to a minimum SNR of 2 per pixel and Voronoi binned to a SNR of 10 per pixel per bin, as was done with the Frontier Fields sample. The best-fit parameters from the emcee models of galaxy 4423 were used to create a total potential model, mapped onto the Voronoi barycentres of the mock galaxy.

sources.summary: A2744 06 is used as a showcase clear jellyfish galaxy, with a long ionized gas tail, undisturbed stellar kinematics, and tail velocities exceeding disk gas rotation.

quotes.quote: We use as an example here the galaxy A2744 06, that is a clear ram-pressure stripped galaxy. In particular, we would define it a jellyfish galaxy, as it has a long ionized gas tail. The comparison of the two (shown in the left and right panels of the second row in Fig. fig:a2744_06 ) allowed us to infer the signature of the ram pressure, given that the stellar kinematics is not disturbed, as expected if the interaction of the galaxy with the ICM only affects the gaseous component of the disk. A clear velocity gradient can be seen along the tail that reaches 300 with respect to the galaxy center: we interpret this as ionized gas left behind along a tail from the galaxy moving not only in the plane of the sky, but also with a non negligible component of the motion along the line of sight. Indeed, the maximum projected line-of-sight velocity of the gas in the galactic disk is 150-200 km/s (for the receding side, or -150-200 km/s for the approaching side), which is smaller than the line-of-sight velocity of the gas in the tail.

sources.summary: A2744 03 is described as a very low-mass, probably dwarf irregular, star-forming galaxy with disturbed tail-like morphology and LINER-like tail emission.

quotes.quote: A2744 03 is a very low mass galaxy ( (M_ /M_ )=7.98 ), probably a dwarf irregular, for which we could not measure the stellar kinematics. It is clearly star forming, and it has a disturbed morphology, reminiscent of a tail pointing toward the north of the image and in the HST RGB image (Fig. fig:RGB ), even if the ionized gas extends to the south of the stellar disk. There is no appreciable stellar continuum, but the kinematics of the gas shows a hint of a small rotation ( 30 ), entirely similar to the gaseous velocity dispersion. No other possible parent galaxy is seen close to this object. The entire galaxy seems star forming, while the tail appears to be LINER-like.

sources.summary: A2744 04 is a low-surface-brightness dwarf with no nearby parent galaxy, a north-west stripped-gas tail, weak rotation, and unresolved kinematics.

quotes.quote: A2744 04 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy, with a stellar mass of ( (M_ /M_ )= 8.18 ). It shows a tail extending toward the north-west (see Fig. fig:RGB ) direction and again it shows no detectable stellar continuum. No other galaxies are present in the surroundings, and we therefore think of this galaxy as a small star forming galaxy with a tail of stripped gas. The emission seems to show a small rotation ( 50 ), again compatible with the gas velocity dispersion. The small size of this galaxy suggests that we can not disentangle the two contributions (velocity dispersion and rotation velocity), though.

sources.summary: A2744 10 is a low-mass galaxy with a pronounced south-west tail, increasing tail gas velocity, decreasing extinction, and star-formation-dominated disk and tail emission.

quotes.quote: A2744 10 is a rather low-mass galaxy ( (M_ /M_ )=9.3 ) with a pronounced tail in the south-west direction extending for 13 kpc and clearly visible both in and in the [ OII ] . Within the stellar disk, it shows a low gas rotation ( 50 ) and velocity dispersion ( 70 ), while the stellar kinematics is much more confused, due to the small size of this galaxy. The gas velocity increases along the tail extension up to 150 . The A _V is high at the galaxy center, and decreases to low values in the external zone of the disk and in the tail, with a decrease in the outer part of it, in correspondence with a region with lower projected velocity and some spaxels apparently dominated by LI(N)er--like emission. From the analysis of the emission lines involved in the BPT diagram it turns out that the whole disk and the stripped tail are dominated by star formation.

sources.summary: The paper applies its IR leaker diagnostic to A2744 YD4, emphasizing uncertain magnification and revised extended [CII] detections that could decrease its LyC-leaker probability.

quotes.quote: We note that the actual luminosities for the CII _ 158 m and OIII _ 88 m emission lines of MACS1149 JD1 and A2744 YD4 are formally dependent on the amount of magnification which was assumed to be factors of 10 and 2 for each galaxy, respectively. Because the dividing line between being classified as a leaker and non-leaker in our model is not parallel to the one-to-one line, our conclusions about MACS1149 JD1 and A2744 YD4 will change if the actual magnification is much different from what is assumed. Furthermore, we note that a recent reanalysis of ALMA data for MACS1149 JD1 and A2744 YD4 has revealed [ CII ] detections that are more spatially extended compared to the [ OIII ] Carniani2020 . These results would place these galaxies much closer the the low-redshift [ CII ] SFR relation, consistent with the predictions from our simulations Katz2019b ; however, it would move the galaxies to the right on the [ CII ] [ OIII ] diagram, decreasing their probability of being LyC leakers.

sources.summary: The paper describes A2744 07 as a PSB galaxy with centrally concentrated AGN emission and extraplanar material, while morphology metrics show both RPS and PSB features.

quotes.quote: This is the case of A2744 07, where we find centrally concentrated emission associated with an AGN see for details Werle2022 .

In both the concentration , asymmetry and the gini , M _ 20 figures, the galaxies A2744 01 and A2744 07 are located at opposite regions of the morphology space. Both of these galaxies exhibit RPS and PSB features (with A2744 01 being primarily RPS and A2744 07 primarily PSB) and are likely to be in an intermediate phase between the two types. Their extreme locations in morphology space therefore suggests that the visual indicators of the disturbance are at their highest toward the end of the RPS phase, and are quick to vanish after the stripping ceases.

sources.summary: The paper describes A2744 YD4 as a lensed z=8.38 Y-band dropout galaxy with ALMA-inferred dust mass and a measured star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: 2017ApJ...837L..21L observed a Y band dropout lensed by the cluster Abell 2744. The galaxy A2744 YD4 has a spectroscopic redshift of z=8.38 . ALMA observations indicate a dust mass of 5.5^ +19.6 _ 1.7 10^6 \ and a star-formation rate of 20.4^ +17.6 _ 9.5 \ for a Chabrier IMF.

sources.summary: The paper highlights A2744 01 as a ram-pressure stripped galaxy with a clear H tail, no disk emission lines, and evidence for recently exiting strong stripping.

quotes.quote: One of the RPS sample galaxies, A2744 01, has a clear tail in H but no emission lines within the disk, suggesting that it is in an intermediate phase between the two types.

One galaxy of interest in this cluster is A2744 01, which was classified both as a RPS and PSB galaxy, due to its long tail of stripped material but lack of star formation or nebular emission lines. The direction of the galaxy's tail suggests that it has recently passed through the region characterized by very strong ICM X-ray emission, indicating that the galaxy may have just exited a phase of very strong RPS.

gmw_00002340.name: CEERS 99715
gmw_00002340.overview: CEERS 99715 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy with an estimated redshift of approximately 9.77 [arXiv:2304.05378]. Its spectrum exhibits a sharp continuum break at 1.3 microns and a potential emission line near the red edge of the spectral range [arXiv:2304.05378].

sources.summary: The paper reports CEERS 99715 as a z~9.77 galaxy with a 1.3 micron spectral break, high continuum-break S/N, and an apparent red-edge emission line treated cautiously.

quotes.quote: The spectra for galaxies CEERS 99715 (MSA ID 80026) and CEERS 35590 (MSA ID 80041) exhibit breaks at 1.3 \ m, with little to no flux detected at shorter wavelengths. Integrating the spectra over wavelength intervals 1.35--1.75 m and 0.85--1.25 m, above and below the breaks, yields S/N = 11.8 and 0.0 for CEERS 99715 and 12.1 and 1.0 for CEERS 35590, respectively. By eye, the break for CEERS 99715 appears to be ``sharper'', while that for CEERS 35590 seems more gradual, but given the low S/N per pixel ( <3 at < 1.4 m) this apparent difference may not be significant. For CEERS 99715, the 2D and 1D spectra show what appears to be an emission line that peaks at the reddest pixel in the spectral range (5.294 m), as if the line were truncated by the bandpass cutoff and/or the pipeline processing. In the rectified 2D spectrum the putative line is centered in the cross-dispersion direction within 0.5 pixel of the blue continuum, and visual inspection of the three separate nods shows that it appears to be detected in each.

For CEERS 99715, the best solutions obtained with the different Ly break fitting methods are in good agreement with each other (see Table tab:redshift ). As a result, as discussed in sec:redshift , we adopt the result from the ``modified-- eazy '' method, which gives a Ly break fit ( z=9.77^ +0.37 _ 0.29 ) as our best redshift for CEERS 99715.

gmw_00002341.name: CEERS 35590
gmw_00002341.overview: CEERS 35590 is a high-redshift galaxy with an adopted redshift of approximately z = 10.01 [arXiv:2304.05378]. Its spectrum exhibits a Lyman break at 1.3 microns, a blue spectral slope, and no convincing emission lines [arXiv:2304.05378].

sources.summary: The paper reports CEERS 35590 as a z~10 galaxy with a 1.3 micron break, no convincing emission lines, and an adopted redshift from modified-eazy full-spectrum fitting.

quotes.quote: The spectra for galaxies CEERS 99715 (MSA ID 80026) and CEERS 35590 (MSA ID 80041) exhibit breaks at 1.3 \ m, with little to no flux detected at shorter wavelengths. Integrating the spectra over wavelength intervals 1.35--1.75 m and 0.85--1.25 m, above and below the breaks, yields S/N = 11.8 and 0.0 for CEERS 99715 and 12.1 and 1.0 for CEERS 35590, respectively. By eye, the break for CEERS 99715 appears to be ``sharper'', while that for CEERS 35590 seems more gradual, but given the low S/N per pixel ( <3 at < 1.4 m) this apparent difference may not be significant.

In the case of CEERS 35590, the best solutions derived from the three Ly break fitting methods described in sec:redshift are more discrepant and inconsistent with the photometric redshift estimate. Moreover, if this source were actually at z 8.4-9.2 and considering the detection of strong UV continuum with a blue spectral slope (see sec:beta_slope ), we would expect 4960,5008 and H emission lines to be unambiguously detected at 4.6-5.1 \ m, but these lines are not observed. The best solution ( z = 10.01_ 0.19 ^ +0.14 ) obtained when applying the ``modified-- eazy '' method to the full NIRSpec spectrum (not just the break region) is in much better agreement with both the photometric redshift, the location of the Ly break and the absence of emission lines, which shift beyond the red wavelength limit of NIRSpec. We therefore adopt the result from the ``modified-- eazy '' fit to the full spectrum as our best redshift for this object.

gmw_00002342.name: NGC 1260
gmw_00002342.overview: NGC 1260 is an early-type S0/Sa galaxy located within the Perseus cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0612408, arXiv:2108.10445]. It features a dusty lane [arXiv:astro-ph/0612408, arXiv:1102.3511], an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0612408], and ongoing star formation despite its overall quenched properties [arXiv:2108.10445].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1260 as an early-type Perseus-cluster host with solar-like metallicity, an active nucleus, a dust lane, and nearby star-forming gas around SN 2006gy.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy, NGC 1260, is dominated by an old stellar population with solar metallicity. However, our high resolution adaptive optics images reveal a dust lane in this galaxy, and there appears to be an H 2 region in the vicinity of the SN.
The Chandra observations reveal a variable source at the position of the nucleus of NGC 1260. The spatial coincidence lead us to attribute this source to an active galactic nucleus.
NGC 1260 is an early-type galaxy within the Perseus cluster of galaxies. Its Heliocentric recession velocity is 5760 km s ^ 1 and its velocity dispersion is 201 12 km s ^ 1 (Wegner et al. 2003).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1260 as SN 2006gy's massive S0/Sa host, with galaxy-centered radio emission, current star formation, and below-main-sequence quenched properties.

quotes.quote: The radio emission of PTF10qaf, PTF10uhf, and SN 2006gy are dominated by the galaxy center rather than the SN positions.

The stellar masses of the hosts of SN 1999bd, SN 2006gy, PTF10qaf, and PTF10uhf are in the largest range among the sample of SLSNe lelo15, perl16, schu18 . In particular, the hosts of SN 2006gy and PTF10uhf have the largest stellar mass with M_*/M_ > 11 perl16, schu18 . It is known that larger stellar mass galaxies have higher fraction of obscured star formation e.g., whit17 . found that more than 90 of star formation is obscured for galaxies with M_*/M_ > 10.5 . Although the host of SN 2006gy, NGC 1260, is a S0/Sa galaxy, it shows signatures of current star formation with a far-IR luminosity of L_ FIR /L_ = 9.85 meus00, smit07 The IR-based SFR is 0.9 M_ yr ^ 1 , which is comparable to the radio-based SFR.

The SN 2006gy host is below the main sequence, suggesting a quenched property. This is consistent with its classification of S0/Sa, but at the same time the host has ongoing star formation as found in this work and previous studies smit07 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1260 as SN2006gy's apparent Perseus-cluster host at redshift 5703 km/sec, but argues its close alignment could be accidental if the progenitor came from NGC 891.

quotes.quote: The Smith et al. paper ^1 mentions that the galaxy in which SN2006 appears is a ``minor member of the Perseus cluster''. Indeed it is, NGC 1260, with a redshift of 5703 km/sec.
The strongest line of galaxies, however, consists mostly of E's and S0's originating from the large Sb (NGC 891) and ending on NGC 1260 and the supernova SN2006gy.
The near proximity ( 1'') to NGC 1260 would then be an accident.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1260 as an S0 host of SN 2006gy, with a not especially blue NUV color and evidence that the galaxy is dusty.

quotes.quote: Regarding morphology of host galaxies, NGC 1260 (label 1) and NGC 774 (label 3) are classified as S0 in the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED).

Our sample includes the host galaxy of SN 2006gy (NGC 1260, label 1), which is one of the most luminous supernovae ever discovered. Even though the progenitor of this highly luminous SN IIn is generally believed to be a very massive star e.g., Smith10 , the NUV color of the host galaxy is not particularly blue compared to the other SN II/Ib host galaxies. Like NGC 4589, this galaxy has also been found to be fairly dusty Ofek07 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1260 as SN 2006gy's host, notes the supernova lies near its bright nucleus, and uses host-nucleus spectroscopy/background subtraction.

quotes.quote: Supernova (SN) 2006gy is an extremely luminous SN found by qui06 near the nucleus of the host galaxy NGC 1260 [ = (m-M) =34.45 mag ] .
The direction of the slit was set to position angle (PA) 75 to take spectra of both the nucleus of the host galaxy NGC 1260 and the SN simultaneously.

Background subtraction Since the SN was very close to the bright nucleus of the host galaxy ( 0 9 ), the subtraction of the background component should be carefully performed, especially for late phase data.

gmw_00002343.name: IRAS F01475-0740
gmw_00002343.overview: IRAS F01475-0740 is a Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1105.2448, arXiv:1504.02772] that may be hosted by an S0 lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1504.02772]. It exhibits rapid X-ray variability [arXiv:1105.2448] and possesses strong, unresolved radio emission with a flat far-infrared-to-radio spectrum [arXiv:1504.02772].

sources.summary: The paper argues that IRASF 01475-0740 has modest X-ray absorption and iron emission but can host a heavily absorbed nuclear component whose corrected luminosity matches optical/IR estimates.

quotes.quote: IRASF 01475-0740 The XMM-Newton spectrum of IRASF 01475-0740 is well fitted by a power-law absorbed by a column of N_ H = 4.1 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 . While this will produce some reddening and extinction in the optical, it is unlikely to be sufficient to suppress the optical broad lines entirely unless the absorber has an anomalously high dust-to-gas ratio (.f. MCG-6-30-15; ). The X-ray spectrum also shows line emission between 6 and 7 keV.

For simplicity given the limitations of the data we fit the extreme case of 4 coverage (i.e. a spherical distribution of matter), and we constrained the column density of the heavily absorbed component to N_ H =2 10^ 24 |^ +65 _ 0.8 cm ^ 2 . Although it is not formally required in the fit, this demonstrates a clear scenario in which the multiwavelength data can be reconciled, as such a column is easily sufficient to suppress the direct nuclear emission in the optical. By correcting for absorption in this heavily obscured component, we can make another prediction of the intrinsic luminosity of the nucleus and calculate the corresponding bolometric luminosity. This now agrees well with estimations from L_ O III and L_ 12 m . The XMM-Newton observation also shows no clear signs of variability.

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS F01475-0740 as a peculiar X-ray-unabsorbed Seyfert 2 whose rapid variability, hidden broad-line region, and low absorption favor unusually high dust-to-gas obscuration.

quotes.quote: In this paper we report Suzaku exposure on the X-ray unabsorbed Seyfert 2 galaxy , in which a hidden broad line region was detected through spectropolarimetric observation. The X-ray data show rapid and significant variations on time scales down to 5 ks, indicating that we are viewing its central engine directly. A newly obtained optical spectrum and previous optical/X-ray data suggest that state transition is unlikely in this source. These make \ a very peculiar X-ray unabsorbed Seyfert 2 galaxy which can only be explained by absorption from materials with abnormally high dust-to-gas ratio (by a factor of > 4 larger than Galactic).

We finally note that \ also appears peculiar in infrared spectrum. It is the only Seyfert 2 galaxy with mid-IR silicate emission in the Spitzer sample of Hao07 . This makes it more like a type 1 AGN, suggesting smaller dust absorption opacity in IR comparing with other Seyfert 2 galaxies. This is however consistent with the small X-ray absorption detected, suggesting that the dust might be dominated by small grains which could steepen the optical/IR extinction curve.

sources.summary: J0150 0725 is described as a Seyfert 2 galaxy, possibly in an S0 host, with unresolved strong radio emission and a flat nonthermal FIR-to-radio spectrum.

quotes.quote: J0150 0725 This is a Seyfert 2 galaxy with a possible S0 host . It has a strong radio emission but is unresolved on VLA scale . It has a flat FIR-to-radio spectrum, indicative of a strong nonthermal component .

gmw_00002344.name: UGC 3789
gmw_00002344.overview: UGC 3789 is a nearly face-on, late-type galaxy [arXiv:1602.06960] classified as SAab [arXiv:1309.5166] that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2111.09316] with a water-megamaser disk [arXiv:1309.5166, arXiv:2207.06594]. The galaxy is dominated by multiple star-forming rings [arXiv:1602.06960], a grand-design nuclear spiral [arXiv:1705.09762], and subtle interior dust lanes [arXiv:1304.4254].

sources.summary: The paper highlights UGC 3789 as an unusual megamaser host with a grand-design nuclear spiral but no large-scale bar, plus kiloparsec rings that may relate to a merger or intrinsic dynamics.

quotes.quote: All of our tightly wound (tw) nuclear spirals belong to galaxies lacking a large-scale bar, in agreement with the results of 2003Martini . It is intriguing to note that one of our three grand-design nuclear spirals, in UGC 3789, also appears to lack a large-scale bar. While and other early works 2000Englmaier, 2004Martini argued that grand-design nuclear spirals are associated mostly with strongly barred galaxies, our findings are fully consistent with later works, e.g., and , who find no evidence of such a correlation on the smallest scales.

also predict that grand-design nuclear spirals should preferentially occur with circular ( x_2 type) kpc-scale rings -- such a ring is indeed seen in UGC 3789. The galaxy also contains a pair of rings in an 8-shaped structure at scales of 20 kpc. Their presence suggests that the grand-design nuclear behavior may have been caused by a recent merger. We note, however, that such ring structures are not necessarily a result of interactions and may also arise from the intrinsic dynamics of this galaxy 2004Kormendy .

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 3789 as a water-megamaser galaxy whose nucleus revealed an AGN, enabled Hubble-constant work, shows heavy obscuration, and lacks a formal spin solution.

quotes.quote: UGC 3789 Until the relatively recent discovery of water megamasers in its nucleus, UGC 3789 D 49.6 Mpc, reid13 was not known to host an AGN braatzgugliucci08 . Later on, UGC 3789 became a key galaxy to measure and refine the Hubble constant measurement using its masers reid09,braatz10,reid13 . Similarly to NGC 2960 and NGC 6323, there are no public optical spectra from which the O [ III ] line could be used to infer the bolometric luminosity. On the other hand, similarly to NGC 6264, castangia13 found evidence of heavy obscuration (possibly Compton-thick) using data. A re-analysis of the spectrum returns a bolometric luminosity L_ bol = 4.6^ +9.6 _ 2.0 10^ 43 .

In two cases (NGC 4258, UGC 3789) there is no formal solution between the expected and measured luminosities, while for NGC 6323 the two luminosities differ by more than an order of magnitude, suggesting that the X-ray intrinsic luminosity might be overestimated.

If the issue were related to the adopted bolometric correction, a simple estimate shows that the assumption of a bolometric correction of 10 instead of 20 would make UGC 3789 barely consistent with the most extreme prograde spin value, shifting up the measured L_ bol of 0.25 dex.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC3789 as a nearly face-on late-type galaxy dominated by rings, star-forming ring structures, short spiral arms, and large-scale disk/bar features.

quotes.quote: UGC 3789 UGC3789 (Figures fig:UGC3789-1 and fig:UGC3789-2 ) is a late-type galaxy that is seen nearly face-on and dominated by two rings, one close to the center at SMA =3.8'' ( 900 ), and the second one at SMA =23'' ( 5.6 ).
Both rings contain star forming regions and form the boundary of a disk, respectively. but the larger ring, as in NGC3393, might as well delineate a large-scale bar.

The inner ring is nearly round, while the second ring shows marked flattening and asymmetry. Two short spiral arms emerge at a PA 170 but varies by several degrees from the center outwards. A third, weaker ring is discernible in the profile at 40'' ( 10 ) and marks the edge of the visible large-scale disk.

sources.summary: The paper updates UGC 3789's disk-megamaser modeling, reduces overly conservative position and acceleration error floors, and measures a 51.5 Mpc distance with improved precision.

quotes.quote: UGC 3789 The disk megamaser in the galaxy UGC 3789 was discovered by , and the first VLBI map of the system was presented in . This map was combined with acceleration measurements by to produce an angular-size distance estimate of 49.9 7.0 Mpc to the system. obtained additional data and improved the distance measurement to 49.6 5.1 Mpc, and we use the data from that latest paper to produce the updated measurements presented in tab:UpdatedDiskFitting and app:DiskModelingResults .

Our updated fit indicates that the error floors used by for the position measurements were too conservative; we find a best-fit x position error floor of 5 1 as and a best-fit y position error floor of 6 1 as, compared to the previous (fixed) values of 10 as for both x and y positions. We find that the data do not provide strong constraints on the velocity measurement error floors for either the systemic or the high-velocity masers, and that the posteriors for both largely follow the Gaussian-distributed prior range of 2.0 1.0 . Our best-fit acceleration error floor is 0.34 0.06 , again indicating that the value of 0.57 was too conservative. The net result of the updated modeling is a distance measurement of D = 51.5^ +4.5 _ 4.0 Mpc, which represents a 3.8 0.4 increase over the previous value and an improvement in the measurement precision from 10 to (+8.7 ,-7.8 ) .

sources.summary: UGC 3789 is described as an SAab water-maser-disk galaxy whose low-SNR H I map shows a ring-like structure, clear velocity gradient, and insufficient data to constrain inclination or position angle.

quotes.quote: UGC 3789 also hosts a water maser disk, is an SA()ab galaxy RC3 , and is at a distance of 49.9 7.0 Mpc Braatz10 . The systemic velocity is 3229 km s ^ 1 . The observation also has low SNR = 6 and shows a ring-like structure with a diameter of 1 3 at the 1 level on the moment-0 map (Fig. fig:ugc3789 ). There is a clear velocity gradient. Neither inclination nor position angle can be constrained from the data alone.

sources.summary: The paper fits UGC 3789 as the thinnest megamaser disk in the sample, estimating the black-hole offset, central position, maser scatter angle, and reduced chi-square.

quotes.quote: In this work we perform a Bayesian statistical fit to estimate the mass-to-distance ratio and the recessional redshift of 10 different black holes hosted at the centre of active galactic nuclei, namely the galaxies NGC 5765b, NGC 6323, UGC 3789, CGCG 074-064, ESO 558-G009, NGC 2960, NGC 6264, NGC 4388, J0437+2456 and NGC 2273. Our general relativistic method makes use of the positions in the sky and frequency shift observations of water megamasers circularly orbiting the central black hole on their accretion disks.

UGC 3789 Although this maser disk is the thinnest of our group of megamaser systems, it was possible to estimate the BH offset y_0 . The statistical fit rendered the BH position to be near the geometric center, with the maser features scattered about the midline with an angle = 10 . The best reduced ^2 fit was 1.430.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 3789 as a megamaser disk galaxy with a circumnuclear disk, inner ring, interior dust lanes, and subtle two-arm dust structure.

quotes.quote: Circumnuclear structures and megamaser disks In three galaxies (NGC 2273, NGC 2960 or Mrk 1419, and UGC 3789) we see evidence for a circumnuclear disk. In the case of NGC 2273 and UGC 3789, there is an inner ring which manifests as a spike in the ellipticity profile, and then dust-lanes interior to the ring most readily identified with spiral arms.
There is dust structure apparent in UGC 3789, although it is subtle.
UGC 3789 appears to show two arms reaching towards the center inside the inner ring.

sources.summary: The paper says UGC3789 has masses that seem well constrained by the linear approach, with low-dispersion data, though nonlinear fitting failed despite the broad radial domain.

quotes.quote: We have applied the model to 7 AGN disks whose rotation has already been probed through water maser emission. For NGC3393 and UGC3789, the masses seem well constrained through the linear approach.

Finally, for IC1481, UGC3789 and NGC3393, dispersion is less than 10 , so both mass components should be obtained with ``accuracy''.
