Also, the SED from UV to near-infrared indicates an old stellar population with the age of 1.0 Gyr in SXDF-NB209-17, while SXDF-NB2315-7 is likely to be a young dusty star-bursting galaxy with the age of 0.5 Gyr and dust extinction of 2.0 mag in A_V (Figure fig;compact_SED ). These results suggest that both of them are good candidates for the progenitors of red nuggets at z=1.5-2.0 , but they are in the midst of the different evolutionary phases on the way to quiescent galaxies. While SXDF-NB2315-7 is in the starburst phase, SXDF-NB209-17 is probably a similar population to the young quiescent (post-starburst) galaxies reported by .

gmw_00002386.name: SXDF-NB2315-7
gmw_00002386.overview: SXDF-NB2315-7 is a massive, compact star-forming galaxy with a relaxed, bulge-like morphology [arXiv:1311.4260]. It is characterized as a young, dusty starbursting galaxy with high star-formation activity and a stellar mass exceeding 10^11 solar masses [arXiv:1311.4260].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SXDF-NB2315-7 as a massive compact star-forming galaxy with red-nugget-like density, bulge-like morphology, high star-formation activity, and a young dusty starburst SED.

quotes.quote: In our emitter sample, we find two massive, compact star-forming galaxies with M _*>10^ 11 M _ and r_ e <2 kpc (SXDF-NB209-17 and SXDF-NB2315-7 in Figure fig;M_Re ). Their surface stellar mass densities are as high as those of red nuggets at z=1.5 -2.0 2012ApJ...746..162N . Moreover, the two compact HAEs show high S e rsic indices of n 2 , which indicate relaxed bulge-like morphologies. They can directly turn into compact quiescent galaxies at high-redshift just by stopping their star-formation activities without changing the sizes or structures.
In contrast, the star-formation activity of SXDF-NB2315-7 is still high despite its large stellar mass of M_*>10^ 11 M_ .

Also, the SED from UV to near-infrared indicates an old stellar population with the age of 1.0 Gyr in SXDF-NB209-17, while SXDF-NB2315-7 is likely to be a young dusty star-bursting galaxy with the age of 0.5 Gyr and dust extinction of 2.0 mag in A_V (Figure fig;compact_SED ). These results suggest that both of them are good candidates for the progenitors of red nuggets at z=1.5-2.0 , but they are in the midst of the different evolutionary phases on the way to quiescent galaxies. While SXDF-NB2315-7 is in the starburst phase, SXDF-NB209-17 is probably a similar population to the young quiescent (post-starburst) galaxies reported by .

gmw_00002387.name: Tri II
gmw_00002387.overview: Triangulum II is an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy and a satellite of the Milky Way [arXiv:1510.03856, arXiv:1604.00838]. It is a highly dark-matter-dominated system [arXiv:1510.03856, arXiv:1603.07321] that completed at least 80 percent of its star formation by redshift z ~ 6 [arXiv:2108.04271].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Triangulum II as a very faint, dark-matter-dominated Milky Way satellite based on velocity and metallicity dispersions, while finding no strong evidence for disruption or association with nearby stellar debris.

quotes.quote: lae15a \ recently discovered Triangulum II, a satellite of the Milky Way. Its Galactocentric distance is 36 kpc, and its luminosity is only 450 L_ . Using Keck/DEIMOS, we measured the radial velocities of six member stars within 1.2 \ of the center of Triangulum II, and we found a velocity dispersion of _v = _ ^ + km s ^ 1 . We also measured the metallicities of three stars and found a range of 0.8 dex in [ Fe/H ] .

The velocity and metallicity dispersions identify Triangulum II as a dark matter-dominated galaxy. The galaxy is moving very quickly toward the Galactic center ( v_ GSR = km s ^ 1 ). Although it might be in the process of being tidally disrupted as it approaches pericenter, there is no strong evidence for disruption in our data set. The ellipticity is low, and the mean velocity, v_ helio = km s ^ 1 , rules out an association with the Triangulum--Andromeda substructure or the Pan-Andromeda Archaeological Survey (PAndAS) stellar stream.

sources.summary: The paper treats Triangulum II as an ultra-faint, metal-poor dwarf spheroidal with a very large inferred dark-matter J-factor, while stressing that few stellar velocities make follow-up essential.

quotes.quote: The recently discovered object Triangulum II appears to be an ultra faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy which may be one of the most dark matter dominated objects yet known. In this work we try to estimate the potential of this object for studies of the indirect detection of self-annihilating dark matter by obtaining its astrophysical J-factor. We perform a basic estimate of the velocity gradient to look for signs of the halo being tidally disrupted but show that the observed value is statistically compatible with zero velocity gradient. We solve the spherical Jeans equation using Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) engine GreAT and the Jeans analysis part of the CLUMPY package. We find the results point towards a very large J-factor, appearing to make Triangulum II one of the best targets in the search for dark matter.

As mentioned in the introduction, Triangulum II appears to be a metal poor dwarf galaxy with a large mass to light ratio. Only 13 member stars have been studied in detail by one group hotstellar and six stars by a second group kirby . By mapping out the positions of the stars, we were able to determine that five of the stars overlap between the two sets and there are only 14 stars in total available for analysis.

sources.summary: The paper re-evaluates Triangulum II as an ultra-faint dwarf, finding unresolved velocity dispersion, lower mass than previously estimated, and chemical evidence consistent with enrichment.

quotes.quote: From new multi-epoch Keck/DEIMOS measurements of \ member stars in Triangulum II, we constrain the velocity dispersion to be _v < km s ^ 1 (90 C.L.). Our previous measurement of _v , based on six stars, was inflated by the presence of a binary star with variable radial velocity. We find no evidence that the velocity dispersion increases with radius. The stars display a wide range of metallicities, indicating that Triangulum II retained supernova ejecta and therefore possesses or once possessed a massive dark matter halo.

Thus, neither we nor K15a can resolve the velocity dispersion of Tri II . The mass within the half-light radius of a spheroidal galaxy is proportional to the square of the velocity dispersion. Therefore, the downward revision to _v implies a downward revision to the mass within the half-light radius of Tri II, which K15a estimated to be M_ 1/2 = (8.9^ +6.8 _ 3.0 ) 10^5 M_ .
This new mass limit indicates that Tri II does not have the largest mass-to-light ratio ( M/L ) of any non-disrupting galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper treats Triangulum-II/Tri-II as a newly discovered, possible ultra-faint dark-matter-dominated dwarf spheroidal galaxy, models its dark matter density, and reports no significant Fermi-LAT gamma-ray excess.

quotes.quote: Triangulum-II, a newly discovered dwarf spheroidal galaxy, is a strong candidate for the indirect search of dark matter through the detection of ray emission that could originate from the pair- annihilation of weakly interacting massive particles (WIMPs). We here report on the analysis of almost seven years Fermi Gamma-Ray Space Telescope data of Triangulum-II which was taken during its all sky survey operation mode. No excess ray emission has been detected above 100 MeV from Triangulum-II. We derive the upper limits on ray flux assuming both the power-law spectra and the spectra related to WIMP annihilation.

Triangulum-II (hereafter referred to as Tri-II), which is one of those newly discovered dSphs, has recently been closely investigated by the Pan-STARRS Survey . This survey has concluded that Tri-II is either an ultra-faint and DM dominated dwarf galaxy or a globular cluster. A number of calculations have also claimed that Tri-II may indeed be a potential target to search for the signatures of pair-annihilation of the WIMPs. The present investigation of Tri-II, that is described in this paper, is motivated by the the above findings.

sources.summary: The paper treats Triangulum II as a likely ultra-faint dwarf candidate with metallicity and alpha-element spreads, low neutron-capture limits, and classification evidence favoring a galaxy over a cluster.

quotes.quote: Hansen We present high-resolution spectroscopy of four stars in two candidate ultra-faint dwarf galaxies (UFDs), Grus I (Gru I) and Triangulum II (Tri II). Neither object currently has a clearly determined velocity dispersion, placing them in an ambiguous region of parameter space between dwarf galaxies and globular clusters. No significant metallicity difference is found for the two Gru I stars, but both stars are deficient in neutron-capture elements. We verify previous results that Tri II displays significant spreads in metallicity and [ /Fe ] . Neutron-capture elements are not detected in our Tri II data, but we place upper limits at the lower envelope of Galactic halo stars, consistent with previous very low detections.

Triangulum II The case of Tri II was already extensively discussed by Kirby15,Kirby17,Martin16,Venn17,Carlin17 , generally finding that it is most likely a UFD rather than a star cluster. Our high-resolution abundance results are consistent with the discussion in Venn17 and Kirby17 , namely that we find a difference in [ Fe/H ] between these two stars at about 2 significance, and TriII-46 has lower [ /Fe ] ratios compared to TriII-40. Kirby17 previously found very low Sr and Ba abundances in TriII-40, and our Ba limit on TriII-46 is consistent with overall low neutron-capture element abundances in Tri II (though additional data is needed to confirm that TriII-46 is well below the halo scatter). Tri II thus likely satisfies criteria (2) and (3), though it is unclear if it satisfies criterion (1) (see Kirby17 , figure 2).

sources.summary: The paper models binary-star effects in Triangulum II, adds MMT spectra, finds 16 likely members, and leaves the dwarf's velocity dispersion unresolved with a 3.4 km/s upper limit.

quotes.quote: We present new MMT/Hectochelle spectroscopic measurements for 257 stars observed along the line of sight to the ultra-faint dwarf galaxy Triangulum II. Combining with results from previous Keck/DEIMOS spectroscopy, we obtain a sample that includes 16 likely members of Triangulum II, with up to 10 independent redshift measurements per star. For a previously-identified (spatially unresolved) binary system in Tri II, we infer an orbital solution with period 296.0_ 3.3 ^ +3.8 days , semi-major axis 1.12^ +0.41 _ 0.24 AU , and a systemic velocity 380.0 1.7 km s^ 1 that we then use in the analysis of Tri II's internal kinematics. Despite this improvement in the modeling of binary star systems, the current data remain insufficient to resolve the velocity dispersion of Triangulum II.

The Triangulum II (Tri II) ultra-faint dwarf galaxy provides an interesting case study. The original kinematic study of Tri II, based on single-epoch spectroscopy of six member stars, measured a velocity dispersion of 5.1^ +4.0 _ 1.4 km s^ 1 , suggesting a dynamical mass-to-light ratio of 3600_ 2100 ^ +3500 in solar units, and an extremely high dark matter density of 4.8_ 3.5 ^ +8.1 M_ / pc ^3 kirby2015 . Both studies found evidence for nonzero metallicity dispersion, supporting the conclusion that Tri II is a dwarf galaxy embedded in a massive dark matter halo, and not a self-gravitating star cluster.
We present a mean velocity of v = -382.3 0.7 km s^ 1 from this analysis.

sources.summary: The paper uses Triangulum II as its strongest dwarf-galaxy case for PBH abundance constraints, with exclusions depending strongly on the assumed fraction of destroyed stars.

quotes.quote: The highest merit factor corresponding to the strongest constraints is found for Triangulum II. It is important to note, however, that this galaxy is not an exception; several other galaxies have similar constraining power.

In Fig.  fig:constraints we show the resulting constraints on the PBH abundance f = _ PBH / _ DM as a function of the PBH mass for Triangulum II, the most constraining galaxy of those listed in Table.  tab:1 . The blue and orange regions are excluded assuming =0.5 and =0.2 , respectively. We do not show the constraints for masses below 10^ 18  g because in this range of masses the time of star destruction, Eq. ( eq:destruction ), becomes comparable or exceeds the star lifetime.

We present the result for Triangulum II in Fig.  fig:constraints , using as a benchmark the value =0.5 . The resulting constraints depend crucially on the assumed value of : they are marginal for the adopted value =0.5 and would disappear for 0.9 . Clearly, a quantitative determination of this parameter from dwarf galaxy modeling is of major importance.

sources.summary: The paper reviews Triangulum II discovery and size measurements, reports HST structural and magnitude results, and highlights spectroscopy and metallicity evidence favoring a galaxy classification.

quotes.quote: Triangulum II This was the first discovered ultra-faint satellite from the PS1 data by , who then took follow-up imaging with the Large Binocular Cameras at the Large Binocular Telescope (LBT). Unable to determine whether this satellite was a cluster or dwarf galaxy, gave it the double name of Laevens 2 and Triangulum II. The initial a_ h, exp value from (reported as r_h ) was 3 9 , although they included the caveat that with the low contrast of the stellar overdensity to the background, deeper data would provide a stronger measurement.

We measured a_ h, exp =3 50^ +0.59 _ 0.65 , larger, but within 1.4 agreement with and ; our value is smaller than the measurement, though consistent within 0.4 . Our a_ h, p =3 25^ +0.51 _ 0.59 measurement is larger but within 1.7 agreement with the Plummer measurement and 2.2 agreement with the value. Our and values are consistent with both the and measurements.

Numerous spectroscopic studies of Tri II have been published kirby2015tri2,martin2016,venn2017,kirby2017,ji2019 , often with results hinting at a dwarf galaxy nature, but with no definitive conclusions. The most recent spectroscopic study by combined new MMT/Hechtochelle data with the existing Keck II/DEIMOS set and presented an upper limit on the velocity dispersion of 3.4 km s ^ 1 at the 95 confidence level.

sources.summary: The paper derives the HST-based color-magnitude diagram and star formation history of Triangulum 2, finding early stellar-mass assembly and quenching older than 11.5 Gyr within a seven-UFD comparison.

quotes.quote: We present the color-magnitude diagrams and star formation histories (SFHs) of seven ultra-faint dwarf galaxies: Horologium 1, Hydra 2, Phoenix 2, Reticulum 2, Sagittarius 2, Triangulum 2, and Tucana 2, derived from high-precision Hubble Space Telescope photometry. We find that the SFH of each galaxy is consistent with them having created at least 80 of the stellar mass by z 6 . For all galaxies, we find quenching times older than 11.5 Gyr ago, compatible with the scenario in which reionization suppresses the star formation of small dark matter halos. However, our analysis also reveals some differences in the SFHs of candidate Magellanic Cloud satellites, i.e., galaxies that are likely satellites of the Large Magellanic Cloud and that entered the Milky Way potential only recently.

On average, we find _ 90 = 12.06 0.72 Gyr ago for Magellanic satellites and _ 90 = 12.68 0.23 Gyr ago for non-Magellanic satellites (this becomes 12.67 0.41 if we exclude Sag 2); even though they are comparable within the errors, there is a marginal difference between these values (a two-sample KS test of the average distributions gives a p-value of 5.7 10^ 10 ). Indeed, the candidate Magellanic satellites appear to have more prolonged SFHs compared to non-Magellanic ones, possibly due to the fact that they should have resided in low-density environments for most of their lifetimes; however, the uncertainties are too large to reach a definitive conclusion.

sources.summary: The paper concludes Triangulum II is a dwarf galaxy, citing high velocity-dispersion and metallicity-spread reports plus an unsegregated mass profile inconsistent with expectations for a star cluster.

quotes.quote: martinetal2016b obtained Keck/DEIMOS spectroscopy for 13 member stars of Triangulum II and found a high central velocity dispersion of = 4.4^ +2.8 _ 2.0 km/sec. They also found that the velocity dispersion increased further outwards, indicating either a strongly dark matter dominated system or the ongoing tidal disruption of the system. kirbyetal2015b confirmed the high central velocity dispersion, and also found a metallicity spread of [ Fe/H ] =0.8 dex among the member stars.

We obtain a mass segregation parameter R_ H,Bright /R_ H,Faint = 0.99 0.09 , in good agreement with the assumption that Triangulum II is unsegregated. As a star cluster we would expect to find a value around 0.80 due to the low relaxation time of Triangulum II. We therefore conclude that the system is a dwarf galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses Triangulum II as a dark-matter-dominated ultra-faint dwarf satellite to estimate wave-dark-matter soliton parameters and halo mass constraints.

quotes.quote: We have considered in this work data from recently discovered Draco II and Triangulum II Milky Way dwarf satellites. The measurements of the luminosity of these objects suggest that they are systems with a mass-to-light ratio of 10^7 in the outer regions and therefore completely DM dominated. With such extreme DM environments, these dwarfs provide a great laboratory to study dark matter physics despite the contribution of baryonic feedback.
We find: r_c 105 pc ; m_ 22 5.6 for Draco II and r_c 160 pc ; m_ 22 3.8 for Triangulum II. The corresponding core masses, using Eq. eq:mc , are M_c^ DraII 1.5 10^7 and M_c^ TriII 2.3 10^7 .

sources.summary: The paper treats Triangulum II as a key HAWC dwarf-spheroidal target, noting its large J factor, zenith transit, DM-profile uncertainty, and influence on combined annihilation and decay limits.

quotes.quote: Triangulum II has a particularly large J factor and transits near the zenith for HAWC. However, it was discovered recently and it still has large uncertainties in its DM profile. Because of this, we show the joint dwarf limit both with and without Triangulum II in Figures 15dwarffigs_annihilation and fig:15dwarffigs_decay .
The HAWC combined limits with Triangulum II are strongest for the W^+W^- channel for M_ 30 TeV .
Like the dark matter annihilation results, Segue 1, Coma Berenices, and Triangulum II are dominant, though for decays, Bootes I and Draco also contribute to the combined limits.

sources.summary: The paper treats Triangulum II as an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal with one of the largest annihilation J factors, while emphasizing large uncertainties from its limited kinematic sample.

quotes.quote: Secondly, we find that among analysed dSphs, the ultra-faint dwarf galaxies Triangulum II and Ursa Major II are the most promising but large uncertain targets for dark matter annihilation while the classical dSph Draco is the most robust and detectable target for dark matter decay.

On the other hand, among ultra-faint dSphs, Triangulum II and Ursa Major II are the top and second largest J value, even when including classicals. However, since their kinematic sample size is limited, 1 uncertainties in these values are very much larger than those of all luminous dSphs. Therefore, all the ultra-faint dSphs, except for Canes Venatici I, have pronouncedly larger uncertainties of J than their classical counterparts.

sources.summary: The paper uses Kirby memberships for Triangulum II, keeps five members, explains the scattered proper-motion clump, and reports strong agreement with Simon.

quotes.quote: Triangulum II We use the catalog of Kirby_17 which provides binary memberships. After our cuts we are left with 5 members; the star classified as uncertain member in the source paper is eliminated by our cuts. These stars do not form a clear clump in proper motion space, but due to the faintness of the stars, and the possible larger population due to Tri II low Galactic latitude, a large scatter is not surprising. Our motion agrees very well with the motion of Simon_18 .

sources.summary: The review describes Triangulum II as a recently discovered DES system considered for dark-matter searches, with newer member-star measurements reducing its velocity-dispersion constraints.

quotes.quote: Another interesting system that has recently been discovered in the DES and has been considered as a target is Triangulum II . The first measurement indicated a velocity dispersion of 5 km/s, with a factor of two rise to larger radii . However, most recent measurements of 13 member stars constrain the velocity dispersion to < 4 km/s, with no indication of a sharp rise at large radii. The initial results are likely explained by the contamination from binary stars .

sources.summary: The paper includes Tri II among six dwarf spheroidal targets, finds it has the highest expected dark-matter amount, and says CTA observability remains uncertain because its stellar kinematics are limited.

quotes.quote: In particular, we focus our analysis on the classical dSph Draco I (Dra I) and the ultra-faint Coma Berenices (CBe), Canes Venatici I (CVn I), Segue 1 (Seg 1), Reticulum II (Ret II) and Triangulum II (Tri II).

In Fig. fig-2 , we also show the profiles of J and D for the dSph with the highest expected DM amount (Tri II) and that with the most robust DM halo parameters (Dra I). ht Our MCMC examination of the first six DM halos in a completely uniform framework of input data allows us to produce J and D values for dSphs in agreement with the literature.

We also note that, within the analyzed data sets, the dSph Tri II is potentially observable with CTA as a single targets in 100 h integration. However, the limited statistics available on its stellar kinematics (which is also possibly contaminated by binary stars ) prevents to draw any firm conclusion, again emphasizing the importance of collecting good and clean stellar data for such targets in order to evaluate them as viable candidates for DM searches with Cherenkov telescopes.

gmw_00002388.name: GraL J024612.2 184505.1
gmw_00002388.overview: GraL J024612.2 184505.1 is a close-separation, possibly gravitationally lensed quasar [arXiv:1912.08977]. While survey images and spectra show a clear spatial extent, current data cannot exclude the possibility that the system is a binary quasar [arXiv:1912.08977].

sources.summary: The paper treats this object as a close-separation possibly lensed quasar; Gaia and survey images show two or extended sources, while Keck confirms spatial extent but cannot exclude binarity.

quotes.quote: Individual Objects GraL J024612.2 184505.1 --- This is a close separation ( 1 001), possibly lensed quasar. Gaia DR2 indicates two sources, the brighter at G 18.4 mag, and fainter/brighter flux ratio of 0.440 0.006 . PS1DR2 and DECaLS images cleary show spatial extent (e.g. see Figure DoublyImagedQSOs ), but the PS1 catalog contains a single source. The confirming Keck spectrum shows a clear spatial extent but fails to separate the components. Considering that neither absorption nor evidence for the lensing galaxy was detected in the current spectroscopic data, binarity cannot be excluded yet.

gmw_00002389.name: GraL J034611.0+215444.6
gmw_00002389.overview: GraL J034611.0+215444.6 is a newly identified gravitationally lensed quasar system [arXiv:1912.08977]. It consists of a close, sub-arcsecond pair of blue sources that are interpreted as a doubly imaged quasar [arXiv:1912.08977].

sources.summary: The paper identifies this as a newly found close lensed quasar pair; Gaia resolves two blue sources, Palomar shows spatial extent, and shared narrow absorption supports a doubly imaged quasar interpretation.

quotes.quote: GraL J034611.0+215444.6 --- This newly identified lensed quasar consists of a close, sub-arcsecond ( 0 988), separation pair of blue sources. Gaia DR2 indicates two sources, the brighter at G 18.8 mag, and flux ratio 0.812 0.007 . PS1DR2 images show spatial extent, but the catalog contains a single source. The confirming Palomar spectrum shows a spatial extent but fails to separate the components, except at the reddest wavelengths.

We observe that blueward of the \ emission line, there is a deep, narrow absorption line almost reaching zero flux. This is only possible if the two components show a similar absorption, confirming that this system is most likely a doubly-imaged quasar. No radio or X-ray counterparts are reported in NED.

gmw_00002390.name: GraL J081830.5+060137.9
gmw_00002390.overview: GraL J081830.5+060137.9 is a gravitationally lensed broad-absorption-line quasar at a redshift of z=2.363 [arXiv:1912.08977, arXiv:2109.06881]. The system contains two close components resolved by Gaia [arXiv:1912.08977], with absorption features likely associated with a foreground lensing galaxy at z=1.0 [arXiv:2109.06881].

sources.summary: The paper describes this source as a spatially extended BAL quasar with two Gaia components and multiple foreground absorbers, noting previous quasar-pair classification but independent lens identification.

quotes.quote: GraL J081830.5+060137.9 --- The confirming Keck spectrum shows a clear spatial extent, but fails to separate the two components. Gaia DR2 indicates two close sources ( 1 147), the brighter at G 17.8 mag, and flux ratio 0.160 0.001 . PS1DR2 and DECaLS images show spatial extent, but the PS1 catalog contains a single source. The quasar is a broad absorption line (BAL) quasar, with multiple absorption components observed out to 5000 km s ^ 1 . Multiple foreground \ absorption systems are also seen, including absorption from both \ and \ at z = 1.507 and \ absorption at z = 1.007 (with \ from the lower redshift system lost to the dichroic).

This source was reported by the SDSS-III BOSS quasar lens survey 2016More , though they consider it to be a quasar pair rather than a confirmed lensed quasar based on the non-detection of a lensing galaxy in their moderate-depth optical follow-up imaging (420 s i band image from the SOAR 4-m telescope). They note that if this quasar is lensed, then the non-detection would imply a high-redshift lensing galaxy, at z > 1 , which would be consistent with the foreground absorption systems detected here if one of those systems is lensing the background quasar. This source is also independently identified as a lens by 2019arXiv191204336H .

sources.summary: The paper confirms both J0818+0601 sources as z=2.363 quasars and reports literature evidence that the system is a gravitationally lensed broad-absorption-line quasar with a z=1.0 lens.

quotes.quote: J0818+0601 : The broad \ and \ emission lines in both spectra confirm that both sources are quasars at z=2.363 . More16 classified it as a binary quasar because of the non-detection of the lens galaxy, however, recent spectropolarimetric observations identified it as a gravitationally lensed broad absorption line quasar Hutsemekers2020 . The absorption features are likely related to a lensed galaxy at z=1.0 .

gmw_00002391.name: GraL J090710.5+000321.7
gmw_00002391.overview: GraL J090710.5+000321.7 is a newly identified lensed quasar with a close separation of 0.983 arcseconds [arXiv:1912.08977]. Gaia observations indicate two components, while spectra show absorption lines from two foreground systems at redshifts of 0.682 and 0.771 [arXiv:1912.08977].

sources.summary: The paper identifies this as a newly found close lensed quasar; Gaia and imaging show two or extended components, Keck shows spatial extent, and foreground absorbers imply a deflector mass.

quotes.quote: GraL J090710.5+000321.7 --- This is a newly identified close separation ( 0 983) lensed quasar. Gaia DR2 indicates two sources, the brighter at G 20.2 mag, and flux ratio 0.84 0.02 . PS1DR2 and DECaLS images show spatial extent, but the PS1 catalog contains a single source. The confirming Keck spectrum shows a clear spatial extent but fails to separate the components.

Absorption lines from \ and \ are clearly seen from two foreground systems, at z = 0.682 and z = 0.771 . Similar to GraL J034611.0+215444.6, here we also observe narrow absorption lines (cf. CIV) almost reaching zero flux. Assuming that the lens is located at the intervening absorber redshift z=0.682 (0.771) and given the angular separation of 0 98, we derive a mass of 4.7 10^ 11 (5.9 10^ 11 ) M_ for the deflector and a proper comoving length 12 (13) kpc for the absorber cloud.

gmw_00002392.name: GraL J125955.5+124152.6
gmw_00002392.overview: GraL J125955.5+124152.6 is a wide-separation double quasar system associated with a bright X-ray source [arXiv:1912.08977]. Because no lensing galaxy is apparent in imaging, it is considered a likely non-lensed binary quasar [arXiv:1912.08977].

sources.summary: The paper describes this widest-separation system as an X-ray-associated double quasar candidate with two Gaia and PS1 sources, strong self-absorption, and no apparent lensing galaxy, making non-lensed binarity likely.

quotes.quote: GraL J125955.5+124152.6 --- This system, the widest separation system in this sample ( 3 513), is associated with the bright ROSAT X-ray source 1WGA J1259.9+1241, and is listed in the Chandra Source Catalog 2010ApJS..189...37E . It was reported as a binary quasar in 2006AJ....131....1H and as a double quasar in 2009ApJ...693.1554F . Gaia DR2 indicates two sources, the brighter at G 19.7 mag, and flux ratio 0.896 0.006 . PS1DR2 and DECaLS images cleary show two sources, and the PS1 catalog also contains two sources. The Keck spectrum shows strong self-absorption in one of the two components.

We note that ambiguity in whether this source is truly a lensed quasar, versus a double, or binary quasar. In particular, the entropy method will identify elevated entropy from double quasars as compared to single quasars, regardless of whether they are distinct sources or two lensed images of the same source with a temporal delay. Though the same ambiguity is true for other sources, the wider separation of this pair with no lensing galaxy apparent in imaging to date makes this the most likely non-lensed, double quasar, in this sample.

gmw_00002393.name: GraL J155656.1 135210.1
gmw_00002393.overview: GraL J155656.1 135210.1 is a doubly imaged quasar candidate at a redshift of z=1.42 [arXiv:1912.08977]. Observations reveal two components separated by approximately 0.9 arcseconds, with absorption lines indicating a potential lensing galaxy at a redshift of z=0.393 [arXiv:1912.08977].

sources.summary: The paper says Keck and NTT observations show two separated, similar spectra for this source, supporting a doubly imaged quasar at z=1.42 with an absorber likely associated with the lensing galaxy.

quotes.quote: GraL J155656.1 135210.1 --- The confirming Keck spectrum shows clear spatial extent, but fails to separate the two components. Absorption lines are seen from \ and \ at z = 0.393 , potentially associated with the lensing galaxy. Gaia DR2 indicates two sources ( 0 959 separation), the brighter at G 19.3 mag, and flux ratio 0.609 0.004 . PS1DR2 images show two sources but the PS1 catalog contains a single source.

The Keck conclusions are corroborated by the reduction of NTT observations. The spectra are well separated in both observations. Their similarity suggests a doubly-imaged quasar at redshift z=1.42 , but binarity cannot be completely excluded. Assuming that the lens is located at the absorber redshift z=0.393 and given the angular separation of 0 9 between the lensed components, we derive a mass of 1.8 10^ 11 M_ for the deflector and a proper comoving length for the absorber cloud 7 kpc.

gmw_00002394.name: GraL J220015.6+144859.5
gmw_00002394.overview: GraL J220015.6+144859.5 is a doubly imaged, gravitationally lensed quasar with two distinct spectral and imaging components [arXiv:1912.08977]. Absorption lines detected at a redshift of 0.762 are potentially associated with the lensing galaxy [arXiv:1912.08977].

sources.summary: The paper describes this wavelet-selected lensed quasar as having two distinct spectral and imaging components, foreground absorption likely associated with the lensing galaxy, and a derived deflector mass.

quotes.quote: GraL J220015.6+144859.5 --- The Keck spectrum of this wavelet-selected lensed quasar shows two distinct components, with one spectrum contaminated by a Galactic M-star, visible as the red source in Figure DoublyImagedQSOs . Absorption lines from \ and \ are clearly seen in both lensed images at z = 0.762 , potentially associated with the lensing galaxy. Gaia DR2 indicates one source at G 20.5 mag with large astrometric errors. PS1DR2 and DECaLS images, and the PS1 catalogue, contain two sources ( 2 5 separation). Assuming that the lens is located at the absorber redshift and given the angular separation of between the two lensed images, we derive a mass of 4.9 10^ 12 M_ for the deflector and a proper comoving length for the absorber 33 kpc.

gmw_00002395.name: GraL J234330.6+043557.9
gmw_00002395.overview: GraL J234330.6+043557.9 is a broad-lined quasar at a redshift of 1.604 [arXiv:1912.08977]. It is identified as a gravitationally lensed system with two close components [arXiv:1912.08977], though alternative analysis suggests it may be a quasar-star pair [arXiv:2306.08376].

sources.summary: The paper presents this as a newly confirmed close lens: Palomar and Keck resolve a broad-lined quasar into two images, with one redder component showing stronger lens-galaxy absorption.

quotes.quote: GraL J234330.6+043557.9 --- This newly confirmed lens consists of a close separation pair ( 1 231).

Gaia DR2 indicates two sources, the brighter at G 18.7 mag, and flux ratio 0.778 0.009 . PS1DR2 and DECaLS images cleary show two sources, but the PS1 catalog contains a single source. The initial Palomar spectrum shows a spatially extended z=1.604 broad-lined quasar, separated into two components only at the reddest optical wavelengths and with one lensed component showing stronger absorption lines. The Keck follow-up observations clearly resolve the two images at all wavelengths, and identify multiple absorption lines at z=0.855 which appear much stronger in one of the lensed quasar components, which also is redder in color (see Appendix).

It is assumed that this foreground absorption corresponds to the lensing galaxy, with one of the lensed quasar images intercepting a dense, dusty region of the lensing galaxy, such as a giant molecular cloud. Given the angular separation of 1 23 between the two lensed components, we estimate a mass of 8.6 10^ 11 M_ for the deflector and a proper comoving length for the covering absorber cloud 28 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J234330.58+043557.9 as a reported lensed quasar classified as a quasar-star pair, arguing Gaia detected a lensed image plus a superposed star.

quotes.quote: We find that two reported LQs, SDSS 081331.28+254503.0 Reimer2002,Jackson2015 and SDSS J234330.58+043557.9 Krone-Martins2019 , are classified as quasar-star pairs according to our proper motion/parallax criteria. Both of these two LQs have two Gaia detections. We think that Gaia only detected one of the lensed images and the other Gaia detection came from a superpositioned star with large proper motion or parallax.

gmw_00002396.name: Mrk 71
gmw_00002396.overview: Mrk 71 is a nearby, metal-poor starburst galaxy located 3.4 Mpc away [arXiv:2109.14691, arXiv:2304.09898]. It serves as a local analogue to high-redshift star-forming galaxies and Green Peas, dominated by a young super star cluster known as Knot A [arXiv:2109.14691, arXiv:2304.09898].

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 71 as a nearby Green Pea analog in NGC 2366 whose broad emission-line wings are best explained by a clumpy, radiation-driven superwind from Knot A.

quotes.quote: James AURA for ESA, Space Telescope Science Institute, 3700 San Martin Drive, MD 21218, USA We propose that the origin of faint, broad emission-line wings in the Green Pea (GP) analog Mrk 71 is a clumpy, LyC and/or Ly driven superwind. Our spatially-resolved analysis of Gemini-N/GMOS-IFU observations shows that these line wings with terminal velocity >3000 \ originate from the super star cluster (SSC) Knot A, and propagate to large radii. The object's observed ionization parameter and stellar surface density are close to their theoretical maxima, and radiation pressure dominates over gas pressure. Together with a lack of evidence for supernova feedback, these imply a radiation-dominated environment.

Mrk 71 is a starburst in the nearby, metal-poor 12+ O/H=7.89; Izotov1997 galaxy NGC 2366. This system is the nearest 3.4 Mpc; Tolstoy1995 analog of the Green Peas (GPs), a class of local ( z 0.2) high-ionization parameter galaxies of intense cosmological interest due to high incidence of Lyman-continuum (LyC) escape . The properties of Mrk 71/NGC 2366 are fully consistent with those of GPs in terms of ionization parameter, specific star formation rate, metallicity, and parameters linked to low LyC optical depth Micheva2017 . Mrk 71 is dominated by Knot A, a super star cluster (SSC) that is still enshrouded by its natal gas (Figure fig:Haimages a), yet dominates the galaxy's nebular luminosity and ionization parameter.

sources.summary: The paper measures oxygen abundance in Mrk 71 with optical and far-infrared lines, finding little temperature-fluctuation bias and describing it as a nearby high-redshift star-forming-galaxy analogue.

quotes.quote: We present a measurement of oxygen abundance in the nearby (3.4 Mpc) system, Mrk 71, using a combination of optical and far-IR emission lines to measure and correct for temperature fluctuation effects. Our far-IR result is inconsistent ( > 2 significance ) with the metallicity from recombination lines and instead indicates little to no bias in the standard T_e method, ruling out the long-standing hypothesis that the ADF is explained by temperature fluctuations for this object.

We selected the target Markarian 71 (Mrk 71; also called NGC 2366-I, or mislabeled as NGC 2363 ) for a case study based on its physical properties. Mrk 71 is one of the most nearby (distance = 3.4 Mpc ) analogues to high- z star-forming galaxies, with high electron temperature ( T_e 15,000 K ), high ionisation parameter ( U 2.2 ), and compact morphology, which are consistent with young starbursts (stellar age of the primary western component is 1 Myr ) . Such properties are extreme among nearby galaxies, but common in star-forming galaxies at z 2 and similar to recent JWST-based measurements at z 8 . Moreover, low dust attenuation in Mrk 71 makes it especially suitable for comparing optical and infrared (IR) fluxes.

sources.summary: The paper argues that temperature inhomogeneities causing the O2+/H+ abundance discrepancy in the star-forming galaxy Mrk 71 cannot be ruled out because calibration, reddening, and density assumptions change the result.

quotes.quote: In a very recent work, claim that the scenario of temperature inhomogeneities proposed by ( t^2>0 ) is not able to explain the O ^ 2+ /H ^ + abundance discrepancy observed between the calculations based on the optical [ O III ] collisional excited lines (CELs) and the O II recombination lines (RLs) in the star forming galaxy Mrk 71. In this work, we show that conclusions of depend on several assumptions on the absolute flux calibration, reddening correction and the adopted electron density. In fact, using the data of in a different way and even considering their 1 uncertainties, it is possible to reach the opposite conclusion, consistent with t^2=0.097 ^ +0.008 _ 0.009 . Therefore, the existence of temperature inhomogeneities causing the O ^ 2+ /H ^ + abundance discrepancy in Mrk 71 can not be ruled out.

Figure tested the presence of temperature inhomogeneities in the star forming galaxy Mrk 71. To carry out their analysis, used an optical spectrum from the Keck Cosmic Web Imager (KCWI) at the W. M. Keck Observatory as well as IR spectra from the Far Infrared Field-Imaging Line Spectrometer (FIFI-LS) at the Stratospheric Observatory for Infrared Astronomy (SOFIA) and from the Photodetector Array Camera and Spectrometer (PACS) at the Herschel Space Observatory.

gmw_00002397.name: GP J1219+1526
gmw_00002397.overview: GP J1219+1526 is a starbursting Green Pea galaxy [arXiv:2011.02549, arXiv:2110.07741] characterized by extreme nebular excitation [arXiv:2109.14691] and an unresolved morphology [arXiv:2110.07741]. The galaxy hosts perpendicular bi-conical outflows [arXiv:2011.02549] and exhibits symmetric, power-law emission-line wings that suggest the presence of radiation-driven winds [arXiv:2109.14691].

sources.summary: The paper treats 1219+1526 as a Green Pea with contaminated Si spectra, perpendicular bi-conical outflows, high Ly escape fraction, and predicted LyC emission.

quotes.quote: We fit the SALT model to the 1190.42 , 1193.28 \ \ doublet ( 1185 1201 ) for all of the Green Peas except galaxies 1133+6514 and 1219+1526 whose spectra suffer contamination from Milky Way absorptions. The best fit for each galaxy is provided in Figure f3 and the associated parameters have been recorded in Table tab:best_fits_SiII . For galaxies 1133+6514 , 1219+1526 , and 1054+5238 best fit parameters were extrapolated from the ( 1260.42 ) \ transitions, and are highlighted in green in Figure f3 .

Galaxies 0911+1831 , 1054+5238 , 1137+3524 , 1244+0216 , 1249+1234 , and 1424+4217 have spherical outflows in Si II, while galaxies 1133+6514 and 1219+1526 have perpendicular bi-conical outflows. Galaxy 0926+4427 is our only galaxy with a bi-conical outflow observed edge-on. (Even though > in galaxy 0926+4427 we have chosen to include 0926+4427 in this group since within our uncertainties.) Lastly, galaxy 0303-0759 is our only galaxy with a bi-conical outflow oriented parallel to the line of sight. Members of these groups are circled in blue, red, violet, and green, respectively, in Figures f8 and f9 . Because \ photons scatter multiple times in the \ medium the geometry of the gas plays a crucial role. Specifically, \ photons will escape toward directions with the minimum \ column density. In the case of bi-conical geometry, photons will encounter the lowest \ column density in the direction perpendicular to the outflow. The orientation of the outflow with respect to the line of sight, then, will determine the visibility of \ photons by an observer. This seems to be confirmed with the data: galaxies 1133+6514 and 1219+1526 , with outflows oriented perpendicular to the line of sight, have the first and third largest values of the escape fraction out of our 10 Green Pea galaxies, while galaxy 0303-0759 , our only galaxy with a bi-conical outflow oriented along the line of sight, has the lowest \ with respect to all galaxies.

Given their outflow geometries, we predict galaxies 1133+6514 and 1219+1526 (and possibly 0926+4427 ) to be LyC emitters. Recall that the outflow geometries of these galaxies leave a portion of the galaxy uncovered with respect to the line of sight. Moreover, the Si II lines of these galaxies have weak absorption equivalent widths and low values of the kinematic quantity, v_ 90 /v_ cen , where v_ 90 is the velocity at which equivalent width reaches 90 of the continuum, and v_ cen is the velocity at 50 the equivalent width. These characteristics were suggested by as a proxy for LyC leakage. 0926+4427 has a bi-conical outflow observed edge-on and may be capable of leaking LyC radiation in the direction of the observer, but our predictions suggest it won't be as strong of an emitter as the other two galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper observes GP121903 as one of three Green Pea galaxies, presenting its SDSS identifier, OSIRIS spectrum, extreme emission-line properties, and chemical/stellar-population modelling.

quotes.quote: The sample consists in three galaxies selected among the brightest GPs in the sample presented by . Their SDSS references are: http://skyserver.sdss.org/dr16/en/tools/explore/summary.aspx?plate=458&mjd=51929&fiber=185 SDSSJ030321.41-075923.2 , http://skyserver.sdss.org/dr16/en/tools/explore/summary.aspx?plate=1745&mjd=53061&fiber=463 SDSSJ101157.08+130822.0 and http://skyserver.sdss.org/dr16/en/tools/explore/summary.aspx?plate=1766&mjd=53468&fiber=557 SDSSJ121903.98+152608.5 . The first GP can also be found in the SHOC catalogue (SDSS HII-galaxies with Oxygen abundances Catalog) by kniazev_strong_2004 as SHOC148. For simplicity's sake, however, the GPs will be referred within this manuscript as GP030321, GP101157 and GP121903.

In Fig. fig:sample_spectra , we present the OSIRIS spectra of the three observed galaxies in the rest frame. Both the spectra and the images illustrate the characteristic properties of GP galaxies, namely unresolved morphology in SDSS and green colour due to extreme nebular emission, in particular high EW( [ OIII ] 4959,5007 ) and emission lines falling in the '-band. Finally, from the lower panels in Fig. fig:sample_spectra the Balmer jump ( 3646 ) can be easily appreciated on GP101157 and GP121903.

sources.summary: The paper uses GP J1219+1526 as an extreme Green Pea comparison object, finding its broad 5007 wings follow power laws like Mrk 71 and may indicate radiation-driven winds.

quotes.quote: As part of a larger study of GP line profiles, we obtained a MIKE/Magellan echelle spectrum of the GP J1219+1526, which exhibits among the most extreme nebular excitations Jaskot2014, Ravindranath . The spectra were taken 2015 January 12 and reduced with the CarPy pipeline Kelson2000 . This object is one of the GPs exhibiting mostly symmetric non-Gaussian profiles and broad wings. We tried multiple-Gaussian, Lorentzian, power-law, and exponential distributions and find that, while the core of the 5007 profile of J1219+1526 is best fit by a Lorentzian, its broad wings are power laws with slope 4 .
Figure fig:GPs shows that Mrk 71, J1219+1526, and LBV-V1 all have power-law wings, suggesting that the underlying physical mechanism may be similar in all three objects.

We argue that smooth, symmetric, power-law wings, as exhibited by Mrk 71 and J1219+1526 are signatures of radiation-driven winds, where nebular emission originates from a filled volume of small clumps, thus generating a smooth profile in both space and velocity.

gmw_00002398.name: Hoag's Object
gmw_00002398.overview: Hoag's Object is an isolated ring galaxy [arXiv:1802.07294, arXiv:2011.01238] featuring a completely detached ring of young, star-forming stars [arXiv:1802.07294, arXiv:1803.02346] surrounding an old, round elliptical core [arXiv:1802.07294, arXiv:2011.01238]. This peculiar structure may have formed through the accretion of cold gas onto a pre-existing elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1108.3079, arXiv:2011.01238].

sources.summary: The paper uses Hoag's galaxy as the isolated prototype for JO171, comparing its detached young ring, central spheroid, ring extent, stellar mass, and lack of ram-pressure tails.

quotes.quote: This galaxy resembles the Hoag's galaxy, one of the most spectacular examples of ring galaxies, characterized by a completely detached ring of young stars surrounding a central old spheroid. At odds with the isolated Hoag's galaxy, JO171 is part of a dense environment, the cluster Abell 3667, which is causing gas stripping along tentacles.

The structure of JO171 looks similar to the Hoag's object: the two galaxies have a central spheroid of similar luminosity (-20.8, -20.7 in the B band, respectively), and the same effective radius of 2 kpc Finkelman+2011 . Both spheroids are almost round (ellipticity e=0.03 for Hoag's, 0.05 for JO171). The ring extends approximately between 12 and 25 kpc in the Hoag's galaxy, and from 6.5 to 20 kpc in JO171. We note that if we considered a ring extending out to 25 kpc the results that we present in the following would not be altered. The mass of stars in the ring of Hoag's galaxy was estimated from its luminosity and simple assumptions on the stellar populations by Finkelman+2011 , and is 3 10^9 M_ , smaller than that of JO171 ( 1.1 10^ 10 M_ ).

sources.summary: The paper uses Hoag's Object as a massive ring-galaxy case, estimating unstable-orbit regions that may explain its luminosity and stellar-distribution gap.

quotes.quote: This is the case of the Hoag's Object, where a central galaxy is surrounded by a massive ring with on-going star formation Hoag1950 . Optical Schweizer1987, Finkelman2011 and radio Brosch2013 observations show that the mass of the ring is approximately 1/3 of the central galaxy. Observations have identified sources which appear to be similar to Hoag's Object Moiseev2011, FinkelmanBrosch2011, MutluPakdil2017 .

For instance, we can estimate the width of this region for Hoag's Object. In this object the ring of hot, blue stars is located around the central elliptical galaxy. Using the image provided by the Hubble Heritage program a rough estimate shows that the inner edge of the ring is approximately a distance of 0.9 of the average radius of the ring itself. According to the observational result from Finkelman2011 , the mass ratio M_0/M_ ring 3 and the major radius of the ring R 17'' .

sources.summary: The paper describes Hoag's Object as the prototype Hoag-type galaxy, notes that its ring formation remains unsettled, and argues its round spheroid without a disc favors an external secondary feature.

quotes.quote: This class of peculiar objects are named after the prototype ``Hoag's Object'' first reported by Hoag (1950). However, most of these objects differ in some distinct way from Hoag's Object and none has a perfectly round core in its centre (Schweizer et al. \ 1987; Wakamatsu 1990). The mechanism that formed the ring in Hoag's Object was debated in the past (Brosch 1985, Schweizer et al. \ 1987), and is not yet fully understood.

The hypothesis that the ring in Hoag's Object was formed in a disc at a time when a bar was present and later dissolved was first put forward by Brosch (1985). Indeed, most Hoag-type galaxies show elongated cores with discy features, and probably represent an evolved phase of barred early-type galaxies where the bar has almost completely dissolved (e.g., NGC 6028 in Wakamatsu 1990). However, Hoag's Object itself appears as a pure round spheroid with no trace of a disc. This argues against an interpretation of the ring as a formerly barred disc and in favour of an externally generated secondary feature.

sources.summary: The paper describes Hoag's Object as an isolated ring galaxy with an old triaxial elliptical body, a massive low-density HI disc, and a ring plausibly formed by cold gas accretion.

quotes.quote: These new data, together with HI and optical information from the literature, are used to demonstrate that Hoag's Object is a relatively isolated system surrounded by a luminous quasi-spiral pattern and a massive, low-density HI disc. The main stellar body is an old, mildly triaxial elliptical galaxy with very high angular momentum. We review previous formation scenarios of Hoag's Object in light of the new data and conclude that the peculiar morphology could not represent a late phase in barred early-type galaxies evolution. In addition, no observational evidence supports late merging events in the evolution of the galaxy, although further tests are required before safely dismissing this idea.

Combining all the information we propose a new scenario where the elliptical core formed in the early Universe with the HI disc forming shortly after the core by prolonged `cold' accretion of primordial gas from the intergalactic medium. The low gas density does not allow intense star formation to occur everywhere in the disc, but only along a tightly wound spiral pattern of enhanced density induced by the triaxial gravitational potential.

sources.summary: The paper uses Hoag's Object as a comparison case, describing its young ring, old elliptical core, low-density environment, and possible gas-accretion origin.

quotes.quote: It is interesting to compare gLSBGs with the famous Hoag's Object, an unusual giant ring galaxy in a low-density environment discovered by Hoag1950 . The blue and young ring surrounds an elliptical galaxy hosting old metal-rich stellar population Finkelman2011 . The radius of the ring is about 25 kpc and it has a clearly visible spiral-like pattern.

Finkelman2011 proposed that the peculiar structure and observed properties of the Hoag's Object are the result of the gas accretion onto a pre-existing elliptical galaxy. The Hoag's Object could thus be a special case of a ``failed'' gLSBGs in which instead of a giant LSB disc, the star-forming ring was formed due to some specific properties of the system, e.g. a triaxial potential of the elliptical galaxy, which could lead to the prominent gap between the core and the ring.

sources.summary: The paper cites Hoag's Object as an outstanding ringed galaxy: an almost perfect ring around a fast-rotating elliptical, with earlier bar-remnant interpretation superseded by external gas-accretion or polar-ring scenarios.

quotes.quote: One example of an outstanding ringed galaxy is Hoag's Object (HO: Hoag 1950; Finkelman, Moiseev, Brosch Katkov 2011; Brosch et al. 2013) where an almost perfect ring surrounds an elliptical galaxy that is also a fast rotator. Brosch (1985) suggested that HO's central object could be the remains of a bar that drove the formation of the ring, more or less in the way proposed by Buta Combes (1996), with the bar subsequently relaxed back into the disk. However, this proposal could not be sustained and later HO was assumed to be the result of external gas accretion, perhaps directly from the cosmic web or via an interaction with a gas-rich galaxy, possibly in the form of a ``polar ring''.

gmw_00002399.name: NGC 4045
gmw_00002399.overview: NGC 4045 is a late-type spiral galaxy located approximately 32.1 Mpc away [arXiv:2203.11955, arXiv:2204.13408]. It hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2203.11955], a small bulge [arXiv:2204.13408], a bar [arXiv:2204.13408], and multiple ultraluminous X-ray sources [arXiv:2203.11955].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4045 as a 32 Mpc spiral AGN host containing a new hyperluminous X-ray source, its low-luminosity nucleus, another ULX, and a past Type II supernova.

quotes.quote: Here we report the discovery of a new HLX candidate in NGC 4045 with , and subsequent observations with , \ and . NGC 4045 is a spiral galaxy at a distance of 32.1 Mpc as determined from the Tully-Fisher relation tully16 with a redshift of z=0.00659 , and hosts an optically identified AGN gavazzi11 . The galaxy also hosted the type II supernova SN 1985B kosai85 .

We detect two other X-ray sources that are likely associated with NGC 4045. An X-ray source at R.A. = 12h 02m 42.276s, Decl.=+1 \ 58 \ 36.50 \ is coincident with the \ position of the nucleus of the galaxy, 29 \ from the ULX. It has a count rate of 1.9 0.4 10^ 3 , corresponding to a flux of 3 10^ 14 \ in the 0.5--8 keV band, implying a luminosity of 4 10^ 39 \ at 32 Mpc. This source is likely to be the AGN of NGC 4045, which is at low luminosity. The other source was at R.A. = 12h 02m 42.799s, Decl.=+1 \ 58 \ 56.98 \ in the northern spiral arm of the galaxy, 50 \ from the ULX, and close to, but not coincident with AT2019wbg (Figure fig_ngc4045_img ). It has a count rate of 2.0 1.4 10^ 4 , corresponding to a flux of 1.7 10^ 14 \ in the 0.3--10 keV band, implying a luminosity of 2 10^ 39 \ at 32 Mpc, and therefore also a ULX.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4045 as a decomposition case study, finding a small bulge, extended type III disc, bar, rings, and nuclear lens; ignoring the bar would overestimate its spheroid.

quotes.quote: To illustrate the process, here we present a case study using the late-type galaxy NGC 4045.

The galaxy has a small bulge (dashed-dotted red red curve), a large extended type III truncated disc ( blue blue curve), and a bar orange orange curve, see Erwin2005bar, Erwin2013, Diaz-Garcia2016, Font2017, Font2019 . Along the and PA profiles, there are two `bumps' at R_ eq 3 and 7 , corresponding to the inner spiral arm-like structures. Comeron2014 classified NGC 4045 as a (R_ 1 'L)SAB(rs,nl)ab galaxy containing two outer rings and a `nuclear lens' The `nuclear lens' appears to be the head of the spiral arms that run perpendicular to the so-called `bar' in this galaxy.
Failing to account for the elongated bar structure in NGC 4045 would result in the fitted S ersic function substantially over-estimating the mass and size of the spheroidal component of this galaxy.

gmw_00002400.name: ESO399-IG20
gmw_00002400.overview: ESO399-IG20 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 active galaxy [arXiv:1303.3653]. Photometric monitoring reveals that its broad-line emitting region has a nearly face-on, disk-like shape with an inclination of approximately six degrees [arXiv:1303.3653].

sources.summary: Photometric reverberation mapping of ESO399-IG20 measures its BLR size, separates host contribution, and finds a nearly face-on disk-like BLR explaining its narrow-line Seyfert-1 appearance.

quotes.quote: We present photometric reverberation mapping of the narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy ESO399-IG20 performed with the robotic 15 cm telescope VYSOS-6 at the Cerro Armazones Observatory. Through the combination of broad- and narrow-band filters we determine the size of the broad-line emitting region (BLR) by measuring the time delay between the variability of the continuum and the H emission line. We use the flux variation gradient method to separate the host galaxy contribution from that of the active galactic nucleus (AGN), and to calculate the 5100 luminosity L_ AGN of the AGN. Both measurements permit us to derive the position of ESO399-IG20 in the BLR size -- AGN luminosity R_ BLR L_ AGN ^ 0.5 diagram.

We found that the BLR has a disk-like shape with an inclination of i=6 3^ and an extension from 16 to 20 light days. This nearly face-on BLR can explain the appearance of ESO399-IG20 as a narrow-line Seyfert-1 galaxy. We successfully separated the host-galaxy contribution from the total flux through the flux variation gradient method (FVG). The average host-galaxy subtracted AGN luminosity of ESO399-IG20 at the time of our monitoring campaign is L_ AGN = (1.69 0.25) 10^ 43 erg s^ 1 .

gmw_00002401.name: MCG +8-11-11
gmw_00002401.overview: MCG +8-11-11 is a bright Seyfert 1.5 galaxy [arXiv:1406.4497] hosting an active galactic nucleus with a supermassive black hole [arXiv:1102.4959]. Its radio structure features a strong nuclear component elongated in the north-south direction [arXiv:2005.08569, arXiv:astro-ph/0406597], and it exhibits long-term variability in both X-ray and millimeter bands [arXiv:1102.4959, arXiv:2403.19524].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes MCG+8-11-11 as a bright Seyfert 1.5 galaxy, finding no required warm absorber, clear Fe-line emission, Compton-thick distant torus reflection, and a soft excess.

quotes.quote: We present a spectral analysis of the 118 ks High Energy Transmission Gratings ( ) observation of the X-ray bright Seyfert 1.5 galaxy , in conjunction with 100 ks of archival data, aimed at investigating the signatures of warm absorption and Compton reflection reported from previous and studies of the source. Contrary to previous results, we find that warm absorption is not required by the data. Instead, we report upper limits on absorption lines that are below previous (marginal) detections. line emission is clearly detected and is likely resolved with 0.02 keV with the data. We applied self-consistent, broadband spectral-fitting models to the and data to investigate this and other signatures of distant absorption and reflection.

Utilizing in particular the model, we find that the data are consistent with reprocessing by a distant, neutral torus that is Compton thick ( 10^ 24 cm^ 2 ) and out of the line-of-sight. However, we do not find compelling evidence of a relativistically-broadened Fe-K emission line, which is often expected from type 1 AGN. This is consistent with some, although not all, previous studies of . A well-measured edge is identified by the near 0.5 keV, indicating neutral absorption in the line of sight that is consistent with galactic absorption; however, the absorption may be partially intrinsic to the source. The data are consistent with the presence of a soft excess, a feature that may be missed by considering the data alone. galaxies: active -- galaxies: individual (MCG+8-11-11) -- galaxies: Seyfert -- X-rays: galaxies

sources.summary: MCG +8-11-11 shows a relativistic Fe K line without matching high-energy reflection; the self-consistent reflection model fits poorly and overpredicts the Compton hump.

quotes.quote: We investigate here the two extreme cases where the Relativistic Relline model gives better fit to the data, with a ^2>50 , compared to the more self-consistent Relativistic Pexmon model: MCG +8-11-11 and one observation of IC 4329A (ID: 702113020). The case of MCG +8-11-11 has already been noted by Bianchi+10 . They found a detection of relativistic Fe K line, but without any reflection component at higher energies. The results of our analysis are fully consistent with this result (see Table relline ). When the Relativistic Pexmon model is applied to the data, it leads to an inadequate fit with = 122.74 compared to the Relativistic Relline model.

The reflection fraction R for MCG +8-11-11 is very small and well constrained R = 0.12_ 0.05 ^ +0.06 . Conversely both the narrow and relativistic lines are well detected and have a combined equivalent width of 156 eV, corresponding to a total reflection fraction of R 1 . Figure MCGextra shows the unfolded spectrum for this source together with all the components of the Relativistic Pexmon model.

sources.summary: The paper treats MCG + 8 11 11 as a peculiar bright X-ray Seyfert, reporting historical variability, low reflection, broad-band hard-X-ray observations, black-hole mass and luminosity estimates, and a modest Eddington ratio.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert 1.5 \ ( z = 0.0205 ) is one of the brightest Seyfert galaxies in the X-rays and historically showed flux variations by a factor of 3--4 on time scale of years (Fig. lc_mcg ). This source has been extensively observed in the past below 10 keV, but only few observations were performed at hard X-rays before the advent of the present hard X-ray satellites INTEGRAL , SWIFT and Suzaku . Due to the relatively low amount of reflection and the properties of the Fe line, it has been suggested that the observed reflection is produced in the accretion disc and that the narrow core of the Fe K line is emitted in the broad line region, whereas no evidences for a classical torus are found in the X-ray spectrum of this AGN , making \ a very peculiar Seyfert galaxy.

With a black hole mass of M_ BH = (1.5-13) 10^7 M_ , an X-ray luminosity of L_ 0.1-300 keV = 2.7 10^ 44 erg s^ 1 and assuming a twice as large bolometric luminosity, MCG + 8 11 11 shows an Eddington ratio of L_ Bol /L_ Edd = 0.03-0.3 , placing it among the most efficient hard X-ray selected Seyfert galaxies . Some of the past studies of \ reported the presence of a strong reflection component R = 1-2.5 . Yet, a good statistical constrain was only obtained with XMM-Newton data but limited at energies below 10 keV or combined with low effective exposure ISGRI data ( , corresponding to the first point in Fig. var_spe_mcg ), until Suzaku together with SWIFT /BAT observations provided a high signal-to-noise, broad band coverage between 0.6 and 150 keV . The latter work finds that when the broad band spectrum is fitted, the reflection fraction drops to values of R = 0.2-0.3 , marginally consistent with the upper limit we find in INTEGRAL data.

sources.summary: The paper finds MCG+08-11-011 is the only sample source with coronal-temperature variation, showing hotter-when-brighter behavior and evidence for an outflowing corona.

quotes.quote: Of the 18 sources, at the 90 confidence level, evidence for variation in kT_e was found for only one source namely MCG+08-11-011. For this source between two epochs, separated by about five years, we found kT_e to decrease from 57 ^ +29 _ 16 keV to 30 ^ +11 _ 7 keV. During the same period, the flux decreased from (12.60 to 14.02) 10 ^ 11 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and the optical depth increased from 1.68 to 2.73. We thus found a positive correlation between flux and coronal temperature with a reduction of about 40 in optical depth. Our observations tend to favour the vertically outflowing corona scenario for the observed variation in kT_e in MCG+08-11-011.

Like all other bright Seyfert galaxies, MCG+08-11-011 also became softer when it brightened in X-ray 2003ApJ...593...96M, 2014A&A...563A..57S, 2014ApJ...794...62B . In Fig. figure-4 the softer-when-brighter trend is shown for this source. We also found MCG+08-11-011 in its higher flux state (14.02 ^ +1.12 _ 0.11 10^ 11 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 ) on August 2016 (epoch A) with a hotter corona of kT_ e = 57 ^ +29 _ 16 keV.

sources.summary: The paper reports MCG+08-11-11 has previously resolved central and extended radio components; new maps show N-S elongation, compact flux, and a steep spectrum.

quotes.quote: MCG+08-11-11 VLA maps at 8.4 GHz revealed a central component (P.A. 128 deg) and an extended one on scales 1.2 kpc in the N-S direction WilsonWillis1980,Schmitt2001 while VLBI observations a 2 and 6 cm resolve the center into three components (0.7" length NW-SE direction, P.A. = 127 deg)with a component having a flatter spectrum (recognized as the core) and a 2.4 kpc emission on N-S direction. Behar2018 observed it at 100 GHz with CARMA finding a flux density of 7.53 0.19 and a spectral slope, calculated within the CARMA 100 GHz band, of 0.47 0.29. Smith2020 1" resolution 22 GHz map reveal a morphology compatible with ours, with a flux density 0f 13.8 smaller than our 15.6 0.8 .

Our radio maps exhibit a en elongated structure in the N-S direction with P.A. = 160 1 and 131 16 deg at 22 and 45 GHz, respectively. The flux densities for the compact component are of order of 15.7 - 9.3 , with a spectral index of 0.75 0.11. The above results are compatible with previous VLA works at similar resolutions e.g. WilsonWillis1980,UlvestadWilson1986 .

sources.summary: The paper applies broadband photometric reverberation mapping to MCG+08-11-011, using high-cadence light curves to extract an H light curve and support a broad-line lag measurement.

quotes.quote: Here we present an ICCF-Cut method for broadband PRM to obtain the H broad line lag and apply it to four Seyfert 1 galaxies, MCG+08-11-011, NGC 2617, 3C 120 and NGC 5548. All of them have high quality broadband lightcurves with daily/sub-daily cadence, which enables us to extract H lightcurves from the line band by subtracting the contributions from the continuum and host galaxy. Their extracted H lightcurves are compared with the lagged continuum band lightcurves, as well as the lagged H lightcurves obtained by spectroscopic RM (SRM) at the same epochs. The consistency of these lightcurves and the comparison with the SRM H lags provide supports to the H lags of these AGNs, in a range from 9 to 19 days, obtained by the ICCF-Cut, JAVELIN and ^2 methods.
The data of MCG +8-11-011 and NGC 2617 are obtained from 2018ApJ...854..107F and 2017ApJ...840...97F .
We obtain the single-epoch spectrum of MCG +8-11-011 using the Beijing Faint Object Spectrograph and Camera (BFOSC) of the Xinglong 2.16-m telescope in China.

sources.summary: The paper models MCG+08-11-011 variability, decomposing near-infrared emission into accretion-disk and dust-torus components and deriving a 1635 K dust component, 6-day lag, and power-law disk spectrum.

quotes.quote: Applying this analysis to the long-term VHK monitoring data of MCG+08-11-011 that were obtained by the MAGNUM project, we found that the (H-K) color temperature of the dust component is T = 1635 K 20 K, which agrees with the sublimation temperature of dust grains, and that the time delay of K to H variations is t 6 days, which indicates the existence of a radial temperature gradient in the dust torus. As for the disk component, we found that the power-law spectrum of f_ ^ in the V to near-infrared HK bands varies with a fixed index of 0.1 -- +0.4, which is broadly consistent with the irradiated standard disk model. The outer part of the disk therefore extends out to a radial distance where the temperature decreases to radiate the light in the near-infrared.

The (H-K) color temperature of 1635K here derived for MCG+08-11-011 agrees well with the sublimation temperature of 1500-1800 K for graphite/silicate grains (Salpeter 1977; Huffman 1977). Hence, this component represents the innermost part of the dust torus heated to the sublimation temperature by the accretion disk.
Combining this and the results in section 3 from the regression analysis, we found that the power-law SED with =-0.1 +0.4 prevails in the optical to near-infrared wavelength region (Figure 4).

sources.summary: The paper describes MCG+8-11-11 as having a prior warm absorber and soft X-ray absorption edge, while this work detects one absorber at higher ionization.

quotes.quote: MCG+8-11-11 - This source have been studied by . The slope =1.8 . The single component warm absorber 460 . There is an absorption edge in soft-Xrays with 0.041 . We too detect one WA component but with a much higher ionisation parameter.

sources.summary: The paper uses MCG+08 11 11 as a comparison RQ Seyfert 1, noting slight millimeter and X-ray increases, no correlated variability, and larger X-ray than millimeter fractional variability.

quotes.quote: A second simultaneous mm/X-ray campaign of an RQ AGN was recently reported by petrucci23 for MCG+08 11 11 ( z=0.02 ), a Seyfert 1 galaxy with a black hole mass comparable to that in IC 4329A, M_ SMBH 2.8 10^7 M_ . The variability in the 3--10 keV X-ray band obtained by and the 100 GHz flux inferred by NOEMA were studied on time scales of 14 hours. Both fluxes showed slight increases, corresponding to 1.06 times and 1.19 times for the mm and X-ray flux, respectively, but no correlated variability was found.

While no clear evidence of correlated mm/X-ray variability was found for NGC 7469 and MCG+08 11 11, both AGN showed significant variations in both bands during the monitoring campaigns: F_ var ^ X-ray 25 and F_ var ^ mm 13 for NGC 7469 (with the maximum amplitude of a factor of 2 between days), and F_ var ^ X-ray 7 and F_ var ^ mm 2 Note here that fractional uncertainty of the NOEMA observation is comparable, 2 . for MCG+08 11 11. In both cases, the X-ray fractional variability was larger than the one in the mm band.

sources.summary: The paper treats Seyfert galaxy MCG 8-11-11 as a plausible ultrahigh-energy cosmic-ray source, emphasizing its X-ray and gamma-ray luminosities, closer redshift, and S0-primary viability.

quotes.quote: The quasar 3C 147 and the Seyfert galaxy MCG 8-11-11 are attractive candidates.

Just outside the 2 error box of the primary's incoming direction is the Seyfert galaxy MCG 8-11-11. It is also unusual, with large X-ray and low-energy gamma-ray luminosities ( 4.6 10^ 44 ^ 1 in the 20-100 region and 7 10^ 46 ^ 1 in the 0.09-3 region). At a redshift of z=0.0205 , it is much closer than 3C 147, but it is still too distant for the flux to be consistent with the observed proton flux at lower energies elbert .
For sources such as MCG 8-11-11, S^0 clearly can account for the observed event without upsetting the proton flux at lower energies.

sources.summary: The paper reports continuum reverberation mapping of the Seyfert 1 galaxy MCG 08-11-011, measuring wavelength-dependent optical/near-IR lags and an accretion disk larger than thin-disk predictions.

quotes.quote: We report the results from a photometric reverberation mapping campaign carried out with the C18 telescope at the Wise Observatory from 2019 to 2020, targeting the active galactic nucleus (AGN) MCG 08-11-011.

We find wavelength-dependent lags, ( ) , up to 7 days between the multiband light curves of MCG 08-11-011. The observed lags are larger than predictions based on standard thin-disk theory by a factor of 3-7 .

In this work, we analyzed six months of densely sampled (daily cadence) photometric monitoring data of the Seyfert 1 galaxy MCG 08-11-011, and we present detections of optical and near-IR inter-band continuum time lags.

sources.summary: The paper describes MCG 8 11 11 as having a strong nuclear radio component, northwest compact elongation, larger northern emission, and a single somewhat resolved VLBI component.

quotes.quote: MCG 8 11 11 (0551+464) Y : Our map shows a strong nuclear component with a compact elongation extending toward the north-west (P.A. 45 ^ ). There is also larger-scale extended emission towards the north. The bright central component is coincident with the optical nucleus Clements81 , and the total extent of the north-south structure is 3 arcsec. The radio image of at 8.4 GHz (VLA A array) is similar to ours.

sources.summary: The paper models MCG 8-11-11 as a Seyfert 1 hard-X-ray source whose weak reflection requires a large inclination, with acceptable fits sensitive to treatment of the Fe-line band.

quotes.quote: We found values of corresponding to : 81 for IC 4329a , and NGC 4151 , and 67 for MCG 8-11-11 . These large inclination angles reflect the small amount of reflection in these objects. Using Fig.  rdemu , one can check that these angles are in good agreement with the previously derived R parameter values : 1.03 ( =60 ), 1.64 ( =0 ) and 0.43 ( =65 ), respectively for IC 4329a , MCG 8-11-11 and NGC 4151 .

In MCG 8-11-11 the best fit values of in model b is found to be close to 1., while ignoring the 6-7 keV channels lead to =0.51 but with a ^ 2 value very close to that obtained with fixed to 1 (model ).

sources.summary: The paper finds broad ionised reflection in MCG+8-11-11, with moderate reflection strength and spectra typical of broad relativistic reflection whose reflector is ionised.

quotes.quote: Evidence for a broad and ionised reflection component at 95 confidence is found in five observations: MCG+8-11-11, Mrk 110, NGC 7469(1), Mrk 6 and NGC 7213, with the first three being significant even at 99 confidence.

The REFLIONX reflection strengths of the accretion disc in MCG+8-11-11, Mrk 6, Mrk 110 and NGC 7469(1) are between R = 0.55-0.97 , which is slightly less than the strength, R = 1 , expected by a flat accretion disc subtending a solid angle of 2 below a central point source.

Hence the spectra of MCG+8-11-11, Mrk 6 and Mrk 110 show properties entirely typical of sources with broad, relativistic reflection components, with the sole difference being that the reflector is ionised.

gmw_00002402.name: WISEA J152228.01 + 274141.3 source
gmw_00002402.overview: The WISEA J152228.01 + 274141.3 source is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy that coincides with the radio core of a wide-angle-tailed radio source [arXiv:2107.01795]. Oppositely directed radio jets from the galaxy bend sharply and form a trail behind it due to interactions with the dense intracluster medium [arXiv:2107.01795].

sources.summary: The WISEA source is identified as the likely AGN host and radio core feeding a bar-shaped remnant radio galaxy whose jets trail through the dense cluster medium.

quotes.quote: We suggest that the remnant radio galaxy has been seeded by the lobes of the active galactic nucleus (AGN), hosted by the WISEA J152228.01 + 274141.3 source, demonstrating the connection between AGNs and remnant radio sources.

The two oppositely directed radio jets emanating from the apex of the WISEA J152228.01 + 274141.3 source initially traverse toward the southeast and northwest directions. As the galaxy plows through the dense intracluster gas, these jets traversing in opposite directions form a trail, after sharp bends in the jets, behind the WISEA J152228.01 + 274141.3 source due to interaction with the ICM.

Furthermore, the position of the WISEA J152228.01 + 274141.3 source exactly coincides with the likely radio core position of the wide-angle-tailed radio source. The source diagnostic based on the number of low-energy relativistic electrons also suggests that the remnant radio galaxy is fed by the small radio tail of the wide-angle-tailed FR I radio galaxy.

gmw_00002403.name: HIZOA J0836-43
gmw_00002403.overview: HIZOA J0836-43 is an optically obscured, gas-rich disk galaxy hidden behind the Milky Way [arXiv:0808.4040, arXiv:2405.15629]. It is a luminous infrared starburst galaxy undergoing active, inside-out disk building fueled by its large gas reservoir, with no signs of a merger or strong active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0808.4040, arXiv:1112.0379].

sources.summary: The paper presents HIZOA J0836-43 as an extreme gas-rich hidden disk galaxy undergoing luminous infrared starburst activity, with weak AGN signatures, a prominent evolved bulge, and inside-out stellar disk growth.

quotes.quote: HIZOA J0836-43 is an extreme gas-rich ( M_ HI =7.5 10^ 10 M_ ) disk galaxy which lies hidden behind the strongly obscuring Vela region of the Milky Way. Utilizing observations from the Spitzer Space Telescope , we have found it to be a luminous infrared starburst galaxy with a star formation rate of 21 M_ yr^ 1 , arising from exceptionally strong molecular PAH emission ( L_ 7.7 = 1.50 10^ 9 L_ ) and far-infrared emission from cold dust. The galaxy exhibits a weak mid-infrared continuum compared to other starforming galaxies and U/LIRGs. This relative lack of emission from small grains suggests atypical interstellar medium conditions compared to other starbursts. We do not detect significant [ Ne v ] or [ O iv ] , which implies an absent or very weak AGN.

The galaxy possesses a prominent bulge of evolved stars and a stellar mass of 4.4( 1.4) 10^ 10 M_ . With its plentiful gas supply and current star formation rate, a doubling of stellar mass would occur on a timescale of 2 Gyr. Compared to local galaxies, HIZOA J0836-43 appears to be a ``scaled-up" spiral undergoing inside-out formation, possibly resembling stellar disk building processes at intermediate redshifts.

sources.summary: The paper presents HIZOA J0836-43 as an unusually massive, gas-rich local LIRG with extended star formation, strong PAH emission, disk-like star formation efficiency, and z~1-like disk building.

quotes.quote: HIZOA J0836-43 is the most massive ( M_ HI = 7.5 10^ 10 M_ ) galaxy detected in the HIPASS volume ( = -90 \ to +25 , v < 12 700 km s ^ 1 ) and lies optically hidden behind the Milky Way. Markedly different from other extreme \ disks in the local universe, it is a luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) with an actively star forming disk ( > 50 kpc), central to its 130 kpc gas disk, with a total star formation rate (SFR) of 20.5 M_ yr^ 1 . Spitzer spectroscopy reveals an unusual combination of powerful polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon (PAH) emission coupled to a relatively weak warm dust continuum, suggesting photodissociation region (PDR)-dominated emission.
