Perseus I indeed lies in the vicinity of one of the planes, LGP1 (offset by 141 15 kpc, compared to the plane's root-mean-square [ RMS ] height of 55 kpc), as defined by the dwarf galaxies UGC 4879, Leo A, Leo T, Phoenix, Tucana, WLM, Cetus, IC 1613 and Andromeda XVI.

sources.summary: The paper reports discovery of Perseus I, also called Andromeda XXXIII, as a low-surface-brightness dwarf near M31 with distance and structure consistent with a possible Andromeda satellite.

quotes.quote: We present the discovery of a new dwarf galaxy, Perseus I/Andromeda XXXIII, found in the vicinity of Andromeda (M31) in stacked imaging data from the Pan-STARRS1 3 survey. Located 27.9 away from M31, Perseus I has a heliocentric distance of 785 65 , compatible with it being a satellite of M31 at 374^ +14 _ 10 from its host. The properties of Perseus I are typical for a reasonably bright dwarf galaxy ( M_V = -10.3 0.7 ), with an exponential half-light radius of r_h = 1.7 0.4 arcminutes or r_h = 400^ +105 _ 85 at this distance, and a moderate ellipticity ( = 0.43^ +0.15 _ 0.17 ). The late discovery of Perseus I is due to its fairly low surface brightness ( _0=25.7^ +1.0 _ 0.9 mag/arcsec ^2 ), and to the previous lack of deep, high quality photometric data in this region. If confirmed to be a companion of M31, the location of Perseus I, far east from its host, could place interesting constraints on the bulk motion of the satellite system of M31.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Perseus I as one of three newly studied M31 dwarf satellites, confirming its systemic velocity and measuring its average metallicity from Keck/DEIMOS spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: We present Keck II/DEIMOS spectroscopy of the three distant dwarf galaxies of M31 Lacerta I, Cassiopeia III, and Perseus I, recently discovered within the Pan-STARRS1 3 imaging survey. The systemic velocities of the three systems ( v_ ,helio = -198.4 1.1 , 371.6 0.7 , and 326 3 , respectively) confirm that they are satellites of M31.

We also use our spectroscopic data to determine the average metallicity of the 3 dwarf galaxies ( = -2.0 0.1 , 1.7 0.1 , and 2.0 0.2 , respectively). All these properties are typical of dwarf galaxy satellites of Andromeda with their luminosity and size.

sources.summary: The paper describes Perseus as a Pan-STARRS1 dwarf followed up spectroscopically, with structural parameters, deeper Solo observations, and relevance to Local Group satellite-plane studies.

quotes.quote: Perseus Perseus I, also known as And XXXIII, was discovered by in the Pan-STARRS1 3 survey and followed up with spectroscopy. The structural parameters derived are based on the maximum likelihood method Martin2008 . The Solo observations are about 2 magnitudes deeper than the PanSTARRS data. The location of this dwarf makes it an interesting object with respect to the ``planes of satellites" around the Local Group (see Pawlowski2014 for a discussion of Perseus in this context).

gmw_00002418.name: UGC 3697
gmw_00002418.overview: UGC 3697 is a superthin disk galaxy that is part of a small galaxy group containing the peculiar elliptical galaxy UGC 3714 [arXiv:astro-ph/0412391]. It features an extremely warped stellar disk, a transient dynamical state likely caused by a recent satellite accretion event rather than tidal interactions [arXiv:astro-ph/0412391].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 3697 as an extremely warped superthin disk galaxy whose present morphology is attributed to recent satellite accretion rather than tides from UGC 3714.

quotes.quote: Here we describe preliminary results from the analysis of one of these systems, UGC 3697. UGC 3697 has been referred to as the ``Integral Sign'' galaxy ( ) owing to the pronounced S shaped bending of its stellar disk (Fig. 1). Although some degree of warping is observed in the stellar and/or gaseous disks of a large fraction of galaxies ( ), few are are warped as dramatically as UGC 3697. The rarity of warps with similar amplitudes ( 15 of the disk diameter) suggests UGC 3697 is in a transient dynamical state.

While UGC 7321 is a rather isolated system ( ), UGC 3697 is part of a small galaxy group that includes the peculiar elliptical UGC 3714 and several dwarf galaxies ( ). UGC 3714, at a projected distance of 39 kpc from UGC 3697, is the optically brightest group member, and has long been presumed to be responsible for exciting the warp of UGC 3697 via tidal effects (e.g., ).

However, tides are rather ine ff icient warp drivers (e.g., ), and our new observations have for the f irst time revealed strong evidence that an accretion event rather than tidal effects are responsible for the present morphology of UGC 3697. We estimate that a mass of \ of a few times 10^ 8 M_ has been recently accreted onto UGC 3697 from an infalling satellite.

gmw_00002419.name: J1131 0204
gmw_00002419.overview: J1131 0204 is a minor merger system containing two resolved stellar nuclei embedded within a galactic disk [arXiv:1011.5246, arXiv:1603.07925]. The galaxy displays a bar structure, spiral arms, and tidal features [arXiv:1603.07925], and it is identified as a candidate binary active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1003.3467, arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1131 as a binary-AGN candidate minor merger, with the closest nuclear pair, a dominant disk, spiral arms, bar structure, and a radio AGN in J1131E.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1108+0659 and SDSS J1146+5110 (J1108 and J1146) are confirmed to be binary AGNs. For the other two targets, SDSS J1131-0204 and SDSS J1332+0606 (J1131 and J1332), the current data are still consistent with the binary AGN scenario, but the possibility of only one AGN ionizing both components in the merger cannot be ruled out. The four optically selected kpc-scale binary AGNs were observed using the WFC3 onboard the \ in Cycle 18 under program GO 12363, between 2011 March and June (with observing dates listed in Table tab:obs ).

J1108, J1146 and J1332 are major mergers (see discussion below) where the two stellar nuclei are comparably bright. J1131 is a minor merger which displays a clear dominant disk structure with bars and spiral arms. Tidal features are prominent in the Y band images in all cases. J1108 displays an extended low-surface brightness component to the east of the galaxy. J1131 shows features of perturbation in the spiral disk. A bar structure is observed along the line of the two nuclei. J1146 has an extended tidal tail and the bulges are elongated and twisted. J1332 also displays extended tidal features and its bulges appear to be distorted.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSSJ 113126.08-020459.2 as one of four kpc-scale binary AGNs, with double stellar components, merger tidal features, and spatially separated active emission-line nuclei.

quotes.quote: Here we report initial results on the discovery of four binary AGNs, SDSSJ 110851.04+065901.4 , SDSSJ 113126.08-020459.2 , SDSSJ 114642.47+511029.6 , and SDSSJ 133226.34+060627.4 . Deep NIR imaging revealed double stellar bulges in SDSSJ 110851.04+065901.4 , SDSSJ 113126.08-020459.2 , and SDSSJ 133226.34+060627.4 , while SDSSJ 114642.47+511029.6 's SDSS image shows clear evidence of a recent merger.
The NIR images show two stellar bulges in each of these systems. In addition, all four systems show tidal features suggestive of mergers.

The spatial correspondence between the two stellar continuum peaks and two \ velocity peaks in all cases strongly indicates we are seeing two active galaxies (Figure fig:2dspec ). We see unambiguous spatial separations between the red and blue velocity components in all targets and in each strong emission line.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1131 0204 as a binary AGN candidate with two stellar nuclei, matching emission-line velocity components, a galactic disk, extended ionized gas, and residual spiral structure.

quotes.quote: J1131 0204 . This object was reported in Liu_etal_2010b . It shows two resolved stellar nuclei separated by 0.6 . The slit spectroscopy confirmed the coincidence of the two \ velocity components with the two NIR nuclei. Its K_s band image and 2d spectrum for the \ region are shown in Fig. \ fig:1131-0204 . The two stellar nuclei are embedded within a galactic disk, and the long ``spur'' features seen in the 2d spectrum to >15 kpc are ionized gas emission from the disk which traces the galactic rotation curve. The best-fit K_s band model consists of one S e rsic bulge ( n 3 ) and one de Vaucouleurs bulge embedded in an exponential disk.

gmw_00002420.name: J1146+5110
gmw_00002420.overview: J1146+5110 is a confirmed binary active galactic nucleus system [arXiv:1603.07925] that contains double stellar nuclei [arXiv:1011.5246, arXiv:2011.09961]. The system is a major merger [arXiv:1603.07925] characterized by an extended tidal tail [arXiv:1603.07925], twisted bulges [arXiv:1603.07925], and double-peaked emission lines [arXiv:2011.09961].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1146 as a confirmed binary AGN major merger, with a large nuclear separation, extended tidal tail, twisted bulges, and blue inner spiral-arm structure.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1108+0659 and SDSS J1146+5110 (J1108 and J1146) are confirmed to be binary AGNs. For the other two targets, SDSS J1131-0204 and SDSS J1332+0606 (J1131 and J1332), the current data are still consistent with the binary AGN scenario, but the possibility of only one AGN ionizing both components in the merger cannot be ruled out. The four optically selected kpc-scale binary AGNs were observed using the WFC3 onboard the \ in Cycle 18 under program GO 12363, between 2011 March and June (with observing dates listed in Table tab:obs ).

J1108, J1146 and J1332 are major mergers (see discussion below) where the two stellar nuclei are comparably bright. J1131 is a minor merger which displays a clear dominant disk structure with bars and spiral arms. Tidal features are prominent in the Y band images in all cases. J1108 displays an extended low-surface brightness component to the east of the galaxy. J1131 shows features of perturbation in the spiral disk. A bar structure is observed along the line of the two nuclei. J1146 has an extended tidal tail and the bulges are elongated and twisted. J1332 also displays extended tidal features and its bulges appear to be distorted.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSSJ 114642.47+511029.6 as one of four kpc-scale binary AGNs, with double stellar components, merger tidal features, and spatially separated active emission-line nuclei.

quotes.quote: Here we report initial results on the discovery of four binary AGNs, SDSSJ 110851.04+065901.4 , SDSSJ 113126.08-020459.2 , SDSSJ 114642.47+511029.6 , and SDSSJ 133226.34+060627.4 . Deep NIR imaging revealed double stellar bulges in SDSSJ 110851.04+065901.4 , SDSSJ 113126.08-020459.2 , and SDSSJ 133226.34+060627.4 , while SDSSJ 114642.47+511029.6 's SDSS image shows clear evidence of a recent merger.
The NIR images show two stellar bulges in each of these systems. In addition, all four systems show tidal features suggestive of mergers.

The spatial correspondence between the two stellar continuum peaks and two \ velocity peaks in all cases strongly indicates we are seeing two active galaxies (Figure fig:2dspec ). We see unambiguous spatial separations between the red and blue velocity components in all targets and in each strong emission line.

sources.summary: The paper finds J114642.47+511029.6 has the next-smallest velocity offset, visually identifiable double-peaked [O III], narrow component FWHMs, and the only SDSS-image double nucleus.

quotes.quote: The next smallest velocity offset is for J114642.47+511029.6 (287 4 instrumental resolution). In this case, we can visually identify the double-peaked [ O III ] lines. It is expected that the distinguishability of double-peaked lines will depend on the FWHM of each line (i.e. it will be easy to distinguish double-peaked lines if their FWHMs are small and will be hard if their FWHMs are large). The FWHMs of J114642.47+511029.6 are 266 \ and 276 \ for the blueshift and redshift component, respectively. Their FWHM values are somewhat smaller than the average value of FWHM of the total sample, which is 317 .
We find only one object has a double nucleus in its center (J114642.47+511029.6).

sources.summary: The paper treats J1146+5110 as a binary AGN candidate with marginally resolved 2MASS nuclei, slit-confirmed velocity components, optical double nuclei, and complex southern-nucleus NLR structure.

quotes.quote: J1146+5110 . This object was reported in Liu_etal_2010b . The double stellar nuclei were marginally resolved in the 2MASS K_s image, and our slit spectroscopy subsequently confirmed the coincidence of the two \ velocity components with the two NIR nuclei. This is also one of the few cases that show resolved optical double nuclei in the SDSS images. The southern nucleus itself seems to have a complex NLR geometry and two NLR velocity components. The double nucleus was well resolved in the NIR AO imaging in Fu_etal_2010 , with a luminosity contract of 0.7 magnitude (0.28 dex).

gmw_00002421.name: J1332+0606
gmw_00002421.overview: J1332+0606 is a major galaxy merger containing two stellar nuclei [arXiv:1603.07925] that are spatially coincident with two active emission-line velocity components [arXiv:1003.3467, arXiv:1011.5246]. The system exhibits extended tidal features [arXiv:1603.07925, arXiv:1003.3467], distorted bulges [arXiv:1603.07925], and is classified as a binary active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:1603.07925, arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1332 as a binary-AGN candidate major merger, with double stellar nuclei, extended tidal features, distorted bulges, and hard X-ray emission from J1332NE.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1108+0659 and SDSS J1146+5110 (J1108 and J1146) are confirmed to be binary AGNs. For the other two targets, SDSS J1131-0204 and SDSS J1332+0606 (J1131 and J1332), the current data are still consistent with the binary AGN scenario, but the possibility of only one AGN ionizing both components in the merger cannot be ruled out. The four optically selected kpc-scale binary AGNs were observed using the WFC3 onboard the \ in Cycle 18 under program GO 12363, between 2011 March and June (with observing dates listed in Table tab:obs ).

J1108, J1146 and J1332 are major mergers (see discussion below) where the two stellar nuclei are comparably bright. J1131 is a minor merger which displays a clear dominant disk structure with bars and spiral arms. Tidal features are prominent in the Y band images in all cases. J1108 displays an extended low-surface brightness component to the east of the galaxy. J1131 shows features of perturbation in the spiral disk. A bar structure is observed along the line of the two nuclei. J1146 has an extended tidal tail and the bulges are elongated and twisted. J1332 also displays extended tidal features and its bulges appear to be distorted.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSSJ 133226.34+060627.4 as one of four kpc-scale binary AGNs, with double stellar components, merger tidal features, and spatially separated active emission-line nuclei.

quotes.quote: Here we report initial results on the discovery of four binary AGNs, SDSSJ 110851.04+065901.4 , SDSSJ 113126.08-020459.2 , SDSSJ 114642.47+511029.6 , and SDSSJ 133226.34+060627.4 . Deep NIR imaging revealed double stellar bulges in SDSSJ 110851.04+065901.4 , SDSSJ 113126.08-020459.2 , and SDSSJ 133226.34+060627.4 , while SDSSJ 114642.47+511029.6 's SDSS image shows clear evidence of a recent merger.
The NIR images show two stellar bulges in each of these systems. In addition, all four systems show tidal features suggestive of mergers.

The spatial correspondence between the two stellar continuum peaks and two \ velocity peaks in all cases strongly indicates we are seeing two active galaxies (Figure fig:2dspec ). We see unambiguous spatial separations between the red and blue velocity components in all targets and in each strong emission line.

sources.summary: The paper reports J1332+0606 as a binary AGN candidate whose optical and J-band nuclei coincide with two velocity components and are modeled with three stellar components.

quotes.quote: J1332+0606 . This object was reported in Liu_etal_2010b . The two stellar nuclei were clearly resolved in optical (SDSS) and J band images, and are spatially coincident with the two \ velocity components seen in our slit spectroscopy. Its J band image and 2d spectrum for the \ region are shown in Fig. \ fig:1332+0606 . The best-fit J band model consists of three components: a S e rsic bulge ( n 2 ) for the southern nucleus, an exponential disk for the northern nucleus, and an exponential disk with a scale-length of r_s 1.6 . The luminosity ratio of the three components is 2:1:1 . The less luminous northern stellar component corresponds to the stronger \ emission component seen in the slit spectrum, as in J1322+2631.

gmw_00002422.name: J1356+1026
gmw_00002422.overview: J1356+1026 is an ongoing ultraluminous infrared galaxy merger [arXiv:1404.4875, arXiv:1807.04298] containing two nuclei separated by less than three kiloparsecs [arXiv:1504.01391, arXiv:2111.13578]. The northern nucleus hosts a luminous, obscured Type 2 quasar [arXiv:1404.4875, arXiv:1807.04298] that drives a large-scale, 20-kiloparsec ionized gas outflow [arXiv:1504.01391, arXiv:1404.4875].

sources.summary: SDSS J1356+1026 is described as a luminous Type 2 quasar with double peaks, twin stellar nuclei, a 20 kpc outflow, and ambiguous dual/offset AGN X-ray evidence.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1356+1026 is a luminous Type 2 quasar with double-peaked emission lines in its SDSS spectrum LI10.1 , and follow-up observations have shown that this system is incredibly complex and interesting. Two emission knots seen in long-slit observations GR11.1 that correspond to two stellar nuclei seen in near-infrared ( K_s band) imaging led to conclude that the double peaks in J1356+1026 are likely produced by dual AGNs. The two emission sources, J1356+1026NE and J1356+1026SW, are separated by 1 31 (2.89 kpc). However, observed extended emission in integral-field spectroscopy, in addition to the double stellar nuclei in near-infrared ( K_p ) imaging, that caused them to argue that the double peaks are produced by an extended narrow-line region powered by a single AGN, and not dual AGNs. Further clarification of the complex nature of SDSS J1356+1026 came from observations of the extended emission, which attributed to a spectacular 20 kpc scale outflow driven by radio-quiet quasar feedback originating from the quasar in J1356+1026NE.

We detect both J1356+1026NE and J1356+1026SW in 0.3-8 keV X-rays, with significances of >5 and 4.4 , respectively. The X-ray source positions are in agreement with the two stellar bulges observed in HST /F160W, to within 1.0 times the astrometric errors, and the X-ray spectrum is well-fit by a spectral index =2.11 , which indicates a very soft spectrum. This, when combined with the low soft X-ray to luminosity ratio of each source (Table tbl-3 ; Table tbl-4 ), indicates that photoionization may be contributing to the observed flux of soft X-rays.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1356+1026 as a post-merger system with double nuclei, quasar-powered extended emission, no VLBA detection, and redshifted 21-cm absorption.

quotes.quote: Out of the ten double-peaked AGN, five (J0738+3156, J0842+0547, J1203+1319, J1243-0058 and J1517+3353) appear as single, undisturbed systems in the available optical images [ Dark Energy Camera Legacy Survey DECaLS; dey2019 , PanSTARRS-1 chambers2016 and SDSS ] . The remaining AGN are either part of galaxy pairs and interacting systems or show disturbed morphology. J0009-0036 has a companion galaxy at a projected separation of 40 kpc and a velocity separation of 460 . J1526+4140 is undergoing merger with another galaxy at 5 kpc and 150 . The optical continuum image of J0912+5320 shows two peaks and a tidal tail, while that of J1352+6541 shows signatures of a possible tidal disturbance. J1356+1026 is a post-merger system that has been observed as part of our \ 21-cm absorption survey in low- z galaxy mergers dutta2018 . Long-slit spectroscopy and NIR imaging of this system show double nuclei separated by 2.9 kpc greene2011,shen2011 . Moreover, there have been observations of extended \ emission in this system, suggesting large-scale outflows powered by feedback from the obscured, radio-quiet quasar greene2012 . Three of the AGN that appear as single systems (J0738+3156, J0842+0547 and J1203+1319) have been observed with the Keck Laser Guide Star Adaptive Optics system and the NIR camera NIRC2 mcgurk2015 . Based on these imaging observations, J1203+1319 has a double spatial structure on scales of <3 arcsec, indicating it is a candidate dual AGN. J0738+3156 and J0842+0547, on the other hand, show single spatial structure with no companions within 3 arcsec. Furthermore, long-slit optical spectra have been obtained for four AGN (J0738+3156, J0842+0547, J1243-0058 and J1517+3353) by comerford2018 . Based on these spectra, J0842+0547 and J1243-0058 are classified as outflows, whereas J1517+3353 is classified as an outflow composite, i.e. an outflow that includes many gas clouds at different velocities. On the other hand, J0738+3156 has a disturbed rotation-dominated profile.

Based on high-resolution ( 0.2 arcsec) multi-band (8-12 GHz) VLA observations muller2015 , J0009-0036 shows a two-sided radio jet suggesting that a radio jet-driven outflow could produce the double-peaked emission. In addition, six of the AGN have been imaged with VLBA at milliarcsecond (mas; 10 pc) resolution liu2018a . Three of them (J0009-0036, J0738+3156 and J1356+1026) remain undetected in the VLBA 8.4 GHz images. J0912+5320 shows three components in the VLBA image, an unresolved central core and northern and southern components extending over 25 mas, indicative of a jet structure. J1243-0058 shows a compact, unresolved component in the VLBA image, and there are no other radio sources stronger than 1 mJy within 1 arcsec. J1352+6541 shows a faint component at 2.4 mas from the primary component.

The \ 21-cm absorption detected towards J1356+1026 is discussed in detail in see their figure 2 and section 3.1.8 dutta2018 . Briefly, the absorption has a velocity width encompassing 90 of the total optical depth ( 90) of 216 , and corresponds to \ 5 10^ 21 \ for \ = 100 K and \ = 1. The \ 21-cm absorption profile is best fit with a single Gaussian component. The location of the peak optical depth is redshifted by 150 \ from the systemic redshift that corresponds to average of the two \ emission lines (see Fig. fig:sdss_gmrt_spec ), and is aligned with the stronger of the two \ emission peaks. We note that the frequency range over which blueshifted \ 21-cm absorption could be detected in this system is affected by RFI.

sources.summary: The paper presents J1356 as a merging two-nucleus QSO2 with complex disturbed ionized-gas kinematics, a large blueshifted [O III] bubble, and a likely radio-accelerated redshifted outflow.

quotes.quote: J1356 J1356 is a merging system with two nuclei, north and south, observed in the optical ( Greene12, Harrison14 ) and in molecular gas ( Sun14, Ramos22 ). From our GTC/MEGARA data we find the north nucleus to be the brightest, as it is also the case for CO. According to Ramos22 , it contains 55 of the total molecular mass in the system. The kinematic maps shown in Fig. fig:J1356_extended are then centred at the [ O III ] peak of the north nucleus. The ionized gas shows positive velocities north-east of the north galaxy, and negative to the south-west, but the major axis is not aligned with the CO major axis (PA=110 ^ ) reported by Ramos22 .

Overall, the kinematics are complex and disturbed because of the ongoing merger. In the case of this QSO2 we detect both the approaching and receding sides of the outflow, with higher values of vel _ 05 and vel _ 95 corresponding to an enhancement in W _ 80 . We detect the [ O III ] outflowing ``bubble'' proposed by Greene12 , which is expanding towards the south up to 12 kpc from the north nucleus (maximum R _ out 12.6 kpc), with a PA 197^ .

sources.summary: The paper presents hot X-ray gas aligned with a 20-kpc ionized bubble in SDSS J1356+1026, an ongoing ULIRG merger hosting a luminous obscured quasar and expanding shell.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present observations of hot X-ray gas that is aligned with a known 20-kpc--scale ionized gas bubble in the radio-quiet quasar SDSS J135646.10+102609.0 (SDSS J1356+1026 hereafter). SDSS J1356 + 1026 is an on-going merger and ultra-luminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) located at z =0.123 ( D_L = 568 Mpc). The Northern nucleus hosts a luminous ( L_ bol 10^ 46 erg s ^ 1 ) obscured quasar that was originally discovered in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey SDSS; yorketal2000 based on its 5007 emission zakamskaetal2003,reyesetal2008 . The source was also flagged as a possible dual active nucleus, because of the presence of multiple velocity components in the SDSS spectrum, although that classification has been questioned liuetal2010,fuetal2012 .

Our interest here lies in the 20 kpc outflow that we discovered in long-slit observations with Magellan Paper I hereafter greeneetal2009,greeneetal2012 . In our Magellan spectrum, we detect the line splitting that is characteristic of an expanding ``bubble'' (Figure fig:xrayimage ). The shell region extends 10 kpc ( 4 ) to the South of the quasar hosted by the Northern nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper presents J135646+102609 as a double-peaked type 2 AGN/Seyfert in a disturbed merger, with two merging nuclei and an interacting pair hosting an obscured quasar.

quotes.quote: J135646+102609 has aroused great interest in the recent literature. Liu et al. ( ) included this object in a sample of type 2 AGNs with double-peaked NEL. Its morphology is clearly disturbed, suggesting an ongoing merger.

The presence of two merging galactic nuclei has been demonstrated from optical (Greene et al. ; Fu et al. ) and near-infrared (Shen et al. ) observations. Greene et al. ( ) have published two long-slit Magellan LDSS-3 spectra and additional spectral band data. They consider that this object is a pair of interacting galaxies that hosts a luminous obscured quasar in its northern nucleus.
Note that the optical spectrum from LAMOST or SDSS is that of a typical unobscured Seyfert 2 galaxy, all lines being narrow.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1356 + 1026 as a ULIRG merger with an obscured Type 2 AGN, molecular and ionized outflows, compact radio emission, and detected absorption.

quotes.quote: J1356 + 1026 This system is classified as an ULIRG and consists of two merging nuclei separated by 2.5 kpc. The northern nucleus hosts a luminous obscured Type 2 double-peaked \ AGN greene2009,liu2010,fu2011 . CO molecular emission has been mapped from this system sun2014 . Both molecular and ionized gas outflows have been observed emerging from the obscured quasar sun2014,greene2012 . Extended X-ray emission has also been observed around this quasar, that is co-spatial with the ionized gas outflow.
This quasar shows compact radio emission in our GMRT 1.4 GHz map. Absorption is detected against the radio emission, and is best fit with a single Gaussian.

sources.summary: The paper studies spatially resolved UV and X-ray diagnostics of the obscured quasar SDSS J1356+1026, concluding its galactic-scale superwind is radiation-pressure dominated.

quotes.quote: Here, we report the first spatially resolved UV emission spectroscopy of a prototypical (radio-quiet) quasar-driven superwind around the obscured quasar SDSS J1356+1026 at z=0.123 . We observe ratios of O VI/C IV, N V/C IV, and C IV/He II that are remarkably similar for outflowing gas clouds 100 pc and 10 kpc from the nucleus. Such similarity is expected for clouds with AGN radiation pressure dominated dynamics.

Here, we present the first sensitive and spatially resolved UV spectra of a prototypical quasar-driven superwind at low redshift, SDSS J135646.10+102609.0 (SDSS J1356+1026). SDSS J1356+1026 is a radio-quiet, obscured quasar at redshift z=0.123 driving a prototypical superwind on galactic scales Greene:2012 . The AGN has an estimated bolometric luminosity of L_ bol 2 10^ 46 and a black hole mass of M_ 10^8 M _ Sun:2014 . The outflow is characterized by kinematically disturbed ionized gas with double-peaked velocity structure (full width 800 \ in projection, inconsistent with bound gravitational motion) observed in [ O III ] emission at 10 kpc from the nucleus that can be modelled as outflowing shells see Figure figure:J1356 ; Greene:2012 .

sources.summary: J1356+10 is described with a few-kiloparsec jet, faint 160 kpc lobe emission, merger-related ionised structures, continuum halo overlap, and kinematic evidence for an ionised bubble.

quotes.quote: The jet sizes are in the range 1-few kpc (J0945+17, J1000+12, J1356+10, the Teacup), 10 kpc (J1517+33), several tens of kpc (J0841+01, J0853+38, J0907+46). Additional extended AGN related radio structures (e.g. lobes or radio bubbles, plumes, hot spots) are detected in J0853+38 (472 kpc), J0841+01 (40 kpc), J1000+12 (43 kpc), J1356+10 (160 kpc), the Teacup (19 kpc). The radio component at 11 kpc from the AGN in J0945+17 may be a jet or a lobe. The lobe emission from the 160 kpc radio source in J1356+10 is extremely faint, and additional observations are necessary to unambiguously confirm and accurately image this emission.

All QSO2 in our sample except J0907+46 show unambiguous evidence for mergers/interactions based on the distorted optical continuum morphologies and the presence of prominent tidal continuum features. The H image confirms this is also the case for J0907+46 (Fig. fig0907a ). Many of the large scale ( 10 kpc) ionised features identified in our sample are remnants of the interaction/process. In some cases, they trace entire or partial tidal tails, bridges or shells (e.g. J0841+01, J0907+46, J1000+12, J1316+44 and J1356+10; see also Villar2010,Villar2017 ). The extended ionised gas frequently overlaps partially with wide large and amorphous scale continuum halos clearly associated with a merging or interaction event (e.g. J0802+25, J1437+30, J1356+10, the Teacup). Five QSO2 in our sample present firm or tentative evidence for giant bubbles of ionised gas: J0841+01, J0945+17, J1000+06, J1356+10 and the Teacup.

sources.summary: The paper treats J1356 as a spectacular merging QSO2 system with two nuclei, a dominant northern host, high SFR, extended CO features, and molecular outflow signatures.

quotes.quote: SDSS J135646.10+102609.0 (J1356) This QSO2 is hosted in a spectacular merger system showing a completely distorted optical morphology. There are two nuclei (N and S), separated by only 1.1 (2.4 kpc). The N nucleus is the QSO2, whose host galaxy is the dominant member of the merger in both the optical and molecular gas 2014ApJ...790..160S .

Although the underlying stellar population is dominated by old stars 2009ApJ...702..441G , the SFR derived from its IR luminosity is the largest in our sample (69 M _ yr ^ 1 ; see Table tab2 ), likely a consequence of the ongoing merger. Other components of the merging system, as identified from the ALMA and HST morphologies by 2014ApJ...790..160S , are the S nucleus and the western arm (W arm hereafter). The latter is a huge stellar feature containing a large percentage of the molecular gas in the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1356+1026 as a disturbed ULIRG binary-AGN candidate with two optical continuum sources, offset emission knots, a rich emission structure, and a southern bubble.

quotes.quote: J1356+1026 . This object was studied in Greene_etal_2011 . It shows two continuum sources in the optical separated by 3 kpc, corresponding to the north and south knots of \ emission seen in the slit spectrum in Greene_etal_2011 . The two knots of \ emission have a relative velocity offset of 200 \ km s^ 1 . The slit spectrum in Greene_etal_2011 shows a rich \ emission structure, including a giant \ bubble to the South of the southern continuum. Its K_s band image is shown in Fig. \ fig:1356+1026 . This is a galaxy with a highly disturbed morphology and its IRAS fluxes indicate that it is a ULIRG.

sources.summary: J1356+1026 shows evidence for two nuclei; some archival CO data were excluded for resolution concerns, while deeper ALMA CO(2-1) was used later.

quotes.quote: Further, J1108+0659 and J1356+1026 show evidence of hosting two nuclei.

We also note the availability of CO(1-0) and CO(3-2) data for J1356+1026 from , however due to the differences in resolution (at 1.3 and 0.6 arcsec for CO(1-0) and CO(3-2) respectively) and therefore high chance of over-resolving the total CO emission compared to the QFeedS observations, these are excluded from any analysis.
However, we note that J1356+1026 does have a CO(2-1) detection in the deeper ALMA data from which we utilise in later analysis.

gmw_00002423.name: J0002+0045
gmw_00002423.overview: The active galactic nucleus J0002+0045 features two velocity components that are spatially offset by 0.8 arcseconds [arXiv:1011.5246]. It has a companion galaxy located 5 arcseconds to the northeast at the same redshift, which lacks line emission [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper notes J0002+0045 has spatially offset velocity components without matching NIR double nuclei, plus a same-redshift northeast companion lacking line emission.

quotes.quote: J0002+0045 . For this object the two velocity components of \ are spatially offset by 0.8 , but they do not have a corresponding pair of nuclei seen in the NIR image. Fig. \ fig:0002+0045 shows the K_s image and the 2d spectrum. Note that this object has a north-east companion 5 \ away, which does not have \ emission; this companion galaxy is at the same redshift as J0002+0045 measured from stellar absorption features in the slit spectrum.

gmw_00002424.name: J0116 1025
gmw_00002424.overview: J0116 1025 is an active galactic nucleus hosted by a galaxy with a clear disk component [arXiv:1011.5246]. The system features two spatially offset emission peaks without a corresponding double nucleus, and a small neighboring galaxy lies approximately 4 arcseconds to the west [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper interprets J0116 1025 as a disk-host AGN with two offset emission peaks, no corresponding double nucleus, and a nearby western galaxy outside the slit.

quotes.quote: J0116 1025 . This object has two spatially offset \ emission peaks, which correspond to the blue- and red-shifted components in the double-peaked line profile respectively. The spatial offset of the two \ components is 1.1 , and there is no corresponding double nucleus seen in the NIR image. Fig. \ fig:0116-1025 shows the K_s image and the 2d spectrum. The host galaxy clearly has a disk component. There is also a small galaxy about 4 \ away from the center of J0116-1025 to the West, which was not covered by our slit observation.

gmw_00002425.name: J0135 0058
gmw_00002425.overview: J0135 0058 is a disk galaxy that exhibits an apparent disk morphology in near-infrared imaging [arXiv:1011.5246]. The system features a faint, southeastern tidal structure and displays a slight velocity gradient consistent with a rotational disk [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0135 0058 as a disk system with a southeastern tidal feature, nearly constant flux ratio, slight velocity gradients, and a preferred rotational-disk explanation.

quotes.quote: J0135 0058 . Fig. \ fig:0135-0058 shows the K_s image and the 2d spectrum for this object. The long slit was placed to cover the tidal feature to the south-east of the galaxy seen in the NIR image, and hence was misaligned with the major axis of the disk.

The / \ flux ratio is almost constant until the outermost apertures (6 and 7), where the / \ decreases as it is now tracing the faint tidal feature seen from the 2d spectrum shown in Fig. \ fig:0135-0058 . There is a slight velocity gradient for the blue- and red-shifted components. Although the spatial offset between the two \ components is only 0.2 \ (possibly due to the misaligned slit position angle), we believe a rotational \ disk is the best explanation given the velocity gradient seen in the 2d spectrum as well as the apparent disk morphology seen in the NIR.

gmw_00002426.name: J0135+1435
gmw_00002426.overview: The active galactic nucleus host galaxy J0135+1435 exhibits two spatially offset velocity components [arXiv:1011.5246]. These components lack corresponding double nuclei in the near-infrared and are best explained by a rotating disk [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper finds J0135+1435 has offset emission components without NIR double nuclei, central flux-ratio changes, clear velocity gradients, and a rotational-disk interpretation.

quotes.quote: J0135+1435 . Fig. \ fig:0135+1435 shows the K_s image and the 2d spectrum for this object. The two velocity components of \ are spatially offset by 0.8 , while no corresponding pair of nuclei was seen in the NIR.

The / \ flux ratio increases towards the center of the continuum emission. Velocity gradients for both \ components are clearly seen in Fig. \ fig:0135+1435_diag , and there is some indication of increasing line width toward the center of the continuum emission, although decomposition into two components is not always successful at each aperture. This object is best explained by a rotational \ disk, with an asymptotic flat rotation velocity V_ i 200 kms^ 1 .

gmw_00002427.name: J0156 0007
gmw_00002427.overview: J0156 0007 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy that exhibits a disk-like morphology in near-infrared imaging [arXiv:1011.5246]. The galaxy's narrow emission lines and weak velocity gradients suggest that its dominant motion is coherent disk rotation [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0156 0007 as a disk-like AGN whose offset velocity components, weak gradients, narrow lines, and aperture behavior favor coherent rotational disk motion.

quotes.quote: J0156 0007 . Fig. \ fig:0156-0007 shows the K_s image and the 2d spectrum for this object. A disk morphology is apparent in the NIR image. The two velocity components of \ are spatially offset by 0.7 , with no corresponding pair of nuclei seen in the NIR.

The / \ flux ratio is almost constant at each aperture location. Weak velocity gradients can been seen for both \ components. At most locations the line width is narrow, which suggests that coherent rotation is the dominant motion. This object is best explained by a rotational \ disk, with an asymptotic flat rotation velocity V_ i 150 kms^ 1 .

gmw_00002428.name: J0400 0652
gmw_00002428.overview: J0400 0652 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy that displays a smooth, single profile in near-infrared imaging [arXiv:1011.5246]. Its narrow-line region kinematics are complex, showing a high central velocity dispersion, velocity gradients, and multiple components likely driven by a combination of disk rotation and outflows [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0400 0652 as NLR kinematics, with extended emission, no AO double nucleus, high central dispersion, velocity gradients, and evidence for multiple components.

quotes.quote: J0400 0652 . This object shows a smooth single profile in the K_s image, while its slit spectra show spatially resolved \ emission extending to 3 . A subsequent NIR AO image obtained by Fu_etal_2010 did not reveal a double nucleus at 0.1 \ resolution.

The 2d spectrum along PA =95^ shows rather complicated \ emission region kinematics. It shows a high velocity dispersion near the center, and some velocity gradient along the slit direction, which is indicative of disk rotation and/or outflows. Clearly there are more than two \ components. On the other hand, the 2d spectrum along PA =173^ shows much less structure, which is presumably caused by the different spatial coverage. This object is best explained by NLR kinematics, possibly involving both outflows and rotation.

gmw_00002429.name: J0837+1500
gmw_00002429.overview: The active galactic nucleus host galaxy J0837+1500 has been observed using near-infrared K_s band imaging and spectroscopy [arXiv:1011.5246]. This system displays two distinct velocity components that are spatially offset by 1.5 arcseconds, though no corresponding pair of nuclei is detected in the near-infrared [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper presents J0837+1500 with K_s imaging and spectroscopy showing velocity components offset by 1.5 arcseconds and no corresponding pair of NIR nuclei.

quotes.quote: J0837+1500 . Fig. \ fig:0837+1500 shows the K_s image and the 2d spectrum for this object. The two velocity components of \ are spatially offset by 1.5 , with no corresponding pair of nuclei seen in the NIR. Fig. \ fig:0837+1500_diag shows the 1d slices of the 2d spectrum at different distances from the peak of the continuum emission.

gmw_00002430.name: J0851+1327
gmw_00002430.overview: J0851+1327 is an edge-on disk galaxy [arXiv:1011.5246]. Its two velocity components are spatially offset by 0.6 arcseconds, but they lack a corresponding pair of near-infrared nuclei and are instead best explained by a rotational disk [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0851+1327 as an edge-on disk galaxy whose offset velocity components lack NIR double nuclei and are best explained by a rotational disk.

quotes.quote: J0851+1327 . Fig. \ fig:0851+1327 shows the K_s image and the 2d spectrum for this object. The two velocity components of \ are spatially offset by 0.6 , with no corresponding pair of nuclei seen in the NIR. This galaxy has an edge-on disk component seen in the NIR, which is also seen in its SDSS optical image and the 2d spectrum shown in Fig. \ fig:0851+1327_diag . This object is best explained as a rotational \ disk, given the disk morphology seen in the NIR and in the optical.

gmw_00002431.name: J0958 0051
gmw_00002431.overview: J0958 0051 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy that displays a disk component in near-infrared imaging [arXiv:1011.5246]. The galaxy exhibits two spatially offset velocity components with clear velocity gradients, which are best explained by a rotational disk [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper reports J0958 0051 as a disk-component AGN with offset velocity components, no NIR double nuclei, clear velocity gradients, and a rotational-disk explanation.

quotes.quote: J0958 0051 . Fig. \ fig:0958-0051 shows the K_s image and the 2d spectrum for this object. A disk component is seen in the NIR image. The two velocity components of \ are spatially offset by 0.8 , with no corresponding pair of nuclei seen in the NIR.
Velocity gradients are clearly seen for both \ components. This object is best explained by a rotational \ disk.

gmw_00002432.name: J1038+0255
gmw_00002432.overview: The active galactic nucleus J1038+0255 exhibits two velocity components that are spatially offset by 1 arcsecond [arXiv:1011.5246]. This structure is best explained by either a rotational emission-line disk or biconical outflows, as no corresponding pair of nuclei is observed in the near-infrared [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1038+0255 as having offset velocity components without matching NIR nuclei, best explained by either a rotational emission-line disk or biconical outflows.

quotes.quote: J1038+0255 . Fig. \ fig:1038+0255 shows the K_s image and the 2d spectrum for this object. The two velocity components of \ are spatially offset by 1 , with no corresponding pair of nuclei seen in the NIR. This object is best explained by either a rotational \ disk or biconical outflows.

gmw_00002433.name: J1146 0226
gmw_00002433.overview: The galaxy J1146 0226 features a smooth, single stellar bulge [arXiv:1011.5246]. Its two-dimensional spectrum displays a clear velocity gradient across the slit, indicating that the system is best explained as a rotational disk [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper interprets J1146 0226 as a rotational disk: its smooth K_s bulge and 2D spectrum show a rotation-curve-like velocity gradient across the slit.

quotes.quote: J1146 0226 . Fig. \ fig:1146-0226 shows the K_s image and the 2d spectrum for this object. The K_s image shows a smooth single stellar bulge, while the 2d spectrum shows a clear rotation-curve like velocity gradient across the slit. Under poor spatial resolution or if the object were observed at higher redshifts, the rotation-curve would be unresolvable, and the \ emission would appear as two distinct velocity components spatially offset by a few kpc. This object is best explained by a rotational \ disk.

gmw_00002434.name: J1341+2219
gmw_00002434.overview: J1341+2219 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy that displays a disk component in near-infrared imaging [arXiv:1011.5246]. Its narrow-line velocity components are spatially offset, and the system is best explained by a rotating disk rather than a pair of nuclei [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1341+2219 as a disk-component AGN with offset narrow-line velocity components, no NIR pair of nuclei, and a rotational-disk interpretation.

quotes.quote: J1341+2219 . Fig. \ fig:1341+2219 shows the K_s image and the 2d spectrum for this object. A disk component is seen in the NIR image. The \ part of our 2d spectrum has low quality so we show the \ region instead. The two NEL velocity components are spatially offset by 1.7 , with no corresponding pair of nuclei seen in the NIR. This object is best explained by a rotational \ disk.

gmw_00002435.name: J1552+0433
gmw_00002435.overview: J1552+0433 is a disk galaxy with a disk component that appears warped at its north-west edge [arXiv:1011.5246]. The galaxy is classified in the narrow-line region kinematics category, featuring two emission components spatially offset by 1.2 arcseconds [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1552+0433 in the NLR-kinematics category, noting offset emission components, a warped disk-like K_s image, and no expected binary bulge pair.

quotes.quote: J1552+0433 . For this object the two \ components are spatially offset by 1.2 \ in their peak emission, as seen from its 2d spectrum shown in Fig. \ fig:1552+0433 . Its K_s image shows a disk component, which seems to be warped at the north-west edge. Nevertheless, two stellar bulge components separated by 1.2 \ would have been seen in the NIR image if this object were a kpc-scale binary AGN. Thus we classify this object in the NLR kinematics category.

gmw_00002436.name: J1630+1649
gmw_00002436.overview: The active galactic nucleus J1630+1649 exhibits two velocity components that are spatially offset by 0.8 arcseconds [arXiv:1011.5246]. However, near-infrared adaptive optics imaging shows no double nucleus, suggesting the system is best explained by a rotational disk [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1630+1649 as lacking NIR double nuclei despite offset velocity components, including AO imaging, and favors a rotational-disk explanation.

quotes.quote: J1630+1649 . Fig. \ fig:1630+1649 shows the K_s image and the 2d spectrum for this object. The two \ velocity components are spatially offset by 0.8 , with no corresponding pair of nuclei seen in the NIR. A recent NIR AO image of this object at 0.1 \ resolution did not show a double nucleus either Fu_etal_2010 . This object is best explained by a rotational \ disk.

gmw_00002437.name: J2304 0933
gmw_00002437.overview: The active galactic nucleus J2304 0933 is hosted by a galaxy with a clear near-infrared disk component [arXiv:1011.5246]. It features spatially offset narrow-line emission components, but adaptive optics imaging reveals no double nucleus, suggesting its properties are driven by narrow-line region kinematics [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper treats J2304 0933 as most likely NLR kinematics, with a clear NIR disk, spatially offset narrow-line components, and no AO-resolved double nucleus.

quotes.quote: J2304 0933 . For this object the NIR image shows a clear disk component. The seeing was quite poor for the slit spectroscopy observation, but the two NEL components seem to be spatially offset by 0.8 \ in their peak emission. A recent NIR AO image of this object reveals no double nucleus at 0.1 \ resolution Fu_etal_2010 . It is thus most likely a case of NLR kinematics.

gmw_00002438.name: J0942+1254
gmw_00002438.overview: The galaxy J0942+1254 displays spectacular tidal features in both optical and near-infrared imaging [arXiv:1011.5246]. These structures indicate a recent merger, and the system is suspected to host a binary active galactic nucleus with a small projected separation [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper flags J0942+1254 as a likely small-separation binary AGN candidate because its images show spectacular tidal features despite no resolved NIR double nucleus.

quotes.quote: J0942+1254 . This object was observed with two slit positions. Both of the 2d spectra show no spatial offset ( <0.2 ) between the two \ velocity components. No double nucleus was seen in the NIR image. However, the optical and NIR images of this object show spectacular tidal features, indicative of recent mergers. This object deserves a higher-resolution observation, and we suspect it is likely to be a small projected separation binary AGN.

gmw_00002439.name: J1009+0133
gmw_00002439.overview: The active galactic nucleus J1009+0133 features a minor companion galaxy located approximately 1 arcsecond north-west of its peak near-infrared emission [arXiv:1011.5246]. Although this near-infrared structure is suggestive of a binary system, further observations are required to confirm the hypothesis [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper considers J1009+0133 suggestive of a binary because of a minor NIR companion, but says a new slit position is needed for confirmation.

quotes.quote: J1009+0133 . This object shows a minor companion 1 \ north-west of the peak emission in the NIR image. Unfortunately due to our slit position, we couldn't confirm that these two stellar nuclei correspond to the two \ components seen in the 2d spectrum. Although the NIR image alone is suggestive of a binary, a new slit position is needed to test this hypothesis.

gmw_00002440.name: J1019+0134
gmw_00002440.overview: The galaxy J1019+0134 exhibits a disturbed morphology in its outskirts, which indicates a recent merger event [arXiv:1011.5246]. Its spectrum shows two line components spatially offset by 0.3 arcseconds, which may represent either a binary system or narrow-line region kinematics [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1019+0134 as morphologically disturbed with offset line components; current data cannot distinguish a binary from NLR kinematics.

quotes.quote: J1019+0134 . The NIR image shows a disturbed morphology in the outskirts, indicative of a recent merger event. The two \ components are spatially offset by 0.3 \ as shown in the 2d spectrum. Seeing in the NIR is 0.7 . This could either be a binary or NLR kinematics. New slit spectroscopy with a different position angle and/or better spatial resolution are needed to distinguish the two scenarios.

gmw_00002441.name: J1322+2631
gmw_00002441.overview: J1322+2631 is a minor-merger system with a mass ratio of 6.4:1 between its southwest and northeast stellar bulges [arXiv:1504.01391]. The system is a dual active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:1504.01391] that exhibits double-peaked emission lines [arXiv:1011.5246, arXiv:1504.01391] and contains two main stellar nuclei [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1322+2631 as ambiguous because its line components may not coincide with multiple K_s nuclei, but poor seeing leaves a binary interpretation possible.

quotes.quote: J1322+2631 . For this object we only have a slit spectrum covering the \ region. The two narrow line components are spatially offset by 2.1 \ as shown in the 2d spectrum. In the K_s band image we see two main stellar nuclei separated by 2.3 , and there is also a third (minor) companion to the southwest, about 2.5 \ from the central brightest stellar component.

A close examination of the 2d spectrum suggests that the two \ components are probably not spatially coincident with the two main stellar nuclei, even though their separation happens to be consistent with that of the two stellar nuclei. If this were true, it would imply a kinematic origin for the double-peaked \ emission in this object. However, the seeing in our 2d optical spectrum is poor and the locations of the stellar continua are not well determined.

sources.summary: SDSS J1322+2631 is described as a minor-merger dual/offset AGN candidate with two emission components, two stellar bulges, and an X-ray detection in the more massive SW bulge.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1322+2631 is one of the Type 2 double-peaked AGNs selected in , and the follow-up long-slit observations at a position angle of 79^ East of North showed two emission components separated by 2 1 SH11.1 . Since the system was not reobserved at a second position angle, 2 1 is a lower limit on the true spatial separation between the emission components on the sky, and we measured this true separation ( 2 35 , or 5.94 kpc) via the two emission components apparent in the HST /F814W observations of this system (Section hst ). This system is a minor merger with a mass ratio of 6.4:1 between J1322+2631SW and J1322+2631NE. Two X-ray sources spatially coincide with the two stellar bulges to within 0.31 times the astrometric error. We detect 29.8^ +0.0 _ 5.1 counts (for >5 significance detection) in the more massive stellar bulge, J1322+2631SW, but an upper limit of only 0.6 counts in the less massive stellar bulge, J1322+2631NE.

gmw_00002442.name: J2255 0812
gmw_00002442.overview: J2255 0812 is a galaxy characterized by a disturbed disk component [arXiv:1011.5246]. Its active nucleus features two narrow-line emission components with a small spatial offset, alongside extended line emission from ionized gas in its rotating galactic disk [arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper describes J2255 0812 as a disturbed disk with slightly offset narrow-line components and rotating-disk emission, while still requiring sharper data.

quotes.quote: J2255 0812 . The NIR image shows a disturbed disk component, and the two NEL components show a small spatial offset, 0.5 , in the 2d spectrum. Extended line emission is seen on opposite sides of the central continuum, which is caused by ionized gas in the rotating galactic disk. This object is likely a case of NLR kinematics, but a higher resolution imaging observation and/or different slit positions are needed to rule out the binary scenario.

gmw_00002443.name: UGC 7889
gmw_00002443.overview: UGC 7889 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:1203.3226]. It is characterized by a high stellar mass, a low gas fraction, a relatively metal-rich composition, and a red ultraviolet color [arXiv:1203.3226].

sources.summary: The paper highlights UGC 7889 as a Virgo blue compact dwarf with red UV color, high stellar mass, low gas fraction, metal richness, and revised FUV extinction.

quotes.quote: In one object, UGC 7889 (= NGC 4641; see its images in Figure fig:img ), the A_ FUV value reaches 6.9 mag. This object is one of the few BCDs in Virgo detected by ALFALFA and, among the dwarf s-sed sample, it is one of the reddest in m_ FUV m_ NUV color, has one of the highest stellar masses, has a low gas fraction and is relatively metal-rich. Given its UV color, the high A_ FUV value of UGC 7889 derived by the SED fitting is consistent with that predicted by the IRX- relation (see below) as calibrated for starburst galaxies Meurer1999 , but other methods such as the Balmer decrement and the IRX-SFH-color relation (see below), give quite lower extinction estimates. Given the possible degeneracy of its red color arising from either high extinction or strong SF quenching in the Virgo environment, it is probable that the large extinction implied by the SED fitting for this galaxy is unreliable.

With this improvement in the SED fitting, the mean values for the ALFALFA dwarf s-sed sample become A_ FUV = 1.33 mag and A_ NUV = 0.96 mag, with formal uncertainties of 0.42 and 0.32 mag, respectively. In particular, the A_ FUV value is reduced to 2.36 mag for UGC 7889. Since the A_ FUV estimates are reduced by 0.14 mag on average, the SFR is reduced correspondingly by 0.06 dex (see values in SFR ).

gmw_00002444.name: DES J0454 4448
gmw_00002444.overview: DES J0454 4448 is a high-redshift quasar first identified using Dark Energy Survey data [arXiv:1504.03264, arXiv:1701.04852]. Spectroscopic observations indicate the quasar has a redshift of approximately 6.09 to 6.10 [arXiv:1504.03264, arXiv:1701.04852] and is detected in the infrared by WISE [arXiv:1504.03264].

sources.summary: The paper reports DES J0454 4448 as a spectroscopically confirmed z=6.09 quasar, selected as an i-band dropout and detected by WISE.

quotes.quote: Here we report the discovery and spectroscopic confirmation of the z_ AB , Y_ AB = 20.2, 20.2 (M _ 1450 = 26.5) quasar DES J0454 4448 with a redshift of z = 6.09 0.02 based on the onset of the Lyman- forest and a HI near zone size of 4.1 _ 1.2 ^ +1.1 proper Mpc. The quasar was selected as an i-band drop out with i z = 2.46 and z _ AB < 21.5 from an area of 300 deg ^2 . It is the brightest of our 43 candidates and was identified for spectroscopic follow-up solely based on the DES i z and z Y colours. The quasar is detected by WISE and has W1_ AB = 19.68 .

sources.summary: The paper includes VDESJ0454 4448 as the first DES-identified z=6.10 quasar, reobserved in year-one DES data with a recalculated redshift consistent with R15.

quotes.quote: VDESJ0454 4448 (z = 6.10) VDESJ0454 4448 was the first object identified from DES and was the subject of R15; details of the spectroscopic observations are included therein. It is included here as it was covered again by the year one release from the DES and was the second highest ranked candidate in the independent data analysis in this paper. The redshift was recalculated for this object as part of this analysis and was found to be 6.10 0.01 which is consistent with the value given in R15 of 6.09 0.03.

gmw_00002445.name: ID07
gmw_00002445.overview: ID07 is one of the faintest blue Lyman-alpha emitting galaxies [arXiv:2002.02028]. It lacks an Infrared Array Camera detection and exhibits a very steep ultraviolet-continuum slope of approximately 3.4 [arXiv:2002.02028].

sources.summary: ID07 is one of the faintest blue LAEs, lacks an IRAC detection, and has a very steep UV-continuum slope near 3.4 with relatively large uncertainty.

quotes.quote: All galaxies except ID07 were detected in at least one IRAC band.
The other two galaxies ID07 and ID43 have very steep slopes 3.4 . But the errors _ 0.4 are also larger, because they are relatively faint.

gmw_00002446.name: ID28
gmw_00002446.overview: ID28 is a luminous, spectroscopically confirmed Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy at a redshift of approximately 6 [arXiv:2002.02028]. Photometric measurements indicate a flat ultraviolet-continuum slope, which is well-fitted by models of a young, dust-free stellar population with an estimated age of 4 million years [arXiv:2002.02028].

sources.summary: ID28 is a luminous spectroscopically confirmed z~6 LAE with a UV-continuum slope near 2.6; improved H_160 photometry flattened the slope, and standard young dust-free SED models fit it well.

quotes.quote: Among the six blue galaxies, two galaxies ID28 and ID30 have 2.6 0.2 . Their slopes become flatter compared to our previous measurements ( 2.8 and --3.5 with _ 0.4 ) in jiang13a . This is mainly due to the significant improvement of the photometry in H_ 160 . The new photometry shows that the two objects become much `brighter' in H_ 160 , resulting in the flatter slopes.

Our results show that the dust reddening is consistent with zero and the typical age is 4 Myr (the minimum allowed age) for all five galaxies with 2.6 and IRAC detections. Two galaxies ID28 and ID30 with 2.6 are well fitted.

gmw_00002447.name: ID30
gmw_00002447.overview: ID30 is a luminous, spectroscopically confirmed Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy at a redshift of approximately 6 [arXiv:2002.02028]. It has a blue UV-continuum slope of 2.6, which is well fitted by young, dust-free spectral energy distribution models with a typical age of 4 million years [arXiv:2002.02028].

sources.summary: ID30 is a luminous spectroscopically confirmed z~6 LAE with a UV-continuum slope near 2.6; improved H_160 photometry flattened the slope, and standard young dust-free SED models fit it well.

quotes.quote: Among the six blue galaxies, two galaxies ID28 and ID30 have 2.6 0.2 . Their slopes become flatter compared to our previous measurements ( 2.8 and --3.5 with _ 0.4 ) in jiang13a . This is mainly due to the significant improvement of the photometry in H_ 160 . The new photometry shows that the two objects become much `brighter' in H_ 160 , resulting in the flatter slopes.

Our results show that the dust reddening is consistent with zero and the typical age is 4 Myr (the minimum allowed age) for all five galaxies with 2.6 and IRAC detections. Two galaxies ID28 and ID30 with 2.6 are well fitted.

gmw_00002448.name: ID43
gmw_00002448.overview: ID43 is a relatively faint galaxy characterized by an extremely blue ultraviolet slope of approximately 3.4 [arXiv:2002.02028]. Even when excluding its Y_105 band data, this steep slope remains at 3.2 [arXiv:2002.02028].

sources.summary: ID43 has an extremely blue UV slope around 3.4, remains steep even without Y_105, is relatively faint, and cannot be fit reasonably by the explored SED models.

quotes.quote: The other two galaxies ID07 and ID43 have very steep slopes 3.4 . But the errors _ 0.4 are also larger, because they are relatively faint. For ID43, if we exclude its Y_ 105 band data, the slope becomes 3.2 0.6 .
For ID43 with 3.4 0.4 , we are not able to find a reasonable fit to explain its extreme slope.

gmw_00002449.name: ID61
gmw_00002449.overview: ID61 is a high-redshift galaxy that exhibits an obviously elongated shape [arXiv:2002.02028]. The system appears to be interacting, with a minor component that is brighter at longer wavelengths [arXiv:2002.02028].

sources.summary: ID61 is elongated and likely interacting, with a redder non-steep UV slope; the authors cannot separate its components reliably and exclude it from later blue-LAE discussion.

quotes.quote: ID61 is the only galaxy that obviously has an elongated shape. It seems to be an interacting system, and its minor component is brighter at longer wavelengths. Our current \ images do not have enough resolution to reliably separate the two components. The magnitudes quoted in Table 1 are the total magnitudes, and the slope ( 1.41 ) is not steep. We will not discuss this galaxy later.

gmw_00002450.name: ID63
gmw_00002450.overview: ID63 is a high-redshift galaxy with rich photometric constraints [arXiv:2002.02028]. It exhibits a strong IRAC color that is inconsistent with very low metallicity, and its extreme ultraviolet slope can be reasonably reproduced by a two-population stellar model [arXiv:2002.02028].

sources.summary: ID63 has the richest photometric constraints, a strong IRAC color inconsistent with very low metallicity, and a two-population model can reasonably reproduce its 2.9 UV slope.

quotes.quote: We focus on ID63 that has more observed data points than the others. The results are shown in Figure fig:SEDmod2 .

The best-fitted dust extinction is all (nearly) zero, and ages are 1--4 Myr. In panel (a), we try two low metallicities 0.02 and 0.005 Z_ , and find that the IRAC bands are poorly fitted. ID63 is much brighter in IRAC 1 than it is in IRAC 2. Many such galaxies have been found, and it is believed that the IRAC 1 flux is boosted by strong \ line emission at this redshift. At a very low metallicity, however, the O/H ratio is reduced, which cannot produce the (IRAC 1 -- IRAC 2) color seen in ID63.

We obtain a reasonable result with 2.9 for ID63, shown in Figure fig:SEDmod2 (d). Although this is not a real multi-population SED modeling, it provides a hint that the combination of different populations may be a solution to produce extreme slopes.

gmw_00002451.name: ID64
gmw_00002451.overview: ID64 is a bright, compact galaxy located in the Subaru/XMM-Newton Deep Survey [arXiv:2002.02028]. It is an isolated source that is well-detected in the K band and exhibits a blue ultraviolet slope of approximately -2.8 [arXiv:2002.02028].

sources.summary: ID64 is among the brightest SXDS galaxies in the sample, has robust compact photometry and K-band detection, and shows a UV slope near --2.8 with reduced uncertainty.

quotes.quote: The two SXDS galaxies ID63 and ID64 are the brightest in our sample, and can be well detected in the K band from the ground. We have adopted the VLT HAWKI-I K_ s band photometry from gal13 . The total magnitudes are 25.72 0.17 and 25.76 0.17 mag, respectively. We also find that the Y_ 105 band data points of the two objects largely deviate from the linear fits. The two galaxies are bright, compact, and isolated in the \ images, so their photometric measurements are robust.

Two galaxies ID63 and ID64 respectively have 2.9 and --2.8 with _ 0.15 , which are consistent with our previous measurements 2.8 and --3.0, but with reduced errors. The main reason for the consistent results is that the two objects are bright, and the previous measurements were reliable.

gmw_00002452.overview: 2054 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z=5.978 [arXiv:2109.13257]. It is a [CII] emitter situated at a projected distance of 20.0 proper kiloparsecs from a strong OI absorber [arXiv:2109.13257].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2054 as a z=5.978 [CII] emitter 20 pkpc from a strong OI absorber, measuring its [CII] luminosity, SFR limits, and massive halo.

quotes.quote: At the redshift of the \ absorber, an emission line is detected at 4.3- significance which we interpret as \ emission from a galaxy at the \ absorber's redshift. We refer this \ emitter as 2054 in the following.
Further, from our moment-0 image (Fig. 1a), the projected impact parameter between 2054 and the \ absorber is 3.5 , corresponding to 20.0 proper kpc (pkpc) at z=5.978 .

The velocity-integrated \ luminosity of 2054 is estimated to L_ CII = (7.0 1.7) 10^ 7 L_ [ ref. ] , corresponding to a based SFR ( SFR_ [ CII ] ) of 6.8 1.7 M_ yr^ 1 [ ref. ] . No submillimeter continuum is detected at the position of 2054, yielding a 3- upper limit of 37 Jy. Converting this luminosity to a total infrared SFR ( SFR _ TIR ) yields the upper limit SFR _ TIR < 11 [ ref. ] . To estimate the halo mass directly from the luminosity, we adopt a relation between L_ [ CII ] and halo mass at z 6 black ref. , by assuming that 2054 resides in the galaxy main sequence. Then, the derived halo mass is 4.1 10^ 11 \ M_ , with 1- scattering of 0.5 dex.

gmw_00002453.name: NGC 6868
gmw_00002453.overview: NGC 6868 is a giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0606165] and the brightest member of the Telescopium group [arXiv:astro-ph/0210574, arXiv:1001.2567]. It hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0711.3607, arXiv:2403.03974] with central LINER emission [arXiv:0711.3607, arXiv:1008.0009], a compact molecular gas disc [arXiv:2210.14922], and a dust lane [arXiv:2304.11194].

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 6868's inner stellar populations, finding old metal-rich stars, residual central star formation, a dust lane, and alpha-enhancement structure interpreted as merger evidence.

quotes.quote: We use Gemini integral field unit observations to map the stellar population properties in the inner region ( 680 470   pc 2 ) of the galaxy NGC 6868. In order to understand the physical and chemical properties of the stellar content of this galaxy, we performed stellar population synthesis using the starlight code with the MILES simple stellar population models. We measured the absorption line indices Fe4383, Mg 2 , Mg b , Fe5270, Fe5335 for the whole FoV, and used them to derive Fe3 and [ MgFe ] '. These indices were used to derive [ /Fe ] . This galaxy is dominated by old metal-rich populations (12.6 Gyr; 1.0 and 1.6 Z _ ) with a negative metallicity gradient.
We also found a recent ( 63   Myr ) metal-rich (1.6  Z _ ) residual star formation in the centre of the galaxy. A dust lane with a peak extinction in the V band of 0.65  mag is seen.
We interpret this as the result of a past merger with another galaxy with a different [ /Fe ] history, thus explaining the [ /Fe ] maps.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6868 as an E3 elliptical in GR28, radio and infrared emitting, with central LINER-like ionized gas likely powered by a low-luminosity active nucleus and shocks.

quotes.quote: NGC 6868 is classified as E3 in RC3 The Third Reference Catalogue of bright galaxies ( vaucouleurs ). . It is part of the GR28 cluster which contains as well the galaxies NGC 6861, NGC 6870, NGC 6851 and NGC 6861D maia89 . Figure figngc6868 shows an R band image of the field containing NGC 6868 in two scales (obtained from the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database - NED). The effective radius of NGC 6868 is Re=40 car93a . NGC 6868 is a radio source sava77 and emits in the infrared as indicated by 60 and 100 m IRAS observations.

Concerning the ionized gas in NGC 6868, the ratios [ NII ] _ 6584 /H , [ OI ] _ 6300 /H and [ SII ] _ 6717,31 /H consistently suggest the presence of a LINER at the galaxy center. These results, together with the stellar population synthesis, suggest that the main source of gas ionization in NGC 6868 is non-thermal, produced by a low-luminosity active nucleus, probably with some contribution of shocks to explain ionization at distances of 3.5 kpc from the nucleus.

sources.summary: Spitzer IRS spectra show NGC 6868 is a LINER early-type galaxy with unusual PAH emission, old stars dominating short-wavelength MIR light, and processed neutral PAHs linked to recent carbon-star rejuvenation.

quotes.quote: We present the analysis of Spitzer -IRS spectra of four early-type galaxies, NGC 1297, NGC 5044, NGC 6868, and NGC 7079, all classified as LINERs in the optical bands. Their IRS spectra present the full series of H _2 rotational emission lines in the range 5--38 m, atomic lines, and prominent PAH features. We investigate the nature and origin of the PAH emission, characterized by unusually low 6-9/11.3 m inter--band ratios.

We find the old stellar population contributes about 97.5 , 98.5 , 99.6 , and 98.5 of the observed fluxes at 5.6 m for NGC 1297, NGC 5044, NGC 6868, and NGC 7079, respectively. We then may consider that the old stellar population dominates the MIR emission at shorter wavelengths.

We advance a new scenario, where this PAH emission is naturally explained in terms of the presence of an underlying intermediate age population of carbon stars, which are continuously feeding the ISM with pristine carbonaceous material. If our interpretation is correct, the anomalous PAH spectra are strong evidence of a rejuvenation episode that affected the evolution of the ETGs in the recent past.

sources.summary: NGC 6868 contains a compact molecular gas disc centered on the radio core, with absorption consistent with ordinary disc clouds aligned with the continuum.

quotes.quote: NGC 6868 's molecular gas is centred on the radio core, extends over approximately 0.5 kpc, and has a mass of 1.3 0.2 10^ 8 M _ . The gas appears to be in a disc viewed at a small angle of inclination (perhaps 10-20^ ) and has a velocity range of approximately 300 km/s. We are likely seeing typical clouds in this galaxy (i.e. those away from the core and in the more extended disc) for three reasons.

Firstly, the column density of molecular gas along the line-of-sight to the continuum is relatively high at 960 M _ /pc ^ 2 , so an alignment between the continuum and several of its molecular clouds is expected. This expected column density is also very similar to the actual column density of the absorption (1100 M _ /pc ^ 2 ). When chance alignments between the continuum and molecular clouds visible in emission are responsible for the absorption, they would likely have velocities which match the emission, as is the case. The absorption regions also have relatively low velocity dispersions ( = 6.4 km/s, FWHM = 15 km/s), typical of clouds within the disc of a galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6868 as a control E2 galaxy, noting evidence for a recent central merger and a poor globular cluster system with bimodal colors.

quotes.quote: NGC 6868 , the brightest galaxy of the loose group Telescopium, an E2 galaxy, at 26.8 Mpc , which has probably suffered a recent merger in its central part (Hansen et al., 1991). This galaxy will be used as our control sample.

NGC 6868 To this ``control--sample'' galaxy the magnitude limits applied were 22.0 B 24.5 and 20.0 R 23.0 and the GCLF turnover is located at B = 25.7 0.2 and R = 24.1 0.2 . The S_N values found for this galaxy were 1.8 1.1 and 1.9 1.0 , for the core and power law models, respectively, and show a poor GCS around this galaxy. The radial profile has a regular value slope of 1.4 0.3 . The color distribution shows two sub--populations with peaks at (B-R)_0 = 1.12 0.07 and 1.42 0.07 ( [ Fe/H ] = -1.48 0.22 dex and 0.55 0.22 dex ). 51 of the GCs are part of the blue population and 49 of the red population.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 6868 as the brightest Telescopium group elliptical, showing a cold front and cool spiral X-ray tail best explained by subgroup-merger sloshing.

quotes.quote: Both the brightest group member, elliptical galaxy NGC 6868 ( 20^h09^m54.1^s , 48^ 22'46 4 , B_T=11.66 , NED) and the second brightest group galaxy, SA0 galaxy NGC 6861 ( 20^h07^m19.5^s , 48^ 22'12 8 , B_T = 12.12 , NED), 25 7 to the west, show signs of recent interactions and are likely to merge.

Surface brightness and temperature maps of the brightest group galaxy NGC 6868 show a cold front edge 23 kpc to the north, and a cool 0.62 keV spiral-shaped tail to the south. Analysis of the temperature and density across the cold front constrains the relative motion between NGC 6868 and the ambient group gas to be at most transonic; while the spiral morphology of the tail strongly suggests that the cold front edge and tail are the result of gas sloshing due to the subgroup merger.

X-ray surface brightness and temperature maps of NGC 6868 show a cold front 23 3 kpc to the north of the galaxy nucleus and a spiral-shaped tail of cool 0.62 keV gas to the south, suggesting relative motion between the galaxy and the surrounding IGM. Analysis of the density and temperature across the edge constrains the relative motion between NGC 6868 and the IGM to be at most transonic ( 460 ). While we cannot rule out the possibility that the tail is composed of ram-pressure stripped galaxy gas, the tightly wound spiral-like morphology of the tail in the temperature map strongly suggests that that the tail is a result of the `sloshing' of gas in the gravitational potential, set in motion by the previous gravitational encounter between the NGC 6868 and NGC 6861 subgroups.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 6868 as a borderline Telescopium-group elliptical whose sparse entropy data roughly follow the predicted slope and precipitation limit.

quotes.quote: The remaining multiphase elliptical (NGC 6868, _v = 252 km s^ 1 ), is sparsely sampled but appears to be intermediate between the supernova-sweeping and precipitation-limited cases.

NGC 6868 Blue symbols in the panel for NGC 6868, one of two large galaxies in the Telescopium group, represent the three data points from Werner+2014MNRAS.439.2291W . A teal line shows a steady flow solution with _v = 252 km s^ 1 and r_0 = 2  kpc. The data are sparse but consistent with the predicted entropy slope ( _K = 0.7 ) from equation ( eq-alpha_K_vc ).
This slope makes the entropy profile of NGC 6868 nearly parallel to the precipitation limit, and it tracks near t_ cool / t_ ff 20 (V15).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC6868 as a nearby elliptical Abell S851 BCG with LINER emission, old metal-rich stars, possible historic merger signatures, settled molecular gas, and strong absorption.

quotes.quote: NGC6868 Galaxy and environment NGC6868 is a nearby elliptical and the brightest cluster galaxy of Abell S851. The central radio source has a flat spectrum with a core flux density of 105 mJy at 5 GHz Hogan2015b . Rickes2008 find LINER emission from the galaxy centre due a combination of photoionization, a low-luminosity AGN, and shocks. Recently, Benedetti2023 have mapped the galaxy's stellar populations using Gemini GMOS IFU. They find it to be dominated by old metal-rich stars, and suggest a historic merger as an explanation for spatially varying metallicity gradients.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6868 as the giant elliptical dominating the elongated Telescopium group, with low X-ray emission centered on the galaxy and several bright group members nearby.

quotes.quote: The last studied group, NGC 6868 Telescopium, LGG430; gar93 , extends for more than 2 degrees on the sky and shows an elongated form in the East-West direction. It is dominated by the giant elliptical galaxy NGC 6868 (E2) and by the galaxies ESO233-G035 (S0), NGC6870 (SAB(rs)ab) and NGC 6861D (SA(s)). The group shows a low X-ray emission with L _ X 10^ 41 h ^ 2 erg s ^ 1 centered on NGC 6868 galaxy beu99 . gar93 identified 11 galaxies brighter than M_ V =-18 as group members, while ram96 identified five other members.

gmw_00002454.name: Cosmic Snake
gmw_00002454.overview: The Cosmic Snake is a strongly lensed, star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.036 [arXiv:2109.06206]. It has a stellar mass of approximately 4.0e10 solar masses and a star formation rate of 30 solar masses per year [arXiv:2307.07551, arXiv:2109.06206].

sources.summary: The Cosmic Snake is a strongly lensed z~1 main-sequence galaxy with high magnification; the paper measures its resolved molecular gas and SFR, finding local-like resolved KS slopes.

quotes.quote: The Cosmic Snake and A521 are two strongly lensed galaxies located behind the galaxy clusters MACS J1206.2-0847 and Abell 0521, respectively. They have several multiple images that are magnified by factors of a few to hundreds. For both of these galaxies we can see an arc including several images of the source galaxy with significant stretching and amplification, as well as an isolated counter-image with almost no stretching and amplification of a few (see Figs. fig:CS_image , fig:CS_ci_image , and fig:A521_image ). These galaxies are representative of MS star-forming galaxies at z 1 , with the Cosmic Snake having a stellar mass M_ =(4.0 0.5) 10^ 10 M_ and SFR =30 10 M_ .yr^ 1 , and A521 having M_ =(7.4 1.2) 10^ 10 M_ and SFR =26 5 M_ .yr^ 1 .
We find for the Cosmic Snake SFR = 1.5 0.1 M_ .yr^ 1 .kpc^ 2 and M_ mol = 570 60 M_ .pc^ 2 . For A521 we have SFR = 1.8 0.1 M_ .yr^ 1 .kpc^ 2 and M_ mol = 430 50 M_ .pc^ 2 .

We were able to perform a linear regression on the measurements in the Cosmic Snake for scales 1600 pc , obtaining slopes of n^ CS _ 200 pc = 1.00 0.08 , n^ CS _ 400 pc = 0.9 0.1 , n^ CS _ 800 pc = 1.0 0.3 , and n^ CS _ 1.6 kpc = 1.1 0.4 .
