These slopes are similar to those typically found in local galaxies. For A521 no overall slope could be inferred at any scale.

sources.summary: The paper studies the Cosmic Snake as a strongly lensed z=1.036 galaxy, measuring its counter-images, stellar mass, star formation rate, and compact radial scale lengths.

quotes.quote: The Cosmic Snake arc in MACS J1206.2-0847 and the arc in Abell 0521 (which we refer to as A521) are two strongly lensed galaxies. We see several counter-images of them that are stretched and magnified from a few to hundreds of times (see Figs. fig:snake_rgb and fig:A521_rgb ). The Cosmic Snake galaxy has four multiple images along an arc with magnifications between 10 and >100 (Fig. fig:snake_rgb ). All of them correspond to the northern part of the source galaxy. The northern and southern regions are images of slightly more than half of the galaxy, whereas the central region shows two highly magnified counter-images, both with fewer than 20 of the galaxy.

With a total lensing-corrected M_ of (4.0 0.5) 10^ 10 M_ , a total lensing-corrected SFR of 30 10 M_ .yr^ 1 , and a redshift of z=1.03620 cava_nature_2018 , the Cosmic Snake is representative of MS star-forming galaxies at z 1 (e.g. speagle_highly_2014 ). The integrated M_ and SFR of the Cosmic Snake were measured on the isolated counter-image to the north-east, so that they also contain the 50 of the mass and SFR that is absent in the arc.

sources.summary: The paper describes the cosmic snake as a strongly lensed massive galaxy with a star formation rate of 30 solar masses per year, whose inferred clump masses and sizes depend on image magnification.

quotes.quote: The effect on clump properties due to different observational resolution is evident in the recent study of a strong gravitationally lensed massive galaxy ( M_ 4 10^ 10 \ M_ ) "the cosmic snake" 2018NatAs...2...76C and the comparison to its counter image. With the SFR 30 \ M_ \ yr^ 1 it is comparable to galaxies on the main sequence at z 1-2 . Furthermore, the identified clump masses and sizes tend to correlate with the different magnifications of the images, giving 10^7 \ M_ at high magnification ( 30 pc resolution) and several 10^8 \ M_ at low magnification ( 300 pc resolution).

gmw_00002455.name: A521
gmw_00002455.overview: A521 is a strongly lensed star-forming spiral galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1.04 [arXiv:2109.06206, arXiv:2307.07551]. It is representative of main-sequence star-forming galaxies at this epoch, hosting a stellar mass of 7.4 10^10 solar masses and a star formation rate of 26 solar masses per year [arXiv:2109.06206, arXiv:2307.07551].

sources.summary: The paper treats A521 as a strongly lensed z=1.044 star-forming galaxy with visible spiral structure, measured stellar mass, star formation rate, and long radial scale lengths.

quotes.quote: The A521 arc (Fig. fig:A521_rgb ) shows five counter-images of a source galaxy at z=1.04356 with magnifications between 3 and >50 . The north-eastern counter-image is almost not stretched. This counter-image clearly shows that the A521 galaxy has the spiral structure typical of local late-type disky spiral galaxies. It is amplified by a factor growing from 2 on the easternmost part to 5-6 on the western spiral arm. Both spiral arms are visible in the second counter-image, which is the most elongated and magnified.

A521 has an M_ of (7.4 1.2) 10^ 10 M_ and an SFR of 26 5 M_ .yr^ 1 . These quantities were derived from the spectral energy distribution (SED) fit on the HST photometry (see Sect. sec:HSTdataA521 ) measured on the north-eastern counter-image. A521 is also representative of MS star-forming galaxies at z 1 .

sources.summary: A521 is a strongly lensed z~1 main-sequence galaxy with measured molecular gas and SFR surface densities, but the paper finds no reliable resolved KS slope at any scale.

quotes.quote: The Cosmic Snake and A521 are two strongly lensed galaxies located behind the galaxy clusters MACS J1206.2-0847 and Abell 0521, respectively. They have several multiple images that are magnified by factors of a few to hundreds. For both of these galaxies we can see an arc including several images of the source galaxy with significant stretching and amplification, as well as an isolated counter-image with almost no stretching and amplification of a few (see Figs. fig:CS_image , fig:CS_ci_image , and fig:A521_image ). These galaxies are representative of MS star-forming galaxies at z 1 , with the Cosmic Snake having a stellar mass M_ =(4.0 0.5) 10^ 10 M_ and SFR =30 10 M_ .yr^ 1 , and A521 having M_ =(7.4 1.2) 10^ 10 M_ and SFR =26 5 M_ .yr^ 1 .
We find for the Cosmic Snake SFR = 1.5 0.1 M_ .yr^ 1 .kpc^ 2 and M_ mol = 570 60 M_ .pc^ 2 . For A521 we have SFR = 1.8 0.1 M_ .yr^ 1 .kpc^ 2 and M_ mol = 430 50 M_ .pc^ 2 .

We were able to perform a linear regression on the measurements in the Cosmic Snake for scales 1600 pc , obtaining slopes of n^ CS _ 200 pc = 1.00 0.08 , n^ CS _ 400 pc = 0.9 0.1 , n^ CS _ 800 pc = 1.0 0.3 , and n^ CS _ 1.6 kpc = 1.1 0.4 . These slopes are similar to those typically found in local galaxies. For A521 no overall slope could be inferred at any scale.

gmw_00002456.name: GRB 970508 and its host galaxy
gmw_00002456.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 970508 is a faint, late-type blue compact or starburst galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9809111, arXiv:astro-ph/9906031] that is best fit as an Scd starburst H II galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0107399]. It exhibits high central surface brightness [arXiv:astro-ph/9809111], internal extinction [arXiv:astro-ph/0102493], and is structurally modeled as an exponential disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0508197].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB970508 host as a faint late-type, high-surface-brightness blue compact/starburst galaxy after OT fading, with normal pre-Sbc spectra excluded.

quotes.quote: After 200 days any power-law decay has ceased and the OT contamination to the host galaxy flux is already less than the observational errors. We derive the broad band spectrum of the host galaxy, without OT, and compare it with the average continuum spectra of galaxies of different Hubble types. The spectral distribution of normal galaxies with types earlier than Sbc are confidently excluded.

But in that case the GRB970508 host galaxy turns out to be in the region of blue compact galaxies with heightened central surface brightness. The present late-time observations of the optical counterpart GRB970508 allows us to think that in 470 days after the GRB the observed BVR_cI_c fluxes can be attributed only to the GRB host galaxy. The intrinsic physical properties of the possible host galaxy for GRB970508 correspond to a late-type galaxy, with its M_B 18.0 , 19.0 (depending on the cosmological model) clearly fainter than the knee of the general Schechter luminosity function at about 20.8 mag. The latter, together with the closeness of the spectral distribution for Mrk 1267 in the UV range of spectrum, allows us to attribute GRB970508 host galaxy to the blue compact galaxies. Altogether, the observed absorption lines Mg ii , Mg i and Fe ii , the evidence for emission line [ O ii ] and evidence for a high surface brightness are consistent with a starburst process going on in the present GRB host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper finds the GRB 970508 host is a faint dwarf-like galaxy, estimates R and U magnitudes plus sub-L* luminosity, and suggests starburst or blue compact dwarf categories.

quotes.quote: New U and R-band images taken on 11 May showed that the source was declining in brightness (Castro-Tirado et al. 1997) and by ten months later, it almost reached the quiescent level, revealing a 25th mag galaxy in the R-band (Bloom et al. 1998a, Castro-Tirado et al. 1998a). The decay of the optical flux could be modelled in terms of a power law decay, with F_R t^ 1.19 0.01 ( ^2/dof=2.56 ), assuming a host galaxy with R = 25.72 0.20 according to Bloom et al. (1998). On the basis of an extrapolation of the V- and B-band measurements into the U-band, assuming a given spectral shape (Bloom et al. 1998b, Zharikov et al. 1998), we obtain U = 26.56 0.25 for the host.

With respect to the host galaxy, and depending on the value of the K correction and spectral index ( F_ ^ ) considered, we obtain M_ B =-17.53 0.5 ( =-3 ) or M_ B =-16.6 0.6 ( =-1.56 ). The first value is consistent with that reported by Zharikov et al. (1998). In both cases the object is well below the knee of the galaxy luminosity function, M^ _ B 20.6 (Schechter 1976) and the luminosity of the host galaxy L relative to the characteristic luminosity L^ is 0.06 or 0.15 depending of the value of M_ B considered. The host galaxy corresponds to any of the following categories: a starburst, a red dwarf starburst, a irregular dwarf, a HII galaxy or a blue compact dwarf galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper fits the GRB 970508 host with an exponential disk, finds an ellipticity consistent with prior work, but notes a de Vaucouleurs profile cannot be ruled out.

quotes.quote: For example, the host of GRB 970508, (Fruchter et al. 2000) was best fit with an exponential disk with a scale length of 0 046 0 006, and an ellipticity of 0.70 0.07. We find almost the same result: the ^2 of the exponential disk profile is clearly lower (0.57 vs. 0.85 for the ^ 1/4 profile). The axis ratio that we find (0.39) corresponds to an ellipticity of 0.61, which is also consistent with the result of Fruchter et al. (2000). Although Figure 2 seems to graphically show that the exponential and de Vaucouleur profiles are equally well fit, the central part of the profile is better fit with the exponential. However, this is partially misleading, as although GRB 970508 is better fit by an exponential model, the ^ 1/4 model cannot be ruled out by the data (with ^ 2 =0.85).

sources.summary: The paper models BVRI photometry of the host galaxy of GRB 970508, finding significant internal extinction, estimating its absolute magnitude and reddening, and fitting the continuum SED with a high-extinction population-synthesis template.

quotes.quote: We present one of the results of BVRI photometry of the hosts of GRB for the host galaxy of GRB 970508 and the theoretical modeling of its continuum spectral energy distribution (SED) to show that it is important to take into account internal extinction in the host galaxies. We compared the BVRI broad-band flux spectrum of the host to template SEDs of local starburst galaxies and found that there is a significant internal extintion in this host. Moreover, this comparison allows us to derive the absolute magnitude ( M_ B_ rest ) and rouhgly estimate reddening ( A_V ). Population synthesis modeling of the continuum SED for different reddening laws ( and ) demostrates that the observational data of the host galaxy of GRB 970508 are best fitted by the spectral properties of a model SED with extinction of A_V 2 .

sources.summary: The paper models GRB 970508 light curves with a constant host component, reports late-time BVRI magnitudes for the pure host, and fits its SED as an Scd starburst H II galaxy.

quotes.quote: Four independent power-laws (the smooth thin lines) determined by fitting the observed BVR_cI_c light curves with a two-component model, the sum of the OT GRB 970508, fading according to a power-law (but after the brightness maximum in Fig. BVRIlight_curve a ), and a constant brightness host galaxy ( F = F_o t^ +F_c with different ) are shown (for details see Sokolov et al., 1999).
IV ) after approximately 200 days any power-law decay ceased and the OT contribution to the host galaxy flux was already less than the observational errors.

Late-time observations of the GRB 970508 optical counterpart in July-August 1998 show that the optical counterpart did not change during the last half-year, and we can conclude that we observe a pure GRB host galaxy with \ B = 25.77 0.19, \ V = 25.25 0.22 , \ R_c = 24.99 0.17 , \ I_c = 24.07 0.25 , already without the GRB optical remnant contribution. Comparison of the BVRI spectral energy distribution of the GRB host with local starburst galaxies leads to the best fits for Scd starburst H II galaxies (Sokolov et al., 1999; 2001).

gmw_00002457.name: GN26
gmw_00002457.overview: GN26 is a compact submillimeter galaxy at a redshift of approximately 1.2 [arXiv:1108.0838, arXiv:1009.2495] that exhibits a high star-formation rate [arXiv:astro-ph/0612520] and resides within a protocluster core [arXiv:2302.10323]. The system features extended radio emission and a VLBA-detected active galactic nucleus core, which may indicate an ongoing merger [arXiv:2402.01569].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GN26 as a secure 70 micrometer SMG detection, deblends its 160 micrometer flux, and finds its infrared colors and high star-formation rate indicate active star formation.

quotes.quote: This procedure uncovered two secure identifications of SMGs at 70 m, GN26 and GN13 (following the naming convention of Pope et al. 2005). These sources have 70 m SNRs of 12 and 8, respectively, and hence 70 m positional uncertainties of about 1 3 and 1 5 (including the Spitzer 1 \ pointing uncertainty).

At 160 m the only SMG detected is GN26. The beamsize at 160 m is 40 , so 70 m and/or SCUBA sources which are close together may be blended into one 160 m source. Examination of the 70 m MIPS image suggests that the 160 m flux density of GN26 has some contribution from another 70 m source (at z = 0.46 ) not associated with GN26. We therefore deblended GN26 with a double Gaussian fit, fixing positions to the IRAC counterparts of the two 70 m sources which contribute to the 160 m flux density. We find that GN26 has S_ 160 = 110 27 mJy, which is 60 of the flux density of the 160 m complex.
GN13 and GN26 have star-formation rates of 30 10 and 800 270 M _ yr ^ 1 , respectively.

sources.summary: GN26 is treated as a z=1.21 submillimetre source with PACS, CO, SED, and IRS information; its red color is linked to moderate or strong silicate absorption.

quotes.quote: One of the sources that meets the selection criteria is the well studied sub-millimetre source at z = 1.21, GN26 for which Pope et al. (2008) have published an IRS spectrum. The detection of this source in the PACS bands has also been discussed by Dannerbauer et al. (2010) and Magnelli et al. (2010) while Frayer et al. (2008) has reported the detection of CO(2 1) emission. In Fig. 11, we show the full SED of the source, overlaid with the best CE01 template for the far-IR part and the observed IRS spectrum for the mid-IR part of the SED. The IRS spectrum of GN26 is also presented separately in an inset panel.

This source has 70, and according to Fig. 9, it should have a silicate optical depth of 1. Although the S/N of the IRS spectrum does not allow for a robust measurement of _ 9.7 , its IRS spectrum is very similar to that of the composite spectrum of SMGs presented in Pope et al. (2008), and for which they report _ 9.7 1 in excellent agreement with what we expected based on this source's colour. We should also note that GN26 was one of the 13 sources used for the construction of the composite spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper highlights J123634.5+621241 as a debated GOODS-North source with optical-tracing extended radio emission, a possible merger, one VLBA-detected AGN core, and classifications spanning star-forming galaxy, X-ray AGN, and starburst.

quotes.quote: The source J123634.5+621241, is particularly interesting, in that there is extended emission tracing the optical image, with potentially two radio peaks in the data (see Figure  figure:faintsources ). This may indicate a merging system. However, only one of these peaks is detected in the VLBA image. This peak is clearly a radio AGN core.

The classification of this source is one of the most debated issue in the GOODS-North field. While Kirkpatrick2012 and Muxlow2005 classify the source as a star-forming galaxy, Xue_2010 and Xue2016 classify the source as an X-ray AGN. Muxlow2020 classify the source as an extended merging starburst system undergoing intense star formation.

sources.summary: The paper describes HDF 169 as a compact SMG with double-peaked CO emission, rotation-curve-like kinematics, high mass surface density, and an ambiguous disk-or-late-merger interpretation.

quotes.quote: HDF 169 (SMM J123634+6212, z = 1.22) -- The integrated spectrum of this compact galaxy is double-peaked, and its P-V diagram resembles a rotation curve, both indicative of a compact rotating system. It has a mass surface density of 10^ 3.3 M _ pc ^ 2 . argued that the very high volume and surface densities characterising compact systems such as this could only plausibly be achieved during the final stages of a major merger. However we feel that this argument alone here is insufficient to judge with any degree of certainty whether this system is a quiescent disk galaxy or a late-stage merger.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SMM J123634 as a gas-rich SMG with a newly detected protocluster environment, multiple overdensity peaks, and filamentary or clumpy large-scale structure.

quotes.quote: We confirm all three previously discovered protoclusters, and we detect five new ones around the SMGs SMM J123634 ( z=1.225 ), ID.19 ( z=2.047 ), SMM J123607 ( z=2.487 ), SMM J123606 ( z=2.505 ), and GN10 ( z=5.303 ). A wavelet-based analysis of the protocluster fields shows that the SMGs are located in protocluster cores with a complex morphology (compact, filamentary, or clumpy) and an average size of (0.4-1) Mpc.

However, in two cases, namely the SMGs SMM J123634 and SMM J123707, there are multiple overdensities that are detected when using the L18 and B19 catalogs. Altogether, for a given photometric redshift catalog (B19 or L18), the multiple overdensities reported for SMM J123634 and SMM J123707, separately, have similar properties in terms of significance, size, projected coordinates, and thus miscentering _ ov (see the density maps in Appendix app:ppmplots ). In other cases (e.g., HDF850.1, SMM J123634, SMM J123606, SMM J123618, SMM J123707, SMM J123711, and SMM J123712) our detected overdensities have evidence for large-scale filamentary or clumpy structures, as typically found in still-assembling protoclusters such as SSA22 at z=3.09 Steidel98,Umehata2019 , the Spiderweb at z=2.16 Jin21 , and Hyperion at z=2.45 Cucciati2018 .

gmw_00002458.name: GN13
gmw_00002458.overview: GN13 is a low-redshift submillimeter galaxy that is faint at 850 micrometers [arXiv:astro-ph/0612520]. Its infrared colors indicate that it is an actively star-forming but cooler galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0612520].

sources.summary: GN13 is treated as a secure low-redshift 70 micrometer SMG detection that is faint at 850 micrometers; its infrared colors indicate a cooler galaxy.

quotes.quote: This procedure uncovered two secure identifications of SMGs at 70 m, GN26 and GN13 (following the naming convention of Pope et al. 2005). These sources have 70 m SNRs of 12 and 8, respectively, and hence 70 m positional uncertainties of about 1 3 and 1 5 (including the Spitzer 1 \ pointing uncertainty).

As expected, both sources (GN26 and GN13) are in the low redshift tail of the submm redshift distribution. Furthermore, we notice that these two sources are among the faintest at 850 m in the submm sample (both have S_ 850 < 2.5 mJy) and therefore they are not typical of the full submm sample presented in Pope et al. (2006) or indeed other samples of SMGs.

For GN13, Figure 2 shows that the S_ 70 /S_ 24 ratio is that of an actively star-forming galaxy, with 1 -1.5, while the S_ 70 /S_ 850 ratio indicates it is a cooler galaxy. This may be due to broad silicate absorption falling into the 24 m band; the S_ 70 /S_ 24 ratio can be strongly affected by PAH features and silicate absorption, which are not fully accounted for in the models.

gmw_00002459.name: GN12
gmw_00002459.overview: GN12 is a submillimeter galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0501679, arXiv:astro-ph/0612520] associated with a steep-spectrum radio source and a faint Chandra X-ray counterpart [arXiv:astro-ph/0501679]. The radio source contains a compact component with a one-sided extension [arXiv:astro-ph/0501679].

sources.summary: J123646+621448 is an unclassified steep-spectrum source with a compact one-sided radio extension, a faint galaxy counterpart, submillimeter identification, and faint Chandra emission.

quotes.quote: J123646+621448 (Unclassified): has a steep radio spectrum, ( >0.84 ) and contains a compact component with a one-sided 0.5 arcsec extension at a position angle of 160 degrees. It is not identified on the HFF frame to approximately I = 25 mag. However, on the lower resolution CFHT frame it overlies a faint but distinct galaxy ( I = 25.5 mag). Chapman et al. (2004b) identify this system as a sub-mm source (S _ 850 =10.3 2.2 mJy). There is a faint Chandra counterpart.

sources.summary: The paper reports that GN12 has a nearby 70 micrometer source 4.7 arcsec away, but the counterpart does not match the SCUBA identification and is likely random.

quotes.quote: Two more SMGs (GN12 and GN32) have a nearby 70 m source at distances of 4.7 and 9.5 , respectively. The 70 m sources near GN12 and GN32 have IRAC and 24 m counterparts which do not match the identifications for the SCUBA source (Pope et al. 2006). Although it is likely that some fraction of the 70 m flux density is associated with the submm galaxy, it is difficult to determine this fraction because of the other 24 m sources in the vicinity. Using Equation (1), the random probability that 2/30 SCUBA sources have a 70 m source within a distance of 10 \ can be determined to be 28 . This is consistent with the 70 m sources near GN12 and GN32 being random matches.

gmw_00002460.name: GN32
gmw_00002460.overview: GN32 is a submillimeter galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0612520]. It is located 9.5 arcseconds away from a nearby 70-micrometer source, though this source is likely a random match that does not correspond to the submillimeter galaxy's identification [arXiv:astro-ph/0612520].

sources.summary: The paper reports that GN32 has a nearby 70 micrometer source 9.5 arcsec away, but the counterpart does not match the SCUBA identification and is likely random.

quotes.quote: Two more SMGs (GN12 and GN32) have a nearby 70 m source at distances of 4.7 and 9.5 , respectively. The 70 m sources near GN12 and GN32 have IRAC and 24 m counterparts which do not match the identifications for the SCUBA source (Pope et al. 2006). Although it is likely that some fraction of the 70 m flux density is associated with the submm galaxy, it is difficult to determine this fraction because of the other 24 m sources in the vicinity. Using Equation (1), the random probability that 2/30 SCUBA sources have a 70 m source within a distance of 10 \ can be determined to be 28 . This is consistent with the 70 m sources near GN12 and GN32 being random matches.

gmw_00002461.name: SDSS J011024.51+141238.7
gmw_00002461.overview: SDSS J011024.51+141238.7 is an elliptical host galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.06079 [arXiv:1904.01425]. It is dominated by an old stellar population with a high stellar mass and a low star formation rate [arXiv:1904.01425].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J011024.51+141238.7 as the likely elliptical host, with matching redshift, an old stellar population, high stellar mass, and low but nonzero star formation.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:host , we present the unusual location of the event: in the outskirts of the E galaxy SDSS J011024.51+141238.7. This galaxy is observed at a redshift z=0.06079 , consistent with the redshift derived from the H emission line in the spectra of \ (see Sect. sec:spectroscopy ).

Host galaxy Identifying the host of \ with certainty is crucial for putting this event in context. Our initial association of \ with the galaxy SDSS J011024.51+141238.7 is primarily due to \ having a redshift consistent with that of the nearby galaxy. We compared the host spectrum, acquired from the SDSS Science Archive Server (SAS), to templates of various galaxy types Kinney1996 . It is most consistent with being an E galaxy.

It is best described by a galaxy, dominated by an old stellar population, with a large stellar mass content of (M/M_ )=10.88^ +0.56 _ 0.07 and a low SF rate (SFR) of SFR = 0.20^ +0.49 _ 0.07 \ M_ \ yr^ 1 . The age of the stellar population and the large mass corroborate the conclusion from the SDSS spectrum that this is indeed an E galaxy.

gmw_00002462.name: NGC 3175
gmw_00002462.overview: NGC 3175 is a peculiar edge-on spiral galaxy that hosts an intense nuclear source [arXiv:2403.05709, arXiv:astro-ph/0103086]. While active star formation and gas are confined to its inner disk, the galaxy's outer disk is quiescent and lacks detectable neutral hydrogen [arXiv:astro-ph/0103086].

sources.summary: The paper reassesses NGC 3175, arguing a previous preliminary halo detection may have confused nuclear and disk emission; after removing the nucleus, a single-component fit indicates no halo.

quotes.quote: NGC 3175: This galaxy was previously studied by , who found a preliminary detection of a radio halo. However, they performed their analysis without removing the intense nuclear source present in this galaxy. When fitting the z profile, the nuclear emission could be misinterpreted as the disk emission, and in turn, the disk emission was interpreted as the presence of a halo. In our work, the nuclear source of this galaxy had to be removed before analysis.

The emission appears broad on the radio contour maps, but this may be due to the low inclination. A group of blobs is present on one side of the disk; however, they were excluded from the region used to build the z profile, as it was impossible to determine whether they were part of the galaxy or unrelated sources. Contrary to , we found the best fit using a single-component exponential function, suggesting the absence of a radio halo.

sources.summary: NGC 3175 is described as a peculiar edge-on spiral with H I confined to the inner disk, ongoing central star formation, and a quiescent red outer disk.

quotes.quote: Australia Telescope Compact Array \ observations reveal the existence of 5.8 10^8 \ of \ gas in the central 7 kpc of the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 3175. The detected \ and CO gas can explain why star formation, as traced by other emission processes, is going on in the inner part of its disk. On the other hand, the entire outer disk, beyond 3.5 kpc radius, shows no \ emission, has a very red colour and exhibits neither radio continuum nor H emission. This indicates that the outer part of NGC 3175 is quiescent, i.e. not forming stars at a measurable rate. Its \ deficiency and the small extent of the \ layer, which is confined to the boundaries of the optically visible disk, make NGC 3175 a peculiar spiral galaxy.

gmw_00002463.name: Maffei 2
gmw_00002463.overview: Maffei 2 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1404.2987, arXiv:1312.4433] located in the Local Sheet [arXiv:2403.06187] that suffers from severe Galactic extinction [arXiv:1404.2987, arXiv:1312.4433]. The galaxy hosts a young, shock-dominated starburst [arXiv:1009.1831] with modest star formation [arXiv:1312.4433] and is rich in molecular gas [arXiv:1101.1751, arXiv:1312.4433].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes Maffei 2 as a low-latitude barred spiral with severe extinction, derives a plausible TRGB color, and estimates its distance at 3.52 Mpc.

quotes.quote: Maffei 2 The barred spiral galaxy Maffei 2 is located at low Galactic latitudes ( b = -0.^ 33 ). The Galactic extinction towards Maffei 2 was estimated to be even more severe than Maffei 1 spi73,fin07 . Although its apparent V magnitude, V = 12.41 , is by about 1 mag dimmer than that of Maffei 1, after correcting for extinction, Maffei 2 is potentially as equally dominant as Maffei 1 in the IC342/Maffei group fin07 . The extinction correction for Maffei 2 is particularly ambiguous.

With E(B-V)=1.165 the de-reddened TRGB color of Maffei 2 becomes 1.054^ +0.040 _ 0.036 , which is more consistent with what would be expected from its morphological type. With E(B-V) derived, the F110W distance modulus and distance toward Maffei 2 is 27.73^ +0.19 _ 0.19 and 3.52^ +0.32 _ 0.30 Mpc.

sources.summary: The survey reports Maffei 2 as a first HC3N detection and HC3N-rich galaxy, while cautioning that its nearby extent probably inflates the measured HC3N/HCN ratios.

quotes.quote: New detections This is not only the first text to put together a survey of all extragalactic HC _3 N emission line data, but it also reports the first HC _3 N detections in six galaxies: Circinus , IC 860 , IRAS 17208-0014 , Maffei 2 , NGC 1068 , and NGC 3079 . The galaxies in our survey that do not fulfil the criterion _ s _ mb are IC 342 , M82 , Maffei 2 , and NGC 253 , and we therefore expect the real HC _3 N/HCN and HC _3 N/HNC ratios to be somewhat lower for these galaxies. The galaxies thus seen as HC _3 N-luminous or HC _3 N-rich are NGC 4418 , IC 342 , Circinus , M82 , Maffei 2 , Arp 220 , and IRAS 17208-0014 . This is particularly the case for M82 , IC 342 , and Maffei 2 . The HC _3 N/HCN ratios of M82 , Maffei 2 , IC 342 , and NGC 253 are probably overestimated (see Appendix app:nearby2 ), and the correlation coefficient is = 0.65 when these four objects are excluded.

sources.summary: The paper compares prior and new NH3 measurements of Maffei 2, emphasizing its two velocity components and compact overall NH3 emission structure.

quotes.quote: Maffei 2: measured the NH _3 (1,1), (2,2), (3,3), and (4,4) transitions (did not detect the (4,4) transition) at _b 70^ and v 10 km s ^ 1 . measured the (1,1), (2,2), (3,3), and (4,4) transitions, while measured the (6,6) transition toward this galaxy, all at _b 40^ and v 15 km s ^ 1 . The NH _3 emission in all of these measurements is comprised of two velocity components at 80 and +6 km s ^ 1 .
The overall NH _3 emission is located within a 20^ 5^ structure with position angle 30^ .
Our measurements of the NH _3 (1,1), (2,2), and (4,4) transitions toward Maffei 2 reflect the same two-velocity component structure previously noted.

sources.summary: Maffei 2 is treated as the youngest, shock-dominated starburst in the sample, with low-density halo gas, a denser region, and no clear dense gas core.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, the central regions of NGC 253 and Maffei 2 appear to resemble quite closely the giant molecular cloud complexes within our Galactic central regions where the low velocity shocks and massive star formation dominate the chemistry of the molecular material ( Martin06b ). Through the HNCO/CS ratio it has been shown that, the nuclear region of NGC253, though less dominated by UV radiation than in M 82, is significantly more UV pervaded than in IC 342 or Maffei 2.

First, Maffei 2, the galaxy with the youngest starburst, shows a large low density halo (Region I 10 ^4 cm ^ 3 ) and a more denser Region II with densities of 10 ^5 cm ^ 3 . This galaxy does not show signs of a dense gas core (Region III), probably because its starburst is still starting.

sources.summary: The paper describes Maffei 2 as an obscured SBbc spiral whose central molecular zone lies in a gas-rich bar, contains about 5 x 10^7 solar masses of molecular gas, and has modest star formation.

quotes.quote: Maffei 2: Although highly obscured by the Milky Way in the foreground, near-infrared images show Maffei 2 to be a well organized SBbc: spiral at D = 3.9 Mpc. Due to the large foreground extinction, the optical magnitude of Maffei 2 is highly uncertain, but it probably is the least luminous galaxy in our sample. The CMZ molecular disk is located within a gas-rich bar and contains M _ mol 5 10 ^7  M _ of gas (Meier et al. 2008).
For this pilot project we adopted estimates of the SFRs of 0.1 M _  yr ^ 1 for IC 342 and Maffei 2, 0.5-1 M _  yr ^ 1 for M83, and 3-10 M _  yr ^ 1 for NGC 253.

sources.summary: The paper describes Maffei 2 as a massive Local Volume galaxy with revised distance estimates that affect its inclusion in the Council of Giants ring.

quotes.quote: As Neuzil2020 also points out, the distances to Maffei 1 and Maffei 2 are particularly sensitive since they are in the `Zone of Avoidance' of our galaxy and hence subject to redening. In case of the Maffei 2, we took into account recent works by Tikhonov2018 and Anand2019 , where the authors discovered that this galaxy lies at a distance of 5.7 Mpc, instead of 3.5 Mpc, as previously thought.
In fact, for galaxies _ 10 (L_K) 10 we do recover the orginal Council of Giants, though excluding Maffei 1 and 2 due to their different distances in the LVG catalogue Karachentsev2013 .
Note that both Maffei 1 and 2 do lie in the Local Sheet, which is why HINORA picks them up as inliers in the rings found by it.

gmw_00002464.name: J0921+4509
gmw_00002464.overview: J0921+4509 is a compact, blue galaxy [arXiv:1601.03057, arXiv:1707.01913] known as a Lyman continuum emitter [arXiv:1802.06378]. It exhibits a moderate star-formation rate [arXiv:1601.03057], and observations do not exclude the possible presence of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1601.03057].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0921+4509 as a known LyC emitter and refits it with a clumpy dust geometry, deriving higher H i covering fraction but a consistent LyC escape fraction.

quotes.quote: The other nine galaxies are known LyC emitters J1503+3644, J0925+1409, J1152+3400, J1333+6246, J1442-0209, J0921+4509, Tol1247-232, Tol0440-381, and Mrk 54 from izotov2016a, izotov2016b, borthakur2014, leitherer2016 .

We refit two galaxies: J0921+4509 and J1152+3400, which have an average C_f ( H i ) and low C_f ( H i ), respectively. Adopting a clumpy geometry (b) , we derive =0.236 and 0.239 (versus \ = 0.224 and 0.134 in model (a) ) and C_f ( H i ) =0.976 and 0.912 (versus C_f = 0.754 and 0.506 in model (a) ), respectively. These values and Eq. eq:f_holes predict a LyC escape fraction of =0.024 and 0.088 at = 912 , which is consistent, within 1 , with the values derived using the uniform dust screen model ( =0.017 0.007 and 0.092 0.027 ).

sources.summary: J0921+4509 is described as a blue galaxy with possible 21 percent LyC escape, high stellar mass and star-formation rate, and possible AGN contamination.

quotes.quote: At z 0.24 , Borthakur2014 discovered a similarly blue galaxy, J0921+4509, that could have a LyC escape fraction of (21 5) . J0921+4509 is characterized by a moderately high stellar mass ( 10^ 10.8 M _ ), metallicity (12+log(O/H)=8.67), and a moderate star-formation rate, SFR =55 M _ yr ^ 1 . However, the analyses of hard X-ray and radio emissions of this galaxy did not exclude the possible contribution by an AGN Jia2011,Alexandroff2012 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses J0921+4509 as a compact LyC leaker, describing COS extraction choices, confirming its LyC flux, and comparing its outflow profile.

quotes.quote: In particular, for the compact galaxy J0921+4509 we used a 25-pixel aperture that includes all flux from a point source, while the more extended galaxies from Leitherer16 were extracted with a 43-pixel aperture.

Meanwhile, Haro 11 and J0921 + 4509 are observed with the G130M grating, and we degrade the observations by convolving with a Gaussian that has a velocity width of 75 , approximately the resolution of the G140L observations.
For J0921 +4509 we confirm the flux measurement from Borthakur14 with high confidence (10.2 , calculated from Poisson distribution).

gmw_00002465.name: RG1
gmw_00002465.overview: RG1 is a low-excitation radio galaxy with a projected linear size of approximately 840 kpc [arXiv:1701.06915]. It is classified as a genuine hybrid morphology radio source, displaying opposite spectral index trends in its northern and southern lobes [arXiv:1701.06915].

sources.summary: RG1 is classified as a foreground low-excitation radio galaxy and a genuine HyMoRS, with lobe morphology, polarization, and spectral-index trends supporting the hybrid interpretation.

quotes.quote: The luminosity in infrared (WISE at 12 m, ( L_ / erg s^ 1 ) = 42.7 ), X-ray (Chandra 2-10 kev, (L_ unabs / erg s^ 1 ) = 43.0 ), and radio (178 MHz, ( L_ / erg s^ 1 ) = 40.3 ) points towards a classification as low-excitation radio galaxy LERG, Mingo2014 . The extension of RG1 is approximately 5 , which corresponds to a projected linear size of 840 kpc at z=0.16 . The nucleus of the source has a faint infrared WISE counterpart, an optical DSS counterpart (Fig. fig:opt ) and an X-ray Chandra counterpart (Fig. fig:VLA1500b ) at RA: 23 21 51.1 , Dec: 48 50 20 .

RG1 shows opposite trends in the two lobes. The northern lobe has a steep spectral index ( = -1.2 0.1 ) close to the core which flattens in the outer region ( = -0.88 0.03 ) due to particle reacceleration in the hot-spot and back-flow of aged electrons towards the nucleus. The southern lobe has an opposite trend: the jet is disrupted close to the core and the electrons' spectra steepens from = -0.86 0.06 to = -1.3 0.1 due to synchrotron and inverse Compton looses as the distance from the core increases. Morphology, polarisation and spectral index characteristics all point to the classification of RG1 as a genuine HyMoRS.

gmw_00002466.name: RG2
gmw_00002466.overview: RG2 is a radio galaxy featuring a flat-spectrum core with counterparts in WISE and DSS imaging [arXiv:1701.06915]. Its northern lobe exhibits a Fanaroff-Riley class I morphology, and the source is likely strongly beamed rather than being a hybrid radio galaxy [arXiv:1701.06915].

sources.summary: RG2 has a flat-spectrum core with WISE/DSS counterpart, FR I-like northern structure, probable beaming, and is judged not to be a true HyMoRS.

quotes.quote: The core of RG2 is identifiable by a flat spectral index ( = -0.01 0.06 ) and by a counterpart visible in the WISE and DSS images at RA: 23 21 48.1 , Dec: 48 30 04 (Fig. fig:RG2_opt ). The northern lobe of the source has an FR I morphology and it bends in two locations. Its spectral index is quite uniform along its extension ( 1 ). A compact emission close to the core is the brightest region of the source. It has a rather flat spectral index of = -0.62 0.05 and it is polarised to the level of 16 .

A alternative interpretation could be that this extended emission is not part of the RG2 source but it is a lobe related with the compact source PS3. Although the source is difficult to interpret and probably strongly beamed, the data available to us do not suggest RG2 to be a hybrid radio galaxy. However, a low resolution image (see Fig. fig:radio2 ) without spectral index or polarisation information (as a radio survey image) could have led to the HyMoRS classification of RG2 based on its morphology.

gmw_00002467.name: FS90 110
gmw_00002467.overview: FS90 110 is a compact elliptical galaxy candidate with a small, round morphology [arXiv:0808.2640]. It is the closest projected companion to the dominant galaxy NGC 3258, showing outer isophotes that are elongated toward this bright neighbor [arXiv:0808.2640].

sources.summary: The paper treats FS90 110 as the strongest compact-elliptical candidate, noting its small round morphology, proximity to NGC 3258, high central surface brightness, flat colors, and two-component profile.

quotes.quote: FS90 110 was catalogued by FS90 as a `possible' (i.e. status 3) Antlia member with an E(M32?) morphology. It resembles a cE galaxy (see Fig. Imagenes ), as it is round and small in projected size ( r_ _ T_1 2.4 kpc). It is the closest galaxy in projection to either one of the dominant Antlia galaxies and, interestingly, its R image shows an elongation of the outer isophotes towards its bright companion (see Fig. Imagenes ).

However, it seems to be the most firm candidate for a cE galaxy. If it was an Antlia member, its projected separation from NGC 3258 would be of the same order as those of M32 from M31, and NGC 5486A from NGC 5486. Moreover, it would be much closer to the dominant galaxy than NGC 4486B to NGC 4486, and its effective radius would be smaller than that of NCG 5846A. It presents a high central surface brightness in the brightness profile obtained from the ACS image, and its (C-T_1) and (V-I) colour profiles are quite flat, in agreement with what is found for M32. Its brightness profile shows two components in the equivalent radius range 2 -- 11.8 arcsec, that seems to be described by N > 1 (i.e. n < 1) S ersic indices.

gmw_00002468.name: FS90 165
gmw_00002468.overview: FS90 165 is an early-type lenticular galaxy located in the Antlia cluster [arXiv:0808.2640]. It features a single-component brightness profile, disky isophotes, and a red inner disc [arXiv:0808.2640].

sources.summary: The authors reject FS90 165 as a compact elliptical, describing it instead as a spectroscopically confirmed Antlia S0/early-type galaxy with low surface brightness scaling and a single-component profile.

quotes.quote: FS90 165 From the analysis performed in the previous section, we conclude that FS90 165, an spectroscopically confirmed Antlia member, cannot be considered as a cE galaxy. It follows fundamental relations of low surface brightness early-type Antlia galaxies (see also Paper I), is larger than any confirmed cE object, and presents no compact morphology. It is also located far away in projection from any bright galaxy, and from the central cluster region, a characteristic shared by all confirmed cE galaxies. Its T_1 brightness profile is well fitted by a single S ersic law along its whole radius range, i.e. it does not show two components. However, the S0 morphological classification assigned to this galaxy by FS90 is in agreement with the B4 coefficient values that point to disky isophotes, and with the red inner disc found in its (C-T_1) colour map and confirmed through unsharp masking.

gmw_00002469.name: FS90 192
gmw_00002469.overview: FS90 192 is a compact elliptical galaxy [arXiv:0808.2640, arXiv:1109.6004] that is a confirmed member of the Antlia cluster [arXiv:1109.6004]. It is located near the galaxy NGC 3268 [arXiv:0808.2640] and exhibits photometric properties consistent with a tidal stripping scenario [arXiv:1109.6004].

sources.summary: FS90 192 is presented as an uncertain compact candidate near NGC 3268, with no radial velocity, compact appearance if in Antlia, red/high-surface-brightness offsets, and possible background status.

quotes.quote: FS90 192 There is no radial velocity available for FS90 192. It displays no perceptible colour gradients and, if it was an Antlia member, it would be as compact as other cE galaxies. Furthermore, its projected distance to NGC 3268 would be smaller than that between NGC 4486B and NGC 4486.

Despite all the interesting features mentioned above, we cannot confirm nor rule out that this object is in the background. It does not follow the early-type Antlia members fundamental relations, as it appears shifted towards higher surface brightnesses, fainter magnitudes, or redder colours. M32 would hold a comparable position on the colour-magnitude diagram if it were placed at the Antlia distance. However, FS90 192 does not follow the relation between mean effective surface brightness and luminosity defined by the confirmed cEs. Under the hypothesis that it is a background object, FS90 192 would become an isolated elliptical galaxy of moderate luminosity (highly unlikely), as we have not found any concentration of galaxies in its neighbourhood.

sources.summary: The paper confirms FS90 192 as a compact elliptical Antlia member, red and compact for its luminosity, and offset from early-type dwarf scaling relations in a way consistent with stripping.

quotes.quote: The exceptions are two cE galaxies (FS90 110 and FS90 192) that are new members of Antlia and were originally considered just as ``possible" members, two galaxies originally classified as ``possible" and ``likely" dE members, which turned out to be an Antlia member (FS90 103) and a background systems (FS90 205), and a confirmed member but with a different (irregular) morphology (FS90 221).

Both cE galaxies clearly depart from the locus of constant effective radius but, at first glance, in a different manner. FS90 110 seems to extend the break defined by the brightest galaxies towards fainter magnitudes, but FS90 192 is located with the bulk of early-type dwarf galaxies defining what could be their low limiting r_ eff . If we calculate for their luminosities the corresponding _ eff on the locus of r_ eff = 1 kpc, we obtain that they differ from the measured values in _ eff =2.9 mag arcsec ^ 2 for FS90 110, and _ eff =2.7 mag arcsec ^ 2 for FS90 192. Therefore both cE galaxies depart from the trend defined by early-type dwarf galaxies in a similar manner, defining what could be a photometric criterion to identify candidate cE galaxies. Alternatively, the location of the cEs with respect to the surface brightness-luminosity relation can be judged as consistent with a stripping scenario.

gmw_00002470.name: FS90 208
gmw_00002470.overview: FS90 208 is a confirmed early-type lenticular galaxy located in the Antlia cluster [arXiv:0808.2640]. It features a two-component brightness profile along with strong variations in both ellipticity and position angle [arXiv:0808.2640].

sources.summary: The paper concludes FS90 208 is not compact elliptical, but a confirmed Antlia S0 with two profile components, strong ellipticity and position-angle variations, and possible progenitor relevance.

quotes.quote: FS90 208 Certainly, FS90 208 is not a cE galaxy either. It is a confirmed Antlia member that follows the same fundamental relations as FS90 165 and the rest of low surface brightness early-type galaxies, and it is not compact. Besides, it is not close in projection to any bright galaxy and is far from the centre of the cluster. However, it displays a two component brightness profile and presents important variations in ellipticity and position angle that resemble those shown by M32 choi02 , albeit stronger. Curiously, these variations arise at the same galactocentric radii than those displayed by FS90 110, and behave in a similar way.

All these pieces of evidence lead us to speculate about the possibility that, an object similar to FS90 208 might be the progenitor of a cE galaxy. Thus, it is tempting to look for possible links between these two kind of objects. We may think that a system with similar characteristics as FS90 208 may lose its outermost regions due to the interaction with a bright companion, then becoming compact.

gmw_00002471.name: J 1227 + 0136
aliases_external: J 1227+0136 / R1 in HI 1225+01 NE clump
gmw_00002471.overview: J 1227 + 0136 is a faint, low-surface-brightness blue irregular galaxy [arXiv:1210.0073]. It exhibits a highly irregular morphology with a low oxygen abundance and features a bright central stellar knot [arXiv:1210.0073].

sources.summary: J 1227 + 0136 is described as a faint blue irregular, low-surface-brightness galaxy in the NE clump, with low metallicity, irregular morphology, and a young R1 stellar knot.

quotes.quote: salzer91 presented a detailed optical imaging and spectroscopic study and confirmed that the OC is a blue irregular galaxy with an H 2 region like spectrum. The measured oxygen abundance (12 + log (O/H) = 7.66 0.03 ) is less than a tenth of the solar value. We call it J 1227 + 0136 (simply from its J2000.0 coordinate) throughout this paper. The galaxy is very faint, with the central B band surface brightness _B 24 mag arcsec ^ 2 , and is indeed reported as one of the 700 local low-surface-brightness (LSB) galaxies found in 800 deg ^2 around the celestial equator impey96 .

The galaxy J 1227 + 0136 is clearly detected at the H 1 peak position of the NE clump. Its close-up view is given in Figure NEcomp . As is previously known, the galaxy has a highly irregular morphology with the brightest knot, which we call ``R1'' hereafter, at close to the galactic center. The J2000.0 coordinate of R1 is R.A. 12 ^ h 27 ^ m 46 ^ s .1, decl. +01 ^ 36 ^ m 01 ^ s .

gmw_00002472.name: SW clump
gmw_00002472.overview: The SW clump is considered a leading dark-galaxy candidate [arXiv:1210.0073]. Deep optical imaging has detected no diffuse optical emission associated with the clump, suggesting that star formation may be prevented or delayed [arXiv:1210.0073].

sources.summary: The SW clump is treated as a leading dark-galaxy candidate: deep imaging finds no optical emission, while H I kinematics resemble an edge-on rotating disk with uncertain star formation.

quotes.quote: Nonetheless, the SW clump is usually referred to as the current best example of a dark galaxy candidate, therefore its follow-up studies are of great importance as a benchmark for future surveys. Investigations of the companion galaxy J 1227 + 0136 as well as any optical emission throughout the gas structure are also crucial to resolve the nature of H 1 1225 + 01 as a whole. In this paper, we present an updated analysis of the two clumps by exploiting an original deep optical image and a collection of archival data from ultraviolet (UV) to far-infrared (IR) spectral region.

SW clump No diffuse emission associated with the SW clump or any H 1 components other than the NE clump is found in the MOA R image (Figure image ). The 3 limiting surface brightness is 25.2 mag arcsec ^ 2 , which is slightly deeper than that achieved in the previous attempt (only for the SW region) by turner97 . We smoothed the image on a 10 \ 10 \ box by taking the mode value in each of the boxes, which improved the limiting depth to 28.3 mag arcsec ^ 2 , but did not find any diffuse emission as well in the relevant fields. Therefore the OC to the SW clump, if it is present and extended over > 10 \ (comparable to R1), should have the surface brightness of more than one hundred times fainter than the brightest part of the companion galaxy J 1227 + 0136. At the same time, we cannot reject the possibility that star formation in the SW clump is prevented/delayed for some reasons and the clump is truly invisible in optical light.

gmw_00002473.name: HCG88A
gmw_00002473.overview: HCG88A is an active galaxy that exhibits clumpy hydrogen-alpha emission throughout its disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075]. The galaxy features a faint northwest plume resembling a tidal tail, which suggests a past interaction occurred approximately 100 million years after closest approach [arXiv:1509.06303].

sources.summary: The paper finds HCG88A has a faint northwest plume resembling a tidal tail and argues the plume indicates a past interaction about 100 Myr after closest approach.

quotes.quote: fig:HCG88 ) shows that HCG88A exhibits a faint plume visible to the NW of the galaxy. A similar faint extension is not visible on the opposite side of the galaxy, thus the NW feature cannot be explained as a disk warp but it looks similar to tidal tails sometimes seen in interacting galaxies. The length of this faint extension, as the figure demonstrates, is at least 27 kpc and its average surface brightness is _R 26 mag/square arcsec. The plume is diffuse and is seen on the deep L-band image combination but not in the H image (see below) nor in individual images exposed for 200 sec with the CFHT or in individual SDSS images available on NED http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ .

While the presence of a plume in HCG88A indicates that some kind of interaction did take place, it would be interesting to be able to identify which other object was involved in this interaction and when did this process happen.

Given that HCG88A shows no counter-tail, and neither can the putative interacting companion be easily distinguished in the tail, a possible match could be for a time step t 0.1 (i.e., about 100 Myr following the closest approach) when the tail is relatively bright, with the interactor hiding in the plume.

sources.summary: The paper describes HCG 88a as an active galaxy with clumpy disk H emission, a fairly regular but slightly twisted velocity field, and a symmetric rotation curve.

quotes.quote: shi00 detected only [ N II ] line emission and did not detect H line emission in the nucleus of HCG 88a. They classified this galaxy as an AGN. We detect H emission throughout the disk of HCG 88a, in a clumpy distribution. The velocity field of HCG 88a is fairly regular but slightly twisted around the kinematic major axis. RHF1991 and NSOMT2000 published RCs for HCG 88a, obtained from long-slit spectroscopy, along the major axis of the galaxy.

gmw_00002474.name: HCG88B
gmw_00002474.overview: HCG88B is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075] that exhibits a twisted velocity field [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075] and a completely asymmetric rotation curve [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075]. Its outer regions are misaligned with its brighter body [arXiv:1509.06303], displaying shell-like structures and extensions likely formed from a past accretion event [arXiv:1509.06303].

sources.summary: HCG 88b is described as a barred spiral with clumpy asymmetric emission, a twisted velocity field, and a completely asymmetric rotation curve.

quotes.quote: The SBb galaxy HCG 88b presents a very clumpy monochromatic map, with a strong asymmetric emission in an arm-like feature in the northeastern side and a less strong emission region in the southern region. In contrast, on the northwestern side of the galaxy, almost no emission can be seen. Due to the presence of a bar in this galaxy (SBb), the continuum image shows an asymmetric and irregular shape, rotating from 34 ^ o within 10 arcsec to 160 ^ o at a radius of 30 arcsec. At large radius (50 arsec) the galaxy is rather round. The velocity field shows a large scale twist and a clear signature of a bar in the inner regions.
The RC derived in this study is completely asymmetric, with a total extension of 40 arcsec (15.6 kpc).

sources.summary: The paper describes HCG88B as disturbed, with outer regions misaligned from the bright body, abrupt low-surface-brightness extensions, and shell-like structures from likely accretion.

quotes.quote: The image of HCG88B shows that its outer regions are extended in a direction perpendicular to the elongation of its inner and brighter regions.

The contrast-stretched image shown in the left panel shows also similar extensions with abrupt terminations on opposite sides of the galaxy, with the NW boundary closer to the center of the galaxy than the SW boundary.

In particular, HCG88B which shows shells and weird isophotes probably accreted the object it interacted with at least 1 Gyr ago (a few crossing times of this galaxy) and during this period the oscillations of the accreted object formed the shells and isophote extensions.

gmw_00002475.name: HCG88C
gmw_00002475.overview: HCG88C is a near face-on spiral galaxy featuring a symmetric gas distribution and a faint third arm [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075, arXiv:1509.06303]. The galaxy hosts numerous HII regions and possesses an extended neutral hydrogen distribution that is roughly twice its optical size [arXiv:1509.06303].

sources.summary: HCG 88c is presented as a spiral with symmetric gas, arm-like continuum structure, a regular mildly twisted velocity field, and a well-matched rotation curve.

quotes.quote: HCG 88c shows a rather symmetric gas distribution of what could be a three-arm system. The brightest H knot coincides with the continuum center. The continuum image shows an arm-like structure on the southeastern side of the galaxy and an asymmetric feature (the eastern isophotes are stretched as compared to the western isophotes). The velocity field is regular even if a twist can be seen (less strong than for HCG 88b). The RC of HCG 88c, slightly rising from 4 arcsec (1.6 kpc) to 25 arcsec (9.8 kpc), displays a good match between the receding and approaching sides (Fig. 4b).

sources.summary: The paper describes HCG88C as a near face-on spiral with a faint third arm, many HII regions, and HI extending roughly twice its optical size.

quotes.quote: HCG88C appears to be an almost face-on two-armed spiral on low-contrast images, but higher dynamic range images show a third, fainter, arm emerging just East of the galaxy center and curling northward.
HCG88B and C show many HII regions.

HCG88B and C show connected HI contours in their outer regions. While HCG88B shows approximately the same HI distribution as that of the optical light, C has a more extended HI distribution, about twice that of the optical image.

gmw_00002476.name: HCG88D
gmw_00002476.overview: HCG88D is an edge-on Sc spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075] that may contain a central bar [arXiv:1509.06303]. It features an asymmetric dust lane [arXiv:astro-ph/0301075], a single eastern HII region [arXiv:1509.06303], and a compressed neutral hydrogen tail pointing away from its group [arXiv:1509.06303].

sources.summary: HCG 88d is described as an edge-on Sc galaxy with a dust-lane asymmetry, clumpy H emission, regular velocity field, and a symmetric rising rotation curve.

quotes.quote: The edge-on Sc galaxy HCG 88d shows an asymmetric continuum image with respect to the line of nodes, due to a dust lane. The monochromatic image shows a clumpy structure exhibiting five rather strong H knots. The velocity field is regular. The RC derived in this study is symmetric and rising till 60 arcsec (23.4 kpc) for a rotational velocity of 260 km s^ 1 (Fig. 5b). The solid-body shape is probably a result of absorption effects due to the high-inclination of the galaxy (85 ^ o ).

sources.summary: The paper describes HCG88D as an edge-on disk with symmetric bumps, possible bar-end star formation, one eastern HII region, and a compressed HI tail.

quotes.quote: The edge-on disk (HCG88D) shows two symmetric ``bumps'' in the L-band intensity image. These could be signs of enhanced star formation at the end of a bar, implying that this object could be a barred spiral.
Component D shows a single HII region at its eastern end, closest to the other group galaxies.
Component D shows an HI tail pointing away from the group and its HI contours in the direction of the rest of the group appear more compressed than in the opposite direction.

gmw_00002477.name: Mrk 704
gmw_00002477.overview: Mrk 704 is a polar-scattered Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1103.6268, arXiv:2105.02078] whose host galaxy features a bulge, a bar, and a spiral disk [arXiv:1710.02194]. Its active nucleus exhibits warm absorbers [arXiv:1103.6268, arXiv:2403.13194], a relatively compact corona [arXiv:2404.04493], and a narrow H I emission profile [arXiv:1906.07062].

sources.summary: The paper models Mrk 704 as a polar-scattered Seyfert 1 with partial covering absorption, two warm absorber phases, and weak He-like triplet emission from photoionised warm-emitting clouds.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed study of the ionised environment of the Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 704 using medium and high resolution X-ray spectra obtained with a long observation. The 0.3-10 continuum, well described by a power-law ( 1.86 ) and two blackbodies ( kT 0.085 and 0.22 ), is found to be affected by a neutral partial covering absorption ( N_H 10^ 23 , covering fraction 0.22 ) and two warm absorber components. We identify a low ionisation, 20 , and high outflow velocity, v 1350 , phase producing the O 6 and Fe M-shell unresolved-transition array (UTA). An additional high ionisation warm absorbing phase with 500 and low outflow velocity, v 540 , gives rise to absorption features due to O 7 , O 8 , N 6 , N 7 and C 6 .

We also detected weak emission lines of He-like triplets from O 7 and N 6 ions, thus making Mrk 704 a Seyfert 1 galaxy with both warm absorption and emission. The emission lines are well described by two warm emitting, photoionised media with different densities but comparable , suggesting discrete clouds of warm emission.

sources.summary: The paper reports Mrk 704 has a narrow Gaussian H I profile; a low-S/N Arecibo spectrum likely inflated its previous line width.

quotes.quote: Mrk 704: Mrk 704 has a narrow Gaussian emission profile shape (see Fig. \ batmen2 ). The BusyFit central trough, offset, and polynomial parameters ( , x_ p , and n ) were thus automatically fixed at 0, and we also held the left flank slope ( b_ 1 ) fixed at 0.15 to allow the profile to converge. The spectrum reported by from observations with the Arecibo telescope contains a very low S/N emission line blended with a noise spike on the blueshifted side. The larger W _ 20 value they report of 250 km s ^ 1 compared to ours of 57.8 ^ +12.8 _ 12.9 km s ^ 1 is possibly due to the nearby noise spike's inclusion in the profile measurement. This would also explain the slight offset in their V _ R value of 9510 km s ^ 1 compared to our measurement of 9525.87 ^ +1.60 _ 2.55 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper includes Mrk 704 among AGN with less-confirmed reverberation signatures, finds significant correlations, and infers a relatively compact corona from its lag trends and mass.

quotes.quote: We focus on six AGN observed by XMM-Newton , including the sources well-known to exhibit clear X-ray reverberation lags (IRAS 13224--3809 and 1H 0707--495) and those in which reverberation signatures are not well confirmed (MCG--6-30-15, IZW1, Mrk 704 and Mrk 1040). We employ the sliding-window algorithm and estimate the Granger (intrinsic) Fe-L lags along the light curve as the window moves through.
The correlations are significant in 1H 0707--495, IRAS 13224--3809, 1Zw1, MCG--6-30-15, and Mrk 704.

The amplitudes as well as the trends of the lags of Mrk 704 and Mrk 1040 are comparable to what seen in IRAS 13224--3809. Keeping in mind that the mass of Mrk 704 and Mrk 1040 is 1.3 10^ 8 M_ Ponti2012 and 4.0 10^ 7 M_ Gonzalez2012 , respectively, we can infer their relatively more compact corona compared to IRAS 13224--3809.

sources.summary: The paper says Mrk 704 has previously reported warm absorbers and a partially covering neutral absorber, while this analysis adds another warm absorber component.

quotes.quote: Mrk 704- have found two discrete WA components with 20 and 500 . Both these ionisation states and the ouflow velocities are comparable with our detections. In our analysis we detect an additional component of WA with 3.13 . had detected a partially covering neutral absorber.

sources.summary: The paper fits the saturated AGN host with bulge, bar, and spiral disk components using the hybrid method for bar and disk.

quotes.quote: Mrk 704 (Fig. 11.18) --- \ \ The WFPC2 mosaic image is saturated in the center. The AGN host is fit with components: a bulge, a bar, and a disk with spiral arms. For the bar and disk, we use the hybrid method. Our result is broadly consistent with Orban de Xivry et al. (2011).

sources.summary: The paper compares Mrk 704 as a polar-scattered Seyfert with neutral and warm absorbers, lower neutral-absorber covering factor, a large torus opening angle, and soft-excess evidence.

quotes.quote: For instance, the polar-scattered Sy1 Mrk 704 shows not only a partial covering neutral absorber with N_ H 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 and Cfact=0.22 but also two layers of warm absorbers that are associated with the broad line region laha11 . Meanwhile, polar-scattered Sy1s show a variety of X-ray spectral features. For example, the neutral absorber in Mrk 704 has a much lower covering factor than those in \ and Mrk 231. This indicates that the torus opening angles in polar-scattered Sy1s may not be uniform.

\ and Mrk 704 are respectively located in the lower and higher ends of the global distribution of torus opening angles. Besides, as demonstrated in this work and piconcelli13 , Mrk 231 and \ show no significant evidence of reprocessing emission from the innermost region, e.g. disc reflection (see Section relxill ). In comparison, other polar-scattered Sy1s show evidence of either broad Fe K emission e.g. Fairall 51, svoboda15 or soft excess emission from the innermost accretion region e.g. NGC 3227 and Mrk 704, laha11,newman21 .

sources.summary: The paper models Mrk 704 with two warm absorbers, consistent with work interpreting the line of sight as passing close to the torus and showing extra continuum complexity.

quotes.quote: Mrk 704 Our best fit for this source includes 2 warm absorbers. This result is consistent with what is reported in L2011 Ma2011 , where the absorption is interpreted as the line of sight passing close to the torus. As an example of the complexity that these sources show, Ma2011 produced a best fit model including a second soft excess element and partial covering by a cold absorber.

gmw_00002478.name: Mrk 1040
gmw_00002478.overview: Mrk 1040 is an active Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1106.6139, arXiv:1804.07311] that interacts with a northern companion galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0612741, arXiv:1804.07311]. Its disk features asymmetric spiral arms [arXiv:astro-ph/0612741], non-circular gas motions [arXiv:2109.01802], and gas-dynamical evidence of a bar [arXiv:2109.01802, arXiv:1710.02194].

sources.summary: The paper reports XMM-Newton analysis of Mrk 1040, finding a complex AGN X-ray spectrum, correlated variability, and soft lags on dominant variability timescales.

quotes.quote: As is the case for many AGN, the time-averaged X-ray spectrum of Mrk 1040 as observed by is complex. There is intrinsic neutral absorption, a soft excess feature, multiple Iron line emissions, plus edges and absorption features, probably due to a warm absorber. The complex Iron line features indicate the possibility of a complex blurred reflection component.

The essence of this spectral analysis is that with a high energy spectral index of 1.7 , moderate intrinsic absorption and soft excess, the spectrum of Mrk 1040 is typical of AGNs of its class. The top panel of Figure lcurve shows the background subtracted X-ray 640 sec binned lightcurves of Mrk 1040 in soft, 0.2 2.0 keV (circles) and hard, 2.0 10.0 keV (triangles) energy bands. There is clear strong correlated variability in the two bands on 10^4 sec timescales.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 1040 as a Seyfert galaxy paired with LEDA 212995, comparing their redshifts and spectra, and finding broader, more complex narrow lines plus possible foreground geometry.

quotes.quote: The red-shift (0.0169 0.00015) of the object derived from these emission lines is very close to the red-shift of Mrk 1040 (z=0.01665). The weak narrow and broad absorption lines were detected in the H wavelength band of LEDA 212995 spectra. These absorptions indicate that the companion might be at least partly obscured by Mrk 1040.

The spectra of Mrk 1040 is more complex and to find the velocity difference, the ^2 minimalization routine was applied also to complex Mrk 1040 H and H lines (see Figs. 4 and 5). We found that systemic velocities of narrow lines are consistent with cosmological red-shift of Mrk 1040. Here we should mentioned that narrow lines of Mrk 1040 are broader and more complex and that uncertainties in velocity centroid position is higher than in the case of LEDA 212995. Moreover, as one can see in Fig. 4, the [ OIII ] 4959,5007 \ lines can be satisfy fitted only if we assume two Gaussian per the narrow line. The velocity difference between narrow lines of these two galaxies is z_ LEDA 212995 z_ Mrk 1040 0.0004 , that is in agreement with results from our previous analysis.

sources.summary: The paper reports a peculiar [ FeII ] line-to-continuum distribution in NGC 931, co-rotating disk gas, and marked species-dependent velocity-dispersion differences across its central kiloparsec.

quotes.quote: The H _2 line-to-continuum ratio is constant or drops significantly towards the nucleus in most galaxies, except NGC 2273, NGC 5273, and NGC 7450. For Br , in contrast, the ratio peaks towards the nucleus in many galaxies, consistent with stronger ionization mechanisms towards the active nucleus. These two results both explain the observed central drop of the H _2 /Br ratio. Of the three emission lines, the [ FeII ] line-to-continuum ratio shows the most distinct radial variations. Its profile is centrally peaked in Mrk 1, Mrk 3, NGC 262, NGC 1320, NGC 2273, NGC 3081, NGC 5273, and NGC 7450. Several sources show two maxima (or a central drop, e.g. NGC 1667, NGC 3185, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, NGC 4388, and NGC 5506). Finally, Mrk 1066, NGC 931, NGC 2110, NGC 2992, and NGC 4051 show peculiar distributions, e.g. the distribution in NGC 2110 shows three separate peaks.

However, the gas velocity curves are similar to those of the stars, which define the galaxy disk. This is the case in Mrk 1, Mrk 1066, NGC 262, NGC 931, NGC 1667, NGC 2110, NGC 2273, NGC 3081, NGC 3185, NGC 4388, NGC 5273, and NGC 5506. In these galaxies, all three emission lines display a velocity curve that follows that of the stars and is reminiscent of rotation, thus strongly implying that the line-emitting gas is located in the disk of the galaxy and rotating with the disk. Other galaxies (Mrk 3, NGC 1320, NGC 2992, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, and NGC 7450) display velocity curves different from the stellar velocity curve in one or more emission lines, which may be a signature of non-rotational motion, either in- or outflows or other non-planar motion of the gas.

The radial dispersion profiles in Mrk 3, Mrk 1066, NGC 2110, NGC 2273, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, NGC 5273, and NGC 5506 have a central peak, which indicates a higher kinetic gas temperature in the nucleus. Either the ISM is more turbulent (possibly caused by a jet- or wind-driven outflow), and/or more intensely heated by the incident radiation field. In either case, a peaked dispersion distribution is a strong indication for the AGN governing the physical state of the gas at the nucleus. The dispersion peak is most often present in [ FeII ] (indicating dust destruction close to the nucleus), and/or in Br (indicating a strong radiation field), while the H _2 line shows the least-pronounced central rise. Examples for flat velocity dispersion profiles are NGC 3081, NGC 3185, and NGC 4388, which show no significant change over up to 1.2 kpc in diameter. The velocity dispersion in all three sources is of order 100 km/s, well within the range observed for star forming regions, which is consistent with star formation being the dominant the gas excitation mechanism. Finally, the dispersion profiles in Mrk 1, NGC 262, NGC 931, NGC 1320, NGC 1667, NGC 2992, and NGC 7450 show marked differences between the three emission lines, with variations of 100 km/s or more at a given radius. This indicates that the ionized atomic and molecular gas is responding differently to the central AGN in these sources, perhaps due to the presences of asymmetric energy input (a jet or shocks) together with a stratified medium, e.g. a thin molecular disk in a thick ionized medium.

sources.summary: The review describes Mrk 1040 as an active galaxy whose FPI studies found a double-peaked rotation curve, sharp velocity jump, non-circular disk motions, and possible bar or vortex structure.

quotes.quote: The first publication based on the results obtained from observations made with CIGALE on the 6-m telescope appears to be the study of the kinematics of the active galaxy Mrk 1040 Afanasiev1990dig..book..354A .

Further development of this method was stimulated by the studies of the Mrk 1040 (NGC 931) galaxy, which is distinguished by its double-peaked rotation curve with an unusually sharp jump in the rotation velocity 10 kpc from the center and noticeable non-circular motions in the disk. These peculiarities were interpreted either as an unusually large (20 kpc) bar, invisible due to dust extinction in the strongly inclined disk Amram1992A&A...263...69A , or as the presence of vortex structures Mrk1040_1993AstL...19..319A . The latter interpretation is associated with the hypothesis of hydrodynamic generation of spiral waves in the gaseous disk Morozov1979AZh....56..498M , an alternative to the classical stellar disk density wave theory. The hydrodynamic mechanism required an abrupt jump of rotation velocity similar to that observed in Mrk 1040, which is also reproduced in laboratory experiments on rotating shallow water Fridman1985PhLA..109..228F .

sources.summary: The paper includes Mrk 1040 among AGN with uncertain reverberation signatures and infers a compact corona from lag behavior comparable to IRAS 13224--3809 and its mass.

quotes.quote: We focus on six AGN observed by XMM-Newton , including the sources well-known to exhibit clear X-ray reverberation lags (IRAS 13224--3809 and 1H 0707--495) and those in which reverberation signatures are not well confirmed (MCG--6-30-15, IZW1, Mrk 704 and Mrk 1040). We employ the sliding-window algorithm and estimate the Granger (intrinsic) Fe-L lags along the light curve as the window moves through.
The corona of 1H 0707--495 and MCG--6-30-15 may be analogous to that of IRAS 13224--3809, while in IZw1, Mrk 704 and Mrk 1040 a more compact corona is expected.

The amplitudes as well as the trends of the lags of Mrk 704 and Mrk 1040 are comparable to what seen in IRAS 13224--3809. Keeping in mind that the mass of Mrk 704 and Mrk 1040 is 1.3 10^ 8 M_ Ponti2012 and 4.0 10^ 7 M_ Gonzalez2012 , respectively, we can infer their relatively more compact corona compared to IRAS 13224--3809.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 1040 as interacting with a northern companion, with asymmetric spiral-arm H II emission, strong nuclear and companion emission, and no extraplanar emission.

quotes.quote: Our broadband image (see Figure f:mrk1040 ) 10 shows the presence of a companion galaxy 18 to the N of the nucleus of Mrk 1040, which may be in the process of merging. This was also previously reported by col96 . The emission from the H ii regions in the disk of Mrk 1040 outlines a complex spiral arm structure, with bright H ii regions seen as far out as 119 (38.4 kpc) to the E of the nucleus, and 86 (27.8 kpc) to the W. This asymmetry could be explained by interactions with other galaxies such as the northern companion. The nucleus of Mrk 1040 and that of the companion galaxy, as well as one region in the spiral arms 32 (10.3 kpc) to the W of the nucleus of Mrk 1040 are strong and emitters (a closeup view is shown in Figure f:mrk1040z ), 11 and there is a possible a ring-like structure of radius 20 (6.5 kpc) around the nucleus in .

sources.summary: The paper fits the partly covered host with a pseudo-bulge and spiral disk, while noting gas-dynamical evidence for a bar not obvious in the image.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1040 (Fig. 11.19) --- \ \ We use a mosaic image of WFPC2 for the fit, although it appears to cover only a part of the host galaxy. The image is saturated at the center. We fit the AGN host with a pseudo-bulge ( n=1.19 ) and a disk with spiral arms ( B/T 0.15 ). Although a bar is not clearly seen in the \ image (see also Malkan et al. 1998 and Crenshaw et al. 2003), there is gas-dynamical evidence for a bar (Amram et al. 1992).

sources.summary: The appendix describes NGC 931 as a barred Sy1 galaxy interacting with a nearby satellite, with no comparable star-formation records at the study scales.

quotes.quote: NGC 931 (Mrk 1040) is a barred galaxy (Sbc) with a Sy1 nucleus. Ward78 found that this galaxy interacts with a satellite galaxy located a 10 kpc from NGC 931. We did not find records of SF in other works at the scales tracer with our observations.

gmw_00002479.name: LEDA 87445
gmw_00002479.overview: LEDA 87445 is a bright galaxy located in the outskirts of the Abell 780 cluster [arXiv:2112.09056]. It is situated within the potential hole of an accreting galaxy group that features a long, bright X-ray tail of stripped gas [arXiv:2112.09056].

sources.summary: The paper links LEDA 87445 to a bright X-ray tail and a robust infalling galaxy group whose center, velocity, potential-hole location, and stripped wake support the accretion picture.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: For comparison, the center and velocity of LEDA 87445 are 09 \ 18 \ 27.44-12 \ 22 \ 24.0 and 17236 46 km \ s ^ 1 , respectively.

quotes.quote: degrandi2016 report the discovery of a structure with a spectacular bright X-ray tail in the outskirts of the Abell 780 cluster (hereafter A780) using deep wide-field observations with XMM-Newton and Suzaku . They suggest that this feature is related to an accreting galaxy group that is clustered around the bright galaxy LEDA 87445, 17^ ( 1.1 Mpc) south of the cluster center, with a wake of stripped gas extending behind the group over a projected scale of 760 kpc. The temperature of the gas along the wake is constant at kT_ X 1.3 keV, which is about a factor of two below the temperature of the surrounding ICM.

The 3DSHVG group contains LEDA 87445 and is populated by the same galaxies as the 3DHVG group, with the exception of two galaxies. This suggests that the detection of a galaxy group related to LEDA 87445 is quite robust.

LEDA 87445 is located in the potential hole of group HTG2, which is populated by five galaxies. HTG1 and HTG2 probably correspond to the cores of the main cluster and the LEDA 87445 group, respectively.

gmw_00002480.name: NGC507
gmw_00002480.overview: NGC 507 is a central massive elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2106.07168] and slow rotator [arXiv:1802.00014] that hosts the low-power radio source B2 0120+33 [arXiv:2201.04591]. This radio source features asymmetric, diffuse lobes representing remnants of a past phase of jet activity [arXiv:1011.0567, arXiv:2201.04591].

sources.summary: The paper describes B2 0120+33, identified with NGC 507, as a dying radio source with faint diffuse lobes, no kpc-scale jets, and a weak core becoming important only at high frequencies.

quotes.quote: The radio source is asymmetric, the west lobe being brighter and smaller that the east one. The radio emission is very weak and diffuse. A similar morphology has been observed in GMRT images at 235 and 610 MHz by Giacintucci et al. 2010 (in preparation). At arcsecond resolution the only compact structure is the faint unresolved core. There is no evidence of kpc-scale jets in any of our images. Hence, the extended lobes of B2 0120+33 have been produced by an earlier phase of activity.

We fitted the integrated spectrum of B2 0120+33 with the CI _ OFF model plus a power law which accounts for the emission of the radio core. The radio core however is very faint and its emission becomes important only at frequencies greater than 10 GHz. At lower frequencies the source spectrum is dominated by the fading radio lobes and it can it be modeled with an injection spectral index of _ inj =0.6 and a break frequency of about 440 MHz. According to the spectral fit the duration of the dying phase should be about 30 the source total age.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 507 as a disturbed group-center galaxy hosting B2 0120+33, with radio lobes, X-ray sloshing features, and remnant AGN plasma displaced by group motions.

quotes.quote: We investigated the membership of the group using the "velocity gap" method depropris2002 and we clearly detect the NGC 507 group as a peak at the redshift of the galaxy NGC 507, populated by 74 galaxies. This result is also confirmed using the `shifting gapper' method fadda1996, owers2009 . The value of the bi-weight location estimator of the mean group velocity is 5030 74 , which corresponds to z_ cos =0.01678 0.00025 .

The galaxy NGC 507 hosts a low-power ( P_ 150 MHz =1 10^ 24 W Hz^ 1 ) Fanaroff-Riley I fanaroff1974 radio galaxy named B2 0120+33 parma1986, giacintucci2011, murgia2011 . The radio source shows two asymmetric lobes with a total extension of 70 kpc (see Fig. fig:rgb ) and a faint, unresolved central component that has been detected at frequencies 610 MHz with power equal to P_ core,1400 MHz =1.3 10^ 21 W Hz^ 1 giacintucci2011, murgia2011 .
