The first, VGS 38, was described in the pilot survey, and presents a very similar linear alignment of the three galaxies, suggesting filamentary structure within the void. The H i bridge connecting all galaxies in VGS 31 is more apparent in Figure fig:examples1 , where we have imaged with natural weighting to increase our sensitivity. This system shows further signs of ongoing interactions in the left most galaxy, where a gas poor stellar ring is present just at the boundary of the H i disk.

sources.summary: The paper describes VGS 31 as a three-galaxy interacting void system and Markarian object with one of the sample's highest star-formation rates, lying in the starburst region.

quotes.quote: The second one, VGS 31, is a system of three interacting galaxies forming a filamentary structure inside a void beygu2013 .
VGS 31 and 31a are two Markarian galaxies and have the highest SFRs of the whole sample. They occupy a region overlapping with starbursts.

gmw_00002501.name: VGS 38
gmw_00002501.overview: VGS 38 is an irregular void galaxy that belongs to a system of three galaxies connected by a faint HI bridge [arXiv:1008.4616, arXiv:1609.04920]. Despite its optically disturbed appearance and strong signs of interaction from a major merger [arXiv:1609.04920], the galaxy exhibits relatively smooth rotation [arXiv:1008.4616].

sources.summary: The paper describes VGS 38 as an irregular three-galaxy void system with a common HI envelope, low HI-to-light ratio, strong interaction signatures, and evidence for a major merger.

quotes.quote: VGS 17 and VGS 38 are examples of VGS galaxies with irregular shapes. VGS 38 is a system of three galaxies embedded in the same H i envelope kreckel2012 . In Figure fig:morph , all three members of the system are shown.

On the other hand, VGS 08 and VGS 38 have much lower M_ HI to- L_ B ratios than should be expected, given the fact that they are quite blue and are star-forming galaxies (Figure figure:col_sfr_M ). These galaxies have an irregular shape and the H i disk of VGS 38 shows in particular strong signs of interaction and therefore has large uncertainties in the estimate of the total H i mass.

VGS 38 in a group of three galaxies has clearly undergone major merger as seen in the H i velocity map kreckel2011,kreckel2012 and optical morphology (Figure 5), is another example for rare major merger events in voids.

sources.summary: The paper describes VGS 38 as an optically disturbed void galaxy in a three-galaxy system linked by an H i bridge, yet retaining smooth rotation despite its chaotic appearance.

quotes.quote: VGS 38 has the most optically disturbed appearance, and it was also the only selected void galaxy to have a known companion, VGS 38a, which is only 12 kpc away on the sky and at nearly overlapping velocity. The surprising discovery of a third galaxy, VGS 38b, aligned with the first two, all three of which are connected by a faint 1 H i bridge, results in a system that stretches nearly 50 kpc across the sky. VGS 38, despite its chaotic appearance, exhibits relatively smooth rotation in the direction perpendicular to the line of galaxies. It is difficult to understand how this system could have undergone a major, disruptive interaction while leaving the gas disk unperturbed. It is located at the edge of its void.

gmw_00002502.name: J0006+2452
gmw_00002502.overview: J0006+2452 is a young star-forming galaxy characterized as an extreme helium II emitter [arXiv:2401.16165]. The system exhibits strong nitrogen IV features and requires a contribution of very massive stars to explain its emission strengths [arXiv:2401.16165].

sources.summary: The paper highlights J0006+2452 as an extreme He II emitter with strong N IV features; its N v and He II strengths mark it as young and requiring VMS.

quotes.quote: In particular, J0006+2452, J0110-0501, and J1316+2614 are the most extreme \ emitters in our sample showing EW_ 0 ( ) 4.5 , which is similar than that measured in local star-forming regions with VMS (e.g., R136 star cluster with EW_ 0 ( ) 4.5 ; crowther2016 ).

For example, the N iv 1486 appears stronger in J0006+2452 and J0110-0501 than in R136 and SB 179, although effects of different resolution and SNR between spectra prevent a fair comparison. The blueshifted absorption component of the N iv 1719 P-Cygni line profile is also more pronounced in J0006+2452 and J0110-0501, showing substantially larger terminal velocities than what is seen in the spectra of R136 or SB 179.

The sources that lie on the left-hand side of this figure, J0006+2452, J0036+2725, and J0110-0501, show EW_ 0 (N v ) 6.0 \ and are the youngest systems in our sample. These sources show EW_ 0 (He ii ) 2.7-4.6 , requiring a significant number and contribution of VMS, or IMF upper mass limits between M_ up =225 M _ and M_ up =275 M _ .

gmw_00002503.name: J0036+2725
gmw_00002503.overview: J0036+2725 is a UV-bright starburst galaxy characterized by a very young stellar population estimated to be approximately 3 Myr old [arXiv:2401.16165]. Its spectrum exhibits broad nitrogen IV emission, a nitrogen IV P-Cygni profile, and exceptionally strong nitrogen V absorption [arXiv:2401.16165].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0036+2725 as a UV-bright source with clear broad N IV and P-Cygni VMS signatures, among the strongest N v absorbers and youngest systems in the sample.

quotes.quote: In addition, a significant fraction of our sources show other spectral features that are present in the spectra of individual VMS (e.g., R136-a1, -a2, -a3, or R146 see: crowther2016, brands2022, fabrice2022 ). In particular, broad emission in N iv 1486 and a significant P-Cygni line profile in N iv 1719 are clearly detected in J0006+2452, J0036+2725, J0110-0501, J0146-0220, J1157+0113, J1220+0842, and partially/barely detected in J0115+1837, J0121+0025, and J1335+4330 (see Appendix A1_GTC_spectra ).
J0006+2452, J0036+2725, and J0110-0501 have the strongest absorption in \ with EW_ 0 5 \ and, therefore, they stand out to be the youngest systems in our sample ( 3 Myr).

The sources that lie on the left-hand side of this figure, J0006+2452, J0036+2725, and J0110-0501, show EW_ 0 (N v ) 6.0 \ and are the youngest systems in our sample. These sources show EW_ 0 (He ii ) 2.7-4.6 , requiring a significant number and contribution of VMS, or IMF upper mass limits between M_ up =225 M _ and M_ up =275 M _ .

gmw_00002504.name: J0110-0501
gmw_00002504.overview: J0110-0501 is a young star-forming galaxy characterized as an extreme helium II emitter [arXiv:2401.16165]. The galaxy exhibits strong nitrogen IV and nitrogen V spectral features, which indicate a stellar population containing very massive stars [arXiv:2401.16165].

sources.summary: The paper treats J0110-0501 as an extreme He II emitter with strong N IV features; its N v and He II strengths mark it as young and requiring VMS.

quotes.quote: In particular, J0006+2452, J0110-0501, and J1316+2614 are the most extreme \ emitters in our sample showing EW_ 0 ( ) 4.5 , which is similar than that measured in local star-forming regions with VMS (e.g., R136 star cluster with EW_ 0 ( ) 4.5 ; crowther2016 ).

For example, the N iv 1486 appears stronger in J0006+2452 and J0110-0501 than in R136 and SB 179, although effects of different resolution and SNR between spectra prevent a fair comparison. The blueshifted absorption component of the N iv 1719 P-Cygni line profile is also more pronounced in J0006+2452 and J0110-0501, showing substantially larger terminal velocities than what is seen in the spectra of R136 or SB 179.

The sources that lie on the left-hand side of this figure, J0006+2452, J0036+2725, and J0110-0501, show EW_ 0 (N v ) 6.0 \ and are the youngest systems in our sample. These sources show EW_ 0 (He ii ) 2.7-4.6 , requiring a significant number and contribution of VMS, or IMF upper mass limits between M_ up =225 M _ and M_ up =275 M _ .

gmw_00002505.name: J0115+1837
gmw_00002505.overview: J0115+1837 is a ultraviolet-bright galaxy [arXiv:2401.16165]. Its spectrum exhibits a nearly symmetric, broad helium II emission line profile and partially detected nitrogen IV features associated with very massive stars [arXiv:2401.16165].

sources.summary: The paper notes J0115+1837 has a nearly symmetric broad He II profile and partially detected N IV VMS-related features within the UV-bright galaxy sample.

quotes.quote: The He ii line appears symmetric for some sources (e.g., J0006+2452 or J0115+1837), while for others the line appears asymmetric (J1220+0842 or J1335+4330) or with multiple emission/absorption peaks (e.g., J0031+3545 or J0121+0025).

In addition, a significant fraction of our sources show other spectral features that are present in the spectra of individual VMS (e.g., R136-a1, -a2, -a3, or R146 see: crowther2016, brands2022, fabrice2022 ). In particular, broad emission in N iv 1486 and a significant P-Cygni line profile in N iv 1719 are clearly detected in J0006+2452, J0036+2725, J0110-0501, J0146-0220, J1157+0113, J1220+0842, and partially/barely detected in J0115+1837, J0121+0025, and J1335+4330 (see Appendix A1_GTC_spectra ).

As already highlighted in Fig. HeII_profiles , the spectra of our 13 sources exhibit diverse He ii profiles, ranging from nearly Gaussian/symmetric profiles (e.g., J0006+2452 or J0115+1837, although with different line widths) to asymmetric profiles (J1220+0842 or J1335+4330), or more complex ones with multiple emission/absorption peaks (e.g., J0031+3545 or J0121+0025).

gmw_00002506.name: J0121+0025
gmw_00002506.overview: J0121+0025 is an extremely UV-luminous star-forming galaxy at redshift z=3.244 that exhibits strong Lyman continuum leakage [arXiv:2107.12313, arXiv:2401.16165]. Driven by a young starburst, the galaxy has a high star formation rate and its spectrum rules out a major active galactic nucleus contribution [arXiv:2107.12313].

sources.summary: The paper reports J0121 + 0025 as an extremely UV-luminous z=3.244 star-forming galaxy with strong LyC leakage, young starburst signatures, high SFR, and little evidence for AGN or foreground contamination.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: In this work, we present the discovery and detailed analysis of a new luminous source, SDSS J012156.09 + 002520.3 ( , [ J2000 ] = 20.4837 ^ , 0.4223 ^ ), hereafter J0121 + 0025, an extremely UV-luminous ( M_ UV = -24.1 ) star-forming galaxy at z=3.244 showing copious LyC leakage ( f_ esc, abs 40 ).

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We report the discovery of J0121 + 0025, an extremely luminous and young star-forming galaxy ( M_ UV = -24.11 , log [ L_ Ly / erg s^ 1 ] = 43.8 ) at z=3.244 showing copious Lyman continuum (LyC) leakage ( f_ esc, abs 40 ). High signal-to-noise ratio rest-frame UV spectroscopy with the Gran Telescopio Canarias reveals a high significance ( 7.9 ) emission below the Lyman limit ( < 912 ), with a flux density level f_ 900 = 0.78 0.10 Jy, and strong P-Cygni in wind lines of O vi 1033 , N v 1240 and C iv 1550 that are indicative of a young age of the starburst ( <10 Myr). The spectrum is rich in stellar photospheric features, for which a significant contribution of an AGN at these wavelengths is ruled out. Low-ionization ISM absorption lines are also detected, but are weak ( EW_ 0 1 ) and show large residual intensities, suggesting a clumpy geometry of the gas with a non-unity covering fraction or a highly ionized ISM.

The contribution of a foreground and AGN contamination to the LyC signal is unlikely. Deep optical to Spitzer/IRAC 4.5 m imaging show that the spectral energy distribution of J0121 + 0025 is dominated by the emission of the young starburst, with log( M_ ^ burst /M_ ) = 9.9 0.1 and SFR = 981 232 M_ yr ^ 1 . J0121 + 0025 is the most powerful LyC emitters known among the star-forming galaxy population.

sources.summary: The paper describes J0121+0025 as a young, high-SFR LyC emitter whose UV spectrum has complex He II structure and partially detected N IV VMS-related features.

quotes.quote: The properties of a few of these sources, namely J1220+0842, J0121+0025, and J1316+2614, were already investigated in detail in , for which they find very young stellar populations ( 10 Myr) with SFR 400-600 M_ yr ^ 1 and stellar masses of log( M_ /M_ ) 9.6-9.8 . J0121+0025 and J1316+2614 are also found to be strong Lyman continuum (LyC) emitters, with LyC escape fractions of 40 90 marques2021, marques2022 .

The He ii line appears symmetric for some sources (e.g., J0006+2452 or J0115+1837), while for others the line appears asymmetric (J1220+0842 or J1335+4330) or with multiple emission/absorption peaks (e.g., J0031+3545 or J0121+0025).

In addition, a significant fraction of our sources show other spectral features that are present in the spectra of individual VMS (e.g., R136-a1, -a2, -a3, or R146 see: crowther2016, brands2022, fabrice2022 ). In particular, broad emission in N iv 1486 and a significant P-Cygni line profile in N iv 1719 are clearly detected in J0006+2452, J0036+2725, J0110-0501, J0146-0220, J1157+0113, J1220+0842, and partially/barely detected in J0115+1837, J0121+0025, and J1335+4330 (see Appendix A1_GTC_spectra ).

gmw_00002507.name: J1220+0842
gmw_00002507.overview: J1220+0842 is a compact, dust-poor starburst galaxy at redshift z = 2.469 [arXiv:2009.02177]. It is characterized by a young stellar population [arXiv:2401.16165, arXiv:2009.02177], low metallicity [arXiv:2401.16165, arXiv:2009.02177], and a massive ionized gas outflow [arXiv:2102.00774].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BOSS-EUVLG1 as an extreme UV-luminous z=2.47 starburst and measures a fast ionized gas outflow whose warm phase is too weak to quench star formation.

quotes.quote: BOSS-EUVLG1 is the most ultraviolet (UV) and Ly luminous galaxy to be going through a very active starburst phase detected thus far in the Universe. It is forming stars at a rate (SFR) of 955 118 M _ yr ^ 1 . We report the detection of a broad H component carrying 25 of the total H flux. The broad H line traces a fast and massive ionized gas outflow characterized by a total mass, (M_ out [ M_ ] ), of 7.94 0.15, along with an outflowing velocity (V _ out ) of 573 151 km s ^ 1 and an outflowing mass rate ( M _ out ) of 44 20 M _ yr ^ 1 . The presence of the outflow in BOSS-EUVLG1 is also supported by the identification of blueshifted UV absorption lines in low and high ionization states.

The derived low mass-loading factor ( = 0.05 0.03) indicates that, although it is massive, this phase of the outflow cannot be relevant for the quenching of the star formation, namely, the negative feedback. In addition, only a small fraction ( 15 ) of the ionized outflowing material with velocities above 372 km s ^ 1 has the capacity to escape the gravitational potential and to enrich the surrounding circumgalactic medium at distances above several tens of kpc. The ionized phase of the outflow does not carry sufficient mass or energy to play a relevant role in the evolution of the host galaxy nor in the enrichment of the intergalactic medium.

sources.summary: The paper treats J1220+0842 as a low-metallicity special-case VMS-host candidate with asymmetric He II, strong N IV profiles, and spectral similarity to known VMS-dominated clusters.

quotes.quote: The properties of a few of these sources, namely J1220+0842, J0121+0025, and J1316+2614, were already investigated in detail in , for which they find very young stellar populations ( 10 Myr) with SFR 400-600 M_ yr ^ 1 and stellar masses of log( M_ /M_ ) 9.6-9.8 .
We also used the metallicity measurements of J1220+0842 (12+log(O/H) = 8.13 0.19 ) and J1316+2614 (12+log(O/H) = 8.45 0.12 ) obtained in and , respectively, using the R23 metallicity calibrator.

An additional source, J1220+0842, represents a special case. Its EW_ 0 ( ) =2.52 0.33 \ falls below our selection threshold. However, the source shows strong and broad N iv 1486 and a P-Cygni line in N iv 1719, both of which are also VMS signatures. This galaxy also has the lowest metallicity in our sample (12+log(O/H) =8.13 0.19 , Figure fig_met ), which might be expected to weaken the \ line (due to weaker stellar winds). Given its spectral similarity to the Sunburst cluster and others see Figure fig_compasiron_VMS_clusters Mestric2023 , we add this source to our sample of candidate VMS hosts.

sources.summary: The paper presents BOSS-EUVLG1 as an extremely luminous, dust-poor high-redshift starburst whose QSO-like UV and Ly emission comes from intense early star formation, not AGN activity or lensing.

quotes.quote: In this Letter we study SDSS J122040.72+084238.1, hereafter BOSS-EUVLG1, located at ( ,  )_ J2000 = (185.1697^ ,8.7106^ ) with a redshift z =2.469. It was discovered as part of our search for Extremely UV-Luminous Galaxies (EUVLGs, M_ UV < 23) within the 9300  deg ^ 2 wide extended Baryon Oscillation Spectroscopic Survey eBOSS: abolfathi2018 of the Sloan Digital Sky Survey SDSS: eisenstein2011 . BOSS-EUVLG1 is the most luminous, un-obscured star-forming galaxy known in the Universe by far, with M_ UV = 24.4 and log( L_ Ly / erg s ^ 1 )=44.0.

First classified as a QSO within the Baryon Oscillation Spectroscopic Survey, follow-up observations with the Gran Telescopio Canarias reveal that its large luminosity, M_ UV 24.40 and log( L_ Ly / erg s^ 1 ) 44.0 , is due to an intense burst of star-formation, and not to an AGN or gravitational lensing. BOSS-EUVLG1 is a compact ( r_ eff 1.2 kpc), young (4-5 Myr) starburst with a stellar mass log( M_ * /M_ )=10.0 0.1 and a prodigious star formation rate of 1000  M_  yr ^ 1 . However, it is metal- and dust-poor (12+log(O/H)=8.13 0.19, E(B-V) 0.07, log( L_ IR / L_ UV ) < -1.2 ), indicating that we are witnessing the very early phase of an intense starburst that has had no time to enrich the ISM.

gmw_00002508.name: J1316+2614
gmw_00002508.overview: J1316+2614 is a UV-bright star-forming galaxy at a redshift of z=3.6130 [arXiv:2210.02392]. It is characterized by a young starburst [arXiv:2210.02392, arXiv:2401.16165], strong helium II emission [arXiv:2401.16165], gas inflows [arXiv:2210.02392], and an exceptionally high Lyman continuum escape fraction [arXiv:2210.02392, arXiv:2401.16165].

sources.summary: The paper presents J1316 + 2614 as a UV-bright z=3.613 star-forming galaxy with extreme LyC escape, a young starburst, gas inflows, and possible gas compaction.

quotes.quote: We present the discovery and analysis of J1316 + 2614 at z=3.6130 , a UV-bright star-forming galaxy ( M_ UV 24.7 ) with large escape of Lyman continuum (LyC) radiation. J1316 + 2614 is a young ( 10 Myr) star-forming galaxy with SFR 500 M_ yr ^ 1 and a starburst mass of log( M_ /M_ ) 9.7 . It shows a very steep UV continuum, _ UV 2.59 0.05 , consistent with residual dust obscuration, E(B-V) 0 . LyC emission is detected with high significance ( 17 ) down to 830 , for which a very high relative (absolute) LyC escape fraction f_ esc (LyC) 0.92 ( 0.87 ) is inferred.

Gas inflows are detected in J1316 + 2614 from the blue-dominated peak Ly emission (with a blue-to-red peak line ratio I_ blue /I_ red 3.7 ) and redshifted ISM absorption ( 100 km s ^ 1 ). Our results suggest that J1316 + 2614 is undergoing a gas compaction event, possibly representing a short-lived phase in the evolution of massive and compact galaxies, where strong gas inflows have triggered an extreme star formation episode and nearly 100 LyC photons are escaping.

sources.summary: The paper presents J1316+2614 as a young LyC-emitting galaxy and one of the strongest He II emitters, requiring a very high IMF upper mass limit in VMS models.

quotes.quote: The properties of a few of these sources, namely J1220+0842, J0121+0025, and J1316+2614, were already investigated in detail in , for which they find very young stellar populations ( 10 Myr) with SFR 400-600 M_ yr ^ 1 and stellar masses of log( M_ /M_ ) 9.6-9.8 . J0121+0025 and J1316+2614 are also found to be strong Lyman continuum (LyC) emitters, with LyC escape fractions of 40 90 marques2021, marques2022 .

In particular, J0006+2452, J0110-0501, and J1316+2614 are the most extreme \ emitters in our sample showing EW_ 0 ( ) 4.5 , which is similar than that measured in local star-forming regions with VMS (e.g., R136 star cluster with EW_ 0 ( ) 4.5 ; crowther2016 ).
An exception may be J1316+2614, showing EW_ 0 (N v ) 2.7 \ and EW_ 0 (He ii ) 4.5 , which requires an IMF upper mass limit of M_ up 475 M _ .

gmw_00002509.name: J1335+4330
gmw_00002509.overview: J1335+4330 is a UV-bright starburst galaxy [arXiv:2401.16165]. Its spectrum exhibits an asymmetric He II line profile and partially detected N IV features associated with very massive stars [arXiv:2401.16165].

sources.summary: The paper notes J1335+4330 has an asymmetric He II profile and partially detected N IV VMS-related features within the UV-bright galaxy sample.

quotes.quote: The He ii line appears symmetric for some sources (e.g., J0006+2452 or J0115+1837), while for others the line appears asymmetric (J1220+0842 or J1335+4330) or with multiple emission/absorption peaks (e.g., J0031+3545 or J0121+0025).

In addition, a significant fraction of our sources show other spectral features that are present in the spectra of individual VMS (e.g., R136-a1, -a2, -a3, or R146 see: crowther2016, brands2022, fabrice2022 ). In particular, broad emission in N iv 1486 and a significant P-Cygni line profile in N iv 1719 are clearly detected in J0006+2452, J0036+2725, J0110-0501, J0146-0220, J1157+0113, J1220+0842, and partially/barely detected in J0115+1837, J0121+0025, and J1335+4330 (see Appendix A1_GTC_spectra ).

As already highlighted in Fig. HeII_profiles , the spectra of our 13 sources exhibit diverse He ii profiles, ranging from nearly Gaussian/symmetric profiles (e.g., J0006+2452 or J0115+1837, although with different line widths) to asymmetric profiles (J1220+0842 or J1335+4330), or more complex ones with multiple emission/absorption peaks (e.g., J0031+3545 or J0121+0025).

gmw_00002510.name: DDO 101
gmw_00002510.overview: DDO 101 is an isolated dwarf irregular galaxy located in the NGC 4062 Group [arXiv:1601.05821, arXiv:2006.01735]. The galaxy features a neutral hydrogen disk extending to 1.8 kpc that contains two detected holes [arXiv:2006.01735], as well as low-amplitude gas flaring [arXiv:2010.07948].

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 101 as a distant dwarf irregular whose uncertain Tully-Fisher distance explains a poor nominal rotation-curve fit; larger plausible distances yield good fits.

quotes.quote: DDO 101: DDO 101 (also called UGC 6900) is substantially more distant than the other galaxies. Its distance is typically assumed to be D_ DDO101 = 6.4 Mpc e.g. 2015AJ....149..180O , however the uncertainties on D_ DDO101 are very large. It it too far away for an accurate tip-of-the-red-giant-branch or Cepheid distance measurement, and so its distance must be determined instead from matching its peak rotation velocity to the Tully-Fisher relation 1977A&A....54..661T .

In Figure fig:real_rotcurve_ddo101 , we show results from mass modelling DDO 101 assuming three different distances D_ DDO101 = 6.4 Mpc (as assumed previously in the literature; top row); D_ DDO101 = 12.9 Mpc (the Hubble flow distance, using the cosmological parameters from 2013arXiv1303.5076P ; middle row); and D_ DDO101 = 16 Mpc (the maximum distance reported in NED; bottom row). For D_ DDO101 = 6.4 Mpc, the fit is poor. The rotation curve rises much more steeply than is allowed by the \ model, similarly to the findings in 2015AJ....149..180O . Indeed, the fact that DDO 101 is one of the few isolated dwarf irregulars with a rotation curve that appears `NFW-like' was the key motivation for including it in our sample here.

sources.summary: The paper labels DDO 101 a distance rogue, notes that a 12 Mpc distance can fit a dark halo model, and shows its distance alternatives straddle the relation.

quotes.quote: Finally, one galaxy -- DDO 101 -- has a very uncertain distance; we refer to this galaxy as a `distance Rogue'. We discussed DDO 101 in detail in R16b, showing that for a distance of 12 Mpc it can be well-fit by a \ dark matter halo. We consider its position on the \ relation alongside the i Rogues in Appendix app:robust .

Our one D Rogue -- DDO 101 -- is marked on Figure fig:robust , left panel, by the magenta data points. Since the distance for this galaxy is highly uncertain (see the discussion in R16b), we plot two points for D_ DDO101 = 6.4 Mpc and D_ DDO101 = 12.9 Mpc. As can be seen, these straddle the \ relation. Thus, our D Rogue does not affect our key results and conclusions either.

sources.summary: The paper notes that DDO 101 has a relatively steep inferred dark-matter slope, but argues that NFW-like fits and the slope may be affected by insufficient inner sampling and beam smearing.

quotes.quote: A few galaxies in our sample, such as DDO 101 and DDO 210 whose rotation curves are equally fitted by both NFW and pseudo-isothermal halo models appear to have relatively steeper slopes compared to the other ones. However, as discussed in Section DISK_HALO_DECOMPOSITION , this could be due to insufficient sampling of the dark matter density profiles in the inner region.
This could be the case of DDO 101 and DDO 210. In particular, DDO 101 is most likely to be affected by the beam smearing effect as shown in Fig. Figure7 .

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 101 as an NGC 4062 Group galaxy with an H I disk extending to 1.8 kpc and two detected holes.

quotes.quote: DDO 101 DDO 101 (PGC 37449, UGC 6900) is in the NGC 4062 Group at a distance of 6.4 Mpc with a V-band magnitude of 15 . The \ disk of the galaxy extends to 1.8 kpc. The galaxy contains two holes, one partially broken and another intact.

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 101 as having low-amplitude gas flaring, high 24 micron flux, and a literature HWHM scale-height estimate significantly different from this work.

quotes.quote: The gas distribution flares of a factor ranging from a minimum of 1.1-1.6 (but with large uncertainty) for DDO 101 and DDO 87 to a maximum of 4-4.5 (e.g. DDO 52, DDO 47).
NGC 2366 and DDO 101 have the highest 24 m fluxes, while only upper limits are available for DDO 52 and DDO 87.

For some galaxies in common with our sample, the HWHM calculated by is also compatible within the uncertainties with those in Fig. fig:hHI_all (e.g. DDO 50, DDO 87, DDO 133), but others are significantly different (e.g. WLM, DDO 47, DDO 101, DDO 126).

gmw_00002511.name: A521-sys1
gmw_00002511.overview: A521-sys1 is a main-sequence star-forming spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.043 [arXiv:2301.05715]. It features a rotating disc structure [arXiv:2301.05715], clear spiral arms [arXiv:1802.08451, arXiv:2301.05715], and numerous star-forming clumps containing highly turbulent giant molecular clouds [arXiv:2301.05715, arXiv:1802.08451].

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA CO(4-3) mapping of strongly lensed A521-sys1, identifying 14 turbulent GMCs in a clumpy rotating z=1.043 galaxy with efficient star formation.

quotes.quote: We bring support to this scenario by reporting the new discovery of giant molecular clouds (GMCs) in the strongly lensed, clumpy, main-sequence galaxy, A521-sys1, at z=1.043 . Its CO(4--3) emission was mapped with the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) at an angular resolution of 0.19'' 0.16'' , reading down to 30 pc thanks to gravitational lensing. We identified 14 GMCs, most being virialized, with 10^ 5.9 10^ 7.9 M_ masses and a median 800 M_ pc ^ 2 molecular gas mass surface density, that are, respectively, 100 and 10 times higher than for nearby GMCs. They are also characterized by 10 times higher supersonic turbulence with a median Mach number of 60.

Therefore, we believe that our estimate of _ ff 11 per cent for case (3) is most likely characterising the more efficient star-formation process taking place in the z 1 A521-sys1 galaxy compared to nearby galaxies. We present the analysis of the CO(4--3) molecular gas emission of the A521-sys1 main-sequence star-forming galaxy at z=1.043 . In the HST rest-frame UV to optical images, the galaxy is clumpy, showing several UV-bright clumps Messa22 , and has a nice spiral structure, also traced by the CO(4--3) emission (see Fig. fig:HST-ALMA ) that even exhibits a surface over-density of molecular gas mass in the galaxy radial profile at the location of the spiral arms Nagy22 . The kinematics of both ionized ( [ O ii ] emission) and molecular (CO(4--3) emission) gas reveals a rotating disc, with an inclination-corrected rotation velocity of _ rot = 130 5 km s^ 1 Patricio18,Girard19 .

sources.summary: The paper reports the first resolved study of A521-sys1, describing clear spiral arms, many star-forming clumps, and main-sequence-like mass and star-formation rate for a z~1 disc galaxy.

quotes.quote: A521-sys1 We present here the first study of the resolved properties in this system. This is a remarkable object, that displays clear spiral arms and numerous star-forming clumps Richard2010a . It is also a very good example of a typical disc galaxy at z 1 , with mass and SFR that agree quite well with the main-sequence of star-forming galaxies at these redshifts.

gmw_00002512.name: Cosmic Snake galaxy
gmw_00002512.overview: The Cosmic Snake galaxy is a strongly lensed, rotation-dominated disk galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.036 [arXiv:1907.11586, arXiv:2301.05715]. It features a smooth velocity field [arXiv:1802.08451] and contains 17 high-density giant molecular clouds that support the in-situ formation of stellar clumps [arXiv:2301.05715].

sources.summary: The paper uses MUSE and SINFONI observations of M1206-sys1 to derive resolved metallicity information and a one-dimensional metallicity gradient for the lensed galaxy.

quotes.quote: M1206-sys1 was also observed with MUSE, and its global metallicity can be derived from the integrated spectrum using and emission lines. However, is too faint to derive the resolved metallicity of this galaxy using MUSE data, and we use instead and emission from SINFONI data.

We produce a source plane deprojected distance map, using the ellipticity, position angle and centre from an elliptical fit to the F160W HST image of the complete multiple image of the galaxy. We forward-lens this map using lenstool , and define 1 kpc annular apertures starting at =0 , measuring the average metallicity in these annuli. This approach does not include any correction for seeing, which it is known to flatten gradients Yuan2013 . We fit the data with the linmix https://linmix.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ package. We obtain a slope of -0.039 0.060 dex/kpc, a central metallicity 9.06 0.25 in 12+log(O/H).

sources.summary: The paper compares A521-sys1 with the strongly lensed Cosmic Snake galaxy, whose ALMA data revealed 17 high-density GMCs supporting in-situ formation of high-redshift stellar clumps.

quotes.quote: We obtained the first detection of GMCs in a clumpy, main-sequence, rotation-dominated galaxy---the strongly lensed Cosmic Snake galaxy at z=1.036 --characterized by a stellar mass of M_ stars = (4.0 0.5) 10^ 10 M _ , a star-formation rate of SFR = 30 10 M_ yr^ 1 , and a molecular gas to stellar mass fraction of f_ molgas = 25 4 per cent Dessauges19 . The kinematics, the metallicity, and the radial profiles of surface densities of M_ stars , SFR , molecular gas mass ( M_ molgas ), and dust mass of the Cosmic Snake galaxy were studied by Patricio18,Patricio19 , Girard19 , and Nagy22 , respectively. The combination of the superb angular resolution achieved with ALMA and gravitational lensing allowed to map the emission of the CO(4--3) line of this galaxy at the resolution of nearby galaxies Sun18,Rosolowsky21 , reading down to 30 pc, and to perform the GMC search in the velocity space (in channel intensity maps). We identified 17 GMCs with on average 100 times higher M_ molgas and 10 times higher M_ molgas surface densities than for most of the local/nearby GMCs Dessauges19 .

The high masses of the identified GMCs are close to the stellar masses of the UV-bright clumps detected by Cava18 in comparable numbers in the HST images of the Cosmic Snake galaxy at a similar spatial resolution to the ALMA images. In Dessauges19 we concluded that this corroborates the in-situ formation of high-redshift stellar clumps. Moreover, we proposed that the physical properties derived for the Cosmic Snake GMCs (high mass, high density, and high internal turbulence) suggest that, also at high redshift, GMCs seem to form with properties that adjust to the host galaxy's ambient interstellar medium (ISM) conditions (pressure, turbulence, density, etc.), as observed for nearby GMCs Hughes13,Sun18,Sun20 .

sources.summary: M1206-sys1 is presented as the highly magnified cosmic snake, with head-tail lensing, derived stellar mass, first gas-phase metallicity and star-formation rate estimates, and a smooth rotation-dominated velocity field.

quotes.quote: M1206-sys1 MACS1206, nicknamed the cosmic snake Cava2017 , is another example of the "head and tail" structure: it has a long and highly magnified (although not complete) multiple image and a less magnified counter-image, that contains the total spatial information of this galaxy (see Eichner2013 for details). From the MUSE and HST data, we derive a stellar mass (7.87 0.49 10^ 10 M _ ) and, for the first time, the gas-phase metallicity ( 12+ (O/H) = 8.90 0.06 ) and the star formation rate of (38-43 M _ yr ^ 1 ). This is also a rotation-dominated galaxy, displaying a smooth velocity field. Cava2017 performed SED fitting to the HST data and IRAC photometry, deriving a stellar mass of 4.0 10^ 10 M _ and a SFR _ SED of 30 M _ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00002513.name: IRAS F11119+3257
gmw_00002513.overview: IRAS F11119+3257 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy and type 1 quasar hosting a partially obscured narrow-line Seyfert 1 nucleus [arXiv:1910.05068, arXiv:2003.11427]. The galaxy features heavily obscured star formation [arXiv:1910.05068] alongside molecular and X-ray outflows that extend on kiloparsec scales [arXiv:1910.05068, arXiv:2003.11427].

sources.summary: The paper presents NIR spectroscopy of the ULIRG quasar IRAS F11119+3257, finding kiloparsec-scale mini-BAL outflows, blueshifted emission components, and obscured star formation traced by unusual Paschen emission.

quotes.quote: IRAS F11119+3257 is a quasar-dominated Ultra-Luminous InfraRed Galaxy, with a partially obscured narrow-line seyfert 1 nucleus. In this paper, we present the NIR spectroscopy of F11119+3257, in which we find unusual Paschen emission lines, and metastable 10830 absorption associated with the previously reported atomic sodium and molecular OH mini-BAL (Broad Absorption Line) outflow. Photo-ionization diagnosis confirms previous findings that the outflows are at kilo-parsec scales.

We indeed find that high-ionization emission lines ( , , and ) are dominated by blueshifted components at similar speeds to the mini-BALs. The blueshifted components are also detected in some low-ionization emission lines, such as 3727 and some Balmer lines ( , , and ), even though their cores are dominated by narrow ( FWHM_ NEL = 570 40 ) or broad components at the systemic redshift of z=0.18966 0.00006 .

In the NIR spectrum of F11119+3257, we also find that the Paschen emission lines are unique, in which a very narrow ( FWHM=260 20 ) component is shown in only . This narrow component most probably comes from heavily obscured star formation. Based on the and emissions, we obtain an extinction at the H band , A_H > 2.1 (or a reddenning of > 3.7) , and a star formation rate of SFR > 130 M_ yr^ 1 that resembles the estimates inferred from the FIR emissions ( SFR_ FIR = 190 90 M_ yr ^ 1 ) .

sources.summary: The paper studies quasar J1114 + 3241 as a super-Eddington ULIRG/AGN with powerful outflows, a compact radio counterpart, and a two-sided parsec-scale jet.

quotes.quote: The quasar (J1114 + 3241, B1111 + 329) at a redshift z = 0.189 is a type 1 ultra-luminous infra-red galaxy (ULIRG) and hosts strong molecular outflows, with the emission dominated by the AGN component Veilleux2013, Veilleux2017 . Based on a SMBH mass of M_ bh 2 10^7 M _ calibrated for a sample of similar ULIRG sources Kawakatu2007 , the bolometric luminosity L_ b = 5 L_ Edd , where L_ Edd is the Eddington luminosity Tombesi2015 . Using a correlation relation between infra-red and radio luminosities for starburst galaxies, it is found that for the AGN component far exceeds the starburst contribution Komossa2006 . In addition to the molecular outflows, it also hosts wide-aperture energetic radiation driven X-ray emitting winds, suggesting a likely energy conserving quasar-mode feedback Tombesi2015, Tombesi2017 .

The quasar has a relatively bright radio counterpart. The early survey of the Bologna Northern Cross Radio Telescope (BNCRT) at 408 MHz Colla1970 found a half-Jy radio counterpart. Later, it was observed by more radio telescopes at multiple frequencies. The radio flux densities reported in literature over the past 30 years are listed in Table  tab1 .

sources.summary: ALMA resolves the CO gas and 3-mm continuum of IRAS F11119+3257 on kiloparsec scales, finding a small east-west gradient and weak rotation signature.

quotes.quote: IRAS F11119+3257 has been observed with ALMA in CO(1 0) emission at about 2 .8 resolution veilleux17 , with a main objective of detecting the molecular outflow. Compared to these observations that do not spatially resolve the CO morphology, our ALMA observations at 0 .4 resolution clearly resolve the distribution of both CO gas and 3-mm continuum emission in the host galaxy of IRAS F11119+3257 on 1 kpc scale. However, our data are not deep enough for the detection of broad wings in the CO emission.

A small velocity gradient is present in the east-west direction of IRAS F11119+3257, while the deviation from regular velocity field seen in the northeast region may be indicative of noncircular motion.

For IRAS F11119+3257, we combined our high-resolution CO data with the archival ALMA CO data ( 2 .8 resolution) observed by veilleux17 to produce the PV diagram. The PV plots show that the rotation curve rises steeply in the inner region, becoming flat at radius 0 .5 - 0 .8 ( 0.9 - 1.3 kpc) for I ZW 1, IRAS 06269 0543, and IRAS F15462 0450. However, for IRAS F11119+3257, the rotation curve is much less prominent.

sources.summary: The paper describes F11119 + 3257 as a type-1 ULIRG/quasar host with an ALMA-confirmed extended molecular outflow whose energetics test quasar feedback models.

quotes.quote: The target was F11119 + 3257, an ultraluminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) with unambiguous type-1 quasar optical broad emission lines. The energetics of the accretion disk wind and molecular outflow were found to be consistent with the predictions of quasar feedback models where the molecular outflow is driven by a hot energy-conserving bubble inflated by the inner quasar accretion disk wind. However, this conclusion was uncertain because the energetics of the outflowing molecular gas were estimated from the optically thick OH 119 m transition profile observed with Herschel .

Here, we independently confirm the presence of the molecular outflow in F11119 + 3257, based on the detection of broad wings in the CO(1 0) profile derived from ALMA observations. The broad CO(1 0) line emission appears to be spatially extended on a scale of at least 7 kpc from the center. Mass outflow rate, momentum flux, and mechanical power of (80 200) R_7^ 1 M_ yr ^ 1 , (1.5 3.0) R_7^ 1 L_ AGN / , and (0.15 0.40) R_7^ 1 L_ AGN are inferred from these data, assuming a CO to H _2 conversion factor appropriate for a ULIRG ( R_7 is the radius of the outflow normalized to 7 kpc and L_ AGN is the AGN luminosity).

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 11119+3257 as a ULIRG and radio-loud NLS1 with high-velocity outflows, compact steep-spectrum radio emission, intrinsic extinction, and blueshifted [O III].

quotes.quote: IRAS 11119+3257 . This is a famous ULIRG with high velocity outflows observed at optical and X-ray band 2003MNRAS.340..289L, 2015Natur.519..436T . 2006AJ....132..531K labelled this sources as a radio-loud NLS1 and presented that its radio feature was compact and steep spectrum. The optical spectrum of IRAS 11119+3257 show large intrinsic extinction 2002AJ....124...18Z . Thus a intrinsic extinction A_V = 2.5 mag is corrected to make the Balmer decrement close to 3.1 2008MNRAS.383..581D . The peak of [ O iii ] has a blueshift about 700 km s ^ 1 relative to the peak of H .

sources.summary: The paper changes this source from steep to flat spectrum, arguing the indexes support a powerful relativistic jet connected to outflows in a superluminous infrared galaxy.

quotes.quote: J1114+3241 B2 1111+32 was classified as S source by Komossa06 . However, our spectral indexes indicate instead a flat spectrum. The broad-band index is also strongly inverted, a behavior observed only in seven of our sources. These indexes strongly support the presence of a powerful relativistic jet in this object, possibly connected with the generation of the powerful mildly relativistic outflows observed in this superluminous infrared galaxy Tombesi15 . The map of Fig. fig:J1114p3241 shows only an unresolved core, whose deconvolved size is 230 200 pc.

sources.summary: The paper includes IRAS F11119+3257 in its wind comparison, using X-ray wind properties and preferring CO energetics while noting OH and CO trace distinct molecular outflow scales.

quotes.quote: IRAS F11119+3257 . Also for this system M_ BH comes from the 5100 luminosity and the \ FWHM from NardiniZubovas18 , and L_ BOL from Veilleux+13 . X-ray wind properties are from Tombesi+15 . Molecular outflows are observed in both OH Tombesi+15 and CO Veilleux+17 but on different physical scales, with the former being confined within 1 from the center while the latter being visible up to larger ( 15 ) distances. It is therefore likely that the two molecules trace distinct outflow episodes. For simplicity we show here only the CO energetics, but that determined for the OH is compatible within the quoted uncertainties.

sources.summary: The paper says IRAS 11119+3257 has blue-shifted high-ionization neon, high radio luminosity, and an extreme [Ne v] profile inconsistent with ordinary rotating narrow-line gas.

quotes.quote: For IRAS 05189--2524 and IRAS 11119+3257 the clear blue shift of both the [ Ne iii ] and [ Ne v ] emission implies that none of the higher ionization gas in these sources has a keplerian component. In Fig. fig4 we plot the FW20 line width of the [ Ne iii ] and [ Ne v ] lines as a function of 1.4 GHz radio luminosity for all AGNs and ULIRGs in our sample. This reveals a clear trend of increasing line width with increasing radio power.

Given the high radio luminosities for IRAS 13451+1232, IRAS F00183--7111, IRAS 11119+3257, IRAS 12127--1412NE, and IRAS 05024--1941, the apparent correlation between radio luminosity and [ Ne iii ] line width in Fig. fig4 may indicate that for these sources interaction of expanding radio jets with the dense ISM surrounding the AGN may explain the observed neon line kinematics. Four of the sources in Fig. fig2 which show outflow signatures in their neon lines do not show the tell-tale mid-infrared signature of AGN activity, the presence of a 14.32 m [ Ne v ] line, in their spectrum. While this may indicate their outflows to be starburst-driven, three of these sources, IRAS F00183--7111, IRAS 05024--1941 and IRAS 12127--1412NE, display strong evidence for the presence of a powerful AGN -- e.g. 1.4 GHz radio fluxes 4--10 times in excess of the value expected for star forming galaxies. In addition, IRAS F00183--7111 and IRAS 12127--1412NE have silicate strengths S _ sil ; spoon07 below -2.5, indicating an apparent silicate optical depth of at least 2.5 toward the nucleus.

The most extreme sources in this subsample do not even exhibit a [ Ne v ] component at systemic velocity. Examples are IZw1, IRAS 05189--2524, IRAS 07598+6508, IRAS 11119+3257 and IRAS 15462--0450. This strongly suggests that at least in these sources the observed [ Ne v ] emission cannot arise in NLR gas in keplerian rotation.

sources.summary: IRASF11119+13257 is used as one of the rare AGN with both X-ray and galaxy-scale molecular winds, including OH-traced outflow data and plausible nuclear-to-galaxy wind mass ratios.

quotes.quote: The blue solid lines are the expectations for energy conserving winds ( P_ OF / P_ AGN v_ UFO /v_ OF ) for the cases of Markarian 231 and IRASF11119+13257, the only two sources for which both X-ray winds and molecular winds have been detected (Tombesi et al. 2015, Feruglio et al. 2015).

So far this has been possible in three AGN only, Markarian231 (Feruglio et al. 2015), IRASF11119+13257 (Tombesi et al. 2015) and APM08279 (Feruglio et al., in preparation). In these three cases the ratio between the gas mass of the nuclear and galaxy scale winds is in the right ballpark, but more observations of this kind are clearly needed before drawing a strong conclusion (see also Stern et al. 2016).
We also added to this sample winds traced by high velocity OH from Sturm et al. (2011; I13120-5453, I14378-3651 and I17208-0014), and Tombesi et al. (2015; IRASF11119+13257).

sources.summary: IRAS 11119+3257 is identified as an NLS1 ULIRG with strong silicate absorption, resembling the optical narrow-line counterpart of Mrk 231 but with about twice the starburst intensity.

quotes.quote: Two of them (5-15 m spectra are shown in Figure fig10 ), are the only objects showing a strong 9.7 m silicate absorption: a NLS1 (IRAS 11119+3257) and a BLS1 (Mrk 231), both characterized by ULIRG infrared emission. Mrk 231 is a well known low-ionization broad absorption line quasar (lo-BAL QSO), with a broad line region optically detectable even if the nucleus is heavily obscured in X-rays (Gallagher et al. 2005). Its MIR spectrum shows not only the silicate absorption related to such an absorbed ULIRG, but also a luminous 6.2 m PAH emission (L(6.2,PAH) 3*10^ 42 erg/s) pointing out a significant starburst, even if not dominant (R= 0.17 10^ 2 ). From the comparison of their low resolution spectra, IRAS 11119+3257 appears as the optical narrow line counterpart of Mrk 231. The only difference being the relative intensity of the Starburst, which in IRAS 11119+3257 is about twice the Mrk 231 values.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS F11119+3257 as a bright source with strong molecular and ultrafast outflows, modeled with an absorbed power law plus weak soft blackbody emission.

quotes.quote: IRAS F11119+3257: Studied by 2015Natur.519..436T who found strong molecular outflows in IR using Herschel PACS as well as ultrafast outflows in the Xrays using Suzaku telescope. 2017ApJ...850..151T observed this source with and detected a similar observed flux as ours. We found that this is a bright source and could be modeled with a simple absorbed powerlaw and a very weak bbody for the soft emission.

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS F11119+3257 as a type 1 quasar with a powerful X-ray UFO and molecular outflow, included in its energy-transfer-rate comparison.

quotes.quote: tom15 reported a powerful X-ray UFO in IRAS F11119+3257 and investigated kinetic energy transformation to the large-scale molecular outflows (also see vei17 ).
fer17 compared outflow parameters of three targets, IRAS F11119+3257, Mrk 231, APM 08279+5255, and argued that types of shock between two outflows are not unique among the three targets.

We analyzed the X-ray (XMM-Newton and Suzaku) archive data of the targets that the molecular outflows are detected in IRAM/PdBI observations listed in cic14 , and derived the energy-transfer rates for six Seyfert galaxies (plus type 1 quasar IRAS F11119+3257).

sources.summary: The paper says IRAS F11119+3257 has a large-scale outflow whose west-offset blue and red wings suggest a somewhat lopsided, possibly recent fossil.

quotes.quote: IRAS F11119+3257 has a large-scale outflow that has been interpreted both as energy-conserving Tombesi2015Natur and as significantly weaker Veilleux2017ApJ, Nardini2018MNRAS . Figure 5 in Veilleux2017ApJ shows both blue and red wings are offset to the west of the nucleus, suggesting that the outflow is at least somewhat lopsided and hence could be a (recent) fossil. This lopsidedness may also explain why the outflow has rather low M 100 yr ^ 1 , despite having a high velocity v 1000 km s ^ 1 : if only a few of the sightlines from the nucleus contain outflowing material, the total solid angle is small.

gmw_00002514.name: MCG-03-58-007
gmw_00002514.overview: MCG-03-58-007 is a nearby Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1812.01139] that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1906.00985, arXiv:1812.01139] and exhibits weak star-forming activity [arXiv:1812.01139]. It features a large rotating molecular disk [arXiv:1906.00985] and a central low-velocity molecular outflow [arXiv:1906.00985, arXiv:2009.11294].

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA CO observations of MCG-03-58-007, finding a galaxy-scale rotating molecular disk and central residuals consistent with a low-velocity, momentum-driven molecular outflow.

quotes.quote: We report the first Atacama large millimeter/submillimeter array observations of MCG-03-58-007, a local ( z=0.03236 0.00002 , this work) AGN ( L_ AGN 10^ 45 erg s^ 1 ), hosting a powerful X-ray ultra-fast ( v=0.1c ) outflow (UFO). The CO(1-0) line emission is observed across 18 kpc scales with a resolution of 1 kpc . About 78 of the CO(1-0) luminosity traces a galaxy-size rotating disk. However, after subtracting the emission due to such rotating disk, we detect with a S/N=20 a residual emission in the central 4 kpc.

However, the model does not properly describe the complex CO(1-0) kinematics in the central region of MCG-03-58-007. The data-to-model residuals show a compact structure (within 2 kpc from the galaxy centre) with a 20 significance, whose emission shows two symmetric peaks at v 170 km/s and v +170 km/s . This could be the signature of a low velocity molecular outflow. Under this hypothesis, we applied a simple biconical model and estimated the LOS velocity ( v=170 4 km/s ), mass-loss rate ( M _ out 54 M_ yr^ 1 ), momentum rate ( P _ out 8 10^ 34 g cm s^ 2 ), and kinetic power ( E _ out 1.0 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 ) of such putative molecular outflow.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes a deep observation of the starburst-AGN Seyfert 2 MCG--03--58--007, favoring toroidal obscuration plus a variable ionized disc wind over neutral clump variability.

quotes.quote: We report the results of a detailed analysis of a deep simultaneous 130 ks observation of the nearby ( z=0.0315 ) and bright ( L_ bol 3 10^ 45 ) starburst-AGN Seyfert 2 system: MCG--03--58--007. From the broadband fitting we show that most of the obscuration needs to be modeled with a toroidal type reprocessor such as MYTorus MurphyYaqoob09 . Nonetheless the signature of a powerful disc-wind is still apparent at higher energies and the observed rapid short-term X-ray spectral variability is more likely caused by a variable zone of highly ionized fast wind rather than by a neutral clumpy medium. We also detect X-ray emission from larger scale gas as seen from the presence of several soft narrow emission lines in the RGS, originating from a contribution of a weak star forming activity together with a dominant photoionized component from the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper includes MCG-03-58-007 in its wind comparison, inferring black-hole and bolometric inputs, using the persistent slow X-ray wind component, and adopting CO outflow properties.

quotes.quote: MCG-03-58-007 . As before, M_ BH is inferred from the M_ BH using the \ velocity dispersion 185 , Braito+18 as a proxy for . The L_ BOL is bracketed by the two values quoted in Braito+18 , determined using either \ or IR luminosities. From the same work we take the properties of the X-ray wind, for which we consider only the slowest of the two components observed, which is the only one persisting in different observations. The properties of the CO outflows are taken from Sirressi+19 .

gmw_00002515.name: SGAS J003341.5 + 024217
gmw_00002515.overview: SGAS J003341.5 + 024217 hosts a young stellar population estimated to be 5 Myr old [arXiv:1905.04314]. Although the galaxy lacks obvious optical active galactic nucleus diagnostics, near-infrared nuclear line ratios and broad hydrogen-alpha wings implicate it as hosting a weak active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1710.07294].

sources.summary: S0033+0242 is treated as a young stellar population with a 5 Myr age, broad triangular emission, and undetected 1299 photospheric absorption.

quotes.quote: This trough-to-peak ratio decreases with increasing inferred stellar population age in fig:nv : it is 2.2 for S0033+0242 with an age of 5 Myr and 1.4 for S1429-1202 with an age of 15 Myr. The Si 4 P-Cygni feature P-Cygni is generally observed in supergiants (evolved stars) or in metal-rich stars because the dominant ionization state in main-sequence stellar winds is the Si ^ 4+ state. Thus, the Si ^ +++ ionization state is too low of a temperature to trace the bulk of the O-star wind the opposite behavior as \ above; walborn84 .

Conversely, the youngest populations (Sunburst Arc, RCS Knot E, S0033+0242, and RCS Knot U) have a broad triangular shaped \ emission profile (see the arrows in fig:heii ). The \ emission resembles the redshifted triangular emission profile but without the blueshifted absorption component that creates the P-Cygni profile. There is not \ absorption because 1640 \ is not a resonant transition (it is analogous to H ), therefore the stellar wind is optically thin to the \ recombination emission.

Similarly, the other three \ galaxies with ages less than 6 Myr (RCS Knot E with an age of 2 Myr, S0033+0242 with an age of 5 Myr, and RCS Knot U with an age of 5 Myr) have undetected 1299 \ (equivalent widths of 0.12 0.21 , 0.05 0.07 , and 0.08 0.17 , respectively). Very young populations do not contain the 1299 \ photospheric feature.

sources.summary: The paper says SGAS J003341.5 + 024217 lacks obvious MagE AGN diagnostics, but VLT/SINFONI nuclear line ratios and broad H wings implicate it as a weak AGN.

quotes.quote: The MagE spectrum of SGAS J003341.5 + 024217 does not show obvious AGN diagnostics. However, a VLT/SINFONI observation shows that the nucleus exhibits high-ionization [ O III ] 5007 / H and H / [ N II ] ratios, and also broad wings in H (E. Wuyts, private communication.) It is thus implicated as having a weak AGN.

gmw_00002516.name: SGAS J224324.2 093508
gmw_00002516.overview: SGAS J224324.2 093508 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy [arXiv:1710.07294]. Hubble Space Telescope imaging reveals that it features a spatially extended host galaxy with a central point source [arXiv:1710.07294].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SGAS J224324.2 093508 as one of two sample galaxies with AGN evidence; its MagE spectrum shows broad lines and HST imaging shows an extended host with a central point source.

quotes.quote: Contribution from Active Galactic Nuclei Two galaxies in our sample show evidence of active galactic nuclei (AGN). The MagE spectrum of SGAS J224324.2 093508 shows broad emission lines of Lyman , N V, C IV, and [ C III ] , that together indicate that this galaxy hosts a broad-line AGN. The HST imagery (Figure fig:where_pointed ) shows a spatially extended host galaxy with a central point source.

gmw_00002517.name: 3XMM J150052.0+015452
gmw_00002517.overview: 3XMM J150052.0+015452 is a dwarf starburst galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.145 [arXiv:1702.00792, arXiv:2112.11545]. High-resolution imaging reveals that the host galaxy is composed of a dominant disk and a smaller bulge [arXiv:2112.11545].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J150052.07+015453.8 as a dwarf starburst galaxy hosting a long, luminous nuclear X-ray flare best explained as a tidal disruption event near its central black hole.

quotes.quote: Our follow-up observation of the source with Chandra on February 23rd 2015 provided a well constrained X-ray position coincident with the center of the galaxy SDSS J150052.07+015453.8, to within 0 18 (Figure 1, see SI ). The galaxy lies at a redshift of 0.145, or a luminosity distance of D_L=689 Mpc (for H_0 =70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _ M =0.3 , _ = 0.7 ), with strong emission lines indicative of intense star-forming activity. It has a total stellar mass of 6 10^9 \ (see SI ), comparable to that of the Large Magellanic Cloud. For such a small galaxy, we expect the central supermassive black hole (SMBH) to have mass 10^6 .

No sign of persistent nuclear activity is seen in the optical emission lines of the host galaxy, whose ratios are fully consistent with those expected from a starburst galaxy. There are many other properties of the source that argue against the possibility that it is a standard active galactic nucleus (AGN, see SI ). In particular, no AGN is known to show X-ray spectra as soft as XJ1500+0154 within the 1--4.5 keV energy band or show dramatic quasi-soft to super-soft X-ray spectral change.

sources.summary: The paper links 3XMM J150052.0+015452/XJ1500+0154 to the nucleus of a dwarf star-forming host galaxy, resolving the host into a dominant disk and smaller bulge with HST.

quotes.quote: However, the very special TDE candidate 3XMM J150052.0+015452 (XJ1500+0154 hereafter) stood out in showing a unique spectral evolution that had never been seen before Lin17 hereafter liguko2017 . It is a prolonged TDE candidate coincident with the nucleus of a dwarf star-forming galaxy at z=0.14542 ( D_L=689 Mpc, Lin17).

Host Galaxy Imaging The two HST images of the host galaxy of XJ1500+0154 are shown in the left panels in Figure fig:hstimg . This dwarf star-forming galaxy seems to have a disk and a bulge, and the X-ray emission is consistent with emanating from the center of the galaxy, based on the X-ray position from C10. We therefore fitted the profile of the host galaxy with two S e rsic functions, one for the disk and the other for the bulge. The fitting residuals are shown in the right panels in Figure fig:hstimg , which indicate good fits. Table tbl:galfit lists the fitting results.

gmw_00002518.name: GS3
gmw_00002518.overview: GS3 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy with a total stellar mass of 3.70 × 10⁸ solar masses [arXiv:2207.10708]. Its far-ultraviolet disk is intrinsically more extended than its optical disk, containing large star-forming clumps that each comprise about one percent of the galaxy's total mass [arXiv:2207.10708].

sources.summary: The paper highlights GS3 as a blue compact dwarf whose FUV disk is more extended than its optical disk; its large FUV clumps have star formation and masses about one percent of the galaxy.

quotes.quote: The fitted intrinsic profiles of GS3, GS6, and eight others (except GS12 and GS14) are shallower in the FUV than in the optical, implying the FUV disks are intrinsically more extended than the optical, not just apparently extended because of the broad PSF. FUV measurements were not possible due to non-detection in GS8; GS9 was too compact to model. For GS3 and GS6, the intrinsic FUV scale lengths are 1.4 and 2.4 times their respective optical scale-lengths (see Table 1).

For GS3, the large FUV clumps have SFR of a few 10^ 2 M_ yr ^ 1 . Considering a 100 Myr timescale of FUV emission, their masses would be a few 10^6 M_ (Table 4). This is 1 of the galaxy's total stellar mass, M_ * =3.70 10^8 M_ (Table 5).

gmw_00002519.name: GS6
gmw_00002519.overview: GS6 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy characterized by an extended far-ultraviolet disk [arXiv:2207.10708]. This disk is intrinsically 2.4 times larger than its optical counterpart, hosting young stellar populations and active star formation in its outer regions [arXiv:2207.10708].

sources.summary: The paper presents GS6 as a blue compact dwarf with an extended FUV disk, XUV star-formation surface density and gas density estimates, and young stellar-population ages from UV colors.

quotes.quote: The fitted intrinsic profiles of GS3, GS6, and eight others (except GS12 and GS14) are shallower in the FUV than in the optical, implying the FUV disks are intrinsically more extended than the optical, not just apparently extended because of the broad PSF. FUV measurements were not possible due to non-detection in GS8; GS9 was too compact to model. For GS3 and GS6, the intrinsic FUV scale lengths are 1.4 and 2.4 times their respective optical scale-lengths (see Table 1).

For the average intrinsic surface brightness in the XUV region of GS3 and GS6, the SFRDs are 1.82 0.43 10^ 5 M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 , and 2.25 0.48 10^ 4 M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 respectively. These values would be 10 lower at 0.4Z _ because the FUV emission of a 100 Myr old population is 1.1 times brighter . Our SFRD estimates are comparable to previous measurements that reach 10 ^ 5 10 ^ 6 M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 (Figure 3a) in low-density, extreme environments, e.g., galaxy outskirts or stripped gas tails within galaxy clusters . If the FUV SFRD follows the Kennicutt relation , it would suggest star-formation at an average gas surface density of less than 1 M _ pc ^ 2 . For a steeper slope of the Kennicutt relation as seen in the XUV region of M63 , our SFRDs probe gas (HI+H _ 2 ) surface densities (independent of metallicity because the molecules are fully observed in M63) of 2.9 and 4.9 M _ pc ^ 2 for GS3 and GS6 respectively.

In the case of an instantaneous burst at Z _ , the ages are 7.1 Myr and 12.3 Myr for GS3 and GS6, respectively, whereas for a continuous star-formation history, they are 106 Myr and 306 Myr old (the stellar populations are older by a factor of 2 at 0.4 Z _ ).

gmw_00002520.name: GS14
gmw_00002520.overview: GS14 is classified as a blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2207.10708]. It exhibits excess far-ultraviolet emission despite having a compact far-ultraviolet half-light radius, and contains a clump with an estimated inspiral time of 2.4 Gyr [arXiv:2207.10708].

sources.summary: The paper treats GS14 as a blue compact dwarf with excess FUV emission despite a compact FUV half-light radius, and estimates a 2.4 Gyr inspiral time for clump C14A.

quotes.quote: GS12 and GS14 have R_ h, FUV /R_ h, opt < 1 but L_ x >0 .
For the clump in GS14 (z=0.1), C14A, T_ inspiral 2.4 Gyr.
_ XUV is several Hubble times for most BCDs (except for GS3: 6.6 Gyr and GS14: 3.7 Gyr; Table 5).

gmw_00002521.name: NGC6297
gmw_00002521.overview: NGC6297 is a lenticular S0 galaxy that may have originated as a post-merger remnant [arXiv:2405.16107]. Its structure features a multi-component disk with both inner and outer components, and it hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus at its center [arXiv:2405.16107].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC6297 as an S0 host with possible post-merger origin, multi-component disk structure, and a low-luminosity AGN that may relate to AT2020ohl.

quotes.quote: However, analysis of pre-transient Swift /XRT observations reveal a faint X-ray luminosity 3.4 10^ 41 erg s^ 1 , which points to the existence of a low-luminous AGN (LLAGN) at the centre of NGC6297 ( 2022ApJ...930...12H and references therein).

We found that along with the Sersic profile, two exponential profiles are required to model the surface intensity distribution of NGC6297. The value of the reduced chi-square ( ^2 ) for the `Sersic and `single-exponential' profile is 12.3, while that for the `Sersic and double-exponential' profile is 0.9. This implies that the disk of NGC6297 may have two components an inner disk with a steeper intensity profile and an outer disk with shallower intensity profile.

It is noticeable that NGC6297 is an S0 galaxy which may be a post-merger remnant. The presence of an extended galaxy exponential disk profile in this system ( sec:host ) strengthens the post-merging scenario.

gmw_00002522.name: Mrk 33
gmw_00002522.overview: Mrk 33 is classified as a blue compact dwarf [arXiv:1009.2519] and a dwarf irregular starburst galaxy [arXiv:0806.1020] that contains a compact, rotating polar disk of ionized gas [arXiv:1009.2519]. The galaxy features active star-forming regions [arXiv:2311.16950], with a large fraction of its dust heating occurring in photodissociation regions near OB associations [arXiv:astro-ph/0608003].

sources.summary: The paper studies Mrk 33 as a nearby galaxy with two slit-aligned clusters, one detected He II source, and He II reddening higher than UV and Balmer estimates.

quotes.quote: For this study, we selected star-forming regions in eight nearby galaxies ( 0.0010 z 0.0095 , at distances of 3-37 Mpc) with existing He II 1640 HST-STIS UV observations from the Mikulski Archive for Space Telescopes (MAST). These UV spectra were previously obtained for He 2-10, NGC 3125, Mrk 33, NGC 4214, NGC 4670, and Tol 1924-416 as part of program 9036 (PI: C. Leitherer) taken in 2001–2003, and for NGC 3049 and Tol 89 as part of program 7513 (PI: C. Leitherer) observed in 1999-2000. Additional optical HST-STIS observations, providing He II 4686 line measurements were obtained from the HST program GO-15846 (PI: C. Leitherer), executed in 2020-21. These optical observations were carried out at the exact same location, same orientation, and with the same aperture sizes as the UV observations.

Mrk 33   This galaxy has two bright star clusters aligned within the slit but only source B has detected He II lines. The estimate of E(B-V)_ Balmer was done with Balmer lines measured by SDSS with an aperture ( 3^ ) covering both sources. The UV and Balmer measurements suggest the absence of dust, whereas the He II measurement deviates from this with a dust attenuation of E(B-V)_ He II = 0.17 0.06 mag . By studying the morphology of Source B in Figure fig:slit_alignment , we find that the cluster itself has no strong H emission, indicating that star-formation feedback has already pushed away dust and gas.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 33 as a dwarf irregular starburst case study, fitting its multiwavelength SED and placing it at the high-activity, warmer-dust end of the sequence.

quotes.quote: To investigate the implications of these fits for the determination of physical parameters, we focus on three galaxies spanning different dust properties along the / versus / colour-colour relation in Fig. irx_beta a, for which a wide set of observations are available: NGC 3521, a spiral (SABbc) galaxy at a distance of 9 Mpc; NGC 337, a spiral (SBd) galaxy at a distance of 24.7 Mpc; and Mrk 33, a dwarf irregular (Im) starburst galaxy at a distance of 21.7 Mpc. For all three galaxies, observations from GALEX ( FUV and NUV ), 2MASS ( JHKs ), Spitzer (IRAC and MIPS), IRAS and SCUBA are available, along with integrated \ and \ spectroscopy from . For NGC 3521 and NGC 337, additional constraints are available from RC3 ( UBV ) and ISO (ISOCAM 6.75 and 15 ) photometry. Fig. seds2 shows the models (in black) providing the best fits to the observed ultraviolet, optical, \ and , and infrared spectral energy distributions of these three galaxies (in red). The quality of the fits is remarkable, as quantified by the residuals shown at the bottom of each spectrum.

We find that, as anticipated from Fig. seds2 , the transition from NGC 3521 to NGC 337 to Mrk 33 corresponds to a combined rise in specific star formation rate ( ) and dust temperature ( \ and ), a larger contribution to the total infrared luminosity by warm dust ( ) relative to cold dust ( ), and by stellar birth clouds relative to the ambient ISM ( ), and a weakening of PAH features ( ). These results are consistent with the finding by , on the basis of a more sophisticated physical dust model, that the intensity of the dust-heating starlight increases, and the fraction of dust mass contributed by PAHs decreases, from NGC 3521 to NGC 337 to Mrk 33.

sources.summary: The review highlights Haro 2 as a dustier, metal-richer blue compact galaxy with strong Ly-alpha emission enabled by blueshifted neutral gas and related outflow signatures.

quotes.quote: In contrast to the strongly absorbing dwarfs the second BCG with a published HST \ spectrum, Haro 2, was found to emit a strong \ line with 7 \ emission part only, right panel of Figure fig:dwarfs Lequeux1995 . This is particularly curious because Haro 2 is an order of magnitude more dust- and metal-rich than the dwarf galaxies. Comparing with the absorbing BCDs discussed in Section sect:bcd , the total column density of hydrogen along the line-of-sight is roughly the same, but with two important differences. Firstly, much less of that hydrogen column density is contributed by the neutral phase, although the measured column of \ 10^ 20 \ would still be sufficient to cause a damped absorption. Secondly, the absorption centroid of \ is blue-shifted by 200 \ relative to the systemic frame of rest of the galaxy.

The LIS lines intrinsic to Haro 2 were also found to be blue-shifted with respect to the systemic velocity and by the same velocity as measured from the \ absorption ( 200 ). The peak of the \ emission, however, is instead redshifted by 350 , or roughly twice the blueshift of the neutral medium.

sources.summary: The review presents Haro 2 as a dusty star-forming galaxy with P-Cygni Ly emission, blueshifted absorbing gas, and H evidence for an expanding shell.

quotes.quote: To add to the confusion, a Ly \ emission line showing a complicated profile, but a clear P Cygni component, has been detected in Haro 2, a dustier star-forming galaxy with a Z=1/3 Z_ ( ). The detection of such a profile in the Ly \ emission line of Haro 2 led to postulate that the line was visible because the absorbing neutral gas was velocity--shifted with respect to the ionized gas. This was confirmed by the analysis of the UV O I and Si II absorption lines (blue--shifted by 200 km s ^ 1 \ with respect to the optical emission lines) and that of the profile of the H line ( ).

On the other hand, the Ly \ emission in Haro 2 is accompanied by a broad absorption in the blue wing of the line, with the general appearance of a typical P Cygni profile. The amount of neutral gas that produces the blue absorption trough at Ly \ is rather modest and of the order of N (H I ) = 7.7x 10^ 19 atoms cm ^ 2 . The crucial point here is that the neutral gas responsible for the absorption is not at the velocity at which the Ly \ photons were emitted.
Legrand et al., have obtained high resolution spectroscopic observations of Haro 2 in H with the WHT at La Palma, finding evidence for an expanding shell.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 33 as a blue compact dwarf whose ionized gas forms a stable polar disk likely linked to captured gas or a merger.

quotes.quote: Our analysis shows that all ionized gas in Mrk 33 is concentrated in a compact disk (3 kpc in diameter) which rotates in the polar plane relative to the main stellar body.

The velocity field of the ionized gas can be approximated by the model of a circular rotating thin disk. The major axis of this disk differs significantly from the major axis of the outer isophotes. The major axis position angle and inclination derived from the ionized gas velocity field are PA=163 4^ and i=47 5^ , respectively.

Based on the low-resolution kinematic data in HI, Bravo-Alfaro et al. also suggested that Mrk 33 has recently captured gas in a close interaction or merger with an initially gas-rich companion. Our observations show that the inner polar ionized gas disk has a similar kinematics with the external HI structure.

sources.summary: SMA CO observations of Haro 2 map a dwarf starburst with molecular gas in embedded starburst clumps plus extended emission coincident with an X-ray outflow bubble.

quotes.quote: SMA Observations of Haro 2: Molecular Gas around a Hot Superbubble Sara Beck becksarac@gmail.com 0000-0002-5770-8494 Sara C. Beck School of Physics and Astronomy, Tel Aviv University, Ramat Aviv, Israel Pei-Ying Hsieh Academica Sinica, Institute of Astronomy and Astrophysics, P.O. Box 23-141, Taipei 10617, Taiwan 0000-0003-4625-2951 Jean Turner Department of Physics and Astronomy, UCLA, Los Angeles, CA 90095-1547 \ , a nearby dwarf starburst dwarf galaxy with strong Ly emission, hosts a starburst that has created outflows and filaments. The clear evidence for galactic outflow makes it an ideal candidate for studying the role of molecular gas in feedback processes in a dwarf galaxy. We observed CO(2-1) in \ at the Submillimeter Array in the compact and extended configurations, and have mapped the molecular emission with velocity resolution 4.1 and spatial resolution 2.0 1.6 . With this significant increase of resolution over previous measurements we see that the molecular gas comprises two components: bright clumps associated with the embedded star clusters of the starburst, and fainter extended emission east of the starburst region. The extended emission coincides with an X-ray bubble and has the kinematic signatures of an outflowing cone or of an expanding shell or bubble; the velocity range is 35 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Mark 33 as an infrared dust-model example, deriving a high 24 micron contribution and inferring substantial dust heating in photodissociation regions near OB associations.

quotes.quote: Mark 33: From Figure fig:IRAC4/MIPS band ratios e, we estimate 3.2 . The very high value of P_ 24 =0.46 requires 7 and 0.14 on Figure fig:MIPS band ratios e in order to have enough hot dust to reproduce the observed 24 \ flux. The very large value found for suggests that a large fraction of the dust heating in Mark 33 is taking place in photodissociation regions near OB associations, consistent with the dwarf starburst nature of this galaxy Hunt+Bianchi+Maiolino_2005 .

gmw_00002523.name: PG 0953+414
gmw_00002523.overview: PG 0953+414 is a low-redshift, radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:2212.06183] with a very faint host galaxy of uncertain morphology [arXiv:2102.09190]. The active nucleus features an associated absorption-line system [arXiv:1307.6799] and exhibits a collimated radio jet extending a few kiloparsecs in the east-west direction [arXiv:astro-ph/0606540].

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 0953+414 as a low-redshift radio-quiet quasar with an absorbed power-law spectrum, soft excess, steep continuum, and undetected Iron K line.

quotes.quote: PG 0953+414 (z=0.234) is a low-redshift radio-quiet quasar observed for the first time by the Advanced Satellite for Cosmology and Astrophysics (ASCA). It showed a spectrum well fitted by an absorbed power-law model with a photon index 2.03 0.05 2000ApJ...531...52G . Analysing a observations, 2004A&A...422...85P found that the broad-band continuum could be well fitted either by a broken power-law model or by a double Comptonization model. The source showed the presence of a soft excess and the 2-5 keV spectrum was well fitted by an absorbed power-law model with =2.04 0.11 .

PG 0953+414 The first fit with the phenomenological Model A ( ^2 /dof=596/494) resulted in a steep continuum, =2.55 0.03 with a high energy cut-off E_c= 85_ 29 ^ +118 keV. We found an upper limit for the reflection component: R_ refl <2.67 0.08 . Since does not have a sufficient effective area below 10 keV and Swift /XRT spectrum is background dominated above 3 keV, it was not possible to detect the Iron K emission line.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PG 0953+415 as a quasar sightline, detecting seven BLA candidates, giving the corrected redshift path and S/N, and noting no associated O VI absorption.

quotes.quote: Towards PG 0953+415 we detect seven BLA candidates at z=0.02108 , 0.04382 , 0.09315 , 0.10294 , 0.17984 , 0.19142 , and 0.19360 in the available wavelength range between 1220.0 and 1485.9 ( z=0.219 ). After applying a blocking correction, the total redshift path for finding BLAs towards PG 0953+415 reduces to z=0.202 . The systems at z=0.02108 , 0.10294 , and 0.19142 are tentative detections (see Table 2, last column for a more detailed description of these lines).

The S/N in the PG 0953+415 spectrum is 12 , implying a typical detection limit for broad Ly absorption of log (N/b) 11.4 (equation 4). The systems at z=0.09315 , 0.17984 , 0.19142 , and 0.19360 have been previously identified as H i Ly by Savage et al. (2002). None of the broad Ly candidates towards PG 0953+415 shows associated O vi absorption; the two O vi absorbers identified by Savage et al. (2002) are both multiphase absorbers with H i Lyman series absorption having b<40 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: PG 0953+415 has an associated absorber previously reported in HST spectra, detected in Lyman-series and doublet transitions consistent with fully occulting the quasar engine.

quotes.quote: PG 0953+415 ( =0.2335) -- This associated absorption-line system, previously reported by kpxiii and gan01a in spectra from the HST Faint Object Spectrograph (HST/FOS), is detected only in the Lyman series, and the 1031.926, 1037.617 doublet. [ kpxiii only reported detection of Ly , while gan01a reported detection of Ly \ and the 1548.204, 1550.781 doublet. ] tripp08 also provide measurement of the absorption lines in this system. Figure fig:pg0953 shows velocity-aligned flux profiles of the transitions (left) and an evaluation of velocity-dependent partial coverage (right) in the doublet.

sources.summary: The paper finds the PG 0953+414 host very faint relative to its nucleus, fit only by a fixed-index single-component model with uncertain morphology.

quotes.quote: PG 0953+414: The host galaxy is very faint compared to the nucleus. We can obtain a useful fit only with a single-component model with a fixed \ index. The best fit favors n=1 . We deem the morphology too uncertain to be classified.

sources.summary: The paper finds PG0953+414 elongated east-west in radio emission, lacking matching [O iii] extent, and interprets the structure as a few-kpc collimated radio jet.

quotes.quote: PG0953+414 . This quasar is elongated in the east--west direction in the radio regime (Fig. 4, right). In the corresponding [ O iii ] emission--line image there is no significant extent, especially not in the direction of the radio extension (Fig. 4, left). The radio structure seen with the A--Array does not appear on a B--Array map where the source is unresolved at 4.8 GHz. Thus, a radio jet seems to be directed to the east--west causing extended but collimated radio emission on scales of a few kpc.

gmw_00002524.name: PG 1012+008
gmw_00002524.overview: PG 1012+008 is an interacting quasar system undergoing a merger of two galaxies of near-equal brightness [arXiv:astro-ph/0606540, arXiv:2102.09190]. The system features a perturbed interstellar medium, a southern radio jet, and a small companion galaxy projected to the north [arXiv:astro-ph/0606540, arXiv:2102.09190].

sources.summary: The paper treats PG1012+008 as an interacting quasar system with perturbed gas, a southern radio jet, matching optical isophotes, and uncertain merger effects on the structure.

quotes.quote: PG1012+008 . This quasar is part of an interacting system (Bahcall et al. ). The interstellar medium is perturbed and it is difficult to say whether the structures are caused by the merger or by the radio--jet itself. The radio source consists of a nuclear source with a jet to the south (see Fig. 2, right). The jet seems to bend to the east as indicated by comparing the inner and outer isophotes.

The same "bended" structure is apparent in the optical image where the inner isophotes display the shape of the radio source (Fig. 2, left).
