This indicates that the radio jet and the NLR gas are intimately connected. However, it is not clear what effect the galaxy interaction has on the apparent structures, especially on the "bending".

sources.summary: PG 1012+008 is described as a near-equal-brightness merging system with a modeled AGN host, interacting companion, and small northern companion.

quotes.quote: PG 1012+008: This is an obvious merging system of two, near-equal brightness galaxies, with another possible small companion projected to the north. The host galaxy of the AGN can be fit with two \ components modified by an m = 1 Fourier mode. A similar model is used for the main interacting companion. The small northern companion is fit simultaneously with a single \ component. We masked the northern companion when calculating the nonparametric magnitude of the system.

gmw_00002525.name: PG 1103 006
gmw_00002525.overview: PG 1103 006 is a lobe-dominated, radio-loud quasar [arXiv:2212.07061, arXiv:astro-ph/0505625] that exhibits an X-shaped radio structure with S-shaped jets [arXiv:2212.07061]. The host galaxy has a faint projected companion to its west [arXiv:2102.09190] and shows signs of possible intra-night variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0505625].

sources.summary: The paper describes PG1103 006 as an X-shaped radio quasar with S-shaped jets, extensive wing polarization, jet-precession hints, and magnetic fields consistent with hydrodynamical backflow.

quotes.quote: PG1103 006 This RL quasar ( aka 4C -00.43 ) is an X-shaped radio source (Figure fig6.1 , fig6 ). The jets appear to be S-shaped, especially going North, which could be consistent with jet precession. This quasar has been observed previously by the VLA by Hutchings1998 who did not detect the X-shape as prominently.

The inferred B-field structure in the north-western jet stays aligned with the jet throughout, all the way to the north-western hotspot. The inferred B-field in the radio core is transverse to the jet direction for the optically thick core. The inferred B-field appears to be be bow-shock-like in both the hotspots, as seen in the southern hotspot of III Zw 2.

Extensive polarization is observed in the X-shaped wings. Although the inferred B-fields are aligned with the direction of the wings (see Figure fig6.1 ) , the vectors do not follow the flow ``around the corner'' unambiguously.

sources.summary: The PG 1103 006 host is modeled with a single free-index component, while a faint western projected companion is fit separately and other sources are masked.

quotes.quote: PG 1103 006: We fit the host galaxy with a single \ model with n set free. A faint projected companion to the west of the quasar is fit simultaneously. Other sources in the field are masked.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1103-006 as the sample lobe-dominated quasar and finds its April 2000 light curves indicate possible intra-night variation.

quotes.quote: 1103-006 (PKS 1103-006) This is the only lobe dominated quasar in our sample.

From the DLCs of the source, the standard deviations are 0.026 and 0.020 respectively for DLCs qso - s1 and qso - s2. Thus the variance for the source is about 3 time the variance of the standards, indicating the possible existence of intra-night variation.

gmw_00002526.name: PG 1444+407
gmw_00002526.overview: PG 1444+407 is a radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:0808.0918] featuring a peculiar associated absorption-line system [arXiv:1307.6799]. Its host galaxy is a spiral system [arXiv:0808.0918] that contains a disk and an elongated bulge, which may represent a bar [arXiv:2102.09190].

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1444+407 as a radio-quiet quasar with a smooth elongated host, possible spiral/bar morphology, high radio-quiet-host velocity dispersion, [OII] emission, and ongoing star formation.

quotes.quote: PG 1444+407 PG 1444+407 (labeled 7 in our plots) is a radio-quiet quasar with M _ V = 25.3 and L _ 5 GHz =1.8 x 10 ^ 39 erg s ^ 1 . The QSO spectrum is similar to that of 4C 31.63, showing strong Fe II emission, broad Balmer lines and no detectable forbidden emission-lines. The host galaxy, at M _ V = 22.7, appears smooth and elongated in the north-south direction in the HST images of Bahcall. They suggest the host galaxy has the appearance of an E1, although the light profile is better fit by an exponential disk. It is classified as a spiral galaxy by Hamilton et al. and a bar may be present hutchings92,bahcall97 .

sources.summary: PG 1444+407 has a peculiar associated absorber whose profiles do not trace each other, with kinematically distinct absorption and no partial-coverage evidence.

quotes.quote: PG 1444+407 ( =0.2673) -- This associated absorption-line system, previously reported by tripp08 , is detected in Ly , and the 1031.926, 1037.617 doublet (Figure fig:pg1444 ). The system is peculiar in the sense that the and profiles do not trace each other. The appears in a single component that is offset from the , which appears in two discrete components. Ly \ is covered by the spectra, but is not detected as expected based on the strength of the Ly \ line, assuming there are no partial coverage effects. If partial coverage of the were present, then the Ly \ transition would be stronger (potentially as strong as Ly ).

sources.summary: The PG 1444+407 host requires two components interpreted as bulge plus disk, though the elongated low-index bulge may resemble a bar.

quotes.quote: PG 1444+407: The host galaxy requires two components, which together can be interpreted to be a bulge plus disk system. However, the bulge has an obviously elongated morphology and a \ index suspiciously close to that of a bar ( n 0.2 ). For the sake of concreteness, we attribute it to the bulge.

gmw_00002527.name: PG 1613+658
gmw_00002527.overview: PG 1613+658 is an unabsorbed Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:2306.17334, arXiv:2403.13194] that exhibits large-amplitude optical continuum variability [arXiv:2307.02630] and contains a flared, disk-like hot dust structure [arXiv:2302.01678, arXiv:2309.15931]. The host galaxy is a late-stage merger featuring an eastern tidal tail [arXiv:1311.1978], a possible second nucleus [arXiv:2102.09190], and a nearby companion [arXiv:1311.1978, arXiv:2102.09190].

sources.summary: The paper uses PG 1613+658, a Seyfert I near the north ecliptic pole, to compare long TESS and ASAS-SN power spectra and finds one optical break frequency.

quotes.quote: For this analysis, we used PG 1613+658, a Seyfert I galaxy near the North ecliptic pole observed over Sectors 14 to 26 during the primary mission. The combined light curve is shown in Figure pg1613+658lc . We constructed the PSD for the combined TESS light curve using the same procedures to examine if the low-frequency regime of the combined TESS PSDs is consistent with those measured by ASAS-SN, up to a normalization difference. To do so, we estimated and subtracted the white noise of each PSD. We compare the PSDs of the continuous TESS light curve, the PSDs of ASAS-SN V-band and g-band light curves, and the average of the 13 PSDs for the individual TESS sectors in Figure pg1613+658psd , finding that the TESS and ASAS-SN PSDs are consistent in the overlapping regions and have similar slopes at low frequencies.
Only one break frequency is found at ( _ br / day ^ 1 )=-1.02 0.09 which is within the typical range of _ br,hig of the AGN sample.

Another target in the high frequency break sample, PG 1613+658, the Seyfert I galaxy with 13 continuous TESS sectors discussed earlier, was not included in these fits because it does not have a reported X-ray break frequency. It does have a reverberation mapping black hole mass measurement of (M_ BH /M_ )=8.38^ +0.25 _ 0.16 kaspi00 . Using the break frequency from the PSD of the continuous TESS light curve and the M_ BH _ br relationship (Equation mbh-nubr ), we get (M_ BH /M_ )=6.62^ +0.99 _ 1.01 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 876 as a high-luminosity AGN whose hot-dust monitoring revealed multiple components and a flared dusty disk geometry with an enlarged inner hole.

quotes.quote: Most recently, L23 presented results from a near-IR spectroscopic monitoring campaign on the high-luminosity AGN Mrk 876. The comparison of the mean and variable (rms) spectrum clearly showed that at least two hot dust components are present in AGN, with the second component possibly originating in the accretion disk. However, contrary to the case of NGC 5548, the independent measure of the dust radius via reverberation mapping yielded a value a factor of 2 lower than the luminosity-based dust radius estimate, indicating that the anisotropy effects of the accretion disk illumination and through it the dust geometry can be detected and studied in this way. For Mrk 876, it was concluded that the geometry is most likely a flared, dusty disk with an enlarged 'inner hole', which is very similar to the paradigm commonly assumed for protoplanetary disks.

A first indication that a considerable inconsistency can be present between the luminosity-based hot dust radius and that obtained by other means was found for the high-luminosity AGN Mrk 876 by L23 . The difference between the two values of a factor of 2 could be well explained if the dust was assembled in a flared, disk-like geometry, which is naturally expected to be carved out by the anisotropy of the accretion disk illumination Kaw10, Kaw11 .

Recently, L23 presented the comparison of the mean and variable (rms) spectrum in the high-luminosity AGN Mrk 876, which showed clearly that at least two hot dust components are present in AGN. They found that the total dust emission was dominated (at the 70 level) by the non-variable component, which could have its origin in the outer (flared) part of the dusty disk or the outer dusty edge of the accretion disk or both.

sources.summary: The paper presents near-IR monitoring of Mrk 876, a luminous nearby AGN, finding reduced hot-dust variability, large-grain dust radii, and a flared disk-like dust structure.

quotes.quote: Here we present the first near-infrared (near-IR) variable (rms) spectrum for the high-luminosity, nearby AGN Mrk 876. We find that it tracks the accretion disk spectrum out to longer wavelengths than the mean spectrum due to a reduced dust emission. The implied outer accretion disk radius is consistent with the infrared results predicted by a contemporaneous optical accretion disk reverberation mapping campaign and much larger than the self-gravity radius. The reduced flux variability of the hot dust could be either due to the presence of a secondary, constant dust component in the mean spectrum or introduced by the destructive superposition of the dust and accretion disk variability signals or some combination of both.

Assuming thermal equilibrium for optically thin dust, we derive the luminosity-based dust radius for different grain properties using our measurement of the temperature. We find that in all cases considered the values are significantly larger than the dust response time measured by IR photometric monitoring campaigns, with the least discrepancy present relative to the result for a wavelength-independent dust emissivity law, i.e. a blackbody, which is appropriate for large grain sizes. This result can be well explained by assuming a flared, disk-like structure for the hot dust.

sources.summary: The paper conducts optical continuum reverberation mapping of Mrk 876, finding large correlated variability, broad CCFs, and interband lags longer than simple disk expectations.

quotes.quote: Through 3 years of multiwavelength photometric monitoring in the optical with robotic observatories, we perform continuum reverberation mapping on Mrk 876. All wavebands show large amplitude variability and are well correlated. Slow variations in the light curves broaden the cross-correlation function (CCF) significantly, requiring detrending in order to robustly recover interband lags. We measure consistent interband lags using three techniques (CCF, JAVELIN, PyROA), with a lag of around 13 days from u to z .

Mrk 876 is a prime target for such a study. Its location in the sky allows it to be observed by northern ground-based robotic observatories nearly year-round. It has a history of reliable variability, and has been studied in several other independent campaigns 2000ApJ...533..631K, 2013ApJ...767..149B, 2022MNRAS.511.3005J, Landt2022 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses a possible companion and interaction around 1613+658, noting a nearby galaxy, a cluster environment, an eastern tidal tail, and SFR estimates much higher than earlier work.

quotes.quote: Additionally, a galaxy near 1613+658 reported by is easily visible in the infrared continuum image and the Pa image prior to continuum removal. Without redshift information neither we nor can confirm an association between these objects. The separation is quite close 1 ) and the angular size is comparable ( 0 5 \ 0 8 ), suggesting that the two galaxies are indeed related. Additionally, report that 1613+658 is coincident with a poor cluster of galaxies at the same redshift, providing a pool of potential interaction partners, and that there is a tidal tail to the East of 1613+658 , suggesting recent interaction. However, after removing the continuum contribution to the Pa images (see Section sec:continuum ), the companion nearly disappears.
In particular, reports SFRs of 6.0 and 0.57 M _ yr ^ 1 for 1613+658 and 2214+139 , respectively, while we find values an order of magnitude or more higher.

sources.summary: The letter reports positive broad Balmer line width-flux correlations in PG 1613+658 and argues its single-peaked optical Balmer lines likely originate in gravity-dominated disk-like BLRs.

quotes.quote: In this letter, we report positive correlations between broad line width and broad line flux for the broad balmer lines of the long-term observed AGN PG 1613+658. Rather than the expected negative correlations under the widely accepted virialization assumption for AGN BLRs, the positive correlations indicate much different BLR structures of PG 1613+658 from the commonly considered BLR structures which are dominated by the equilibrium between radiation pressure and gas pressure. Therefore, accretion disk origin is preferred for the observed broad single-peaked optical balmer lines of PG 1613+658, because of the mainly gravity dominated disk-like BLRs with radial structures having few effects from radiation pressure.

PG 1613+658 (=PG1613) is one well known long-term observed AGN in the sample of Kaspi et al. (2000). There are 44 public optical spectra with both broad H and broad H , which can be collected from the website http://wise-obs.tau.ac.il/ shai/PG/. Then, line parameters of emissions of PG1613 are measured as follows All the emission lines are fitted simultaneously within the rest wavelength ranges from 4400 to 5500 and from 6200 to 6900 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies PG 1613 as one of two mapped PG QSOs with only a weak optical spectral-index to continuum-luminosity trend, with LTS fits giving a slope about three times its uncertainty.

quotes.quote: (1): No strong dependence of the optical spectral index on the continuum luminosity can be found for all the 17 QSOs, besides two objects (PG 0026 and PG 1613) having some weak trends (with 3 confidence level) for the relationship.

It is clear that there are no apparently strong correlations between the optical spectral index and the continuum luminosity for the mapped QSOs, besides weak trends for the relationship (coefficient still larger than -0.5 but the two-sided significance level p_ null much smaller than 0.01) for the two PG QSOs of PG 0026 and PG 1613.
It is more clearer that besides the PG 0026 and PG 1613, none of the QSOs has the slope 3 times larger than the corresponding uncertainty of the slope.

sources.summary: The paper reports a Swift/XRT 4.8 keV rest-frame emission line in the Seyfert 1 AGN Mrk 876 and constrains an accretion-disk origin below 6 gravitational radii.

quotes.quote: In this Letter, we report on a detection in a Swift /XRT observation of an emission line at 4.8 keV rest--frame energy of the optically discovered Seyfert type 1 AGN Mrk 876.
Our analysis of the Swift /XRT observation of Mrk 876 (obs id: 00050300004) has revealed an emission line at 4.80^ +0.05 _ 0.04 keV rest--frame energy associated to the source.

Due to the independent constraints of the low inclination of Mrk 876's accretion disk (15.4 deg) and the observed gravitational redshift, we can constrain that the line is emitted from the accretion disk at radii below 6 r_ g (where r_ g = GM_ BH /^ 2 ) from a Kerr black hole.

sources.summary: PG 1613+658 is described as a dramatic late-stage merger with a possible second nucleus and an additional projected companion component.

quotes.quote: PG 1613+658: The host galaxy is a dramatic late-stage merger (see also Hong et al. 2015), with a projected compact source that may be the second nucleus. We adopt two free \ components to fit the main body of the host and another component to account for the projected companion. The distant galaxy to the north is masked.

sources.summary: The paper reports Mrk 876 as unabsorbed, consistent with NuSTAR, XMM-Newton, and Swift studies, and interprets it as a classical Seyfert 1.

quotes.quote: Mrk 876 We report this source as unabsorbed. This result is consistent with work reported in Bo2022 , using NuSTAR data, and Bo2015 with XMM-Newton and Swift . This unabsorbed scenario corresponds to a classical Seyfert 1.

gmw_00002528.name: PG 1626+554
gmw_00002528.overview: PG 1626+554 is a quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0612276, arXiv:2003.10078] that is also detected as an infrared and X-ray source [arXiv:astro-ph/0612276]. Its host galaxy features a bulge and an exponential disk with faint spiral structures [arXiv:2102.09190], and it exhibits a high star formation rate [arXiv:1311.1978].

sources.summary: SBS 1626+554 is described as an infrared and X-ray QSO with broad, asymmetric Balmer components, weak possible narrow components, and several identified permitted lines.

quotes.quote: SBS 1626+554 This object is an infrared (2MASS) and X-ray (ROSAT and EINSTEIN) source. Its spectrum clearly corresponds to a QSO, and the only forbidden lines observed are the \ 4959, 5007 . The permitted lines do not show narrow components, but two broad components are necessary to fit the Balmer lines, whose profiles are slightly asymmetric because the broadest component is systematically redshifted.

As shown in Fig. sbs1626hg , the broadest component of \ is redshifted by 1000 . A similar redshifted component can be measured in , as shown in Fig. sbs1626hb and . The extreme of the red wing of the \ profile is bitten by the telluric absorption band between 7570 and 7700 . All these lines show a fit which is not as good near the peak as in the rest of the line, suggesting the presence of a narrow component, however, too weak to be measured with confidence (S/N 1.5). Apart from the Balmer lines, the only permitted lines observed are \ 4686 (after subtracting the \ blends), a feature identified as \ 5048 , and \ 5876 which is easily recognized in the spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper lacked near-IR/Pa imaging for 1626+554, used a typical extinction map instead, found the analysis robust, and identified it as having the highest SFR by multiple indicators.

quotes.quote: Because we were not able to obtain Pa images of 1626+554 we resorted to performing extinction correction on the 1626+554 images with a typical A_V map produced using the seven quasar hosts for which we posses Pa images. After plotting azimuthally averaged values of A_V against radial distance from quasar centre (in kpc), we determined, by visual inspection, that a typical quasar host had A_V 0.3 out to 1 kpc, A_V 0 beyond 1.6 kpc, with a linear interpolation between 1.0 and 1.6 kpc. We emphasize here that, for reasons that will be discussed in Section sec:lineratios , assuming a typical A_V map does not significantly impact our line-ratio analysis nor our detection for star-forming regions in 1626+554 .
Moreover, our conclusions with regard to 1626+554 , the object for which we have not been able to measure extinction values, appear to be robust.

These two indicators yield a higher dispersion in SFRs but they still give approximately the same range in SFRs as our measurements, specifically, 1626+554 consistently has the highest SFR by all indicators, 1244+026 appears to have the lowest SFR, and all other objects have SFRs of a few tens of M_ yr^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper treats PG1626+554 as a poor SED-decomposition example: sparse, low-quality FIR data make its FIR profile difficult to determine, and the large reduced chi-squared indicates a poor fit.

quotes.quote: For PG1626+554 , the FIR data are of low quality and not well-sampled, with only two data points at wavelength >100 \ m . Therefore, it is quite difficult to determine the FIR profile for this source. Meanwhile, the reduced chi-squared ^2_ is 31.973 which also indicates that the fit is poor.

sources.summary: The PG 1626+554 host is fit as an n=1 bulge plus exponential disk, with similar low-index alternatives and faint spiral residuals.

quotes.quote: PG 1626+554: The host galaxy can be well fit with an n=1 bulge and an exponential disk. Allowing the \ index to be free yields a best fit value of n 0.2 , but the residuals are similar to the case of n=1 , and we finally adopt the latter. Faint spirals are evident in the residuals.

gmw_00002529.name: C1
gmw_00002529.overview: C1 is a low-luminosity quasar host galaxy at a redshift of z = 6.05 [arXiv:2405.02468]. It forms a merging pair with a neighboring quasar system and exhibits exceptionally high [C II] luminosity along with a fast, active galactic nucleus-driven outflow [arXiv:2405.02468].

sources.summary: C1 is a z=6.05 quasar host in a merging pair with exceptional [C II] luminosity and a possible fast AGN-driven outflow.

quotes.quote: This paper presents our ALMA Cycle 7 observations of the [ C 2 ] 158 line and the underlying rest-frame FIR continuum emission toward the twin quasar system at z = 6.05 discovered by our Subaru HSC survey, namely HSC J 121503.42 014858.7 and HSC J 121503.55 014859.3 (C1 and C2, hereafter), separated by 12 kpc projected on the sky Matsuoka2024 .

The brightest [ C 2 ] emission originates from C1 ( 3.5 10^9 L_ ), which is the highest [ C 2 ] luminosity reported for HSC low-luminosity quasars to date that typically have L_ CII < 10^9 L_ 2018PASJ...70...36I,2019PASJ...71..111I,2021ApJ...908..235I,2021ApJ...914...36I , and is as high as those of optically luminous quasars e.g., 2016ApJ...816...37V,2020ApJ...904..130V,2018ApJ...854...97D . This luminosity is also comparable to the luminosities of PJ308 21 a galaxy merger at z = 6.23 , 2019ApJ...880..157D , although C1 ( M_ 1450 = -23.1 mag) is 20 fainter than the quasar nucleus of PJ308 21 ( M_ 1450 = -26.3 mag) at rest-UV wavelength. We speculate that we are witnessing the recent onset of AGN, which will evolve to a more luminous quasar, in our pair system.

We identified a dynamically distinct, fast [ C 2 ] component around C1. Detailed inspection of the line spectrum reveals the presence of a broad wing component, which we interpret as the indication of fast outflows with a velocity of 600 km s ^ 1 . The expected mass loading factor of the outflows, after accounting for multiphase gas, would be 3-8 , as expected for an AGN-driven outflow.

gmw_00002530.name: C2
gmw_00002530.overview: C2 is a companion quasar host galaxy located at a redshift of z = 6.05 [arXiv:2405.02468]. It is separated by 12 kpc from its twin quasar system companion, C1, and actively forms stars at a rate of 63 solar masses per year [arXiv:2405.02468].

sources.summary: C2 is the companion quasar host in the z=6.05 pair, offset from the [C II] bridge but still actively forming stars.

quotes.quote: This paper presents our ALMA Cycle 7 observations of the [ C 2 ] 158 line and the underlying rest-frame FIR continuum emission toward the twin quasar system at z = 6.05 discovered by our Subaru HSC survey, namely HSC J 121503.42 014858.7 and HSC J 121503.55 014859.3 (C1 and C2, hereafter), separated by 12 kpc projected on the sky Matsuoka2024 .

However, we found that the location of C2 is offset from the nearest local maximum of the [ C 2 ] emission by 0''.42 or 2.4 kpc. This local maximum lies between C1 and C2, and we refer to it as the `` Bridge " hereafter. Note that the location of C2 is determined as the flux-weighted center of the HSC z band map. As the z band emission is spatially extended, this location does not necessarily correspond to the exact location of the quasar.
With this, we find SFR _ CII = 305 16 M_ yr ^ 1 in C1 and 63 11 M_ yr ^ 1 in C2.

gmw_00002531.name: GRB 030329 host galaxy
gmw_00002531.overview: The GRB 030329 host galaxy is a subluminous, low-metallicity starburst dwarf galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0508541, arXiv:0804.1856] with a low stellar mass [arXiv:astro-ph/0507488, arXiv:0804.1856]. It features a young dominant stellar population [arXiv:astro-ph/0508541, arXiv:0804.1856] and exhibits a high specific star formation rate [arXiv:astro-ph/0508541, arXiv:astro-ph/0507488].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 030329 host as a compact, subluminous, low-metallicity starburst with a young dominant population, modest extinction, and high specific star formation.

quotes.quote: In this study optical/near-infrared(NIR) broad band photometry and optical spectroscopic observations of the GRB 030329 host galaxy are presented. The Spectral Energy Distribution (SED) of the host is consistent with a starburst galaxy template with a dominant stellar population age of 150 Myr and an extinction A_ v 0.6 . Analysis of the spectral emission lines shows that the host is likely a low metallicity galaxy. Two independent diagnostics, based on the restframe UV continuum and the [ OII ] line flux, provide a consistent unextincted star formation rate of SFR 0.6 M_ yr ^ 1 . The low absolute magnitude of the host ( M_B 16.5 ) implies a high specific star formation rate value, SSFR = 34 M_ yr ^ 1 (L/L^ )^ 1 .

HST observations showed that the host is a dwarf compact (full width half maximum, FWHM 0.5^ ; ). However, to date there are no studies on the host galaxy SED, which gives relevant information on the extinction, dominant stellar population age, star formation rate (SFR) and galaxy type. In the present paper we report imaging and spectroscopic observations of the GRB 030329 host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents photometry and spectroscopy of the GRB 030329 host, describing it as a low-metallicity, starburst, subluminous galaxy with high specific star formation.

quotes.quote: Gorosabel, e-mail: jgu@iaa.es Received / Accepted We present broad band photometry and spectroscopic observations of the host galaxy of GRB 030329. Analysis of the spectral emission lines shows that the host is likely a low metallicity galaxy ( Z 0.004 ). The spectral energy distribution (SED) constructed with the photometric points has been fitted using synthetic and observational templates. The best SED fit is obtained with a starburst template with an age of 150 Myr and an extinction A_ v 0.6 .

We find that the GRB 030329 host galaxy is a subluminous galaxy ( L 0.016 L^ ) with a stellar mass of 10^ 8 M_ . Three independent diagnostics, based on the restframe UV continuum, the [ O ii ] , and the Balmer emission lines, provide a consistent unextincted star formation rate of 0.6 M_ yr ^ 1 , implying a high unextincted specific star formation rate ( 34 M_ yr ^ 1 (L/L^ )^ 1 ). We estimate that the unextincted specific star formation rate of the GRB 030329 host is higher than 93.5 of the galaxies at a similar redshift.

sources.summary: The paper treats the GRB 030329 host as a low-redshift galaxy whose absorption components are blueshifted outside the small host, favoring a starburst-wind origin.

quotes.quote: GRB 030329 Its redshift of z=0.1686 allowed us to observe both Mg I II absorption and the full range of nebular emission lines from the host superimposed on the afterglow spectrum observed on March 29 and April 2 2003 using UVES/VLT Thoene07 . Five narrow absorption components spanning 250 km/s are blueshifted compared to the resolved host emission lines (FWHM: 50 km/s), that reflect the rotation of the galaxy (Fig. 1). The position of the absorption compared to the emission lines puts them clearly outside the relatively small host. The nonvariability of Mg I between the two epochs leads to a minimum distance of 560 pc to the burst for the redmost component. An outflow caused by a starburst wind is therefore a likely origin for the absorption structures.

sources.summary: The paper treats the GRB 030329 host as a subluminous, metal-poor star-forming dwarf and uses HST imaging to compare the host galaxy, starburst environment, and explosion site populations.

quotes.quote: In this paper we investigate archival Hubble Space Telescope (HST) images obtained of the host of GRB 030329 at z=0.1685 gorosabel05 . The host appears to be a subluminous ( L 0.016L^ ), metal-poor, galaxy with a star formation rate of 0.5 \ M_ yr ^ 1 and a total stellar mass of M 10^8 - 10^9 \ M_ , dominated by a rather young stellar population gorosabel05,sollerman05 . The host galaxy will be further discussed in Sect. 4.2.

From the HST images we extract the colours of the precise site from which the GRB originated, and find that the colours are significantly different from those of the overall host galaxy and the surrounding starburst environment. These age estimates are an order of magnitude lower than the ages inferred from the overall host galaxy colours, indicating that progenitor mass estimates based on data for spatially unresolved GRB host galaxies will in general be of limited use.

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 030329 host as a low-to-moderate-metallicity starburst dwarf with no detected sub-millimeter emission, making it unlikely to be a bright quiescent dusty source.

quotes.quote: The host is believed to be a low to moderate metallicity starburst dwarf galaxy with V 22.7 and R > 22.5 (Fruchter et al. ; Matheson et al. ).
There is no evidence for an underlying dusty host galaxy.

There is no indication for any sub-millimeter emission from the host galaxy. It is not surprising that GRB 030329 is not a bright quiescent sub-millimeter source given that (1) it is at a low redshift, (2) the host is probably a dwarf galaxy, and (3) the X-ray and optical observations show relatively little excess absorption from dust above the Galactic value (Tiengo et al. ; Matheson et al. ). However, given the short observing times and less than ideal observing conditions, the limits on the host galaxy fluxes are less restrictive than for some of the other bursts whose host galaxies have been observed by SCUBA (Barnard et al. ; Berger et al. ; Tanvir et al. ; Smith et al. ).

sources.summary: The paper reports DOT observations detecting the GRB 030329/SN 2003dh host in V and R, and SED modeling finds a low-mass, low-SFR galaxy.

quotes.quote: We performed the host galaxy observations of GRB 030329 using 4K 4K Imager mounted on the axial port 3.6m DOT in March 2017. Multiple frames with an exposure time of 600 s were taken in R and V filers. The host galaxy of GRB 030329/SN 2003dh is clearly detected in both filters.
Our analysis suggests for a low mass, and low star formation galaxy for GRB 030329 2005AA...444..711G .

gmw_00002532.name: SDSS J204244.74+153032.1
gmw_00002532.overview: SDSS J204244.74+153032.1 is a quiescent nuclear host galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.219 [arXiv:1711.04577]. Its spectral line ratios indicate a mixture of star formation and active galactic nucleus activity, supporting its classification as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1711.04577].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J204244.74+153032.1 as the quiescent nuclear host of PS1-10adi, with SDSS redshift agreement and line ratios indicating star formation plus AGN signatures.

quotes.quote: Within the root mean square errors, PS1-10adi appeared to be coincident with the centre of Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) J204244.74+153032.1, and was 2 mag brighter at peak in all optical bands than the SDSS data release 7 (DR7) reference magnitudes.
The redshift is in agreement with the SDSS DR7 photometric z = 0.219 0.025 of the host galaxy SDSS J204244.74+153032.1.

Gaussian components were fitted to the line profiles of the +1,600 d spectrum of the (quiescent) PS1-10adi host galaxy, and the narrow-line component ratios were compared to the theoretical curves in a spectral line ratio Baldwin-Phillips-Terlevich (BPT) diagram (Supplementary Fig. 4). Aside from the Balmer lines, the spectrum shows weak forbidden lines suggestive of an dominated host. However, the broad base of the Balmer line profiles suggests the presence of an AGN and supports the classification as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy.

gmw_00002533.name: host galaxy of AT 2018dyb
gmw_00002533.overview: The host galaxy of AT 2018dyb, also known as WKK 6047, is located at a redshift of z=0.017392 [arXiv:2003.13693]. It is an old passive galaxy with no evidence of ongoing star formation [arXiv:2207.06855] and a low star formation rate [arXiv:2003.13693].

sources.summary: The paper treats WKK 6047 as the ASASSN-18pg host, modeling contaminated host photometry, deriving old stellar mass and low SFR, and using its reported redshift.

quotes.quote: The field of the host is heavily contaminated by Galactic sources, including 2 bright stars located off each end of the host galaxy's disk and 4 stars located in front of the host. While the 4 stars obstructing the host do not appear to contribute significant flux to the infrared (IR) data, the 2 nearby bright stars contaminate the 2MASS and AllWISE catalog magnitudes. To avoid contamination from these sources and obtain IR magnitudes of the host galaxy, we downloaded the JHK_S 2MASS images and measured 5 0 aperture magnitudes in each filter.

Based on the fast fit, we find that the host has a stellar mass of M_ =1.7^ +0.1 _ 0.2 10^ 10 M _ , an age of 8.9^ +1.1 _ 1.8 Gyr, and an upper limit on the star formation rate of SFR <2.6 10^ 2 M _ yr ^ 1 . Scaling the stellar mass using the average stellar-mass-to-bulge-mass ratio from the hosts of ASASSN-14ae, ASASSN-14li, and ASASSN-15oi holoien14b,holoien16a,holoien16b , as we have done with previous TDEs e.g., holoien19b , gives an estimated bulge mass of M_B 10^ 9.6 . We then convert this to an estimated black hole mass of M_ BH =10^ 7.0 using the M_B-M_ BH relation from mcconnell13 .
The redshift of WKK 6047 was reported by woudt08 as z=0.017392 .

sources.summary: The paper treats the AT 2018dyb host as an old passive galaxy with no ongoing star formation, a strong 4000-A break, and negligible host reddening or ISP.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of AT 2018dyb, however, is an old passive galaxy , with no evidence of ongoing star formation, and with a limited probability of having any dust. Using the host galaxy spectrum we measure the strength of the 4000 \ break and we find D_ 4000 > 1.53 . We therefore assume negligible reddening and ISP at the host.

gmw_00002534.name: NGC 3156
gmw_00002534.overview: NGC 3156 is an early-type [arXiv:0912.0274, arXiv:1709.09300] post-starburst [arXiv:1709.09300] or E+A galaxy [arXiv:1604.02056, arXiv:1709.00423] containing a pseudobulge [arXiv:1004.5393]. It features a central dust lane that transitions into a nuclear spiral [arXiv:1004.5393], alongside a steep nuclear stellar cusp [arXiv:1709.00423] and modest ongoing star formation [arXiv:0912.0274].

sources.summary: The paper groups NGC 3156 with lower-mass star-forming early types, noting modest current star formation, older SSP ages than NGC 3032, centralized morphology trends, and young populations beyond current PAH regions.

quotes.quote: Widespread Star-Forming Fast Rotators In Figure sfrmstar , the \ galaxies (NGC 3032, NGC 3156, NGC 3489, NGC 4150, NGC 4550) are seen to be those star-forming early-type galaxies with lower stellar masses. Given the comparable molecular content of all star-forming galaxies in our sample (see Table tbl:sfr ), the \ galaxies are consequently also characterized by higher gas fractions and specific star formation rates. As a result, the current star formation event dominates the single stellar population (SSP) ages, which are measured to be young throughout these systems ( < 1 3 Gyr; Kuntschner et al. in prep ). This is consistent with the location of the \ galaxies in Figure ks ; these systems have molecular gas and star formation surface densities similar to those found in late-type galaxies undergoing global star formation.

NGC 3156, NGC 3489, NGC 4150; SFR = 0.03 0.06 \ ) have SSP ages older by 1 2 Gyr, an order of magnitude less molecular gas, and high / \ ratios that are inconsistent with significant on-going star formation .

Lower SFRs in a \ galaxy also correlates with an increasingly irregular star formation and gas distribution, from a regular, disk-like morphology (NGC 3032) to disturbed ionized and molecular gas distributions (NGC 3489; Figure Main-SF and Crocker et al. in prep ) to highly asymmetric molecular gas distributions (NGC 4550; Cro+09 ). These increasingly irregular morphologies additionally correspond to more centralized star formation at lower SFR (compare NGC 3032, NGC 3156, and NGC 4550 in Figure profiles a ). In the absence of a replenishing gas reservoir, \ galaxies will experience declining SFRs, causing them to move to the left in Figure sfrmstar ; the system with the most intense star formation (NGC 3032) will therefore be expected to eventually move towards the other \ galaxies. Likewise, there is evidence that the \ galaxies with lower SFRs previously had more intense star formation events that extended to larger radii, implying earlier positions to the right of their current location in Figure sfrmstar . In particular, the young SSP ages (1 3 Gyr in NGC 3156, NGC 3489, NGC 4150; 6 Gyr in NGC 4550; Kuntschner et al. in prep ) found throughout these galaxies, beyond the star-forming regions observed in PAH emission, indicate that star formation activity occurred out to larger radii in the past than in the present.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3156 as a post-starburst early-type galaxy with molecular gas and young optical ages, while some near-infrared models make its inferred stellar history older or extended.

quotes.quote: IC 0719, NGC 3032, NGC 3156, NGC 3182, NGC 3489 and NGC 4710 were shown to contain molecular gas combes_molecular_2007, young_atlas3d_2014 , corresponding to regions of ongoing star formation davis_atlas3d_2014 , and have young mean stellar ages. NGC 3032 and NGC 3156 are consistent with post-starburst galaxies such as those targeted in .
In particular, all models derive young ages for our two post-starburst galaxies (NGC 3032 and NGC 3156) when fitting their optical spectra.

Use of the M11 models in the near-infrared also leads to young ages for NGC 3032 and NGC 4710, in agreement with the optical, however the post-starburst galaxy NGC 3156 appears old in this case. As with BC03, M11 models require small fractions of young stars in NGC 3098, NGC 3301, NGC 3489, NGC 4608 and NGC 5475. The remainder of the galaxies appear old at both wavelengths. The V16 near-infrared fits imply a small burst in NGC 3032, atop a pre-existing old population, which is the most physically likely scenario. NGC 3156 and NGC 4710 also have extended SFHs, however the mass-weighted mean ages derived using V16 are all old ( t  8yr), contrary to the optical SFHs, which span a large range of mean ages.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3156 as a pseudobulge galaxy, citing There is a dust-lane that near the center becomes a nuclear spiral; The morphology is some what uncertain.

quotes.quote: NGC 3156 .L...*./ S0 Pseudobulge. There is a dust-lane that near the center becomes a nuclear spiral. The morphology is some what uncertain. The decomposition yields n_b=1.7 , and the bulge is not active with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =-0.4 mag.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes HST photometry of the E+A galaxy NGC 3156, finding a high two-body-relaxation TDE rate linked to a central stellar overdensity from recent starbursts.

quotes.quote: We analyze Hubble Space Telescope photometry of one of the nearest E+A galaxies, NGC 3156, to estimate the rate of stellar tidal disruption produced as two-body relaxation diffuses stars onto orbits in the loss cone of the central supermassive black hole. The rate of TDEs produced by two-body relaxation in NGC 3156 is large when compared to other galaxies with similar black hole mass: N _ TDE 1 10^ 3 yr ^ 1 . This suggests that the preference of TDEs for E+A hosts may be due to central stellar overdensities produced in recent starbursts.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3156 as tentative support for the overdensity explanation of TDE host preferences, citing its E+A status, steep nuclear stellar cusp, elevated predicted TDE rate, and small influence radius.

quotes.quote: This overdensity hypothesis has received tentative observational support from observations and dynamical modeling of one of the nearest E+A galaxies, NGC 3156 StonevanVelzen16 ; likewise, broader population studies find that per-galaxy TDE rates are correlated with both the slope LawSmith+17 and the normalization Graur+17 of stellar light profiles on kpc scales (though we caution that the TDE rate is set by stellar dynamics on pc scales).

Some support for this hypothesis was found by StonevanVelzen16 , who analyzed archival Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) photometry of the nearby E+A galaxy NGC 3156, and found an unusually steep stellar density cusp which should produce an elevated TDE rate ( N 1 10^ 3 gal ^ 1 yr ^ 1 ) due to rapid two-body relaxation.

Almost all observed galactic nuclei possess < 9/4 Lauer+05 , but StonevanVelzen16 found that the nucleus of NGC 3156, a nearby E+A galaxy, is at the very least a borderline case and may indeed be ultrasteep; depending on the precise model employed for the point spread function of HST , this galaxy has 2.15 2.31 NGC 3156 also possesses an unusually small influence radius, although measuring this quantity requires extrapolating the fitted power law slope beyond the HST resolution limit.

gmw_00002535.name: host galaxy of PTF 11hrq
gmw_00002535.overview: The host galaxy of PTF 11hrq is a disk-like galaxy characterized by a large population of evolved stars [arXiv:1705.01948]. It features a vigorously star-forming northern region [arXiv:1705.01948] and has nearby companion galaxies that suggest a possible group interaction [arXiv:1705.01948].

sources.summary: The paper finds the PTF 11hrq host is disk-like with evolved stars, a northern star-forming region offset from the SLSN, and nearby companions suggesting possible interaction.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of PTF 11hrq has a disk-like morphology and is characterized by a large population of evolved stars. HST and IFU observations reveal diverse unexpected features. The northern part of host contains a vigorously star-forming region with a [ O III ] 5007 EW of >200 . Intriguingly, the explosion site does not coincide with this region at 5.5 confidence. The UV-to-optical color of the SLSN location is 1.2 mag, after correction for dust extinction, which is among 1 of the reddest pixels, but nevertheless blue on an absolute scale and similar to PTF 12dam.

The host galaxy of PTF 11hrq has a nearby ( 10 kpc) fainter companion at the same redshift, and possibly another one at a distance of about 30 kpc. The small angular separation and the small radial velocity difference of 40 km s ^ 1 suggests that they could be part of a galaxy group.

gmw_00002536.name: host galaxy of PTF 12dam
gmw_00002536.overview: The host galaxy of PTF 12dam is a compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1409.7728] with a disturbed, tadpole-like morphology featuring multiple star-forming knots and an extended tidal tail [arXiv:1705.01948, arXiv:1411.1104]. It is characterized as a starburst galaxy [arXiv:2108.10445, arXiv:1411.1104] with strong emission lines [arXiv:2010.06612, arXiv:1409.7728] and a high star-formation rate [arXiv:1705.01948, arXiv:2010.06612].

sources.summary: The paper finds the PTF 12dam host has disturbed tadpole-like morphology with multiple star-forming knots; the SLSN occurred in a bright, UV-bright knot rather than the bluest region.

quotes.quote: PTF 12dam is among the most nearby SLSNe-I known, and happened in SDSS J142446.21+461348.6 at a redshift of z = 0.1073. The host galaxy of PTF 12dam has also been studied by 2015MNRAS.451L..65T , 2015MNRAS.452.1567C , and 2016ApJ...830...13P , who found a metallicity of 12+ ( O/H )=8.0 throughout the galaxy. This metallicity is low, but not the most extreme compared to other SLSNe host some H-poor SLSNe have 12+ ( O/H ) 7.8 ; see e.g. 2015MNRAS.449..917L . They also observed an extended asymmetric halo or tidal tail. Furthermore, the host galaxy of PTF 12dam has one of the highest star-formation rates (SFRs) among SLSNe hosts.

HST observations of PTF 12dam reveal a complex and disturbed morphology with multiple star-forming knots. Combined with deep ground-based images, this complex represents the head of tadpole galaxy. The SN exploded in one of the five star forming knots.

sources.summary: The paper associates radio emission near PTF12dam with its host galaxy, deriving an unobscured star-formation rate and noting the host is a bright, strong-emission-line SLSN host.

quotes.quote: In addition to the detection of PTF10hgi, with F_ = 47 7 \ Jy, reported in Eftekhari2019 , we detect radio emission near the location of the SLSN PTF12dam with a flux density F_ = 117 12 \ Jy; see Figure fig:ptf12dam . This is comparable to the 3 GHz detection presented in Hatsukade2018 with F_ = 141.5 5.1 \ Jy. The radio emission is offset from the SN position by 1 arcsecond and is instead centered on, and traces, the optical emission from the host galaxy. Thus the emission is most likely related to star formation in the host galaxy (see discussion in sec:sfr ).

In the case of PTF12dam, our radio detection of the host galaxy corresponds to an SFR of 6.7 0.7 \ M_ \ yr^ 1 , comparable to the value inferred from a previous 3 GHz detection ( 4.8 0.2 \ M_ \ yr^ 1 ; Hatsukade2018 ) and an H measurement, 4.8 1 \ M_ \ yr^ 1 Perley2016a . This indicates that the star formation in the host is largely unobscured. X-ray emission is also detected near the location of PTF12dam Margutti2018 , which we conclude is likely related to the host. Finally, we note that the host galaxy of PTF12dam is among the brightest SLSNe hosts with strong emission lines and an absolute magnitude M_g = -19.33 Chen2015 .

sources.summary: The paper links PTF12dam host radio emission to star formation near the galaxy center and describes the host as above the main sequence with high sSFR and starburst nature.

quotes.quote: The radio peak position of PTF12dam is also slightly offset from the SN position and close to the galaxy center. This is supported by the higher angular resolution ( 0 95 0 74 ) observations at 6 GHz by , who claimed that the emission was most likely related to star formation in the host.

The hosts of PTF10qaf, PTF10uhf, PTF12dam, and SN 1999bd have an excess of radio-based SFRs over SED-based SFRs by a factor of 3--12. The radio excess in the hosts of SN 1999bd, PTF10qaf, and PTF10uhf are consistent with the results by hats18 . On the other hand, and did not find a radio excess in the PTF12dam host. This is due to the difference in comparison SFRs adopted by and , where they used the results of . The H based SFR of 3--5 M_ yr ^ 1 lelo15, thon15, chen15, perl16 is consistent with our radio SFR of 3.4 0.2 M_ yr ^ 1 .

The hosts of PTF10qaf and PTF12dam are located above the main sequence, suggesting that they have a starburst nature e.g., rodi11, elba11 . We note that if we adopt the stellar mass of M_*/M_ = 10.24^ +0.22 _ 0.17 for PTF10qaf derived from the SED analysis by , the host is on the main sequence. The high sSFR and starburst nature of the PTF12dam host are consistent with the results of optical spectroscopic observations thon15, chen15 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the PTF12dam host SDSS J142446.21+461348.6 as a compact, low-mass, highly star-forming dwarf with strong nebular emission and low oxygen abundance.

quotes.quote: Here we present new photometry and spectroscopy for PTF12dam from 200-500 days (rest-frame) after peak and a detailed analysis of the host galaxy (SDSS J142446.21+461348.6 at z = 0.107 ).

The host galaxy is a compact dwarf (physical size 1.9 kpc) and with M_ g = -19.33 0.10 , it is the brightest nearby SLSN Ic host discovered so far. The host is a low mass system ( 2.8 10^ 8 ) with a star-formation rate (5.0 yr ^ 1 ), which implies a very high specific star-formation rate (17.9 Gyr ^ 1 ). The remarkably strong nebular emission provide detections of the 4363 and 7320,7330 auroral lines and an accurate oxygen abundance of 12 + (O/H) = 8.05 0.09 .

As PTF12dam is one of the closest SLSNe Ic ever found at redshift z = 0.107 and this provides an opportunity to study a host galaxy in detail. This galaxy has exceptionally strong emission lines and with auroral lines of three atomic species available one can determine abundances more precisely than done before.

sources.summary: The paper directly studies the PTF12dam host as a tadpole, extreme emission-line galaxy with uniform properties, a recent starburst, low metallicity, and a very young stellar population at the SN site.

quotes.quote: Here we present a resolved long-slit study of the host of the hydrogen-poor SLSN PTF12dam probing the kpc environment of the SN site to determine the age of the progenitor. The galaxy is a ``tadpole'' with uniform properties and the SN occurred in a star-forming region in the head of the tadpole. The galaxy experienced a recent star-burst superimposed on an underlying old stellar population. We measure a very young stellar population at the SN site with an age of 3 Myr and a metallicity of 12+log(O/H) = 8.0 at the SN site but do not observe any WR features.

Its host is a ``tadpole galaxy'' SanchezAlmeida with a compact core consisting of several SF regions, where the SN was located, and a fainter tail. Global spectra of the host have been presented by and showing very strong emission lines and a low metallicity which point to recent starburst.

gmw_00002537.name: UGC 00613
gmw_00002537.overview: UGC 00613 is a flat-spectrum radio galaxy characterized by an extremely diffuse envelope and faint radio lobes [arXiv:2103.06573, arXiv:2305.02007]. The galaxy likely resides in an unrelaxed, merging system and exhibits neutral hydrogen absorption features [arXiv:2103.06573, arXiv:2305.02007].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 00613 as a diffuse flat-spectrum radio galaxy with FAST-confirmed H I absorption whose velocity offset likely reflects unsettled gas around the AGN.

quotes.quote: UGC 00613 The absorption feature in UGC 00613, a flat spectrum radio galaxy with extremely diffuse envelope and faint lobes at z = 0.04593 Condon1991 , was first reported by and Song et al. (2021, in preparation), with a line depth of S_ HI,peak 65.0 mJy, an FWHM of 33.6 km s ^ 1 , and d v 31.14 km s ^ 1 . The FAST telescope performed tracking observations on this source with during its early-science phase, with its wide band receiver. Due to the lack of reliable noise diode in this period, the flux of UGC 00613 obtained by the N9020A MXA spectrum analyser were estimated in reference to observations of ASK 378291.0 shown in the upper panel of Fig. figASK . Firstly, the baseline-subtracted wide-band-receiver data of ASK 378291.0 (lower panel of Fig. figASK ) was re-scaled according to the flux level of the same source measured by the 19-beam receiver; then, the same scaling factor for conversion from N9020A MXA's instrumental reading to flux density (measured in mJy) was applied to UGC 00613 observations. Although such a calibration process is not strictly accurate, the resulted line depth, 64.34 mJy, is in good agreements with the Arecibo data.

It should be noted that as can be seen in Fig. figUGC , and also noted by Song et al. (2021, in preparation), the centre of the UGC 00613 absorption feature is redshifted from the optical redshift by as many as 186 km s ^ 1 ( 188 km s ^ 1 for Arecibo data). According to , although the signal-to-noise ratio of the optical data used for z estimation is not high enough to accurately measure the velocity dispersion of the spectral lines, the redshift of this galaxy can be reliably determined as cz 13770 23 km s ^ 1 . Thus, it is unlikely that such a large offset (which is at >8 level of the optical measurement) between HI line and optical velocities arise from observation errors.

sources.summary: The FAST survey re-detects H I absorption in UGC 00613, a flat-spectrum extended radio source probably in a merging system, and fits two absorption components.

quotes.quote: The absorption feature of UGC 00613 was firstly reported in 2021MNRAS.503.5385Z with a flux density depth of S_ , peak = -64.28 6.61 mJy, FWHM of 26.36 1.74 , and dv= 21.24 4.10 , from a one-component Gaussian fitting. UGC 00613 is a flat-spectrum extended radio source 2007ApJS..171...61H which probably locates in an unrelaxed, merging system 2019MNRAS.488.3416H . We blindly re-detected its absorption in beam 10, beam 4 and beam 13 in the CRAFTS drift-scan data. As Figure UGC00613_spec shows, the absorption feature overlaps in the frequency band with an emission feature, which is a chronically present RFI.

The absorption spectra of UGC 00613 and its Gaussian fitting are presented in Figure UGC00613_fit . Two components are found in the absorption feature, and some basic physical information is given in Table radiosource_table and Table absorption_table .

gmw_00002538.name: 4C +27.14
gmw_00002538.overview: 4C +27.14 is a Type II Seyfert galaxy that exhibits the properties of a radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2305.02007]. Observations have detected a faint, broad H I absorption profile in the direction of this radio continuum source [arXiv:2305.02007].

sources.summary: The paper detects faint H I absorption toward 4C +27.14, a Type II Seyfert and radio-loud AGN, in both CRAFTS drift-scan data and FAST follow-up observations.

quotes.quote: 4C +27.14 is a Type II Seyfert galaxy and displays the properties of a radio-loud AGN 2014A&A...561A..67P . Broad absorption profile has been observed in the direction of radio continuum source 4C +27.14 2014ApJ...793..132S for the first time. The absorption lines are detected in the CRAFTS drift-scan data with blind search and the follow-up observation using ON-OFF tracking mode. The absorption signal of 4C +27.14 is faint, only detected in beam 11 in the CRAFTS data.

gmw_00002539.name: NVSS J231240-052547
gmw_00002539.overview: NVSS J231240-052547 is a radio source detected in the NRAO VLA Sky Survey [arXiv:2305.02007]. Its mid-infrared colors identify the galaxy as an active galactic nucleus candidate, and it exhibits newly detected neutral hydrogen absorption with two distinct components [arXiv:2305.02007].

sources.summary: The paper reports the first H I absorption detection toward NVSS J231240-052547, finding two components and a WISE counterpart whose mid-infrared color indicates an AGN candidate.

quotes.quote: The absorption towards NVSS J231240-052547 is here reported for the first time. NVSS J231240-052547 is a radio source detected in the NRAO VLA Sky Survey with flux S_ 1.4GHz = 181.9 mJy. We blindly detected absorption in beam 10, beam 4 and beam 14 in the CRAFTS drift-scan data. The absorption spectra towards NVSS J231240-052547 and its Gaussian fitting are shown in the upper panel of Figure NVSS_J231240-052547_fit . Two components are found in the absorption feature, and some basic physical parameters are given in Table radiosource_table and Table absorption_table .

The WISE magnitudes for WISEA J231240.69-052548.8 are presented in Table wise_table . The W1-W2 colour of value 0.8 is often used as a simple mid-infrared colour criterion for AGN candidates 2012ApJ...753...30S . The W1-W2 colour of 0.827 indicates that is an AGN candidate.

gmw_00002540.name: NVSS J053118+315412
gmw_00002540.overview: NVSS J053118+315412 is an active galactic nucleus with mid-infrared emission dominated by the central engine [arXiv:2305.02007]. It exhibits a symmetric neutral hydrogen absorption spectrum, suggesting that the associated gas traces a regular rotating structure interacting with the host active nucleus [arXiv:2305.02007].

sources.summary: The paper reports a new H I absorber toward NVSS J053118+315412, verifies it with follow-up observations, and interprets its complex absorption as ISM interaction with a host AGN.

quotes.quote: The other new absorber is found in the line of sight towards NVSS J053118+315412. NVSS J053118+315412 has been detected at 1.4 GHz in NVSS with flux S_ 1.4GHz = 40.2 mJy. Its absorption is only detected in beam 12 of the drift scan covering Declination of +31d57m00s. In order to verify this detection, it was observed in follow-up observation using ON-OFF tracking mode.

The WISE magnitudes for WISEA J053118.83+315412.0 are presented in Table wise_table . The W1-W2 colour is 1.630, which is higher than the value of 0.8 often used to select AGN candidates and implies that the mid-IR emission comes mainly from the AGN.

The complicated structures of the absorption profile indicate the interaction between ISM and host AGN. NVSS J053118+315412 exhibits a symmetric absorption spectrum (Gaussian components 2 and 3) relative to the systemic velocity of its host galaxy, suggesting that the gas traces a regular rotating structure.

gmw_00002541.name: Leo P
gmw_00002541.overview: Leo P is an isolated, gas-rich dwarf galaxy located approximately 1.62 to 1.72 Mpc away [arXiv:1310.0044, arXiv:1506.05495]. It features an extremely low oxygen abundance [arXiv:1512.00459], a bright H II region [arXiv:2010.04180], and a stellar population that indicates relatively constant star formation over cosmic time [arXiv:1506.05495, arXiv:2010.04180].

sources.summary: The paper measures Leo P as a nearby gas-rich, extremely metal-poor dwarf outside the Local Group, using LBT photometry to place it at 1.72 Mpc in a low-density environment.

quotes.quote: Leo P is a low-luminosity dwarf galaxy discovered through the blind \ Arecibo Legacy Fast ALFA (ALFALFA) survey. The \ and follow-up optical observations have shown that Leo P is a gas-rich dwarf galaxy with both active star formation and an underlying older population, as well as an extremely low oxygen abundance. Here, we measure the distance to Leo P by applying the tip of the red giant branch (TRGB) distance method to photometry of the resolved stellar population from new Large Binocular Telescope (LBT) V and I band imaging. We measure a distance modulus of 26.19 ^ +0.17 _ 0.50 mag corresponding to a distance of 1.72^ +0.14 _ 0.40 Mpc.

Although our photometry reaches 3 magnitudes below the TRGB, the sparseness of the red giant branch (RGB) yields higher uncertainties on the lower limit of the distance. Leo P is outside the Local Group with a distance and velocity consistent with the local Hubble flow. While located in a very low-density environment, Leo P lies within 0.5 Mpc of a loose association of dwarf galaxies which include NGC 3109, Antlia, Sextans A, and Sextans B, and 1.1 Mpc away from its next nearest neighbor, Leo A. Leo P is one of the lowest metallicity star-forming galaxies known in the nearby universe, comparable in metallicity to I Zw 18 and DDO 68, but with stellar characteristics similar to dwarf spheriodals (dSphs) in the Local Volume such as Carina, Sextans, and Leo II.

sources.summary: The paper measures Leo P as an isolated, gas-rich low-luminosity dwarf at the Local Group edge, with old stars and roughly sustained star formation over cosmic time.

quotes.quote: Leo P is a low-luminosity dwarf galaxy discovered through the blind \ Arecibo Legacy Fast ALFA (ALFALFA) survey. The \ and follow-up optical observations have shown that Leo P is a gas-rich dwarf galaxy with active star formation, an underlying older population, and an extremely low oxygen abundance. We have obtained optical imaging with the Hubble Space Telescope to two magnitudes below the red clump in order to study the evolution of Leo P. We refine the distance measurement to Leo P to be 1.62 0.15 Mpc, based on the luminosity of the horizontal branch stars and 10 newly identified RR Lyrae candidates.

Given the range in the three solutions, the SFH is consistent with a relatively constant level of star-formation activity over cosmic timescales as seen in more massive dIrrs e.g., IC 1613; Skillman2014 . The best-fitting solutions for Leo P also suggest that star formation may have been damped after early epochs (i.e., post-reionization), with little SF occurring between 8-12 Gyr ago. As seen in the middle panel of Figure fig:sfh_metals , the solutions from the three stellar libraries provide a range for the degree and duration of damping.

Regardless, from the SFH it is clear that Leo P experienced star formation at t 10 Gyr, in agreement with the identification of ten RR Lyrae stars. Over the last 4 Gyr, the solutions from all three libraries show continual SF at an approximately constant rate.

sources.summary: Leo P is presented as an isolated, gas-rich low-mass dwarf with extremely low oxygen abundance; the paper inventories its oxygen using gas abundance, star-formation history, and chemical enrichment.

quotes.quote: Hermans Road, Tucson, AZ 85756 4 Department of Physics and Astronomy, Macalester College, 1600 Grand Avenue, Saint Paul, MN 55105 5 Department of Astronomy, Indiana University, 727 East 3rd Street, Bloomington, IN 47405 6 ASTRON, the Netherlands Institute for Radio Astronomy, Postbus 2, 7990 AA, Dwingeloo, The Netherlands 7 Center for Gravitation, Cosmology and Astrophysics, Department of Physics, University of Wisconsin Milwaukee, 1900 East Kenwood Boulevard, Milwaukee, WI 53211 8 Center for Radiophysics and Space Research, Space Sciences Building, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY 14853 Leo P is a gas-rich dwarf galaxy with an extremely low gas-phase oxygen abundance (3 solar). The isolated nature of Leo P enables a quantitative measurement of metals lost solely due to star formation feedback. We present an inventory of the oxygen atoms in Leo P based on the gas-phase oxygen abundance measurement, the star formation history, and the chemical enrichment evolution derived from resolved stellar populations.

Through the Arecibo Legacy Fast ALFA survey ALFALFA, Giovanelli2005, Haynes2011 , an isolated, gas-rich, very low mass galaxy, Leo P M_* = 5.6 10^5 ; McQuinn2015e , was discovered Giovanelli2013 just outside the zero velocity boundary of the Local Group McQuinn2013 . The galaxy has on-going star formation, an \ region Rhode2013 , and one of the lowest gas-phase oxygen abundances ever measured 12+ log(O/H) = 7.17 0.04 ; Skillman2013 . While the oxygen abundance is comparable to that of DDO 68 and I Zw 18, unlike these other galaxies, Leo P shows no signs of interaction or gas infall Bernstein-Cooper2014 that could dilute the metallicity. Instead, Leo P lies on an extension of the M-Z relation to very low luminosities Berg2012, Skillman2013 . All indications suggest that the evolution of Leo P is dominated by internal processes, not external events.

sources.summary: The paper describes Leo P as an isolated extremely metal-poor dwarf with steady star formation, a bright H II region, low oxygen abundance, and improved helium abundance from LBT spectra.

quotes.quote: Leo P was discovered in the wide-field, unbiased \ 21 cm ALFALFA survey giovanelli2013 . It is a relatively isolated galaxy. From HST observations of its RR Lyrae stars and the tip of its red giant branch, its distance has been determined to be 1.62 0.15 Mpc mcquinn2013, mcquinn2015a making it a member of the NGC 3109 association of dwarf galaxies which is just outside of the Local Group zero-velocity surface tully2006 . The stellar populations of Leo P are consistent with relatively constant star formation over the Hubble time mcquinn2015a . A bright H II region was identified in H imaging rhode2013 , allowing for chemical abundance analysis.

In addition to an overall improvement in the fit, we see from the last row of table table:LeoP , a significant drop in the uncertainty in the resulting helium mass fraction. The new uncertainty is more than a factor of three smaller than the previous result. The central value, in contrast, changed by less than 1 leading to a helium mass fraction in Leo P of Y = 0.2475 0.0057 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Leo P as a control case for the optical-counterpart search, describing it as an ALFALFA UCHVC later confirmed as a Local Volume dwarf galaxy with a detected stellar population.

quotes.quote: We demonstrate the method by applying it to two specific data sets: WIYN imaging of Leo P, a UCHVC discovered by ALFALFA and subsequently shown to be a low-mass star-forming dwarf galaxy in the Local Volume; and WIYN imaging of AGC198606, an HI cloud identified by ALFALFA that is near in position and velocity to the Local Group dwarf galaxy Leo T. Applying the search method to the Leo P imaging data yields an unambiguous detection ( > 99 confidence) of this galaxy's stellar population.

Leo P To illustrate our detection method, we apply it to images of the galaxy Leo P, which was discovered by ALFALFA, originally identified as a UCHVC based on its HI properties, and found through optical imaging to be a dwarf galaxy in the Local Volume leop1,rhode13,leop4 . We analyzed the original rhode13 images of Leo P, obtained with WIYN and Minimosaic in March 2012 Images of Leo P obtained with pODI in April 2013 had poor seeing and could not be used for this analysis. . Since these images were taken with broadband BVR filters, the photometry measured by rhode13 was converted from BVR to g and i using the stellar color transformations from sdsscolor . After making slight adjustments to the smoothing and significance testing methods described in Section section:filter to account for the difference in the field-of-view ( rhode13 analyzed a 9.6 \ by 4.8 \ portion of the Minimosaic pointing) we applied our filtering and smoothing technique to the photometric measurements of Leo P.

sources.summary: The paper treats Leo P as a faint, gas-rich isolated dwarf whose low luminosity, rotation velocity, continuing star formation, and BTFR placement challenge standard dwarf-formation models.

quotes.quote: Leo P giovanelli13 and Pisces A tollerud15 are the latest additions to a growing list of faint, gas-rich, isolated dwarf galaxies; a list that dates back at least to the discovery of Leo T irwin07 . These galaxies have luminosities of only a few 10^5 L_ ryanweber08,mcquinn13,tollerud15 and masses 3-4 times higher than their stellar mass. Rotation velocities, derived from radio observations of the , are estimated at v_ rot 12.5 km s^ 1 Leo T: ryanweber08 , v_ rot 17 km s^ 1 Pisces A: tollerud15 and v_ rot 18 km s^ 1 Leo P: giovanelli13,bernsteincooper14 . The available data suggest that all three dwarfs formed stars continuously, although at a very low and highly variable rate clementini12, weisz12, mcquinn13, mcquinn15 .
At a circular velocity of 15 km s^ 1 , comparable to Leo T, Leo P, and Pisces A, over 90 of all halos is predicted to be dark sawala14 .

In short, our simulations including feedback succeed in creating faint, gas-dominated dwarf galaxies lying on the BTFR, such as Leo T, Leo P, and Pisces A, while the control simulations without feedback do not.

sources.summary: The paper treats Leo P as a metal-poor, gas-rich dwarf and nearby high-redshift analog, using HST and Spitzer data to identify evolved AGB candidates in its stellar population.

quotes.quote: We have conducted a highly sensitive census of the evolved-star population in the metal-poor dwarf galaxy Leo P and detected four asymptotic giant branch (AGB) star candidates. Leo P is one of the best examples of a nearby analog of high-redshift galaxies because of its primitive metal content (2 of the solar value), proximity, and isolated nature, ensuring a less complicated history. To search for high-redshift AGB analogs we have observed the nearby dwarf galaxy Leo P (shown in Figure hst_image ), which is the most metal-poor gas-rich galaxy resolvable with current instruments.

Leo P is a dwarf galaxy with a stellar mass of 5.7 10^ 5 M _ and distance of 1.62 0.15 Mpc McQuinn2013,McQuinn2015 . Observations have shown that the ratio of gas to stars is 2:1 and the ratio of total mass to baryonic mass is 15:1 Bernstein-Cooper2014 . Originally discovered by the Arecibo Legacy Fast ALFA survey ALFALFA; Giovanelli2005 , observations have revealed the galaxy's stellar populations McQuinn2015 as well as a luminous star-forming H ii region Rhode2013 .

sources.summary: The paper presents Leo P as an ALFALFA-discovered, low-mass, metal-poor galaxy whose H I signature supports the possibility of similar gas-rich faint systems.

quotes.quote: The recent ALFALFA discovery of the star-forming, metal-poor, low mass galaxy Leo P demonstrates that this hypothesis is true in at least one case.

Leo P was discovered during the normal course of identifying HI detections within the ALFALFA survey when it was noticed that one ultra-compact high velocity cloud (UCHVC) could be associated with an irregular, lumpy light distribution in the SDSS images LeoP_HI . Follow-up optical observations resolved a stellar population and a single HII region, confirming that the UCHVC is in fact a low mass galaxy, Leo P LeoP_optical . We stress that Leo P was confirmed to be a galaxy because its young, blue stellar population was barely visible in the SDSS images; without recent star formation, the underlying older population of Leo P would not have been visible at all in the SDSS images. Leo P was discovered by its HI signature, and its existence strongly argues that other very low mass and (nearly) starless objects are included among the ALFALFA UCHVCs.

sources.summary: Leo P is described as a recently discovered adjacent dwarf irregular likely added to the NGC 3109 association, helping define a tight 1070 kpc alignment with a Local Group velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: We re-consider the association of dwarf galaxies around NGC 3109, whose known members were NGC 3109, Antlia, Sextans A, and Sextans B, based on a new updated list of nearby galaxies and the most recent data. We find that the original members of the NGC 3109 association, together with the recently discovered and adjacent dwarf irregular Leo P, form a very tight and elongated configuration in space. All these galaxies lie within 100 kpc of a line that is 1070 kpc long, from one extreme (NGC 3109) to the other (Leo P), and they show a gradient in the Local Group standard of rest velocity with a total amplitude of 43 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , and a .m.s. scatter of just 16.8 km s ^ 1 . It is shown that the reported configuration is exceptional given the known dwarf galaxies in the Local Group and its surroundings. We conclude that (a) Leo P is very likely an additional member of the NGC 3109 association, and (b) the association is highly ordered in space and velocity, and it is very elongated, suggesting that it was created by a tidal interaction or it was accreted as a filamentary substructure.

sources.summary: The paper describes Leo P as a nearby dwarf whose position, velocity, and distance align it with the elongated NGC 3109 association and Local Group planes.

quotes.quote: Another recently discovered nearby dwarf galaxy, Leo P 2013AJ....146...15G,2013AJ....145..149R , has lead 2013arXiv1310.6365B to re-investigate the NGC 3109 association, a group of dwarf galaxies at a distance of about 1.3--1.4 Mpc from the MW that consists of NGC 3109, Antlia, Sextans A and Sextans B 1999ApJ...517L..97V,2006AJ....132..729T . They realised that Leo P aligns with the four other members of the association in a very narrow, linear structure. As the NGC 3109 association is very close to the LG and has a linear extent of 1.2 Mpc, similar to its distance from the MW, we will investigate its orientation in the context of the LG planes of non-satellite dwarf galaxies.

For Leo P, we adopt the recent TRGB distance modulus by 2013AJ....146..145M , according to which the galaxy is at a distance of 1.72^ +0.14 _ 0.40 Mpc from the Sun. The galaxies in the NGC 3109 association all have similar Galactocentric distances of 1.3--1.4 Mpc, and Leo P is also consistent with this distance range.

sources.summary: The paper includes Leo P as a recently discovered extremely metal-deficient dwarf galaxy valuable for primordial helium regression, finding it consistent with the qualifying dataset.

quotes.quote: Finally, we also include analysis of the recently discovered extremely metal deficient dwarf galaxy Leo P leop . Because of Leo P's low metallicity it is particularly valuable in determining Y _p , and its best fit solution and regression parameters are given in table table:LeoP . Leo P satisfies all of the same quality and reliability criteria as our qualifying dataset, and including it in a regression with the qualifying points returns an intercept of 0.2463 0.0090 and a slope of 97 115. Leo P agrees very well with the regression determined by the qualifying dataset alone (eq. eq:Yp ), and as a result, the regression is essentially unchanged by the addition of Leo P, except for a small decrease in the intercept's uncertainty.

sources.summary: The paper presents first stellar spectroscopy of the low-luminosity dwarf Leo P, highlighting its very low oxygen abundance, massive-star content, prominent H II region, and H-emission rings.

quotes.quote: We present the first stellar spectroscopy in the low-luminosity ( M_V 9.3 mag), dwarf galaxy Leo P. Its significantly low oxygen abundance (3 solar) and relative proximity ( 1.6 Mpc) make it a unique galaxy in which to investigate the properties of massive stars with near-primordial compositions akin to those in the early Universe. From our VLT-MUSE spectroscopy we find the first direct evidence for an O-type star in the prominent H II region, providing an important test case to investigate the potential environmental dependence of the upper end of the initial mass function in the dwarf galaxy regime.
The MUSE data also reveal two 100 pc-scale ring structures in H emission, with the H II region located on the northern edge of the southern ring.

gmw_00002542.name: VCC 1684
gmw_00002542.overview: VCC 1684 is a dwarf early-type galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:1410.1552]. It exhibits emission lines, an asymmetric rotation curve, and a dynamical mass that is average for its class [arXiv:1410.1552].

sources.summary: VCC 1684 is described as an asymmetric-rotation dwarf early-type with emission lines and average SMAKCED dynamical mass; despite no confirmed two dispersion peaks, it is considered a candidate 2 galaxy.

quotes.quote: We do not have evidence for two velocity dispersion peaks in VCC 1684. If these features are not along the photometric major axis of the galaxy, the long-slit spectroscopy presented in this series of papers would have missed them. However, we do classify VCC 1684 as a poor fit (i.e. significant departures of the rotation curve from the best fit Polyex function) and asymmetric rotation curve (i.e. the approaching and receding sides of the rotation curve have different shapes; see Paper II for details).

In addition, we also find emission lines in VCC 1684. Its dynamical mass within the \ is within the average mass of the SMAKCED sample ( M _e = 8.9 M _ ). These properties suggest that VCC 1684 is a good candidate to be a 2 galaxy.

gmw_00002543.name: J093844
gmw_00002543.overview: The active galactic nucleus J093844 is considered a leading candidate for hosting a supermassive binary black hole [arXiv:1908.01799]. Its radial velocity curve shows a systematic, monotonic change of several hundred kilometers per second [arXiv:1702.05465, arXiv:1908.01799].

sources.summary: The paper treats J093844 as a leading SBHB candidate, fitting its emission-line profiles with nearest-neighbor models and finding relatively high mass ratio and constrained optical depth.

quotes.quote: The objects SDSS J093844, J095036, and J161911 are of interest since they have been highlighted by runnoe17 as the most promising SBHB candidates in the sample, based on the properties of their radial velocity curves. More specifically, the radial velocity curves of these candidates show a statistically significant monotonic change in radial velocity over the duration of observations. As shown in Figure fig:cutoff , the nearest neighbor profiles in the synthetic database provide a good description of the observed profiles for J093844, J095036, and J161911, as reflected by their average quality indices, QI = 0.44 , 0.59 and 0.69, respectively.

Figure fig:individualtauF2F1 shows the PDFs for the same three SBHB candidates in terms of the optical depth parameter, _0 , and the ratio of the H emission-line flux contributed by the secondary and primary mini-disk, F_2/F_1 . The value of the optical depth parameter is relatively well constrained for J093844 (left panel) and J161911 (right) and it peaks at _0 1 .

From the three binary candidates considered in this section, J093844 (left) and J161911 (right) have relatively high inferred mass ratios, q 0.7 , and consequently, the fluxes emitted by the two mini-disks are comparable.

sources.summary: J093844 is one of three monitored SBHB candidates with monotonic velocity changes of several hundred km/s, making it a promising high-priority target for continued spectroscopy and host-galaxy imaging.

quotes.quote: The above cautions notwithstanding, we would like to draw attention to three objects in our sample, J093844, J095036, and J161911, which have shown systematic and monotonic velocity changes by several hundred kms s ^ 1 . These represent the most promising cases in our sample and deserve further attention. They are high-priority targets for continued spectroscopic monitoring as well as additional observational tests, such as high-resolution imaging of the host galaxies in search of morphological features associated with a merger.

Based on our spectroscopic monitoring, of these 10 objects there are three (J093844, J095036, J161911) in which the radial velocity curves show monotonic variations by many hundreds of kms s ^ 1 , incompatible with a single BLR bound to a recoiling BH.

gmw_00002544.name: J095036
gmw_00002544.overview: J095036 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1908.01799] and a promising supermassive black hole binary candidate [arXiv:1908.01799, arXiv:1702.05465]. Its radial velocity curve shows a systematic, monotonic change [arXiv:1908.01799, arXiv:1702.05465], and the source exhibits rising high-frequency radio fluxes [arXiv:2011.08872].

sources.summary: The paper highlights J095036 as a promising SBHB candidate whose variable narrow profiles reduce parameter degeneracy but leave optical depth more degenerate than in the other candidates.
